Placed twice back in 2024; comeback run in April wasn't devoid of promise but lot more needed.

Bumper winner is 0-9 over hurdles, best form on soft; promising Kilbeggan chasing debut in May and fair third in Downpatrick handicap on final start for Gordon Elliott; headgear left off here on yard debut back hurdling.
Latest Kilbeggan run only mildly encouraging, lots more needed.
Very ordinary form in maidens and no sign of progress so far in handicaps; first-time cheekpieces need to bring serious improvement.

Sole hurdles win came over a year ago at Clonmel (2m4f) off 111; tried several times in claiming hurdles this year, notably C&D second in May, reportedly found to have blood in nostrils last month when just fair fifth in Downpatrick handicap; has to be treated with some caution but down to a nice mark.
Both wins have been on decent ground (2m); absent since halfway faller at Navan in November 2024 so likely to need this comeback run.
Best handicap run when fourth at Roscommon last year but well below that level twice after and dropped out quickly on seasonal return last month at Sligo; blinkers tried.
Won 2m1f Clonmel handicap in April (yielding), couple of ordinary runs since off revised mark, albeit hampered at same venue on latest; bit to prove now.
Off the mark over C&D in May last year (good) and second in similar event subsequently off 99; recent Clonmel comeback run should have brought him on and one for the shortlist.
Useful Flat form and not much to show over hurdles until much improved for C&D win in late May under more positive ride, clear some way out; 9lb rise for that so could well have more to offer.
Three minor wins on the Flat; has struggled in maiden hurdles, but possible improver now switched to Gordon Elliott on handicap debut.
Lightly-raced 5yo mare was never sighted on handicap debut at Downpatrick; tongue-tie now tried.
Placed over this trip at Bellewstown last summer (good) off 80, badly hampered when pulled up on comeback run at Listowel in late May so that run forgiven; blinkers now tried.
Maiden winner whose standout effort in handicaps when C&D second in May; didn't land a blow in better-class Listowel contest on latest but respected back in grade here.
Long-standing maiden over hurdles but numerous placings, including good second in 2m3f Punchestown lady riders' contest last month; has his chance again, encouraging jockey booking.

Attracted attention of Stewards over C&D in May before much-improved showing when favourite at Sligo last month, every chance when second over 2m5f (good); back in trip here and has to be on the shortlist.
Unexposed 5yo entering handicaps on the back of three poor runs in maidens; hard to fancy off this mark but worth a market check.
Tramore maiden winner in 2024 has largely struggled in handicaps; absent since modest fourth in Tramore Flat maiden last August so likely best watched.
Maiden has struggled off stiff mark in opening handicaps; down 4lb but remains of limited appeal unless first-time tongue-tie brings serious improvement.

Veteran finally made the breakthrough in 2m1f Bellewstown handicap in April and duly followed up at Down Royal off 5lb higher; solid C&D run since off further 8lb higher mark when chasing home a clear-cut winner; contender again.

Point-to-point winner in October 2024; no impact in a trio of maiden hurdles at 2m-3m in the spring of 2025; modest efforts back in point-to-points when last seen in October/November; others make more appeal.
12-1 and never landed a blow on debut in a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle (22.5f, good) 39 days ago; in good hands but may need more time.

Best effort in three point-to-points a 13l second at Tullaherin and the winner of that race has since placed in a bumper; pulled up at Toomebridge on latest, though, and is likely best watched on rules' debut.
No signs yet of winning potential in five bumpers and three runs over hurdles; beaten 63l in mares' hurdle at Kilbeggan latest; rated just 79 and hard to make a case for on form.
Getaway mare is bred to have a future over hurdles, but she was pulled up in both point-to-points; can only watch for now.
Triple-figure odds for all three starts in maiden hurdles and ran accordingly; moves up in trip; seems safe to rule out.
Point-to-point winner at the fifth attempt at Dromahane in April; soundly beaten at long odds in two bumpers and a Sligo maiden hurdle on latest; hard to fancy.

Strong winter maiden hurdle form; disappointed on handicap debut at Fairyhouse in April but runner up returned to maiden company at Cork (24.5f, good) on latest; blinkers replace cheekpieces; rated 117 and sets the standard.

Tailed off on debut at Fairyhouse (2m4f, yld-sft); sent off at just 3-1 upped in trip on better ground at Punchestown (23.5f, gd-yld), but weakened late and beaten 18l into fourth; overlooked by O'Keeffe; needs more.
Long odds and no impact in bumpers at Clonmel and at this venue; best watched stepped up markedly in trip for hurdling debut.
Always near the back before pulled up on debut at Punchestown when 66-1; cheekpieces go on.

Smart hurdler (rated 143) at his best, albeit soundly beaten in competitive handicaps final two starts last term; bit laboured in his jumping on chasing debut at Clonmel in April (soft), but has had time off since and no surprise if he returned with an improved display; has to be considered on favoured ground.

Winning hurdler over 2m4f on heavy for Gavin Cromwell last season; pick of five attempts chasing when outstayed late on by a well-handicapped rival in 2m7f Limerick handicap at Christmas (soft-to-heavy); not so good following month at Navan and given a break since; ground likely on the quick side.
Dual hurdles winner last year at about 2m4f; good bit of chasing experience, notably third in 2m6f Galway beginners' last summer and second in 3m Clonmel novice; fourth in this race last year.

OwnerName: Mrs N Flynn & John Joseph Flynn
Spotlight: 123-rated hurdler whose best form is on softer ground; unseated on last month's chase debut at Punchestown when leading; can't be ruled out if putting in a clear round.
Dual hurdles winner on good ground rated 99; ground to suit, but lots to find with several of these now switched to fences.
Bumper and maiden hurdle win came on very testing ground at Navan in February (2m); didn't count on last month's chase debut at Kilbeggan, but entitled to come on for the experience.

118-rated hurdler won Bellewstown maiden and Kelso novice late last summer on good ground; none too fluent and pulled up on chasing debut at Kilbeggan in late May, so needs to jump much better; yard going well and has his ground.
121-rated hurdler made a promising enough start to chasing when third in 2m7f beginners' here in May, not quite getting home under positive ride; shorter trip here could suit, but more needed.
Downpatrick handicap hurdle winner last summer is rated 95 in that discipline; absent since October and this looks a stiff task on chasing debut.
Point-to-point winner and 126-rated hurdler, winning 2m6f Downpatrick maiden and 2m4f Perth novice last year; below form when last seen in October, but respected on chase bow.

Very useful staying hurdler, winning 2m4f handicap here and 3m contests at Listowel and Leopardstown last term; career-best when last seen finishing third of 24 in 3m Listed handicap at Punchestown festival in April; closely related to yard's good chaser San Salvador, so good chance he'll take to chasing; trip on the short side, but has won around here before so greatly respected.

Dual hunter chase winner did fine in handicaps last term, placed on three occasions including when going close at Leopardstown (2m5f) in March; although flattered, it was a nice run in a Listowel conditions event last time and can go well off a light weight with good ground no issue.

Has won three of his last four chases, two of them coming over C&D, and was impressive when making all in Grade 3 handicap at the Punchestown festival last time; ground to suit again and little to dislike, apart from rising mark.

Three wins over hurdles, 1-14 over fences; second twice over fences on decent ground at Musselburgh in January, over 2m7f on the latter occasion, and his hurdles run at Kilbeggan last time wasn't bad; most of his better runs have been right-handed, though.

Highly tried and has cut little ice, including thrice at Grade 3 level, since winning a 2m novice at Roscommon last summer; appeared to run well behind much higher-rated rivals in conditions contest at Listowel last time, but marksman left him alone; capable of being competitive in first handicap.

Good record at this venue, including C&D win in 2024; 10-1 for Mayo National at Ballinrobe in late May after an encouraging run over hurdles, but was tailed off; chase mark is dropping and capable of going well back here.

Beat smart Oscars Brother in 3m hurdle here when with Gavin Cromwell; maiden over fences but his second of 20 in the Galway Blazers (2m6f) at last summer's festival showed what he's capable of; back from a long break and this smacks of a Galway prep, with yard also having likely favourite in this.
Jumps and travels well but is a weak finisher with a good few seconds to his name; positive is that his sole chase win came after four months off and ground will suit, but seems best right-handed.
Point and bumper winner; off the mark on second chase start in useful five-runner novice over 2m4f at Punchestown; hung right at Limerick the time before, and going left-handed may not be ideal.
Useful hurdler/chaser for Gordon Elliott a few years ago; modest enough form in points and hunter chases since joining this yard in late 2024.

Comes from the point-to-point/hunter chase scene and won 2m7f hunters' on good ground last year; 80-1 when pulled up in the Cheltenham festival hunter chase in March; hard to weigh up and only a 6yo, but prefer to watch.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won big-field Leopardstown 2m4f handicap hurdle at Christmas meeting in 2024; fourth to stablemate Driveonwill in a beginners' at Downpatrick; 3l off runner-up A Snow White Park in another beginners' last time and nice pull at the weights with her; second handicap run and mark looks exploitable.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Didn't go on over hurdles after big-priced debut win; has run well in both chase starts at Tramore, not beaten far in 2m handicap, and 2m6f may have stretched her in a beginners' last time; not sure about racing left-handed, but she does jump left.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Veteran with poor strike rate, but has found a bit of form lately culminating in a win over nearly 3m at Downpatrick; a quick look at his lengthy race record reveals that he hardly ever runs left-handed, though, which has to be a for a reason.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won a point on yielding ground in March; well held in hunter chases since; not crying out to be suported on handicap debut.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Unplaced in 14 starts, four of them over fences, since winning 2m3f Kilbeggan handicap hurdle two years ago; glimmer of promise over hurdles three runs back, poor since.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Second in sole point and in a maiden hurdle here; good start over regulation fences when staying-on third in Downpatrick handicap over this trip.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Modest maiden pointer and unable to be competitive in three hunter chases; hard to make a case for him; tongue-tie.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Sole hurdles win came here, under regular rider, last summer, and placed three times over flights later in 2025; only had three completed starts over fences, best of them his Downpatrick second to subsequent easy winner Dinoland over nearly 3m; early exit last time; this trip seems closer to his optimum.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Three hurdle wins have all been right-handed, but has been placed here; good run behind an improver last time; chase debut and said to jump well at home.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Second veteran runner for northern yard; winner over 3m1f here but that was in 2020; lightly raced and poor form in recent years; they say Darragh O'Keeffe is game enough to ride anything, and here's the proof.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Flat winner was regressive over hurdles before finishing second, from out of the blue, at Down Royal over Christmas; hasn't backed that up and now tries chasing; second-string for local yard.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 16-race maiden over hurdles, albeit his sole placed effort came here over 2m1f last summer; didn't jump well when pulled up on only chase start.

RaceName: Save The Date 7th August Ladies Day Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Point winner and placed in pair of hunter chases last year; around 8l off the old boy Fr Gilligansvoyge over nearly 3m at Downpatrick, his best effort in handicaps so far; change of headgear.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Placed in two of his four starts; Evens fav at Tramore, keen early and onpaced late when beaten 2.5l on good ground; this looks a stronger contest.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Authorized half-sister to four winners is out of a half-sister to Big Buck's; beaten fair and square when favourite for Tramore debut but the winner had solid form; hood stays on.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Ballylinch-bred Make Believe filly is from family of US 1m Grade 1 winner Jack Milton; totally unfancied for Punchestown winners' contest last month, but ran very well despite being too free and pulling herself into the lead, beaten less than 4l.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Nice Newtown Anner Flat pedigree but Fascinating Rock gelding cost buttons at two; nothing in first two starts but really getting the message late on when coming home well for third at Kilbeggan, just behind Mistakenforanother.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Flat-bred daughter of Free Eagle; improved from Clonmel debut when second of 14 at Kilbeggan, just half-a-length in front of the staying-on Tale Of Intrigue.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Order Of St George gelding; unraced dam is related to several useful bumper and hurdle performers, out of smart bumper/2m-3m hurdle winner Refinement; bred to go well in this type of contest.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: 100-1 when last in a Naas bumper in December.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Mishaps twice since promising debut in Roscommon bumper in September, achieving highest RPR of any of these when second to a decent sort who went on to win twice over hurdles in Britain.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
OwnerName: Call It What You Like Syndicate
Spotlight: By Lulamba's sire, Nirvana Du Berlais; dam won over fences in France, half-sister to smart 2m-2m2f hurdle winner Rolling Star.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Elusive Pimpernel 6yo; dam 1m2f/1m5f Flat and 2m hurdle winner; remote second in a point last year; rules debut.

RaceName: Racing Again 22nd July INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Wore a hood and tongue-tie when 15-2 for Punchestown debut in January, keen and went off into a clear lead before weakening to be beaten 24l.

AQPS winner; joined Willie Mullins; Punchestown second in February was quite promising; soundly beaten in the Fred Winter; costly failure at Cork; has left Mullins to join Gordon Elliott; looks vulnerable in this clash with his former stablemate Beauvallon.

Flat maiden in France, where he had his first run in a race won by his stablemate Daryz; made a couple of mistakes when opening his hurdles account at the second attempt; again untidy at times when coping well with a marked step up in class to win a Punchestown festival conditions race; sets a high standard.
Showed ability on sole start on the Flat in Germany at Munich (1m2f, soft) last October; made a satisfactory introduction to hurdling when third in a maiden at Roscommon; tough task here.
Has returned to form on the Flat recently, making the frame in handicaps at Fairyhouse and Down Royal; weak form in two juvenile hurdle runs last year.

Ex-Ballydoyle; won Lingfield Derby Trial last year but well held in Irish Derby and Irish St Leger; bought for 78,000gns and soundly beaten on yard debut in Grade 2 Leopardstown hurdle at Christmas; tried in a tongue-tie here and plenty on his plate in what looks a hot Listed race.
Weak form on the Flat; tailed off on his only start over hurdles; safe to rule out.
Modest form on the Flat (rated 63); has shown no aptitude for hurdling on his first three starts.
Placed in four of seven bumper starts and in all three races over hurdles; weakened after a mistake at the last at Sligo; should be sharper for the run, his first since last November.
Placed in three of his ten runs over hurdles and fourth over C&D in April; carried out when favourite at Clonmel; stable's Flat string is in excellent form.

OwnerName: Trevor Payne/One In A Million Syndicate
Spotlight: Now 0-10 over hurdles, placed twice; probably his best run when second in a C&D maiden in April; unplaced since in a handicap won by the in-form Malbay Madness who runs in the following handicap on the card.

OwnerName: Mrs P Crampton & Oliver Crampton & DJCC Investments Limited
Spotlight: Showed some promise in maidens at Punchestown during the winter; best effort so far third over C&D in April; needs to find a little extra.

OwnerName: P J Morgan & Mrs S L Morgan
Spotlight: Dam half-sister to dual-purpose star Wicklow Brave; not seen since odds-on bumper defeat at Tramore last August.

Twice placed in juvenile hurdles; good chance if forgiven a dismal run in a competitive handicap hurdle at the Easter meeting at Fairyhouse; should be in prime shape for this after taking third place in a charity event at the Curragh last week.
Five-time Flat winner in Britain at up to 1m2f; not at the same level for this yard, including two races over hurdles.
Never showed with a chance first time out in a bumper at this venue in April.
Beaten favourite for a Kelso bumper and soundly beaten in two maiden hurdle outings.

Bumper winner at Thurles last October (good-to-yielding); soundly beaten in Listed bumper at Navan when testing ground may not have suited; can be rated a likely future hurdles winner after Ballinrobe second in May.
Nothing in her three bumper outings to suggest she can make her presence felt.
Fell on his first two outings, always towards the back when 66-1 at Punchestown last time.
Placed twice and other fair efforts in ten bumper outings; posed no threat on hurdles debut.
Half-sister to the top-class Monkfish; limited encouragement from three bumper outings.
Pulled up in sole point-to-point; outran odds of 200-1 when weakening late into a 12l sixth of 17 on hurdles debut at Clonmel; failed to build on that at Punchestown.

Three Dundalk wins up to 1m4f and some decent efforts in defeat over the winter season; towards the rear in a 1m2f Navan handicap in April; two undistinguished juvenile hurdle runs; plenty to prove against that background.
Course winner on Flat in April; third behind Spy and Becasse when favourite at Listowel just over a month ago; every chance of featuring again.

C&D winner last summer during a spell of good form (Flat and hurdles); unplaced in two runs on Flat since a fair sixth at 100-1 in a valuable Fairyhouse handicap hurdle in April; one place behind Putapoundinthejar then.
Fifth behind Malbay Madness at Downpatrick last month; should be sharper for the run, his first since signing off a good spell of form with a narrow Galway win last autumn.

Three-time winner over hurdles; career highlight a win in Red Mills Auction Final at the 2025 Punchestown festival last year; has run creditably in good handicaps; staying on when falling three out at the Punchestown festival.

Has suffered mishaps over fences lately, equipment failure at Tramore, hampered and unseated at Punchestown; second in this race in 2025; could be interesting in first-time blinkers; however, Fiver Friday appeals as the stable's main hope.

Won a handicap at the Galway festival in 2023 and a Punchestown novice chase in December 2024; ran only twice last season; higher rated as a chaser; bright display when third in a conditions hurdle at Wexford in May; could go well here.
Good winner of a Leopardstown maiden hurdle (2m, soft) in March; handicap form is less persuasive, though the form of a Tramore third was boosted by Eastmore here on Thursday.
Gained a third Kilbeggan win in May before broadening his horizons with a Downpatrick success last month; up an aggregate 13lb; may continue on the upgrade.
Handicap chase winner three starts ago; won a big handicap hurdle at last season's Galway festival; probably Ballybrit-bound again; Fiver Friday appears to be the stable's main hope.

French 2m2f hurdle winner on very soft in October 2024; back from an absence to finish second in a Wexford handicap (soft); unseated at Killarney; overhauled by Spy after making most in a 2m Listowel contest, Putapoundinthejar in third; possible contender.

Good form for Shane Nolan in 2024; missed 2025; better again early this year for this stable, backing up Down Royal win with decisive 2m2f Leopardstown success; was 2lb wrong in a strong handicap at Fairyhouse at Easter.
Three wins on the Flat and now 2-16 over hurdles after beating Becasse and Putapoundinthejar at Listowel; up 4lb, could feature again with Josh Williamson's claim.

Useful on the Flat for John Murphy, 2-4 and rated 101; failed to prosper as a juvenile hurdler for this stable after a winning introduction at Navan last November; completely tailed off in the Fred Winter; plenty to prove at this stage.

Has lost way since a fine second in the 2025 Topham; four non-completions from five starts; recent return to hurdling at Kilbeggan failed to spark a revival in form; competes off 13lb lower than chase mark.

Two wins in April, one of them over C&D, the other a good display to beat the Irish Lincoln winner Ribee over 1m2f at the Curragh; solid fourth of 20 in a premier handicap at the same venue in May; had an excuse (struck into) for a below-par Down Royal run; one of three runners for the stable; strong overall credentials.
Former smart bumper horse; career went downhill after C&D maiden hurdle win in 2023; soundly beaten over a longer trip than this at Kilbeggan last month.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Not much promise in a maiden hurdle and bumper last year; has improved, third in maidens at Cork and Downpatrick; in between was challenging when falling at the last at Limerick; 107-rated mare needs to raise her game to win here.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: W N Cotter & R W Cotter
Spotlight: Best of four runs when second in a 2m1f Wexford maiden hurdle last August; led for a long way before getting outpaced in a 2m bumper at Tramore five weeks ago; this trip may be more to her liking.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed in four points, though the form did not amount to much; tailed off last in a Clonmel bumper; showed mild promise with a prominent run at Tramore on hurdles debut, weakened into third.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed in two points and won a Limerick bumper in 2022; returned from almost three years off the track at Hexham in March and was pulled up; remote tenth at Punchestown last month; no sign that she retains her former ability.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: Claire Wylie & Mrs Orla Cleary-O'Kane & Mark McGuckin
Spotlight: Placed in a four-runner point-to-point in March; beaten 33l when fifth of 24 in a 2m Punchestown maiden hurdle on rules debut; subsequent Downpatrick bumper run was less promising.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Good pedigree, showed promise when unfancied on Tramore debut, beaten around 7l in fifth in a fair 2m maiden won by 116-rated gelding, keeping on steadily; failed to improve on that at Downpatrick.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Remote ninth in a maiden hurdle at Kilbeggan; considerable improvement required.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Record of 1-9 in points; sole win in a three-runner race, beat today's rival Georgia Rose; pulled up on final start last season; same fate on hurdling debut at Downpatrick.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Down the field in two maidens at Punchestown; will need to qualify for a handicap mark.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Fell at two out when in the lead in a Stowlin point won by Bambino Fever in May 2024; made amends with a 14l victory next time at Ballindenisk before being bought for £100,000; failed to make an impact in two bumper runs last season; plenty to prove.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Runner-up on sole point start; fair form in bumpers, notably when second at Cork in March; the better of two maiden hurdle runs when a staying-on third over 2m at Fairyhouse in April; outpaced in a 2m2f bumper at Downpatrick in May; this trip should suit better.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
HorseName: Uno Me I Like My T right
Spotlight: Poor overall form including in two maiden hurdles; huge price when fell in a novice chase at Punchestown on latest.

RaceName: Johnny's Race Day Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Half-sister by Jukebox Jury to point/3m hunter chase winner Snap Fish; dam unraced sister to useful hurdle/chase winner Musical Slave; likely to need experience.
Huge odds and well beaten in maidens; pulled up in last four handicap starts; others preferred.

Second runner for yard; much improved when runner-up (albeit beaten 15l) in Wexford maiden hurdle (2m4f, good to yielding) in May; led as far as four out when pulled up before the next on handicap debut at Sligo; others preferred.

Broke her duck over hurdles in style at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) last month when pulling clear by 10l, making all; 8lb higher mark to contend with and won't get as soft a lead here but she's still respected.

Promise in two of her six runs last season, including when fifth in this contest on good off 7lb higher; too keen and soft ground legitimate excuses for below-par effort on yard/seasonal debut at Limerick; worth monitoring.
Placed in a bumper for Nicky Henderson; nothing in four runs for Noel Kelly; has shown improvement in recent starts and continues to slide down the weights; each-way contender.

Two fair efforts in maidens at Downpatrick on good after low-key debut; solid fourth when dropped to 2m at Kilbeggan 19 days ago, making good headway approaching two out but unable to reel them in; top rider booked for handicap debut; player up in trip.
Four-time AW winner at 2m; sole hurdles win in June 2024 at Kilbeggan (2m) off 2lb higher; always in rear over timber at Wexford on penultimate and tailed off on the Flat at Limerick on latest.
Well beaten in a bumper and five maiden hurdles; handicaps should suit better but still difficult to warm to.

Winless in seven starts for Gordon Elliott; sole win for Adrian Murray at Downpatrick (2m3f, good) off 87 around this time last year; fair debut for this yard at Sligo last month after a break; market will reveal expectations.

Showed little in maidens; always at the back at Tramore on handicap debut but massive improvement at Limerick when second of 18 (2m4f, soft; beaten a neck); not guaranteed to back that up on quicker ground from 4lb higher.

Won Punchestown handicap hurdle (2m4f, good) in October off 87; okay chase bow at Downpatrick before first try at the trip when third over fences at that venue four weeks ago (3m1f, good to yielding; 4-1 favourite), outstayed late on; contender back hurdling.

Winner of handicap hurdles last term at Roscommon and Downpatrick before recording wins over fences at Fairyhouse and Haydock; hit-or-miss of late but runner-up of 14 over hurdles at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) three weeks ago; can go well.

Some promise in Downpatrick handicaps in 2024 and best effort last year when runner-up at Kilbeggan (2m2f, good to yielding) in April; returns from 436-day absence but handicapped to go well if she retains all of her ability.

Has improved with each run, promoted to third at Limerick in May after the winner's disqualification and beaten around 4l into third at Sligo (17.5f, good) last month; in the mix back over a more suitable trip.

Best form is over around 2m4f, including when runner-up in Down Royal handicap in March; two poor efforts followed but ran into a solid fifth over 3m at Punchestown last month and could build on that.

Placed in two of three starts in bumpers; chased home the progressive Raise You Up at Limerick on hurdles debut; fortunate victory on third hurdles start when left in the lead at final flight at Listowel (20.5f, good to yielding) in May; first try at this trip but fair opening mark; considered.
Bumper winner last June; gained first hurdles win in good style at Ballinrobe (2m6f, good) on latest; the third has since won over fences; up 7lb but step up in trip is no problem; should go well.

Dual-purpose performer is 3-21 over hurdles with all three gained in an eight-day period in August last year; has been ticking over nicely on the Flat without winning of late; could make his presence felt.

Dual-purpose; won at Sligo (17.5f, good) on handicap hurdle debut last July; made the frame on the Flat a couple of times this year; unseated rider at the first when joint-favourite at Downpatrick last month; can't be ruled out for local trainer but big question marks over this trip.

Seven-time winner, including four over fences; made all to win Wexford handicap chase last August; runner-up in the 4m1f La Touche Cup after a break at Punchestown in May; struggled off this sort of mark when last seen hurdling.

Hurdles/chase winner ran his best race in some time when never-nearer fifth of 12 at Kilbeggan (24.5f, good to yielding) 19 days ago; fairly treated on the pick of his hurdles form; each-way contender.
Best maiden effort when runner-up at Downpatrick (2m6f, good) last August; three runs for next yard offered little; starts out for Claire O'Connell now with plenty to prove.

His three hurdle wins came at Fairyhouse on good ground at 2m4f-3m but nothing since late 2024; some cracking runs in defeat though, including when third off 108 at Cork (3m) in April; tailed off in this race last year.

Shaped with promise in maidens but serious jumping issues evident on handicap debut at Clonmel on return in May; still early days for top yard but will need to cut out the mistakes; this trip may help.
Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner (24.5f, good to yielding) last summer; hasn't been in good form over fences of late, lacking fluency in the jumping department; handicap hurdle debut.
Lightly raced since winning back-to-back 2m4f handicaps in 2023; encouraging comeback run at Kilbeggan but pulled up on latest at Punchestown; the jury is out.

OwnerName: A Trappe & Declan Trappe & Garry Trappe
Spotlight: Dual hurdles winner for Arthur Moore; something amiss on final run for the yard at Sligo when weakening with a circuit to race; first run for Gordon Elliott; market will reveal expectations; blinkers.

OwnerName: Keep Er Lit Syndicate/Donal McArdle/Pink Pony Club Syndicate
Spotlight: Punchestown bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; 150-1 and tailed off on debut for this yard in Grade 3 Red Mills Hurdle at Gowran in February; well beaten in Roscommon handicap last month; cheekpieces.
Placed in two of three point-to-point starts; midfield in first two maidens before runner-up at Tramore (2m6f, good) on latest; workable opening mark and is high on the shortlist.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Drew clear for a 9l success at Ballinrobe (good) last May and that form has worked out well; back from an absence when eyecatching fourth at Listowel (2m4f, yielding); trip was a stretch on that occasion but big player over this distance.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding was well-backed for debut when third to Dee Bee Zed at Punchestown, finding less than what looked likely in the closing stages; Patrick Mullins takes over so entitled to be bang there.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to 2m1f hurdle winner Fourtowns; dam made frame in two bumpers, sister to 2m4f hurdle winner Load Up Time; stable 0-5 in bumpers; best watched.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Weakened quickly in Tipperary bumper last summer but returned from close to a year off the track when landing a gamble at Kilbeggan (good to yielding) on first run for this yard; high on the shortlist.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Top yard but cost only 2,000Euros as a foal; raced too keenly when well beaten on Down Royal debut in May but no surprise if he improved given connections.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Pulled up in mares' maiden point-to-point in April; eyecatching run on debut at Kelso, travelling strongly and only lost second in dying strides; could be a player.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Pulled up in two point-to-points a year apart; sent off at 50-1, clipped heels and slipped up approaching halfway in the race won by Dee Bee Zed at Punchestown.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Gave yard first bumper win in five seasons when landing 50-1 shock at Punchestown (good to yielding) four weeks ago, with several of these behind; there seemed to be no fluke about it and he's of obvious interest for local yard under a penalty.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Failed to trouble the judge in four point-to-point starts; ran well for a long way under positive tactics when around 7l behind Dee Bee Zed at Punchestown four weeks ago; each-way player.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Tailed off on heavy ground in Naas bumper on debut in February when 100-1; could improve for better ground but can only be watched.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Cost £36,000 in February after finishing third of six in heavy-ground maiden point-to-point at Ballinaboola; made a successful start for Gordon Elliott at Wexford (good to yielding) in May; yard won this last year with Stede Bonnet.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Tailed off in a Punchestown bumper in May when 40-1; seems safe to rule out; visored.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Dam unraced half-sister to smart bumper and 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Listen Dear, out of unraced half-sister to smart 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase winner Tempo D'Or; purchased for 100,000Euros after comfortably winning mares' maiden point-to-point at Ballindenisk in May; fascinating runner.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Placed on point-to-point debut for respected producer Ellen Doyle in February 2024; pulled up at Portrush next time; beaten out of sight in two bumpers.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Third foal; dam failed to finish in three points, half-sister to bumper and 2m1f-3m hurdle/chase winner Inaro; stable 0-82 in bumpers.

RaceName: J.H. McLoughlin Oil INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Made modest late headway from the rear when sixth of 11 on debut at Leopardstown's Christmas meeting; regressed at Thurles on soft in February; big step forward required here.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Unique Retreats, P Morrall & J Blackburn
Spotlight: 160,000euros 2yo; dam 6f-1m winner (RPR 101), half-sister to winners Partitia (6f) and Palmetto Bay (German 1m3f-1m4f), out of a 6f winning sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Showcasing; tongue tied; this son of Blue Point can't be discounted, especially if the market vibes are positive.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: £50,000 yearling; dam, 5f AW winner (RPR 55), half-sister to useful 5f-7f winner Run Boy Run, out of a 5f winning half-sister to Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner Smooch; this Sands Of Mali colt is in very good hands and rates an interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Cotai Glory gelding; hinted at promise with a debut sixth over C&D in May but only 11th in 6f York seller 22 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Invincible Army colt; closely related to winners Disillusion (Spanish/French Listed-placed 5.5f/6f; RPR 103) and Inca Dream (Slovakian/Czech 5f/6f) and also a half-brother to KSA 6f/7f 2yo winner Thenayaan; dam unraced; the market can prove an accurate guide for this newcomer.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 18-1, last of five in maiden at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) on his debut 24 days ago; lots more is required from this son of Perfect Power.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 12-1 and hooded, very slowly away when fifth of seven in maiden at Musselburgh (5f, good) on her debut in May; this daughter of Caturra should be all the better for the experience.

RaceName: Rob Over The Hill EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Never dangerous in maidens at Thirsk (5f) and Leicester (7f) this summer; significantly more is needed from this son of Lope Y Fernandez.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 6-1, promising start with fourth of ten in novice at Thirsk (7f, good) 34 days ago, racing wide and fading only late on; that form has been franked so this son of Palace Pier rates a big player with better to come.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 55,000euros yearling; closely related to 1m AW winner Handshake (RPR 70) and half-brother to three other winners, notably Habana (6.5f-1m German 1,000 Guineas; RPR 110); dam 5f and 6f (Italian Group 3) winner (RPR 102); this Sea The Stars newcomer must enter calculations, especially if the market speaks in his favour.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Palace Pier colt who was much improved from his debut when second of seven in C&D novice 18 days ago; he can do better still so needs considering.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 35,000gns foal; dam Listed-placed 1m4f winner (RPR 101); the betting can prove an accurate indicator for this son of Saxon Warrior.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 16-1 and he ran green when last of five in novice at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) on his debut two weeks ago; this Too Darn Hot colt is in very good hands and can build on it here.

RaceName: Matthew Ireland Memorial EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 50,000euros yearling; Space Traveller filly who is a half-sister to three winners, notably Cowardofthecounty (6f/7f Group 3; RPR 105); unraced dam is out of a half-sister to 2,000 Guineas winner Footstepsinthesand; a considered newcomer, although yard also has Rosberg in here.

Bayside Boy gelding who has been brought along steadily in his three runs, a never-dangerous sixth of ten dropped to 5f in novice at Windsor last month; appeals as a likely improver stepping up markedly in trip for his nursery debut with cheekpieces added; interesting.
Mohaather filly who took a big step forward to gain a first win (at the fifth attempt) in 7f Musselburgh seller 12 days ago; still not taken lightly now going into nursery company.
Has cut little ice in a trio of outings, last of nine in 6f Redcar novice two weeks ago; lots more is required from this daughter of Mattmu on her nursery debut.
Mohaather filly; only poor form shown thus far, eighth of ten in 6f Leicester maiden last month; cheekpieces go on for her handicap debut.
Wells Farhh Go filly; only modest form shown in her three starts to date, eighth of 11 in 7f Chester seller three weeks ago; needs this switch to handicap company to spark improvement.

Dual Group 3 scorer at Sandown (1m2f) and Goodwood (1m4f) in 2025; resumed from six months off with a very good second of six in Newbury's John Porter Stakes in April so this veteran looks the one to beat dropped into Listed company here.

Low-mileage 5yo who made it 3-4 when winning a 1m4f handicap at Meydan in December; posted a good third in Group 3 there in January but last of six in Group 1 there (1m4f) in March; yard has an excellent record in this so he's a likely player after a break.
Very useful gelding who landed 9.5f handicap in Meydan in March; posted a good fourth in Goodwood Listed event in May but last of six in similar company there (1m4f) last month; needs to bounce back.
Got back on the up when gaining a second handicap victory of 2026 over 1m2f here last month; this demands a significant step forward though.

Looked on the up when landing a pair of handicaps (around 1m3f) at Kempton and Yarmouth last summer; below that form in 1m4f Listed event at Goodwood and Group 3 at York (1m6f) in May; cheekpieces added; faces another tough ask.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Julie & David Martin, Dan Hall & Partner
Spotlight: Gained a first win in 1m handicap at Hamilton in May and backed it up with a solid third of six in similar event there 17 days ago; this Palace Pier filly can go well again off the same mark.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Gamely gained a breakthrough win at the ninth attempt in 7f Carlisle handicap 19 days ago; this consistent sort must enter calculations despite a 2lb rise.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Fair juvenile maiden who posted a solid fifth on her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f) in May; had too much use made of her when ninth at Sandown (7f) last month and no surprise to see this Mehmas filly get back on track here with cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: A fair ex-Irish 9.5f winner; yet to score for her current yard and she weakened last 1f when fifth of eight in 1m1f handicap at Hamilton in May; drops back in trip now with cheekpieces added and she's not out of things.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Hold My Beer, Gee Gees Racing & N Clark
Spotlight: A fair 6f scorer as 2yo; holding her form well without winning this season, fourth in 1m Newmarket handicap last month; ought to be thereabouts once more.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Bated Breath filly who returned from six months off with a good third of nine in 7f Thirsk maiden 29 days ago; the first two have gone in since so she merits serious consideration back up in trip now going handicapping.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with 1m success at Southwell but she's failed to build on it, only sixth of seven in 7f Catterick handicap 12 days ago; needs to take a step forward.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Has thrived for the move to Marco Botti and gained a third success of 2026 in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap in May; only fourth of six at Lingfield (1m2f) last time but this likeable front-runner is the sort to bounce back.

RaceName: Weatherbys Global Stallions App Coconut Cup Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Miss D Finkler, A Herd, Dr P Holloway 1
Spotlight: New Bay filly who was placed on her first two runs (around 1m) at Southwell and Epsom; too free since though, just sixth of ten on her handicap debut at Nottingham (1m) last month; she remains with potential if proving more tractable here.
Won back-to-back 1m handicaps at Nottingham and Ripon in April and continues in good form, runner-up in 1m1f handicap at Musselburgh 12 days ago; can go well again nudged up 1lb.

Ended a long losing run over C&D in May and posted another solid effort (in a refitted hood) when fourth of 14 in 1m Carlisle handicap ten days ago; this keen-going sort is not out of things off an unchanged mark.
A three-time C&D scorer; right back to his best of late when runner-up in 7f handicaps at Wetherby and Thirsk, caught only late on latter occasion; up 2lb but another bold showing is on the cards.
A four-time scorer over 8.5f here (including twice in May); recorded another very good effort with second of six to Tattie Bogle over C&D 11 days ago; very much one to consider nudged up 1lb.
Scored three times at 7f/1m on the AW in 2025 but his more recent form is far less encouraging, last of eight in 1m handicap at Nottingham last month; visor goes on the first time here.
A three-time 7f/1m winner in 2025; mainly well below par this season though, only 14th in 1m1f York handicap last month; has it to prove at present.

Ridden more patiently than usual when gaining a second success of 2026 over C&D in June and backed it up with a very good second of ten in 7f Thirsk handicap three days ago; a player off an unchanged mark.
Keen-going type who is winless since 2024; not proving the easiest to catch right this season too, beating just one in 1m Carlisle handicap ten days ago.

Clear second from the front off this mark back up to 7f at Leicester in May, despite hanging right late; maybe found it coming too soon, or wasn't suited by the return to the AW, when running poorly a week later; one to consider for a yard that's 5-9 here.
Regressive maiden (5f-1m) who's been tried in two forms of headgear this season; well-held fifth of nine at a big price at Ayr last time and he doesn't make obvious appeal.

Race didn't set up for her when a pleasing third on her turf debut in the spring (first-time tongue-tie) but she hasn't gone on from there; headgear is given a go after she hung left last time and she needs to improve for it.
Generally consistent since a Wetherby dead-heat in the spring off 2lb lower; today's stiff 7.4f should be fine and he looks set to go well once more.

Consistent spell over 6f, following up her second to Truly Special at Carlisle with a win off 2lb lower last time; didn't look to see out 1m at Redcar a couple of times in the spring and stamina remains a postential issue over this testing 7.4f.

Related to some useful winners, mainly at around 6f, but has shown very little to date; not been seeing it out over further, though, and starts handicap life at a lowly level coming back in distance; look for market support.

RaceName: Title Sponsor Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Three runs in course handicaps feature a win (soft) last June and two seconds (1m1f/1m), latterly when claimed late by Hostility on good ground 19 days ago; solid.

RaceName: Title Sponsor Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
OwnerName: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
Spotlight: Not beaten far on his last three starts and it was a solid fifth of 14 in the 1m Carlisle Bell (first-time blinkers, retained) ten days ago; handicapped to be competitive again.

RaceName: Title Sponsor Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Sole win from 22 starts came at Cork in April 2023; went very close over hurdles here in March and was second of ten behind an improving filly at Ayr (1m2f, soft) a fortnight ago; can go well after 1lb nudge.

RaceName: Title Sponsor Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Four wins this year include a C&D success (Hostility second, Jewel Maker fourth) in May and when again making all to beat five others at Musselburgh (1m1f, also good) 12 days ago; creeping up the weights but she's gone back-to-back before and needs respecting.

RaceName: Title Sponsor Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Back in form when second to Krissy over C&D in May and he built on that when getting a fine ride to make his breakthrough here (1m, again good; Bellasio second) 19 days ago; not the easiest but little doubt this new mark still underestimates him.

RaceName: Title Sponsor Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: His close fourth at Newcastle (1m, AW) on his reappearance in March would make him a threat off this mark but it's been downhill since; finished well beaten in this race last year.

RaceName: Title Sponsor Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Dual course winner (including at this trip) whose best run this term came when fourth to Krissy over C&D on his penultimate start; didn't build on it over 1m here next time, though.

RaceName: Title Sponsor Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Patchy form in Irish handicaps last year but her third-place finishes at Killarney (1m3f, good) in May and Ballinrobe (9.5f, good to yielding) put her in the picture; got going too late over 1m6f when last seen in August; requires a market check on this stable debut.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Improved effort when just denied on his handicap debut at Windsor (1m) in May and he made no mistake with an emphatic win over that C&D (also good to firm) 12 days ago; raised 10lb but he's firmly on the up and looks the one to beat.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: 0-14 for this yard but he went very close at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in February and again in April; last month's return to turf at Hamilton (1m1f, good to soft) wasn't up with those efforts though.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Yet to win a handicap but she finished second at Catterick (7f, good to firm) 12 days ago; profile suggests a repeat isn't guaranteed but possibilities if she does back that up.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Three runs here include a neck defeat by stablemate Where's The Crumpet (1m, good to soft) last May and when taking a division of this race (soft) under Gina Mangan last year; that remains her only win and she's been off since April; market may help to decipher if this has been the target.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Both wins came under a 7lb claimer in Ireland and it wasn't a bad effort under Ruby Criscuolo when beaten a shade over 2l at Southwell (1m3f, AW) in May; moderate fourth at Pontefract on Monday, however, which took his record to 0-15 for this yard.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Hit a good vein of form on AW earlier in the year, winning three times including over this sort of trip at Wolverhampton in March; 0-11 on turf but it was a fair effort when fifth at Doncaster last June and he's not ruled out.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Moderate form in six starts, including in two handicaps last month; new cheekpieces have their work cut out.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Two runs here feature a defeat of Top Gun Tina over C&D (good to soft) last May and a fourth in a division of this race last year; return to this venue will need to prompt a revival after some moderate efforts, however.

RaceName: Style Awards Fashion Finale Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
OwnerName: James And Susan Cookson And P Kirby
Spotlight: 22-race ex-French maiden; might have needed her first start since February when down the field at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) last month but that will need plenty of building on.

RaceName: Sheila Petch Appreciation Handicap (For Professional Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Scorched through last summer, winning seven times at 6f-1m on a range of surfaces; back on last winning mark when close fourth here (7f, good to firm) on penultimate start and wasn't disgraced in big-field York handicap three weeks ago; cannot be ignored.

RaceName: Sheila Petch Appreciation Handicap (For Professional Female Jockeys)
OwnerName: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood
Spotlight: Sole win came when justifying short odds in 1m AW maiden in December but he's been running with credit in big-field turf handicaps this term, including when fifth of 20 at York (7f, good; Jannas Journey eighth, The Green Man behind) three weeks ago; likely to go well again.

RaceName: Sheila Petch Appreciation Handicap (For Professional Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Last of his three wins came over C&D (good to firm) just over two years ago but he arrives off the back of three solid placed efforts, including when second over C&D ten days ago; mark is creeping back up but he needs considering.

RaceName: Sheila Petch Appreciation Handicap (For Professional Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Not won on turf since his 2yo days but it was a fairly close third at Musselburgh (7f, good) in April; now 4lb lower after four lesser runs; handicapping case can be made but percentage call is to oppose.

RaceName: Sheila Petch Appreciation Handicap (For Professional Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Won four times last term but he hasn't added to that haul since scoring at Windsor (1m, good to firm) around a year ago; did go close under Laura Pearson at Redcar (6f) off this mark in May, however, and wasn't beaten far when sixth in Newmarket handicap (7f) last time; sole previous attempt in this headgear was inconclusive (didn't stay 1m2f).

RaceName: Sheila Petch Appreciation Handicap (For Professional Female Jockeys)
OwnerName: The Dalby Family And P Kirby
Spotlight: Seven wins include three on turf and it was a first success at 7f when scoring at Newcastle (AW; stays 1m) in December; took a good step forward from his return to turf when a keeping-on fourth at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; respected.

RaceName: Sheila Petch Appreciation Handicap (For Professional Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Good-ground 7f Newbury handicap winner off 1lb higher for Charlie Clover last term but, after rejoining Jane Chapple-Hyam, he found Meydan a struggle in the winter; weakened to be beaten 8l in amateur riders' event at Chester (7f, good to soft) three weeks ago but that was his stable debut after three months off and he's down to a career-low mark; might step forward so watch the market.

RaceName: Helen Stewart Appreciation Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Five-time winner who hasn't won since 2023 but she's run with credit on many subsequent occasions, including on her only visit here when second under Joanna Mason in this race last year; back in form on her last two starts and she's in calculations.

RaceName: Helen Stewart Appreciation Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
OwnerName: A Sinclair, David Carroll & G Gibson
Spotlight: Won a Navan handicap for Joseph O'Brien last spring but suffered a succession of fairly hefty defeats on the Flat and over hurdles for this yard, including when beaten nearly 20l over C&D last month when back from a nine-week break; visor given a whirl.

RaceName: Helen Stewart Appreciation Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Had only won once since the autumn of 2023 until he fired off a hat-trick under Lauren Young up in trip this May, culminating with C&D success (good; made all) off 4lb lower five weeks ago; the Cumberland Plate proved a bridge too far here ten days ago but this is easier.

RaceName: Helen Stewart Appreciation Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Sole win from 22 starts came at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) over two years ago; first go for this yard when close fourth of 14 at the Curragh (1m4f, good to firm) last Friday; that was an encouraging start and he's due a 1lb nudge in future.

RaceName: Helen Stewart Appreciation Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Landed a third Flat win when taking Hamilton amateur riders' handicap (1m5f, good) around a year ago; now 2lb lower but he's been soundly beaten on both starts this year (1m4f Catterick handicap, 1m7f Hexham handicap chase) and needs to stage a revival.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Stylish Stayers Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Thorough stayer whose wins have all been at about 2m; arrives in reasonable form and has placed on two of his three C&D visits; might be the one to follow Laravie home.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Stylish Stayers Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Four-time hurdle scorer and has collected three Flat wins this spring (1m6f-2m1f); turned over at very short odds at Yarmouth (1m6f, good to firm) 16 days ago but the emphasis in that three-runner race was more on speed than ideal for a mare who has won over just short of 3m over timber; can show she's still on a good mark back at 2m+.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Stylish Stayers Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner for David O'Meara but hasn't really threatened to add to tally on the Flat or over hurdles for this yard, including back on the level last month.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Stylish Stayers Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Back in good form this season, most recently when making all to win by over 6l at Musselburgh (2m, good) last month; well-held third in Chester 0-80 since but this is more his level.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Stylish Stayers Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: A three-time winner at up to 2m under varying tactics; two respectable efforts at Ripon this spring but weakened quickly and tailed off at Pontefract latest; can only watch after that.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Stylish Stayers Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Placed at Catterick (1m6f/2m, good) on first two outings this year but this 10yo needs to shrug off a lesser run over 1m6f here 19 days ago

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Won at Catterick over 7f last year and she doubled her tally when scoring over C&D (good) last month; solid third under a 5lb penalty (effectively this mark) six days later and she's firmly in the picture.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Modest win record but he scored at Doncaster (6f) off 5lb higher around a year ago and it was his first go in this grade when running well for second over that C&D (also good to firm) a fortnight ago; Faye McManoman knows him well; respected.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
OwnerName: L Bond/Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi/Ptr
Spotlight: Two AW wins to his name and his close second over C&D (good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) in May was up with his best turf form; back on that latter mark after two lesser efforts so can't be readily dismissed.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Won a 5f AW event off 6lb lower in February and there was no disgrace in chasing home a Hamilton legend at that track (6f, good to firm) in returning blinkers (retained) nine days ago; he is another that cannot be discounted.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Dual 6f winner in Ireland; 0-8 for this yard but he wasn't beaten far despite finishing fifth of six at Hamilton (6f, good to soft; acts on quicker) last month; mark continues to come down and that latest effort suggested he may be rediscovering his level; possibilities.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho
Spotlight: Four-time winner who acts on most surfaces and was very consistent on turf (6f/7f) last term; opened this year's account when making all in amateur riders' race over C&D (good to soft) under Amy Collier last month; not far off that form when fourth off this mark a fortnight ago; should be thereabouts.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Three wins on AW (6f to 1m) but the last of them was well over a year ago and she's mainly struggled since, including when beaten 5l over C&D (soft) four weeks ago; that was just her second run of this term, however, and there's a handicapping case to be made.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
Spotlight: Made a belated debut in May and he created a monumental shock two starts later when scraping home at 200-1 in Ayr maiden (6f, soft) a fortnight ago; trainer subsequently wondered if that ground was important so interesting to see how he acts on this forecast quicker surface; handicap debut.

RaceName: Caledonia Park Designer Dash Handicap (For Pro-Am Female Jockeys)
OwnerName: H S Hutchinson And W Glass
Spotlight: Southwell maiden (6f, AW) winner in late 2024 but she hasn't placed in five turf handicaps and latest fifth of 11 at Pontefract (6f, good; Invincible Ruby third) will need improving on; career-low mark, though.
Two good runs and two lesser ones this season, failing to improve for a sixth furlong/drop to selling company at York three weeks ago; dangerous if at his best.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late G E Dudfield
Spotlight: 33-1, slowly away and green in the race when sixth of eight in a 7f maiden here three weeks ago; should prove a different proposition in time; stable also run Dream On Kent.
Modest form in two maiden runs last month; new blinkers need to have a galvanising effect.

Fourth foal; half-brother to winners Sourire Secret (1m; RPR 66) and Claim That Smile (7f; 64); dam 1m 2yo winner (stayed 1m6f; 66), half-sister to 1m-1m4f winner Barwick; one of two newcomers for Stuart Williams.

Beaten favourite in a 5f novice at Southwell on his debut in May; blinkered for his second run, showing a bit of improvement when third of the six runners in a 6f maiden at Chester last week; drops in grade but improvement is required.

Down the field on debut but he ran well when second in a 7f seller at Chester (good to soft) last month; visored (absent today) when second in a 6f novice at Chepstow last week; one of two runners for the trainer who won this race in 2022 and 2025.
Disappointing when odds-on for a maiden at Musselburgh (5f, good) on Tuesday; his third-place finishes at Musselburgh and York beforehand were solid displays though and he's a leading contender.
80-1 and ran to a modest level in a maiden at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago; no appeal.

8,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Evelyn's Phoenix (7f-1m2f; RPR 72) and Racing Ray (Slovakian/Serbian 6f/8.5f including 2yo); dam Austrian 5f-7f winner, sister to Maxi Boy (Group-placed 6f 2yo); one of two newcomers for the stable; betting to guide.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Third in a useful 7f novice here on debut last August (ran green); heavily backed and won easily on his seasonal return at Yarmouth (6f, soft) three weeks ago; looks the one to beat.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Two Catterick runs this year, finishing third on debut (7f, good to firm) before winning over 6f (good to soft) last month; open to further progress and he should make another bold bid.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to a Group 3 winner; justified market support when overcoming inexperience to win a 6f maiden at Lingfield (AW) in January; easy to back and ran poorly at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) in April, after which he was gelded; tongue tied today; dangerous if back to the debut form.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Good pedigree but she has a run to a modest level in two 6f fillies' novice events this summer.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 33-1 and little short-term promise in a 5f AW maiden last month.

RaceName: Smooth Radio EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Huge prices and tailed off in two runs over further last month.

Ex-Irish filly who's got it together since upped to this trip back on turf, winning easily at similarly undulating Chepstow last weekend; may yet have more to offer and is shortlisted off a 6lb higher mark.
Never looked like getting close to Beryl's Girl at Brighton on her first crack at this trip 12 days ago; she settled better that day and is 7lb better off but has work to do to turn things round.

OwnerName: Paul K Spencer And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Struggled since winning a Polytrack maiden for the Crisford team last August and has come more than a stone down the weights for his current yard this season; goes beyond an extended 1m for the first time and is hard to recommend.

Started handicap life on an unrealistic mark after winning his first two on Tapeta and struggled last year; found his level again in the visor lately, chasing home a subsequent winner before finding 1m4f beyond him last time; one to consider.
Getting on and it's been a while since she won on turf; arrives in fair form but she only holds each-way claims dropped another 3lb.

Goes well here but hasn't won in two years and become expensive to follow, finishing a beaten-favourite in two runs back; soft ground may have been behind his latest effort but he was withdrawn twice in May on account of good to firm, which is a worry.

Stamina doubts on pedigree upped to this trip last time but they proved unfounded, as she made all round Brighton in first-time cheekpieces; that wasn't much of a race and she's 5lb higher but she remains relatively unexposed.
Longstanding maiden who's done most racing over further, latterly 1m6f; not a bad effort in first-time cheekpieces on Polytrack in May but she's had any number of chances.

OwnerName: Gee Gee's Racing & Hold My Beer
Spotlight: Said to have been unsuited by very soft ground last time; today's stiffer test will suit on pedigree (out of a 2m winner) and she's worth tracking in the market now handicapping at a lowly level.

Half-sister to a useful sprinter who's finished well held in three runs spanning the best part of a year (7f-1m2f); starts handicap life off a basement mark but would want to see some support before considering her.

Two AW wins (7f) this year and she ran as well as she ever has when second of ten in a handicap at Carlisle (7f, good) 19 days ago; having a rare 6f run but she isn't short of speed and her usual hood is now discarded; one to take seriously.
Two 5f AW wins last year; didn't get home on soft ground at Haydock (6f) when last seen in May and she is still to convince she wants this far.
Benefitted from the return to sprinting when popping up in a 6f handicap at Newmarket two weeks ago; that effort is backed up by the Topspeed figure and she is a key player despite a 4lb rise.
Won a 5f soft-ground nursery at Bath last September; runner-up in 5f handicaps at Goodwood on her last two starts, looking ready for the return to 6f; each-way claims.

Ffos Las (6f, good) win on debut; close fifth in a 7f AW handicap in January represents her best form but two heavy defeats have followed; has her first run in a 0-70 and with a tongue-tie added; not solid.
Eight-race maiden who is still to improve on her debut effort two years ago; not beaten far in two Class 6 handicaps this year but this demands more.
Improved her RPR with each of her three starts, most recently when third of 12 at Lingfield (7.6f, soft) four weeks ago; cheekpieces (worn on second start) return for her handicap debut; unexposed.
She has won a 7f handicap (0-55) here in each of the last two seasons; well held in a Class 5 handicap at Newmarket last time though and likely vulnerable once more.

Windsor maiden winner (6f, good) for the Kublers last July; ended last season on a low note but returned for new yard with a rallying second at Goodwood (6f, soft) three weeks ago; up 3lb so needs to raise her game.
Down the field in two handicaps (6f, soft and 5f, good) last month; edging down the weights and now tried in cheekpieces; could still have more to offer.

Big prices in three 5f AW runs for Grant Tuer in the spring but she improved run by run, finishing second of eight at Southwell when last seen in April; 6f and turf should suit and she's one to consider on her stable/handicap debut.

Didn't have to improve to reward odds-on backers in a 7f maiden at Catterick in May; creditable fifth in a 1m handicap at Goodwood two weeks ago and the return to 7f in a slightly weaker race should see him on the premises.
Ended his 2yo campaign with a pair of nursery wins; unplaced in two runs in 2026, including tried in blinkers three weeks ago; drops in class in new headgear; not a solid option.
Won twice over 7f as a 2yo; 22-1 and looked as though the run would bring him on at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; cheekpieces first time) two weeks ago; career best required all the same.
Second over C&D on his stable/seasonal debut in April; not quite so good at Haydock one month later but still early days and the C&D form is solid enough; high on the list.

Well-bred filly but proving expensive to follow, beaten at 11-8 or shorter in three of her five starts; didn't get a clear run at it at Thirsk 18 days ago and still wasn't beaten far; up in class but likely to make a bold bid.
Two wins at Wolverhampton (7f) since joining Ivan Furtado, the second of them off this mark in a small field in May; well held in two Class 4 events since; needs to raise his game.
Won a small-field novice at Newcastle (6f) on her debut in November; unplaced in both handicap runs; the switch to turf/return to 7f needs to spark some progress.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
OwnerName: MDP Racing And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Didn't progress in six runs for the Gosdens and the handicapper has been in command for new yard in 2026; latest Redcar effort can be upgraded though and he holds each-way claims off his reduced mark.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Won a similar race over C&D last summer when rated 2lb higher; he has finished second in two of his last three starts, the latest on AW nine days ago, and another big run looks likely.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Bred to be much better than this mark but she has run to a modest level in her four starts, tailed off on her handicap debut (1m2f, good to soft) in May; not a solid option.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Started the campaign with solid 1m efforts at Lingfield (AW) and Bath (firm); disappointed back at Bath last time but no surprise to see him bounce back in this uncompetitive event.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: She has recorded a peak RPR of just 43 in her five starts, albeit her latest fourth at Bath (1m, good; cheekpieces first time) offered hope of a bit better; others are still more appealing.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Should be well handicapped but he has suffered three heavy defeats for his new yard; apprentice takes off a useful 5lb but there are risks attached at present.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: No immediate improvement for the switch to handicaps when fourth of eight over C&D three weeks ago; still has time to do better.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Exposed ten-race maiden; not matched her 2yo form this year but she's dropping down the weights and cheekpieces are now given a go.

RaceName: Appliance City Bunny 'Confined' Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Achieved her best finishing position when third of nine at Wolverhampton (7f) last month but she was still beaten over 7l; new trip may help but she will need to settle better.

Had plenty of racing but she arrives in good form, following her breakthrough Doncaster win (6f, good to firm) with a good second at Pontefract 13 days ago; still feasibly weighted and she is more appealing than many.

OwnerName: A Bangs & Miss J A Challen
Spotlight: Five wins in 2025, including C&D, but rather going through the motions this time around; showed a bit more spark with his front-running sixth at Kempton in May; on a manageable mark and the type to come good again soon.
His form dipped at Newmarket two weeks ago but it was a Class 5 and he had earlier been in fine form on both AW and turf; likely to bounce back quickly.
A change of headgear saw her run out a comfortable winner over 7f here nine days ago, her third turf win since joining James Owen; can start slowly but a 4lb rise isn't beyond her.
Not progressing but he has been more competitive off his reduced mark on his last two starts; others still bring more compelling claims.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Sioux Nation filly; E200,000 breeze up buy in May; dam 5f/6f 2yo winner (inc AW; RPR 84), half-sister to winners Cupid's Glory (7f 2yo Group 3), Clinical (8.5f Group 3), Courting (1m2f Listed) and Prescription (6f Listed), out of 5f/6f winner; likely type on paper.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: No Nay Never filly was sent off at 7-1 on debut at the Curragh (6f, good) and weakened late into an 8.75l fifth behind well-fancied stablemate; the drop back to 5f should suit on that evidence and yard's 2yos often improve plenty second time out so not dismissed.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Mehmas filly; E135,000 yearling; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 85), half-sister to winners Smash Williams (6f 2yo Group 3), She's Trouble (6f 2yo) and Royal Entry (Listed-placed 10.4f/1m4f); from good family and respected source; can go well on debut.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Blackbeard filly was sent off the 6-4 favourite on debut over C&D last month but weakened late into fifth; likely to improve for that initial experience and McMonagle retains the ride; capable of better.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: No Nay Never filly; E110,000 yearling; half-sister to winners Mount Kosciuszko (Group-placed 1m-1m1f inc UAE dirt; RPR 100) and Peak Earnings (US 7.5f/1m turf); dam French 5f/6f 2yo winner (inc Group 2; 108); appealing pedigree; market can guide.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Minzaal filly; E52,000 foal, E160,000 yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to North American Graded stakes-placed 5.5f 2yo/7f winner Appraise, out of 1m1f Listed winner; yard in sparkling form and rookie sire has made a decent start; check market.

RaceName: EMS Copiers Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Not beaten far when fourth of six on debut at Cork (5f, soft) but didn't build on that when soundly beaten over C&D (good) on her next start; stable jockey prefers the chances of My Irish Lady.

Seven-time sprint winner, landed a C&D handicap off 97 in April, making all to beat Indigo Dream by 2.5l; shaped well when third in Listed company over C&D in May (Likedbymike in fourth); beaten favourite 20 days ago when fifth at Cork (Likedbymike second) but wasn't disgraced and likely to bounce back at a track he enjoys.

Improved plenty on three juvenile runs in two starts this year, finishing runner-up to the useful Namiid, pulling well clear of third, at Navan (5f, y) before landing Cork maiden on good in straightforward fashion by 1.5l to Sparky Sparky 20 days ago; bare form still a level below what's required here but should continue to progress.

OwnerName: Mrs Michael J Byrne & Jerry P Murphy
Spotlight: Sole win in 18 starts came in Cork maiden in October 2024; produced career best over C&D in April when 0.75l second to subsequent Group 1 scorer Mission Central in Listed race; finished ahead of Keke when filling the same spot at Cork last month and line can be put through handicap run six days ago as gaps didn't come; leading player if not backing up too fast.

C&D winner courtesy of success in valuable Ballyhane Stakes last year; ran well here over 6f when fourth in handicap on return (beaten 3l by Keke) before landing handicap back to 5f at Navan next, pulling well clear with the second; 17lb worse off with Keke now and has to put a poor effort behind him at Fairhouse 29 days ago.

OwnerName: P J O'Brien, Mrs P Shanahan & M O'Flynn
Spotlight: Very useful two-year-old last year, second to Charles Darwin on debut before finishing fourth in Queen Mary; landed a Cork maiden in cosy fashion on return before finishing 14th of 15 in Listed company but still only beat 6l; travelled well before flattening out when seventh in competitive Ayr sprint 14 days ago on soft; open to improvement and can figure on these terms.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Smart colt; took an age to get up and beat Abletai over C&D in May but proved much sharper next time over 6f when second to Coventry winner at the Curragh; ran okay when beaten 7l into ninth after going off 5-2 favourite in the Norfolk 14 days ago; clearly the most likely winner dropped in class; 5f might be on sharp side but stiff track plays to his strengths.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Only glimpses of promise in three starts before improving markedly for return to 5f over C&D ten days ago, putting up an impressive 2.25l victory over April Mist under this rider having raced alone on the far side; form yet to be tested but she holds claims if building on it.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Placed in three maidens, best effort last time 27 days ago on first go on good ground when beaten 1.25l by Cleodolinda at Navan over 5f; tough ask to break maiden here; place claims.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Useful form in first three starts, showed good attitude to get off the mark at York when beating subsequent winner Divine Whisper by a short head; backed up quickly five days later in the Queen Mary and couldn't make an impact in 15th but didn't disgrace herself; not discounted back in calmer waters.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Kept on late to be beaten 8l into sixth on debut at Navan 27 days ago, Hanney Boy 6.25l ahead; hard to see her making an impact at this stage at Listed level.
