RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Took a huge step forward from moderate debut when running Carry The Flag to within a head 56 days ago over C&D (deadheating with the smart Sun Goddess in second); front three pulled well clear so rates smart form; winner has improved since so it could be difficult to turn the tables but he's a danger with further progression.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Showed ability on first two starts when fourth and third (latter a head behind Hanney Boy) at Dundalk and Cork (soft) before making all over C&D in May, form working out very well and Big Negotiator was 1.25l behind in third; below that when fifth in Lingfield novice 35 days ago, fading after being up with a strong pace; should bounce back and can be in the frame.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Promising debut when a short head second to Velozee at Cork (5f, soft) in April; well below that form when seventh here ten days ago, well held by Brazilian Diva on that run; needs to bounce back to hold any sort of a chance.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Space Blues colt, cost 82,000euros as a 2yo; closely related to 6f 2yo/1m Listed winner Thunder Beauty (RPR 109), half-brother to three winners inc Haku (prolific 1m4f AW; 101) and Quita (German 1m3f-1m6f); dam German 1m3f/1m4f winner; well-bred colt has tall order on debut but market check advised for shrewd connections that excel with 2yos; one of two for stable.

RaceName: Coolmore Stud Blackbeard Tipperary Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell
Spotlight: Ran to a useful level when second and third in two AW maidens but floundered in soft ground at the Curragh 76 days ago when last of six; return to decent ground will help but moderate place claims at best.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: John B O'Hagan & Mrs John Murphy
Spotlight: 5f Dundalk winner last season has yet to make much of an impact in handicap company; beaten 10l on latest at Fairyhouse (6f, good); eased 3lb and the drop back to 5f is a positive so not totally dismissed.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two-time turf winner in 2024, both at 5f on good to firm and good to yielding ground; is now 14lb below his last success and not in bad form when last seen at Dundalk in March; worth checking in the market on return to turf.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Best two runs in maidens came over 7f, including when beaten 4l here last September; underwhelming run on handicap debut at Cork (6f, yielding) later that month and off since; likely best watched on seasonal return and first try over 5f.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill
Spotlight: Gained fourth win of career on latest at Down Royal (5f, soft); effective on quicker ground so conditions no concern and has gone well here before but a 6lb rise makes things tougher (never won off a mark this high).

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-time winner including five on turf; last won over 6f (good) here in May 2025 off 65 and is 5lb lower now; went close in big field over this C&D (good) on penultimate; below par over 6f at Fairyhouse 3 days ago but could rebound back here with rider claiming valuable 7lb.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Nine-time winner including over C&D; no joy yet this season but ran better than the bare result from miles out of the handicap at the Curragh last weekend; this more realistic and lurking on a dangerous mark.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Cork in May off 2lb lower (5f, soft); struggled off this mark twice since, including when well behind Nouvel Espoir at Down Royal on his favoured easy ground latest; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Gained second turf win over C&D on penultimate, beating Nouvel Espoir by 0.5l off 4lb lower; disappointed off revised mark at Dundalk latest; return to this track a plus but must prove she can win off this mark.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner is 0-5 on turf and has failed to trouble the judge; below best in three runs this year at Dundalk and tongue tie now reached for.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success came in a 2yo maiden at Bellewstown in 2024; ran well at Dundalk in March but below best in first-time cheekpieces at Navan 27 days ago; blinkers re-applied now (ran poorly in them before).

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two-time AW winner is yet to score on turf (0-6); not disgraced over C&D (good) on latest and eased 5lb for that 4.25l defeat but more needed to gain first win on the grass.

RaceName: Lawlor's Of Naas Supporting Naas GAA Senior Football Race Day Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: A few placed efforts to his name, including in a Navan nursery (6f, yielding) last season off 9lb higher; bitterly disappointing in maidens at Down Royal (7f, good) and Cork (5f, good) the last twice; mark sliding but revival needed.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won over 7.5f on debut at Roscommon; not disgraced in 6f Group 3 at this venue in May but disappointed stepped back up to 7f at Fairyhouse on latest and McDonogh now jumps ship; however, bred to appreciate this longer trip and a repeat of her Group 3 run here would give her a place shout.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Blue Point filly; 58,000gns yearling, E100,000 2yo; fourth foal; half-sister to Qatari 5f-6.5f winner Temple Of Hera; dam 6f AW 2yo winner (RPR 76), half-sister to winners Electric Waves (5f 2yo Group 3) and Appealing (useful 6f-1m); speedily bred so stamina a query and thrown in at the deep end on debut; market can guide.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Excellent effort when runner up on debut at the Curragh (6f, gd-yld); failed to figure in the Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot latest (6f, gd-fm); now steps up in trip (half-brother won on dirt over 1m); too soon to write off, preferred by McMonagle and could go well.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Mehmas colt ran a promising race when a close third on debut at Leopardstown (7f, good) before justifying skinny odds at Fairyhouse (7f, good), keeping on well (form boosted by the third since); dam a 1m1f winner as a 2yo so this trip should be within reach; obvious chance for top yard.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Showed up well before fading into fifth of 10 on debut at Leopardstown (7f, good); pedigree suggests this trip might stretch here and major improvement needed upped in class.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Rectory Road Holdings Limited & Mrs A M O'Brien
Spotlight: Stable second string on debut at Gowran (7f, soft) and well beaten into third behind fancied stablemate; did shape like further would suit so the step up to 1m is a plus and his dam was a winner at this trip on good ground but major improvement required.

RaceName: Irish EBF Pat Smullen Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Modest debut run at Dundalk in April; runner up when 16-1 in class 2 novice at York (6f, good) but subsequently disqualified (misuse of whip); yard in form and the form of that York race is solid but whether he'll stay 1m is the question (mixed messages in pedigree).

Narrowly denied on debut over C&D (good); beaten 6l upped to 1m2f (good) when runner up at Fairyhouse 11 days later; that may have come too soon and she's been freshened up; leading form claims on that debut effort.

Beaten similar distances in maidens at Dundalk (10.5f) in January and at Down Royal (10.5f, soft) last month; half-sister to a fast ground winner so could improve for this sounder surface; could outrun likely odds.

Promise on debut at the Curragh last June (7f, good) and again on seasonal return at Gowran (1m, gd-yld); poor on yielding ground upped to 9.5f back there on latest but the drop back to 1m and return to better ground are causes for optimism here.
Soundly beaten in maidens at Leopardstown (1m2f, yielding) in April and over C&D (good) in May; new tongue-tie needs to eke out more.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Home-bred Night Of Thunder Filly; half-sister to three winners including Simada (French Listed-placed 10.5f/1m3f AW; RPR 92) and Simiyann (1m6f/2m inc AW; 82); dam placed 7f/7.4f (76), half-sister to 7f/1m Group 3 winner Sinawann; plenty of appeal on paper; check market.
Gleneagles filly; E23,000 yearling; third foal; dam Italian 7.5f 2yo (Listed) and 1m winner (RPR 99), half-sister to Italian 1m2f Listed winner Holy Water; nicely bred and market can guide on debut.
Huge odds and heavy defeats in all four starts; impossible to make a case for.
66-1 and tailed off when last of 10 on debut at Ballinrobe; may need more time.

OwnerName: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited
Spotlight: No impact in maidens but shaped with encouragement in two handicaps before getting off the mark in good style at Ballinrobe (9.5f, y) 12 days ago, htting the line strong to beat Sands Castle by 3.25l; 12lb rise is far from lenient but she's progressive and from a top stable so every chance of going in again with step up in trip to suit.

OwnerName: N J Hitchins & Mrs John M Weld
Spotlight: Ran abysmally on handicap debut at Gowran Park on return but fared better the last twice, finishing midfied both times around 6l adrift; doesn't seem particularly well-treated though and needs step up in trip to change fortunes.

Kept on to take fifth on handicap debut at Ballinrobe (9.5f, gd); stepped forward upped in trip at Limerick 24 days ago, finishing well again to be beaten 3.5l by well-handicapped rival; drop back in trip probably not ideal but capable of winning off this mark.

Didn't progress as a two-year-old last year; backed into favouritism on debut for this yard at Fairyhouse in May but couldn't make an impact; didn't fare much better up to this trip when well-held fifth at Down Royal on soft 14 days ago; probably capable of better but hard to fancy; one of two for stable.
Showed more stepped up to 9.5f on second start in handicap when 7l third to Sweet Baby Zou; form of first two home hasn't worked out however and he needs more.

Shaped well when fifth in nursery off 3lb higher at Gowran Park in October but returned with a desperate run at the same track last month; back to form when beaten over 6l by Rodeo Blues in fifth 12 days ago but needs to find some consistency.

Improved for switch to handicaps and step up in trip when 0.5l second at Roscommon (1m4f, y) in May, pulling clear of subsequent winner; beaten 8.5l into fourth 14 days ago but the return to a sounder surface may suit better and he's a leading player.
Best effort came when placed in Killarney (1m, gd) nursery off 68 last year; hard to recommend based on last three runs and first-time hood needs to be a game-changer.
Five AW runs have been largely poor bar a mildly promising effort in a nursery over a mile in December; off 155 days; tongue-strap goes on and steps markedly up in trip.
Nothing to shout about in four maidens from 7f to 1m2f; makes handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces to go with existing tongue-tie; market best guide as hard to assess.
Poor maiden form and beaten 14l and 10l on both handicap starts, merely keeping on one pace at Fairyhouse 29 days ago; down 3lb and needs more.

Best run for previous yard came when fourth in Down Royal nursery off 61 in September; struggled in four starts for new connections, including when soundly beaten in claimer 57 days ago; visor applied.

Has shown signs he's capable of scoring off this mark with fourth placed finishes at Gowran Park twice (7f, soft) and produced solid effort up to 1m1f first time tackling good-to-firm when beaten 2.5l into fourth at Leopardstown 16 days ago; shaped as if this trip will be well within reach and definite claims.

OwnerName: Hugh J O'Brien & Colm O'Brien & Ms Denise McCourt
Spotlight: Improved slightly on very moderate form when beaten 15.5l into fifth at Down Royal (1m2f, soft), bit keen; down 3lb and no sign of winning potential yet.
Small bit of promise when sixth on nursery debut at Bellewstown (1m, soft) in October; two poor runs this year.

RaceName: TRM Stay Cool Stay Sound Rated Race
Spotlight: Both successes have come at Cork with give in the ground; landed handicap by 1.5l 46 days ago, staying on strongly and beating some consistent older horses; quick ground would be a question mark but he's likeable and progressive.

RaceName: TRM Stay Cool Stay Sound Rated Race
Spotlight: Made a sparkling start to career when landing a Listed Fairyhouse race (6f, yld) on debut in September but failed to back that up when well-held fourth in Kilavullan and finished down the field in Group 3 at Chantilly over 5.5f on return 81 days ago; needs to show more back up in trip.

RaceName: TRM Stay Cool Stay Sound Rated Race
Spotlight: Off the mark at third attempt with straightforward Dundalk maiden win in April; excuses when ninth on handicap debut at this track after and bounced back to land conditions race at Fairyhouse (7f, gd) 29 days ago impressively in first-time tongue-tie; that's retained and he's proving progressive; the most solid option here and one of two for last year's winning trainer.

RaceName: TRM Stay Cool Stay Sound Rated Race
Spotlight: Looked potentially very smart last year, beating Havana Anna in a Cork maiden before landing a Group 3 at the Curragh and finishing runner-up to Composing in the Debutante; ran too freely when last in Group 1 Moyglare Stud Stakes and similar tale when she finished 12th of 13 on her return at Leopardstown; bit to prove now in first-time tongue-tie but has plenty of ability.

RaceName: TRM Stay Cool Stay Sound Rated Race
Spotlight: Looked potentially smart when showing potent turn of foot to score at Navan on debut over 1m2f on heavy; ran okay when beaten 6l into third by Nil Bua Gan Dua at Roscommon last time when tackling quicker ground; talented but drop in trip on good ground unlikely to suit.

RaceName: TRM Stay Cool Stay Sound Rated Race
OwnerName: N W T Dalzell & R P Behan & Mrs John Murphy
Spotlight: York maiden winner for Ballydoyle over 7f before two good runs in Listed company on AW over same distance; ran to a similar level on first start for new yard after break when beaten 2.5l into fifth by Charles Darwin in Committed Stakes at Navan; friendless in market and ran poorly at Newbury 49 days ago; gelded since and chance if reproducing that Navan run back up in trip.

Prominent racer who returned to form to win over C&D (good to firm) a fortnight ago and he followed up at Windsor (5f, good to firm) last Saturday, with the front two clear; up 6lb for last time and this is tougher, but he's clearly progressive; key player.

Won in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) at Goodwood (5f, good) in May and has continued in good heart, not beaten far when eighth of 16 in the competitive 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) before close third of four at Goodwood (5f, soft); he's in with an each-way shout.

Made it two wins from his 11 starts when scoring at Musselburgh (5f, good) last month and respectable fifth of 13 in good 3yo handicap at York (5f, good) last Saturday; each-way claims back down in grade.

Two 2yo wins (good to firm/good to soft) including 5f nursery at Haydock on final juvenile start, and reappeared with near-miss at Wolverhampton (5f, AW); excuses on both starts since, drawn wide at Chester then helping set the race up for the closers at Sandown; not ruled out each-way.

In good form on AW prior to respectable fourth at Sandown (5f, good) in April off 4lb higher; however, two lesser runs have followed; upset in the stalls and withdrawn at York on last intended start (22-1 at the time).

Made debut in April and showed promise among his first three starts before an improved showing on handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Leicester (5f, good) last month, when he comfortably made all; 8lb higher in a better race today but he could have more left in the tank.

2-2 in 2yo campaign, both wins coming last July (5f, good); then absent until 11-2 favourite and merely mid-division in competitive 3yo handicap at York (5f, good) in May; needs to leave that run well behind but she retains potential for her top yard; no surprise to see a big performance.

Impressive on debut at Nottingham (5f, good) last June and respectable eighth at Royal Ascot next time; underwhelming subsequently and well beaten at 66-1 in Royal Ascot handicap (5f, good to firm) on recent reappearance; the drop in grade could trigger a revival but she has something to prove.

Second to Magnetude at Chester (1m4f, good) in May on handicap debut; 10-11 for seven-runner race at Newbury (1m4f, good to soft) on latest outing but drawing 4l clear represented improved form and gave plenty of hope for 1m6f; up 7lb but considered for yard which also runs Hard To Believe.

Won two 1m4f handicaps, gamely making all at Chester on second occasion; pitched in at the deep end for Group 2 Queen's Vase at Royal Ascot (1m6f, good to firm) 17 days ago, in which he was a never-dangerous eighth of 11 at 100-1, but that's earned him a 9lb rise; proving up to that task probably requires further improvement.

OwnerName: Jayar Investments & Mrs L E Ramsden
Spotlight: Seventh to Arc Ole Ole when 2-1 favourite on handicap debut at York in May; made favourite again, however, in six-runner race here (again 1m4f on good) 15 days ago and always close up to win by a neck, finding plenty for sustained pressure; up 3lb but it looks as if 1m6f will suit him well.

Completed a hat-trick by scoring at York (1m4f, good) in May; 16-1 when dropped in from a wide draw in 19-runner King George V Handicap at Royal Ascot (1m4f, good to firm) 16 days ago and ran on well from out the back to finish fifth; raised another 4lb and this extra 2f poses a question but there's no sign yet of getting to the bottom of him.

Carries his head high; 7l fourth of 14 to Arc Ole Ole at York (1m4f, good) in May on handicap debut; bettered that form when staying on best to win a four-runner race at Sandown (1m6f, good) three weeks ago, but 3lb higher today and his sights are raised.

Best form when second on handicap/seasonal debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) in April; however, heavy defeats since in the Lingfield Oaks Trial, a Goodwood handicap and the King George V at Royal Ascot (80-1), all at about 1m4f, leave her with a lot to prove.

1m, heavy-ground winner as 2yo; emerged in second in Doncaster handicaps this season over 1m4f and an extended 1m6f, so he brings proven stamina and effectiveness on good to firm; up 3lb but more to give.

Progressive 3yo last season and she improved again to win a Listed race at Goodwood (1m4f, good) in May on her reappearance; this is tougher but the runner-up has franked that latest form and she could have more to offer for a trainer who excel with similar types; she's one to consider.

German 1m4f Group 2 winner who returned from an absence to finish second in two German Group 1s last autumn; reappeared with close Listed second at Chantilly (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago, a run she may improve for, and she's the one to beat this afternoon; a visor replaces cheekpieces.

3yo who was a Listed winner at Naas (10.3f, good) in May when upped in trip and went close in the Group 3 Jannah Rose Stakes there (10.3f, good) ten days ago, when 5l clear of the third; needs to prove her stamina for this new distance but she might be far away if seeing it out.

Lightly raced 5yo who was a Listed winner and Group 3 runner-up for the Gosdens last season; returned to form on second start for this yard when Listed fourth at Chantilly (1m4f, good to firm) three weeks ago, two places and nearly 2l behind Tiffany; each-way chance in first-time tongue-tie.

Went close in Listed race at York (1m4f, good to firm) last August before good third in Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster (14.5f, good to soft); underwhelming in fifth on both starts this season but she'll be a danger to all if rediscovering her best 3yo form; a tongue-tie is added.

Won two handicaps in the spring (7f/1m, AW/good to firm) and was short-headed in a Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, soft) four weeks ago under Jack Callan (who is again unable to claim his 3lb); she's a 3yo on the up and her dam won at up to 1m4f, but a further chunk of improvement is needed.

Runner-up in the Group 2 Ribblesdale at Royal Ascot (1m4f, good to firm) in June 2024; missed all of last season and not at the top of her game on either outing this term, but she took a step back in the right direction at York (1m6f, good) last time when not seeing out the trip; not ruled out.

Won at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) last October on her second run and a respectable third of six in Listed race at Newbury (1m2f, good) on reappearance; stumbled and unseated soon after the start when 50-1 for the Ribblesdale at Royal Ascot last month; her dam won the Oaks for the yard and she's open to further improvement, but it's necessary in today's company.

Listed runner-up at Gowran Park (1m4f, good) last July on fourth start; soundly beaten in Listed race at Cork (10.4f, yielding) last September and sold out of Donnacha O'Brien's yard for 360,000gns in December; lightly raced and she's one to watch in the betting in first-time cheekpieces on her stable and seasonal debut, but improvement is necessary up in grade.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won last March/April on first two starts for yard; took just one of 16 races since but that was penultimate start (Beverley 1m2f, good to firm) and last month's third of 16 off this mark in a Class 2 at York (1m2f) was as good as he's ever been; stays 1m4f; not the percentage call but not impossible.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Much improved last year, including after his second to Plage De Havre in this race at Haydock; York sixth to Klassleader on reappearance but back to his best when staying-on third of 16 at Royal Ascot (1m6f, good to firm) 18 days ago; needs respect.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Reliable and progressive on a variety of ground and courses, although has never raced at Newmarket; third in this race last year; hit new heights at Epsom (1m4f) on last two outings and makes each-way appeal.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fit from AW and hurdling when good second to Fierce Fortitude at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) in April, keeping on strongly; respectable fourth of 16 to a runaway winner at Epsom (1m2f, good to soft) four weeks ago and should be okay back at 1m4f, but this race looks a bit too hot; 5lb claimer is recruited.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Sole win from 15 races came in France (9.5f, AW) as a 2yo; sinking in the weights and well handicapped judged on plenty of 2025 French form, with a step back in the right direction when fifth of 14 at Sandown (1m2f) three weeks ago on his third start for current yard; cheekpieces go on and first crack at 1m4f looks interesting; a case can be made.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 2023 Royal Ascot winner who has also tasted the high life in top hurdle races; his attentions were turned back to the Flat for four races (UAE and Saudi Arabia) this January-March and he wasn't in peak form, before 12l tenth at Royal Ascot (1m4f; restless in the stalls) continued that trend; falling in the weights but needs a revival.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Made debut in June 2025 and has had just five races (beaten a neck on good to firm on second start); came from the back when second on handicap debut at Haydock (1m6f, good to soft) in September and again when eventually well on top at York (1m4f, good; Plage De Havre third) on reappearance this May; up 8lb but retains significant potential.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Often front-runner; won well over C&D on good to firm last July; somewhat back down the weights and in class when winning another Class 3 in seven-runner race at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm; made all) last Saturday in good style; back up 5lb for this Class 2 but each-way claims.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 10lb higher than when winning this race at its usual home Haydock (good) last July in great style; that was his final race last season but he put up a bold show when third to Klassleader at York this May; non-runner at Royal Ascot where the good to firm ground was deemed unsuitable (third of 12 on it at York last May); big shout if today's ground is okay.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Second on good to firm last June (much better form since); well held when upped to 1m4f for a hot handicap on final start last season; gelded subsequently and won over 1m3f at Southwell (AW) and Hamilton (good; by 5l in good style) in his two races this term; up 8lb and into a much tougher contest, back at 1m4f, but maybe he's putting it all together now.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who scored from Ammes at Redcar when upped to 1m2f in April; creditable third of ten in the Zetland Gold Cup there (again 1m2f on good to firm) in May off this mark; very useful dam stayed 1m6f, so this new trip for her son could be within reach; may have more to give.

RaceName: Betway Old Newton Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won 1m4f/2m handicaps on the AW early this year; beaten just over 7l in hot handicaps at Carlisle (1m3f) and Royal Ascot (1m6f) on last two outings, shaping much better than ninth of 16 suggests in the latter (big move to dispute lead early in straight) but his turf strike-rate is 3-37.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Formerly trained in Hong Kong; won at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in May on third stable start and reportedly unsuited by the good to soft ground when down the field at Newbury (6f) since; could bounce back.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Thirsk (6f, good) in April on second stable start and second of 20 at York (6f, good) in May, before respectable fifth of 14 at Thirsk (6f, good); uncharacteristically below par at York three weeks ago but not written off in view of his previous exploits.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: This C&D brought out the best in him last season with two close seconds in August (both on good to firm); returned to form with close fourth of 12 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month on his second start of the season and he could play a leading role back here.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Placed on first two starts of the season (6f, good) and another big performance when 3l fourth of 20 at York (6f, good) last time, which was his third quick run in succession in May; can go well for bang-in-form yard.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Won two in a row in May (7f, good to firm) and he returned to winning ways when back down in trip at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) eight days ago; this progressive 4yo could take a 4lb rise in his stride.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: His last three wins have come off a similar mark to today's over 6f on the Newmarket courses and he returned to form with a close third here (7f, good) a fortnight ago; every chance he'll be in the mix.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: In good form since returning from a break, winning at Windsor (6f, good to firm) in May before close third at Salisbury (6f, soft); there's still mileage in his mark on his old form and he could be in the shake-up.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: Two turf wins last summer and three AW wins this year, the latest at Wolverhampton in April; ran well when third of five at Salisbury next time but two lesser runs have followed and he needs to raise his game.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
Spotlight: 7yo who often ran well in the US, albeit winning just once (6.5f, firm) back in 2023; a market check is advised on his British debut but it's possible he's best watched.

RaceName: Betway Bet The Responsible Way Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Won at Hamilton (6f, good) last month on second start back from a break and the neck runner-up went one better there next time; he's used to plying to his trade in a lower grade but is not discounted up just 2lb.

Down the field in the Wokingham at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm) but was on the unfavoured far side and prior to that he ran well when fifth of 21 in the Victoria Cup there (7f, good); won over C&D last August; chance.

Won four in a row on AW earlier this year and beaten less than 1l when fourth of 11 at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) in May; went even closer when runner-up at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last time and has solid claims.

Pops up with the odd very big run, such as when capitalising on an easy lead at Chelmsford (1m, AW) last December; he's just 2lb higher today and is not ruled out but he's inconsistent and this is his first start since a heavy Group 3 defeat on dirt at Meydan in February.

Lightly raced since 2yo Group 3 win in France in September 2024; not at the top of his game last winter on his two runs for David O'Meara, and mid-division on stable debut at Newbury (1m, good) in May following a break; however, he faded on that latest start having failed to settle, and this drop back to 7f could be in his favour; 4lb lower today; possible player.

Beaten on his last 15 starts going back to May 2024 and only eighth on both starts this season; however, the latest result doesn't tell the whole story as he suffered a frustrating passage in the home straight at Chester; well handicapped on last season's best and he's one to consider.

Beaten on his last 14 starts and he's been expensive to follow in that time, but he was denied a clear run at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last month and again ran well when fifth of 19 at York (7f, good) last time; could be thereabouts.

He has a lofty mark to defy but he won two in a row in Bahrain in January (7f/1m, good/good to firm; including Local Grade 1) and after a break he caught the eye in the Royal Hunt Cup at Royal Ascot (1m, good to firm) last month, when he made late headway after being badly hampered; interesting.

He's 3lb out of the handicap and he wasn't quite at his best last time; however, he kicked off his campaign with two good runs in defeat and there remains a suspicion he may yet have a bigger performance in him.

OwnerName: Emma Banks, David Manasseh & Austin Whelan
Spotlight: Listed runner-up for Ger Lyons last May (7f, good) but well beaten on final two starts for that yard and same story on last month's stable debut in the Royal Hunt Cup at Royal Ascot; may be best watched for now.

Had a good 3yo campaign last season and she was beaten just a neck on the other Newmarket course (7f, good) this May on her second start back; has run well on this track in the past and she's in calculations up 2lb.
2-3 in 2yo campaign and took a step forward from his first two runs of this season when third at Sandown (7f, good) three weeks ago; could do with settling better but he might not be far away.

Plenty of promise on his first three starts, winning a maiden at York (7f, good) last September on his second outing, and things didn't go his way when fifth of nine on handicap debut at Chester (7.5f, good to soft) three weeks ago; unexposed and could build on that latest performance today.

OwnerName: HM The King & HM The Queen
Spotlight: She's failed to fulfil the promise of last September's debut win at Haydock (6f, good) and was well beaten at 4-1 on reappearance on the Rowley Mile (6f, good to firm) two months ago; however, she stumbled leaving the stalls and pulled hard, and perhaps she'll settle better today in a first-time hood; not written off, and interesting to see if her backers return.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Looked an unlucky loser at Pontefract (6f, good) on handicap/seasonal debut then may not have been suited by the track at Chester (6f, good; sixth); this first crack at 7f is worth a go but he has to bounce back from a heavy defeat at York (6f, good) three weeks ago.

Considerable promise in AW novice races on first two starts (7f/1m) then down the field in 1m2f Classic Trial at Sandown; showed good pace before fading into ninth of 30 in the Britannia Handicap at Royal Ascot (1m, good to firm) and he could benefit from the drop back in trip; trainer has won two of the last three runnings of this race (at usual home Haydock); chance.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £155,000 breeze-up 2yo; attracted support into 15-2 and showed promise when runner-up behind a smart prospect at Lingfield (5f, good to firm) last month; never better than midfield in the Albany at Royal Ascot but she's back in much calmer waters now and sets a clear standard on the figures; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 220,000gns breeze-up buy; made a promising start when fourth in the Hilary Needler at Beverley (5f, good to firm) in May; didn't get very far after an awkward start (unseated) at Redcar last month but it's still early days and she's a big player if she can build on her initial form.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: Poor form when in rear in a Windsor maiden (6f, good to firm) in May and a C&D novice (good to soft) last month; can only be watched.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has good pedigree but he ran green and didn't show much when a remote eighth at Windsor (5f, good to firm; 11-1) last month; should be more streetwise this time but he needs a transformation on his second start.

RaceName: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £5,000 breeze-up 2yo; fourth foal; dam Listed-placed 7f winner (including 2yo; RPR 95), half-sister to winners Queen Of Deauville (5f 2yo) and Investigation (11.4f); the only newcomer in the line-up and he's probably best watched.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 80,000gns Sioux Nation half-sister to four winners including Encourageable (7f AW/1m turf; RPR 100); no impact at Thirsk (5f, good) for Mick Appleby in April but she ran green before making some late headway; should be more streetwise this time and she's a likely improver for new yard.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten around 6l in two novice events at Newbury (6f, good to firm) last summer, with RPRs in the 50s on both occasions; has had wind surgery but she needs a transformation dropped in trip after a year off.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Second of 20 in a sales race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last August is his clear standout effort and a repeat would make him hard to beat; suffered heavy defeats in his next two starts but he returned after wind surgery with a respectable fourth at Brighton two weeks ago; needs to build on that but blinkers could help and he's still lightly raced; respected.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to six winners including Queen Kindly (5f/6f including Group 2; RPR 112), Marhaba The Champ (1m1f-1m4f; 102) and Desert Champion (5f AW; 82); dam 5f/6f winner (including Group 2; 121), out of 6f Group 1 winner; looks the part on paper and he's an interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 60,000gns foal, 1,000gns 3yo; second foal; half-brother to 7f AW winner Gordon Norse (RPR 82); dam unplaced French 5.5f 2yo only start (60), half-sister to French 6.5f 2yo winner Fou Rire, out of 5f 2yo Group 3 winner; already been gelded and he's probably best watched on debut.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Poor form when well held at big prices in two novice events (6f, AW/good to soft) this season.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ran well to a point on Lingfield debut (6f, good) last month but she was beaten 7l in the end and recorded an RPR of 45; this drop in trip could help but she needs lots of improvement.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed ability when third at Beverley (5f, good to firm) in May, but that was a small-field seller and he failed to build on that at Ayr (6f, soft) last month; may not have handled the conditions last time but he needs to bounce back with an improved effort.

RaceName: Ivor Thirst Sprint Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam placed 5f/7f (RPR 66), half-sister to winners Mr Coalville (7.5f-1m3f), Jfoul (7f 2yo), No Nay Alexander (6f) and One For June (6f/7f 2yo); yard is in good form and market should guide on debut.

Went close on Salisbury debut (5f, good to firm) last May but he's been a long way below that form in three subsequent runs; still early days but he was out the back on handicap debut at Windsor (11-1) last month and others are preferred.

Has a patchy profile but he won at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) in May and finished close-up in sixth there 18 days ago; remains feasibly treated on his win in this race last year and he's 2-2 at Nottingham; interesting contender.

OwnerName: Giddy Up You Good Thing Racing Syndicate
Spotlight: Sole win from 27 starts was in a Chelmsford novice (5f, AW) in February 2024; well handicapped on his old form but he returned after 14 months off with a heavy defeat at Southwell (7f, AW) two weeks ago; opposable.

OwnerName: J Sarkar, D Sarkar, O Sarkar & D Sarkar
Spotlight: Won a Chelmsford novice (7f, AW) in the second of his two 2yo runs; made a low-key start in handicaps this spring but he resumed his progress back at 6f when going close at Windsor (good to soft) last month; nudged up 2lb but he's a big player if he can build on that last run.

Reappeared with a smooth win in a Southwell maiden (6f, AW) in April but she was only sixth of eight on handicap/turf debut at Pontefract (6f, good) last month; still unexposed but that was disappointing last time and she needs to kick on again to defy this mark.

Triple turf winner who made it 3-5 on AW when leading close home at Wolverhampton (6f; tongue-tie added) last month; ran another good race when a close second at Ripon (6f, good) last time and he's respected off only 1lb higher.

Won a Ripon maiden (5f, good) last June; highly tried in Listed events in final two juvenile runs but she's not threatened in four handicaps for new yard this season; wasn't beaten far at Goodwood last month but she needs to find more at this new trip.
Not taken to hurdles in a few runs lately; yet to finish placed on the Flat either and still has to prove his stamina over this far, so it's not easy making a case for him.

Solid debut for his new yard, having been picked up from Simon Dow for 11,000gns, a fortnight ago and duly won a small-field affair easily last weekend; not had much racing on turf and a 6lb rise could well be manageable in current form.

OwnerName: Case Racing Partnership & Anita J Lush
Spotlight: Made all in a six-runner renewal of this race last year off a 1lb higher mark (good to firm); not in the same form this time, but this may well have been the target and she ought to fare much better.

Had a lengthy absence prior to two poor efforts for his current yard; handicapper quick to relent and this is easier, so he's worth a market check, but he was taken out on account of good to firm going in May, which would be a worry.
Regressed over hurdles since well held in two Flat starts last August; ran poorly in one previous try in a visor (usually wears cheekpieces), so positives are thin on the ground.
Winless in three years and has regressed for this yard; consisent run came to an end when below-par at Kempton a few weeks ago but he won't mind the return to turf off a career-low mark.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Dowling And & Mrs Marney
Spotlight: Won a small-field novice at Southwell (1m, AW) last September, but she's found things tough since including in four handicaps (1m-1m3f) this season; continues to drop down the weights but she needs to raise her game back up in trip; hood reapplied.

Popped up at a big price in a busy finish on Tapeta (1m) last month but she didn't hit the same level when fourth at Chepstow (1m, good; 5-2 favourite) last time; that wasn't a bad effort but she needs to find more back up in trip; 1lb out of the weights.

Ayr maiden winner who is 0-8 in handicaps but she has dropped a long way in the weights; only fifth of six at Musselburgh (12.4f, good) last time but she got pestered for the lead and may have had stamina issues at that trip; could go well on this return to 1m2f and she's not ruled out.

Off the mark at the fifth attempt with an emphatic win in Epsom handicap (1m2f, good) last August; looks interesting on that form, but she's been well held in her last three runs including a 16l defeat at Salisbury last month; needs to turn things around.

Three turf wins for Richard Hannon last summer and latest was off 1lb higher at Ripon (1m4f, good to firm); mixed results for new yard since March but she's had some close calls and was a creditable second at Doncaster (1m2f; tongue-tie added) last week; on same mark here and has leading claims if she can back that up

Hard to predict and was tailed off on Tapeta two weeks ago but that was after a respectable fourth at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm); has possibilities if she can bounce back again and both wins came over C&D last July.

11-race maiden but was runner-up at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last month and she backed that up with a fourth in a Wetherby handicap (1m2f, good to firm); remains unexposed at this trip and she should be in the mix again.

Made it second-time lucky in handicaps when justifying favouritism at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) in May; held in third over 1m3f at Hamilton last time but he was beaten less than 2l and he didn't seem to quite get home over that trip; still has potential and he's a big player back over this sharper test.

Nathaniel filly out of a 1m1f dirt winner in Japan; didn't show a great deal in three qualifying runs (1m), but she now goes up in trip and is a possible improver on handicap debut; needs a close look in market.

Beaten 7l or more in all five runs including two 1m handicaps (good to soft/good to firm); needs improvement at this new trip but she's in top hands and her dam was a Group 1 winner, so things could click at some point.
Gelded son of Almanzor; struggled in maiden/novice events (7f/1m), but he now goes handicapping off a lowly mark and could show a lot more on this step up to 1m2f; one to keep an eye on.

Has not finished closer than fifth in her six starts and was beaten 13l in a Redcar handicap (7f, good) last time; has some stamina on her dam's side but she needs plenty of progress on this big step up in trip.

17-race maiden but he's on a reduced mark and made some late headway on his stable/seasonal debut at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) two weeks ago; was only beaten around 4l in that race and he could be dangerous back up in trip and down in grade; cheekpieces reapplied.
Six-time AW winner who is 0-11 on turf but he's been placed at Bath (1m, good) and Brighton (1m2f, good to firm) in his last two starts; should go well again and he's respected in change of headgear.

OwnerName: A Quirke & Mrs G B Walford
Spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) last June but she's been out of sorts in her three runs this season; now 3lb lower than for her win but she needs a major revival.

Lightly raced 4yo who caused a 66-1 surprise when landing a Southwell handicap (1m) in January; only 1lb higher here but she struggled in her final two runs during the winter and has plenty to prove again at this new trip.

Has a patchy profile but he won a Newcastle handicap (1m4f, AW) in January and was a close fourth on his Musselburgh reappearance (12.4f, good) last month; this is a deeper race but has possibilities if he gets a good pace back at this trip.

0-8 but she ran well for a long way when third of six in a Class 5 handicap at Doncaster (1m4f, good to soft) three weeks ago; that trip seemed to stretch her and she could make a bold bid on this drop back to 1m2f; first-time blinkers.

OwnerName: Horn And Trumpet Lads & S & A Mares
Spotlight: Generally consistent sort on turf/AW; still 2lb higher than for his last win (September 2025), but he ran better than the bare facts when returned to this sphere at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) last month; has claims if he gets some luck and his turf win was over this trip.

Six-race maiden who has not finished closer than 8l to a winner including in three handicaps (1m-1m2f); may have needed his reappearance run at Newmarket last month but he needs a transformation back up in trip.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Creditable second on reappearance when bidding for a second win in the Palace House at Newmarket; hindered by poor draw at Royal Ascot since; landed this contest (good to firm) 12 months ago, despite carrying 3lb extra, and has no penalty this time; major contender.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Scottish raider who has shown great progress, having gone from winning off a mark of 57 early last season to running well in Listed grade a couple of times, including on penultimate start; this is harder.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Useful handicapper who has shown career-best form on last two starts, winning at York (5f) then creditable third at Epsom (6f); faces a stiffer assignment on these terms, however.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Sometimes slowly away; never dangerous in Group 2 and Group 1 races on last two starts; progressive otherwise, in handicaps prior to good efforts at Listed level; could go well, back down in grade.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Lightly raced filly who had wind surgery prior to a heavy defeat (not beaten solely by poor draw) in the Wokingham last time, failing by a long way to record a second Royal Ascot win; best watched.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Record is 2-4; ran respectably dropped to 5f in Royal Ascot handicap most recently; may do better still but has a tall order in this field, with current rating a long way below the required standard.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Dual Listed winner who faced a stiff task in deep waters at Royal Ascot last time; respectable third to Rumstar in this race last year and was only one place behind that rival in the 2026 Palace House; in the mix.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: 3yo maiden who is often highly tried; seemed to run well, despite traffic issues, in the Scurry over C&D on penultimate start; however, chance of finally winning depends on the effects of new headgear.

RaceName: Coral Charge (Group 3) (Registered As The Sprint Stakes)
Spotlight: Australian mare who has become less consistent with age but is very smart on her day, as three Group 1 wins on British/French soil attest; showed clear signs of a return to form last time when bidding to regain her King Charles III Stakes crown; top rated, back down in grade.

Threatening to win a good prize; unlucky second in last year's Cambridgeshire and finished third, best of the far-side runners, in the Royal Hunt Cup most recently; 1-1 at Sandown; big chance provided the cards drop right.

Low-mileage 4yo who ran well in the C&D event won by Beagle Bay most recently but needs to build on that effort and to prove conclusively that his current mark is surmountable; balance of form gives doubt.

Similar type to Cerulean Bay in that he ran creditably in the Royal Hunt Cup (fourth) last time but is getting little help from the assessor, having proved largely consistent since his last win; solid contender all the same.
Ran respectably at Epsom last time but still has a poor strike-rate in recent years and hasn't won on turf since 2023; the 8yo looks unlikely to prevail in another competitive field.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: 1m winner early in career but has shown best form at 1m1f/1m2f, notably with wins in the 2024 Cambridgeshire and Listed races last year; bit to prove off current mark back down in trip; wears first-time headgear.

Five-time winner at about 1m; creditable third, despite traffic issues, in major handicaps at Doncaster and Ascot (7f) this term; possibilities off same mark back up in distance, provided this race pans out better.

Seemed disappointing last time but perhaps had an issue as a tongue-tie is now fitted; has plenty of respectable handicap form otherwise and was an unlucky third in the Thirsk Hunt Cup earlier in May; not written off.

Prevailed by a neck from Indalo at Newbury in May; unable to confirm the placings in the Royal Hunt Cup but wasn't disgraced; successful on this card in 2023 (7f handicap) and 2025 (this race); solid claims back here.
Landed the Thirsk Hunt Cup, albeit having enjoyed a smoother trip than unlucky Sea Force; not particularly solid on his form since; ended up two places behind River King at Royal Ascot most recently.
Has gained both handicap wins in tight finishes at Class 3 level, including at Redcar latest start; may well come up short in this better grade, for all that he receives weight all round.

Useful but he is 0-12 since a Doncaster novice double in spring 2024 and not particularly solid on 2026 form; on a difficult mark returned to handicap level and isn't sure to uphold a consistent Sandown record.

OwnerName: M Hughes, M Kerr-Dineen And Partners
Spotlight: 3yo who is capable of useful form, most recently a rallying sixth in Royal Ascot handicap (1m2f); however, he still hasn't added to his early 2yo success and isn't crying out for this drop back in trip.
Reasonably consistent, including at Listed/Group level, until running poorly in the Royal Hunt Cup most recently; perhaps best watched as he needs to bounce back from a heavy defeat.
Ex-French 5yo who did well to finish eighth in the Royal Hunt Cup (third British attempt) most recently, having been slowly away; may build on that effort, especially if breaking cleanly.

Unbeaten in two starts, both over 7f (likely to be as effective at 1m); comfortably defied a penalty in Haydock novice event (soft) on seasonal debut; out of a Listed/Group 3 winner for her owner-breeder and brings potential to this rise in grade; adds interest.

Fared about as well as expected when an outsider in the 1,000 Guineas last time; has otherwise shown clear promise, namely an AW success then good effort in the Fred Darling at Newbury; may show further progress back down in class.

Smartly bred filly, by Dubawi out of a Group/Grade 1 winner for her connections; scored with more in hand than the narrow margin suggests at Wolverhampton (8.5f; handicap debut) in latest AW start; interesting turf debutante, being the type to improve further.

Kingman filly who has shown consistent form in 1m2f events this season, while giving the impression this return to 1m may prove ideal; showed her 2yo promise over this trip, winning on debut and good second next time; solid chance, assuming she takes well to first-time headgear.

TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: Made a winning reappearance in AW contest; mixed results back on turf since, most recently coming up well short even allowing for difficult draw in Royal Ascot handicap (1m); limitations seem exposed.

Well-bred filly who cost 3,700,000gns as a yearling; ran well in the Nell Gwyn kept to 7f at Newmarket on seasonal debut; showed no further progress in 1m Listed event at York since but is the sort who should do better still; one of the main players on ratings.
Rather exposed filly whose peak figures may not be entirely reliable and who gained sole success in modest maiden at Bath when facing her easiest task; opposed.
Not disgraced in Royal Ascot handicap (1m) last time but has become a touch exposed since the second of her two juvenile wins and faces a stiff task in this field; trip-wise, may prove best over 7f.

OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: Ballydoyle colt who went close in a strong maiden on debut and is 4-4 since, most recently beating stablemate Hawk Mountain by just under 1l in the Prix du Jockey Club at Chantilly (10.5f); impressive at Chester the time before and that Listed form has worked out well; top on the figures, still improving to boot and represents a trainer who has landed this prize nine times, including with 3yos (among them a French Derby winner) in four of the last five years; duly much respected.

Record of 0-6 since debut success doesn't tell the whole story as he's shown progressive form behind Ballydoyle colts, including the subsequent Derby winner, in last four appearances; did well to finish fourth in the French Derby (10.5f, good) most recently, having met traffic issues and come from a lot further back than the first three; provided the cards drop more kindly, has possibilities in this intriguing rematch with Constitution River and Hawk Mountain.

OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Record of 4-6 includes three Group wins, notably the Futurity at Doncaster last autumn; has taken well to headgear (cheekpieces then blinkers) in two races at Chantilly this season, good second to stablemate Constitution River in the French Derby (10.5f, good) latest; no certainty to reverse placings with that colt, who is still improving, but is building up a solid record and again has place possibilities.

Progressive colt whose record is 4-8 and who has never finished out of the first three; beaten over 3l by similar type Gethin at Newbury early last season but holds a fighting chance in the rematch, having roughly caught up with that rival in terms of ratings; creditable third to sibling Almaqam in the Tattersalls Gold Cup at the Curragh (first Group 1 attempt) most recently; particularly interesting returned to Sandown, being 2-2 over C&D, namely wins in a useful handicap last August (soft) and this year's Gordon Richards Stakes (good).

Progressive colt whose record is 3-6 and who has never finished out of the first two; comfortably bagged AW Listed honours on seasonal debut then posted a highly notable effort in the Brigadier Gerard Stakes over C&D (good) even allowing that he received 7lb from the neck winner Ombudsman, whose subsequent Royal Ascot performance puts him in contention for being the world's current best racehorse; sire won this race in 2020; brings strong claims to this first Group 1 attempt.

Capable of very useful form but he is 0-12 since landing a major handicap at Newbury over two years ago and was unplaced in the Listed Wolferton Stakes at Royal Ascot last month; supplemented at a cost of £50,000, possibly to act as pacemaker for fellow Wathnan Racing runner Gethin.

OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Capable of useful form but is well down the pecking order at Ballydoyle and 0-6 since debut win; never-dangerous 11th of 30, fitted with cheekpieces, in Royal Ascot handicap (1m) most recently; headgear retained; clear bottom of this pack on ratings and is possibly in here as pacemaker for his two stablemates.

Yet to build on wide-margin Beverley debut success (7.5f, good to firm) last August; gelded ahead of his reappearance sixth in a warm conditions race at Kempton (1m) in March; absent again since; the return to 7f could suit this often free-goer but King's Cavalry is the more obvious of the yard's pair.

6f Hamilton winner on good ground under Oisin Murphy as a 2yo; however, never figured on Epsom reappearance (7f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) and subsequent Ffos Las second offers no proof that he's trained on given he was beaten 11l; Murphy is back on board now but it's not enough to tempt.

Won first two starts (both AW; trained by Joseph O'Brien on debut) but his form has levelled off; latest fourth over 7.5f at Chester (good to soft) came in a useful 3yo handicap but he'll need to pull out a bit more to get his head back in front.

Has form figures of 212 over 7f, with the win in a novice at Kempton in May; built on that with an eye-catching effort back in handicap company over C&D (good) three weeks ago, repeatedly denied a run before finishing strongly for second once in the clear; that form has been boosted in recent days; good shout.

Wide-margin Lingfield maiden winner (AW) who ran on for fourth in a 7f Listed race there on reappearance; hasn't confirmed that promise in three subsequent outings back on turf but a wide stall was always going to make life hard in a big field on 7f Goodwood handicap debut in May and it's possible he failed to stay 1m in the Britannia at Royal Ascot since.

Made it third time lucky in novices at Leicester in May and followed up on Doncaster handicap debut four weeks ago (both 7f, good to firm); only just held on from a late closer at Doncaster but impressed with the way he travelled close to the pace for a long way and it's likely there is better to come.

Quietly progressive, always close up when fourth of 12 on handicap debut over C&D (good; unraced on firmer) three weeks ago and he was only just behind Akho Mezzna and Bridge Of Eagles that day on almost the same terms; after just four races, he should still be open to improvement.

Wore cheekpieces last two outings and latest brought easily his best show as a 3yo, when soon close up for a rallying second of 12 over C&D (good; probably acts on good to firm); Bridge Of Eagles and Tambora were in close attendance that day and this one is now 1lb worse off; in the mix.

OwnerName: C & L Washbourn And Mrs S Woodward
Spotlight: All three starts this term were at about 1m2f on good, unraced on firmer; won handicap debut at Goodwood in May; disappointing favourite at York (failed to settle early on) next time but better from off the pace when third of 12 off this mark over C&D three weeks ago, in a tight finish for second with Akho Mezzna and Tambora; each-way contender again for in-form yard.

Sole win came on AW last September but best form when taking minor honours in 1m/1m1f handicaps here (good) on last two outings; made most last time and looks well worth a go at 1m2f for yard among the winners.

Won debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in December; better form in defeat this spring in a novice at Southwell (1m, AW) and handicap at Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) but the Ascot first two left him over 5l behind in five-runner race and he did not make the progress for 1m2f which might have been expected (dam 1m5f/1m6f winner); gelded soon after; needs to resume improvement.

Beaten a neck at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) on second start and improved form again when second of eight in novice at Goodwood (1m2f, good) four weeks later; moves into handicaps and should be capable of better still.

Won handicap/seasonal debut at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) in April but odds-on flop over 11.6f at Haydock next time; settling him was again an issue back over 1m2f in a steadily run race at Nottingham (good) one month ago but he came from the back in six-runner race to win by a neck; up 5lb but the third scored next time and this 3yo leaves the firm impression that there is better still to come from him.

Three runs in France in 2025, landing a 9.5f minor event in September (heavy); changed hands for 40,000gns in October but no show at 33-1 when tailed off on his yard/handicap debut at Chester (1m2f, good to soft) three weeks ago; dropped 3lb but he's hard to latch on to at the moment.

Off the bridle a long way out over 1m at Newmarket 15 days ago but he did stick at it for fourth of 13, suggesting this return to 1m2f for the first time this year may suit; nicely handicapped on his second at Newbury on his final 3yo start.

Returned from a year off to reach the frame in competitive 1m2f handicaps at York (good) and Newbury (good to soft) last September; sold from the Gosden stable for 58,000gns last autumn and absent for a further 287 days ahead of this return for new yard.
Better than ever with 1m2f wins at Newbury and Epsom (made all) on good ground in April but a further 5lb rise proved enough to stop him at York last time; might be vulnerable again.

Second course success when winning this off 1lb higher on good to firm under Rossa Ryan last year; disappointed on Pontefract reappearance but looked back on form when keeping on from the rear for fourth (Spoken Truth second) of ten in the Zetland Gold Cup at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) at the end of May; can make a bold bid in this race again.

Smart efforts in defeat at Listed/Group 3 level last season but hasn't come close to reproducing that form returned to handicaps this season, including with cheekpieces added last time; being eased another 3lb can only help but leap of faith needed.

Ex-Irish 5yo; overall record of 1-14 but he's been in good form for new yard this season, including second in the Zetland Gold Cup at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) in May, with reopposing Flying Frontier back in fourth; bumped into a well-treated rival when runner-up again at Nottingham last time; likely to be on the premises under Oisin Murphy.

Second on soft-ground debut at Redcar in autumn 2024 but his best efforts since have come on AW, bouncing back from a below-par reappearance at York with a fine second in a 1m2f Newcastle Class 2 eight days ago; needs to show he can still do it on turf now.

Built on his solid reappearance at the York Dante meeting when resuming winning ways back on the Knavesmire (1m1f, good) under Ryan Moore three weeks ago; the way he held on determinedly suggests this slightly longer trip should be within range; looks sure to go well.

Back on the mark he defied over 1m6f at Wolverhampton in March but recent efforts haven't been obviously encouraging, for all the 7f trip of the Buckingham Palace at Royal Ascot is almost certainly too short for him these days.

OwnerName: Anoj Don, Daniel Macauliffe Et Al
Annua Racing SL, Ecurie Altima & Marc-Antoine Berghgracgt
Yanis Tarzout, Mme Victoria Anfuso Et Al

Spotlight: Running well this year and was only half a length behind Stolen Kiss (and ahead of two of this field) when last seen two months ago (6f, good to soft), with that rivals going on to finish a close fourth at Royal Ascot recently in a Group 1; should give a solid account and stays 7f, but wins so far have been in a maiden, handicap and a conditions race.

Capable of smart form, and wasn't beaten too far by the in-form Stolen Kiss a couple of times earlier in the year, although behind some of these, before proving 4.5l too good for Zzodiac a month ago over 6f on soft ground; going to need plenty more to win at this level and work to do with a couple of these on earlier meets in 2026.
Nicely bred, being out of Wizz Kid, who notably won the Prix de l'Abbaye in 2012, but hasn't kicked on since her debut success and this looks a very hard task to add another win to her tally.

Smart performer with a decent strike-rate, who comes into this off a clear career best when landing a Group 2 at York (6f, good ground) when the headgear was back on; going unlikely to be very soft judged by the weather forecast plus this race is later in the day, so only the 4lb and more he has to give away the concern; Mickael Barzalona rides for first time.

Supplemented for this; had some good form in 2025 and been running okay this campaign until blitzed, like everyone was, in the Palace Of Holyroodhouse last month behind Bacio; finished ahead of Kailani in a Chantilly Group 3 earlier in the year and should be in the mix for a place at least on his first run over 6f this season and blinkers added; first time Mickael Barzalona hasn't been aboard.

Another in this field who had Stolen Kiss ahead of her twice this season over 6f, finishing behind Mise En Boite last time but ahead of Charibelle, with the latter winning since; overall record suggests he'll struggle toad to her tally at this level.

Knows how to win and mostly run creditably in 2026 before recording a clear career best 34 days ago in a Listed event at Chantilly over 6f on good ground; seems to handle most going, so is one of the more likelier types to land the prize considering she comes out top on adjusted figures.

Not had much racing and been progressing nicely, with his most recent start a win over 6f on heavy ground at Chantilly in a conditions event; this is quite a jump in class, however, and improvement is required.

OwnerName: Messrs J I Bloch & Jonathan Snaith & Nancy Hossack
Spotlight: Moved up-stayed on when 14/10 and 3rd of 11 runners, 3.00 lengths behind STAR MAJOR over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Pretender to the throne?.
Gutsy-now the July! when 5/10 and 1st of 7 runners, 0.05 lengths ahead WISH LIST over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Smashing filly-big say.

OwnerName: Lucky Vest 12 Cc, D D Maclean Et Al
Spotlight: Stayed on when 5/1 and 4th of 6 runners, 3.00 lengths behind ZEITZ over 1400m at SCOTTSVILLE on 17 May 26. Classy-can mow em down.
Led - tired late when 16/1 and 4th of 11 runners, 4.00 lengths behind STAR MAJOR over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Honest sort-nice weight.
Always going well when 10/1 and 1st of 10 runners, 0.60 lengths ahead ISIVIVANE over 1900m at GREYVILLE on 09 May 26. Smart Wsb 1900 win-draw 1.

OwnerName: Greg & Gina Bortz, T Gujadhur & R M Sewgoolam
Spotlight: Rolled in-easy win when 9/4 and 1st of 9 runners, 1.05 lengths ahead NATIVE RULER over 2400m at GREYVILLE on 13 June 26. Top finish (6th) in 2025.

OwnerName: Messrs E G Hart De Keating, M L Jean Hardy Et Al
Spotlight: Ran on too late when 25/2 and 3rd of 12 runners, 2.15 lengths behind QUESTIONING over 1600m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. 7th in 2025 - solid prep.
Eased early-topfin. when 6/1 and 1st of 10 runners, 0.70 lengths ahead ZEITZ over 1800m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Getting better with time.
Too short-no threat when 9/2 and 7th of 13 runners, 4.55 lengths behind QUICKSTEPGAL over 1400m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Ideal prep for G1 winner.
No extra-crop fine when 3/1 and 2nd of 6 runners, 0.60 lengths behind FIRE ATTACK over 2000m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 28 April 26. Reserve 1.
Fought - crop fine when 25/2 and 1st of 11 runners, 0.50 lengths ahead ALADDIN'S LAMP over 1800m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 07 June 26. Could make things pay.

OwnerName: Messrs Cameron Braun, E A Braun Et Al
Spotlight: Rolled-brush-missed when 7/1 and 2nd of 12 runners, 0.05 lengths behind QUESTIONING over 1600m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Top Gr1 efforts-weight...
Ran on-looked winnr when 5/4 and 2nd of 9 runners, 0.25 lengths behind JAZZ CAFE over 2400m at GREYVILLE on 20 June 26. Reserve 2.
Rein drop100-almost when 16/1 and 2nd of 7 runners, 0.05 lengths behind WISH LIST over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Almost pulled it off last.
Shifted in 400m when 28/10 and 4th of 11 runners, 1.80 lengths behind ALADDIN'S LAMP over 1800m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 07 June 26. Never far off-tries track.
Carried-jck-chokeVN when 9/4 and 8th of 10 runners, 4.50 lengths behind ZEITZ over 1800m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Live up to expectation?.

OwnerName: Messrs E C Abraham, Mayesh Chetty Et Al
Spotlight: Slow - ran on well when 7/1 and 2nd of 10 runners, 0.70 lengths behind ZEITZ over 1800m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Honest form-dark horse.

OwnerName: D Dasrath & Mrs C Dasrath, R P Macnab Et Al
Spotlight: Roll out-bump-short when 10/1 and 8th of 11 runners, 11.40 lengths behind STAR MAJOR over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Good filly - can show.
Roll out-bump-unbal when 5/2 and 1st of 13 runners, 0.05 lengths ahead QUICKSTEPGAL over 1400m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Classy filly-can dictate.
Rolled in-bumping when 5/1 and 2nd of 13 runners, 0.05 lengths behind QUICKSTEPGAL over 1400m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. 2nd in 2025 - big claims.

OwnerName: Drakenstein Stud, D D Maclean & G J Player
Spotlight: Ease-switch-brushin when 1/1 and 2nd of 14 runners, 0.20 lengths behind ASIYE PHAMBILI over 1200m at SCOTTSVILLE on 23 May 26. Champion-bit unlucky last.

OwnerName: Mrs D L Erasmus, Mrs L Huntingford & H W Leyenaar
Spotlight: Over-raced-ran on when 9/2 and 3rd of 7 runners, 1.85 lengths behind WISH LIST over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Smart sort - better trip.
One paced run when 13/2 and 5th of 7 runners, 3.20 lengths behind WISH LIST over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Past Gr2 win here-capable.
Just got up when 7/1 and 1st of 10 runners, 0.10 lengths ahead KISSHOTEN over 1400m at TURFFONTEIN STANDSIDE on 07 June 26. Gutsy last win but more to do.
Bumped twice late when 16/1 and 6th of 14 runners, 3.00 lengths behind ASIYE PHAMBILI over 1200m at SCOTTSVILLE on 23 May 26. Goes further-can show.

OwnerName: Bass Racing Pty Ltd T/A Centenary Syndicate
Spotlight: Rolled in-brushing when 20/1 and 5th of 13 runners, 4.25 lengths behind QUICKSTEPGAL over 1400m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Can earn at best.

OwnerName: M J A Ferreira & Camargue Stud Syndicate
Spotlight: Pulled-interference when 17/2 and 6th of 7 runners, 4.90 lengths behind WISH LIST over 2000m at GREYVILLE on 30 May 26. Expect better than last run.
Up there - tired when 50/1 and 11th of 13 runners, 8.85 lengths behind QUICKSTEPGAL over 1400m at GREYVILLE on 06 June 26. Hugely outclassed here.

Mixed form in nine starts, winning Group 3 Athasi Stakes at the Curragh (1m) in May; good fifth to Precise in Irish 1,000 Guineas last time; more exposed than some but could easily take to this style of racing; should stay 1m1f (out of mare by Galileo) and respected.

OwnerName: Frank Ferrogine, Richard Rendina et al
Spotlight: Seems sharply improved this year, particularly impressive when winning Grade 3 Regret Stakes at Churchill Downs (1m1f; won by just over 2l) in May; something to find, but showed good turn of foot last time and could contend in this higher grade.

OwnerName: Michael Dubb, William H Lawrence Et Al
Spotlight: Thriving at present and won for fourth time when taking Grade 2 Wonder Again here (8.5f) in June with a bit more in hand than the bare 1l margin; is in deeper company here but looks full of energy at present and should make bold bid to lift first Grade 1.

Steadily progressive form; won Grade 2 Appalachian Stakes at Keeneland (1m) in April under great ride from Joel Rosario; no answer at all for Faithful Departed in the Regret at Churchill Downs (1m1f) last time; may be better at 1m.

Highly tried last season after maiden and Listed wins here first two starts, but unable to reach top level; placed in Grade 3 at Tampa Bay Downs and Grade 2 back here (beaten about 2l by Fitz Right; good trip, short of pace) this year; clearly useful on her day but others make more appeal.

Very useful type; one place in front of Ultimate Love when fourth in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies Turf at Del Mar (1m) last October; promising on return then looked clearly best horse, albeit winning narrowly, in Grade 2 Edgewood Stakes at Churchill Downs (8.5f), splitting horses with great enthusiasm; another trained to perfection by Brendan Walsh.

Successful on first two starts at end of 2025 at Aqueduct (maiden) and Del Mar (Grade 3 Jimmy Durante Stakes); fourth since in pair of Grade 2s at Keeneland and Churchill Downs (Edgewood Stakes; 8.5f) this spring, narrowly beaten in blanket finish for latter, having looked likely winner; more needed but getting better.

By Frankel out of a Prix Saint-Alary winner; impressive Kempton maiden winner last November; lacked experience when fifth in 1,000 Guineas at Newmarket, then took another step forward when third to Precise in Irish 1,000 Guineas at the Curragh; still plenty promising and must surely go well against this group, particularly as 1m1f will suit.

Won first three starts, a maiden at Colonial Downs then allowance and black type events at Laurel Park; good fifth in Breeders' Cup Juvenile Fillies Turf (1m) at Del Mar; favourite, promising return when beaten head in Listed race (1m) at Laurel Park; bred to be better at 1m1f than shorter.

Very useful filly from Argentina but has won just once from nine tries; good efforts in native country, including second in Grade 1 Enrique Acebal Stakes at San Isidro (1m2f) last November; promising third in allowance here (8.5f; beaten about 3l) on US debut last time, late headway; more needed.

RaceName: Suburban Stakes Presented By Subourbon Life (Grade 2) (Dirt)
