Suburban Stakes Presented By Subourbon Life (Grade 2) (Dirt)

OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Very useful form to beat Pacific Avenue (who has improved since) by 1l in Listed race at Meydan (1m) in February; better effort since when winning Group 2 German 2,000 Guineas at Cologne (1m) by just over 1l, again showing turn of foot; likeable colt, should stay 1m1f, and right in the mix here.

Progressive form in four starts, scoring in a maiden at Keeneland in April, allowance race at Churchill Downs in May and Grade 3 Pennine Ridge at Saratoga (8.5f) last month; favourite, beat Blackmail and Bottas by more than 1l, with something more to spare, in last-named event; ready for step up in grade.

Unbeaten in first three, scoring with more in hand than half-length margin in Grade 3 Transylvania at Keeneland in April; drawn wide and ran on strongly from last, too much to do, when excellent 2l second to Stark Contrast in Grade 1 American Turf at Churchill Downs (8.5f); must go well.

Very smart colt in the making; won maiden at Fair Grounds in January, allowance race at Keeneland in April and Listed race at Churchill Downs (1m1f) in May; deeply impressive in last-named event, taking over in straight at will and clearing away to win by 3l, not at all hard ridden; smooth-travelling, easy-moving colt with a long stride.

Looked smart prospect when winning first two starts last year in maiden here in August and Grade 2 Pilgrim Stakes at Aqueduct (by head) in October; only ninth in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf at Del Mar, but returned to form when about 2l third to West End Kid in Grade 3 Pennine Ridge here (8.5f) one month ago, short of room; looks sure to give a good account.
Promising winner of maiden at Tampa Bay in February and black type Jersey Derby at Monmouth (1m; kicked 2l clear) in May; needs to find a lot more to contend with these.

Useful form on first eight starts, then best race when 4l third to Gstaad in Irish 2,000 Guineas at the Curragh in May, keeping on from rear; good chance based on that, and he could just be getting better as stable returns to peak form; should stay 1m1f.

Very useful form to win maiden at Gulfstream (8.5f, Tapeta) last November and black type event at Aqueduct (1m) in April; 13-1, best effort when about 1l second to West End Kid in Grade 3 Pennine Ridge here (8.5f) last month, always prominent in steadily run race; improving but needs to step up.

Broke maiden on fifth attempt at Churchill Downs in April, gradually asserting to win in workmanlike style; made late headway, never nearer, when 3l second to Touch Of Fire in Listed race on same track (1m1f) following month; needs to improve sharply to reach a place.
Managed two wins in his last six outings; got home in front by half a length in a black type at Laurel Park in May; rises sharply in class and has a lot to prove.
Short-headed here a month ago (6f; Chancheng Sparkle in fifth) and has since filled the same berth in a trial; he might give Jedi Spurs something to think about.

Won both his trials before justifying short odds over 5f here three weeks ago when making all for a comfortable defeat of Chancheng Sparkle; 7lb worse off with the second here but that should be manageable.
Has ability but he's finished behind a few of these in his two runs here (6f/5f); vulnerable again.
Looks sure to win races but he couldn't hold a candle to Jedi Spurs over 5f here last month and will do well to reverse those placings even with a 7lb pull.
No better than sixth in his three trials and he was another who was left for dead by Jedi Spurs here last month.
Has won a trial and finished third in his two official races, last time a fader over C&D (just behind Almighty Warrior); has each-way claims.
Can't beat Almighty Warrior on their C&D clash in May and beat just one home in a recent trial.

OwnerName: Alex Leung King Yue & Mok Tsz Wan
Spotlight: Beaten in trials on either side of finishing down the field here in late May when well behind Almighty Warrior.

OwnerName: Li Wai Yin & Gina Li Wai Shuen
Spotlight: No worthwhile form in trials and was last here 21 days ago in the race won by Jedi Spurs.
Beaten in three trials, finishing third in the latest; unlikely to be good enough.
Placed off much higher mark over C&D on seasonal debut in September but subsequent form has been underwhelming; needs to turn a corner.
Dual winner but out of form in his last three races (and subsequent trials) and wants further ideally.
Running well and he has the same draw as when making all over C&D last week; whacked up 8lb for that but still appeals more than most.
Hasn't kicked on from a promising debut here over 6f in November and was only midfield in a recent trial; this relegation into Class 5 company should at least help.
Has won a Class 5 and was placed in a Class 4 on his penultimate run at Happy Valley (6f); had a bad draw there last time; possible.
Competitive over 6f/7f in Class 4s this season and had excuses when down the field here (7f) three weeks ago; one to consider on his Class 5 debut.
Only 1-22 but he finished well to press an in-form winner and Double Bingo over 7f here 13 days ago, and his only win was at 6f; considered.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Mark Fong Chung, Matthew Fong & Victoria Fong
Spotlight: Has had near misses at Happy Valley and here so needs considering in a race of this nature from a good draw.
Has serious questions to answer after again failing to fire on Class 5 debut three weeks ago.

OwnerName: Dr Anthony W K Chow & Michael Tong Hing Yuen
Spotlight: Another class-dropper but for good reason as he's been struggling for a while now; he didn't get the clearest of runs last time, admittedly.
Unplaced in all 14 starts and his best distance remains something of a mystery; only fourth of eight in a trial late last month.
Outran huge odds to be fourth over C&D last week but he's had plenty of chances now.
Both wins have come over C&D but he's been poor in his last two races and fared no better in subsequent trials.
Consistent front-runner who was a close fourth in Robot Knight's race here in May and a recent trial run (fourth) could have sharpened him up.
Weighted to give Robot Knight plenty to think about on their clash here in May and subsequent turf run wasn't too shoddy.

OwnerName: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho
Spotlight: He'll appreciate this return to dirt and the drop in grade, so having won a recent AW trial he's interesting off a good mark; well drawn as well.

OwnerName: Wong Wing Keung & Cheung Ka Ho
Spotlight: All defeats have been on turf and latest effort wasn't too bad a run; if taking to this surface he might have some part to play now down in grade.
Has only won a 5f novice and has been struggling on the grass; light on dirt experience.

It was a Class 4 in which he was a closing sixth here two runs ago and he dropped unlucky with the draw in a Class 5 last time; failed to shine on dirt the last time he tried it but it's worth another go after winning a recent trial on the surface.
Finished second in his most recent trial but on official form he can be taken on.
Seems better on dirt than turf and was close up behind Robot Knight over C&D in May; 4lb lower now and Zac Purton takes over.
Won narrowly over C&D in May but weakened out of it last time, again over 6f but on turf; this surface is more his scene and he's not handicapped out of it.
0-7 and only eighth in his previous dirt run; drawn wide and struggled in a trial last time.
Recent form doesn't bode well but he has won on dirt and added a recent trial on the surface; not drawn well, though.

OwnerName: Tai Woon Shing & Chan Koon Man
Spotlight: Consistent and improving 4yo who has won twice over C&D this season and hard to fault his recent efforts, even the latest when beaten only 1.5l into fifth; solid chance.
Made all to beat Rising Force over C&D in March but was behind that rival and Young Champion here a month ago, that after another modest effort.
Generally in good form this year and was right behind Rising Force here last time despite being dropped in from his wide draw; chance.
Shock winner over 7f here in April but well beaten since and again in his latest trial; his going days are hard to predict and further suits ideally.

OwnerName: Bryan Kwan Hon Ming, Jessica Kwan Mun Hang Et Al
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season but form has cooled of late and he beat only one home last time, albeit in a tougher Group 3 handicap; would have a chance off this mark if bringing his 'A' game.
Progressive 4yo at Class 3 level in which he nearly won again here three weeks ago on dirt debut; a bit worrying that it was his first run in a Class 2 before then and he managed only fifth.
Dual sprint winner in New Zealand but lengthy breaks are a feature in his profile and now returns from another for his new yard; third and fifth in his recent trials; likely best watched.
Hasn't had much racing at all and recent fourths over 6f were perfectly respectable; however, doesn't appear to be crying out for 7f and he's drawn wide.

Both wins over 1m at Happy Valley in spring of 2025; had placed form through the winter - including here - and he was badly interfered with when midfield after a lengthy break three weeks ago; could go well.
Drawn wide but still respected after recent seconds over C&D, the latest behind a consistent improver; Purton returns.
C&D winner in January off 7lb lower and had continued to run well until last time, since when he's had a break; possibilities.
7-53 with the wins coming over 6f/7f; 14lb below his last winning mark but he's looked well treated for a while now.
Low mileage and has hinted at winning potential at a higher level; can lead so stall 1 comes in handy on his Class 4 debut.

OwnerName: Dr Bosco Chan, Dr Chu Wai Ming Et Al
Spotlight: Won on soft ground in Australia but little to shout about after six starts in Hong Kong.

Has won at trips ranging from 6f to 1m1f; after a break he took big step back in right direction when running on for fifth over 6f here in May but his following run over 7f wasn't so clever; downgraded but he's an 8yo now.
No wins in 11 and even when placed he's not really dropped a blow on the winners; edging down the weights and his turn will probably come.
Dead-heated over 1m in Class 5 in March but it's been a struggle since; only ran last week.
6f/7f wins here were a good while back now and he'll need to perform a lot better than he has in his last two races, both over 6f at Happy Valley; returning here over 7f should at least help.

OwnerName: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen
Spotlight: His recent improvement has come at 1m at Happy Valley, winning in May and then beaten 2.5l off this 6lb higher mark last time; however, he has won over 6f so 7f isn't an issue and Joao Moreira knows him well; not without hope despite a draw that's wider than ideal.
Only had five races and the best was his second when a close fourth at Happy Valley (6f); hasn't progressed and slow starts are holding him back.

OwnerName: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Very opposable on what he's shown so far, though he was badly drawn when trying 7f for the first time three weeks ago.
0-5 but he's a trial winner and has been holding his own in 5f handicaps here on turf; if as effective on dirt then he shouldn't be far away; shapes as though he wants 6f.

OwnerName: Chan Siu Cheung & Calvin Chan Wai Pong
Spotlight: Lightly raced and narrowly made a successful stable debut over C&D in January; should be sharper for finishing second in a recent trial but the draw has done him no favours.

OwnerName: Alan Kiang Ping Fai & Joyce Lee
Spotlight: 1-10 on dirt and 3-19 on turf; 3lb beneath last winning mark but recent efforts not easily forgiven.

OwnerName: Dr Andrew Yu, Dr Alvin Yu Man Ho & Dr Bernice Yu Man Yan
Spotlight: Five-time winner who gives his running more often than not and ran a cracker behind one better drawn on turf here a month ago; this surface is fine but stall 10 is a negative.
Beaten in a bucketload of trials this year and he's probably on an unrealistic mark.
It's been a quiet start in Hong Kong but he won in Australia and returns to the track after landing an AW trial; would have been half interesting but for such a wide draw.

OwnerName: Dr & Mrs Edmund Hon Hing Cheung
Spotlight: Dual 6f dirt winner this year; 7lb higher than for the last of those wins and subsequent defeats suggest the handicapper may have his measure; down the field in recent trials.
Without a win since April 2025; 1m probably too far last time and this season hasn't been without the occasional competitive show over 6f; wouldn't be a surprise should he have some say.
Dual 6f dirt winner; back on preferred surface after low-key turf run last month; however, he is 6lb above his last winning mark.
Won a trial in March but no better than fourth in four subsequent ones; however, Zac Purton was aboard for all those defeats and he keeps the faith on official debut; drawn in 1.
Consistent in trials and on the track, on turf and dirt; did well to threaten an unexposed winner when drawn wide over C&D latest; one of the more appealing options.

13-race maiden with an up-and-down profile; he was beaten a little over 3l on his only previous run on dirt (C&D in April) but that was a Class 3 and he was close up on turf two runs ago; has a big run in him.

OwnerName: Lo Kui Lun, Lo Hoe Et Al
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with a string of good efforts over 5f/6f and is well handicapped if judged on those but has not fired on his three starts since returning from a break in April.
Needs to rebound from a poor run six weeks ago but was runner-up over C&D on his two previous starts and will be a major player from stall 1 if all goes to plan.

Placed on two of his four starts, more recently when close second from stall 10 over C&D a week ago; only slightly better drawn today but has the same mark and Zac Purton keeps the ride; likely contender.

OwnerName: Cheng Wan Wai, Sheena Cheng Tsz Yan & Selina Cheng Tsz Ching
Spotlight: Makes official debut after a fairly encouraging trial but has a tough draw and is best watched.
Never looked dangerous when ninth of 12 on recent debut at Happy Valley; moves up from 5f today.
Six-race maiden; suffered worrying dip in form after C&D third in February but his keeping-on fourth at Happy Valley in May was much more encouraging; considered each-way.
Yet to show any significant promise after three runs (all 6f) and will probably need a drop in grade.

OwnerName: Anthony Chan Wai Choi, Wong Tsz Wa & Kwok Ying Lung
Spotlight: Caused 69-1 surprise over 5f in first-time cheekpieces here in May, his third run, but a lesser effort followed and it's hard to know what to expect from him on this return to 6f.
Soundly beaten on first three starts (6f-7f) and has wide draw today; look elsewhere.
0-9 in Hong Kong but posted a sound effort when third over C&D five weeks ago and competes off the same mark again; can make his presence felt.

OwnerName: Tang Ming, James Tang Sau Yip Et Al
Spotlight: Placed twice over 5f since breakthrough win over that trip in March but form has dipped lately and he has a point to prove on this first 6f attempt.
A maiden after seven starts but lost out only by a head after coming from the rear over 1m last month and promises to be suited by this extra furlong; high on the list.
In good form on dirt in recent months, most notably when winning Class 5 event over today's trip four weeks ago; back up in grade today but turf also suits and he ought to run well again.

OwnerName: Dr Ng Chi Ho, Dr Steve Chuang Et Al
Spotlight: Ran okay over 1m here on last two outings but remains 5lb higher than when winning narrowly over that trip at Happy Valley in February; tries this extra furlong for the first time.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Admund Au Chi Hoe
Spotlight: Beaten only by a neck over C&D when upped in trip last month and has now been placed four times this season; drawn much wider today but seemingly close to a breakthrough win.
Safely held here (1m) on recent stable debut and now 0-18 in Hong Kong; others have more pressing claims.
Displayed improvement when fourth to Firefoot on last month's dirt debut (1m1f) but is quickly back up in grade today and still needs to prove himself on turf.
Unplaced all six starts but outran triple-digit odds when fourth over C&D in May (three runs ago) and the booking of Zac Purton heightens interest.

Close third over C&D when upped in trip in May and also ran well when fourth over 1m in refitted cheekpieces at Happy Valley later that month; still on a workable mark; can make his presence felt again.
Runner-up over this trip at Happy Valley on three of his last five starts, albeit the latest was in lower-grade company, and has each-way claims again.

OwnerName: 23/24 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Infrequent winner who has been out of form since returning from a break in May; not the most obvious answer.

OwnerName: Maurice Lai Tai Hei, John Lo Et Al
Spotlight: Led close home to open Hong Kong account over this trip on dirt last month, his 12th attempt; 6lb higher now but should be fine back on turf and further progress is not out of the question.
Dual 1m winner at Happy Valley this season; back on a good mark now but ran poorly when last in action and has become far too inconsistent to rely upon.
Yet to show anything more than minor promise after five runs (7f-1m) and probably needs a drop in grade.
Belied huge odds when fourth at Happy Valley (1m1f) in April, his second Hong Kong start, but well beaten twice since.
Placed over C&D on all four starts, the last twice when second to same exciting rival; has inside draw and the services of Zac Purton today; good chance.
Out of a middle-distance Group 3 winner; showed only minor promise in recent trials; probably one for further down the line; blinkered on debut.
Fast-starting front-runner who won twice over C&D at start of this year and was placed on his last two outings; drawn much wider than last time, though, and might be vulnerable close home.
Delivered on earlier promise when dominating from the front over C&D last month, his seventh start; has scope for further progress and can make another bold bid.
Low-mileage 3yo who displayed significant improvement when keeping-on second over C&D last month; holds each-way claims at least if able to build on that effort.
0-12 in Hong Kong and change of stables made no difference last time; usual blinkers left off today; probably needs a drop in grade.
Ex-Australian 4yo; unplaced all nine Hong Kong starts and makes very limited appeal from stall 12.
Unable to land a telling blow on any of first four starts (5f-7f); favourable draw not enough to make him of interest.

OwnerName: Lau So Wah & Donna Ng Siu Ying
Spotlight: Not disgraced over 6f on first two starts (turf/dirt) but well down the field when upped to 7f six weeks ago and drawn wide for this return to sprinting.
Much better drawn than when eighth over C&D three weeks ago but has not yet kicked on from his winning debut here in November; others preferred.
Out of a 10.5f winner; won a recent 6f dirt trial and evidently has ability; makes official debut today.
Gained this season's two wins over 1m on dirt but his C&D second in May was arguably his career-best performance; didn't fire on latest outing, though, and remains on a tough mark.
0-7 in Australia and absent since two handicap fourths (5.5f/6f) towards end of last year; probably best watched on Hong Kong debut.
Unexposed 3yo who won easily over C&D three weeks ago and is now 2-3; moves up in grade today but could still have a lot more to offer and commands respect.
Quite lightly raced 4yo who has remained in good form since C&D win on seasonal debut in September; not beaten far when fifth from tough draw here last month and enters calculations.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Ringo Ng Yiu Kwok & Quentin Ng Kwan Wai
Spotlight: Three wins at Happy Valley this season, the most recent on latest outing three months ago; latest 7b rise demands more progress but he's well drawn to attack from the front again.

OwnerName: James Lau Po Man & Alice Woo Wai See
Spotlight: Launched Hong Kong career with five respectable runs, finishing either fifth or six each time, but his latest 2lb drop is not enough to tempt.
Ended three-race career in Australia with 6f maiden handicap win last November; makes Hong Kong debut after eight-month break and has double-digit draw; entitled to benefit from this outing.
Placed in 6f Listed race on last of eight starts in Australia but well down the field (albeit on dirt) on recent Hong Kong debut.

OwnerName: Cheung Ming Man, Cheung Ying Ho Et Al
Spotlight: Nearly caused 65-1 shock over C&D a fortnight ago and remains on a good mark but it's hard to predict whether this regressive and inconsistent 9yo will be in the right mood again.
Dual C&D winner in Class 4 company last year but safely held in this grade on last three starts and faces tough task from stall 12.

Disappointed in first-time cheekpieces (now removed) at Happy Valley three months ago and has been deserted by Zac Purton today but was placed over C&D on first two Hong Kong starts and has obvious each-way claims if judged on those efforts.

Five-time winner, most recently from favourable draw in Class 4 company at Happy Valley in May; had tough draw when safely held on dirt last month but, nevertheless, will probably remain vulnerable in this grade.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Cyrus Li Kin Wai
Spotlight: Won narrowly on dirt in Class 4 in early April but has struggled on all three starts since returned to this higher grade and is drawn wide today; swaps visor for refitted blinkers.
Came good in Hong Kong at sixth attempt, with emphatic C&D success last month, but combination of 10lb rise and outside draw asks a lot of him today.

Placed three consecutive times in this higher grade after Class 4 C&D win in March and had possible ground excuse when below form on latest outing but might remain a shade vulnerable off a mark this high.

2-9 when trained in Australia; endured a wide trip before keeping on for third in competitive 6f contest on Hong Kong debut in May and was denied a clear run when close sixth over that trip a fortnight ago; sure to be suited by this extra furlong; might be the answer.

OwnerName: 24/25 Pierre Ng Pang Chi Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: 0-11 in Hong Kong but kept on from the rear to take second over 6f at Happy Valley last month and looks well worth another crack at 7f; a possible.
Scored over 6f in January but has been below that form lately (blinkers added last time) and needs to up his game on this first attempt at 7f.

Without a win since 2024 and much better known as a sprinter but was persistently denied a clear run and looked unlucky not to finish much closer than eighth over C&D in May; Zac Purton keeps the ride; no surprise if he features.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Alan Leung Wai Zen
Spotlight: Placed twice in this higher grade since Class 4 C&D win in February and can be excused last month's defeat here (hampered/denied clear run); may still have a bigger run in him; shortlisted.

OwnerName: 25/26 Me Tsui Yu Sak Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: 2-8 when based in Australia; struggled when a big-priced outsider for last month's Hong Kong debut and remains best watched.
1-4 when based in Australia, the win gained in a 1m maiden in October; absent since placed over 1m2f the following month and might find 7f too sharp on this Hong Kong debut.

Won 1m maiden on sole 2yo start in Ireland; made low-key start to Hong Kong career over 6f last month but had a tough draw and encountered some traffic; ought to be seen in a better light over this longer trip.

OwnerName: Anthony Poon Tak Ching, Vivica Poon Ip Sze Man et al
Spotlight: Well beaten on first two Hong Kong starts (both 6f); needs huge boost from the new cheekpieces.
Ex-Australian 3yo; made just a mildly encouraging Hong Kong debut when keeping-on seventh here (6f) last month but that run might have brought him on and this longer trip ought to suit; interesting.

OwnerName: Bernie Yim Mei Fung & Yim Man Hay
Spotlight: Won twice over 6f in early part of this season and is also suited by 7f but needs to get back in the groove after some disappointing efforts this year.
Struggled on first two Hong Kong starts (6f/7f); can't be recommended.

OwnerName: Finemore Thoroughbreds, E J Lucas Et Al
Spotlight: Well back and made limited headway at Eagle Farm at this trip. Can improve on a drier deck, and in a slightly easier race.

OwnerName: K T J Dixon & N J McAlister
Spotlight: Terrific in the Qld Day Stakes Eagle Farm two back. Close-up and gave his all to just miss at Ipswich. Not convinced about the mile or gate however.

OwnerName: Australian Bloodstock, F Meduri Et Al
Spotlight: On a winning streak. Showed plenty of fight to secure victory this track. Good draw. Sterner test here but could run a race at odds.

OwnerName: O T I Hillier, A H Collins Et Al
Spotlight: Made late rally from the back at Ipswich. Up to 1600m can help, and can sit closer from good gate. Improver.

OwnerName: P S Farrugia, D F Bonnin & F L De Pasquale
Spotlight: Raced in back half and never likely at Eagle Farm at this journey. First time around Sunshine Coast. Can't enthuse.

OwnerName: Darby Racing, A J Barsby Et Al
Spotlight: Backing up quickly. Iron filly lining up for her 18th career start. Mile suits, and SW at this class suits. Likely improver.
Refused to shirk task in choppy conditions in (Bm70) at Doomben. Others rate stronger.

OwnerName: D R Foster, L K Wahlstrom Et Al
Spotlight: Sent out $101 when unable to threaten in the slosh at Eagle Farm at this trip. Has plenty to find on latest offerings. On trust.

OwnerName: Mrs E Beak, J C Beak & Mrs V G J Beak
Spotlight: On a tight rein and mowed them down late in the TL Cooney at Ipswich. Featured both times here. Can get the mile with a quiet ride.
Settled back and didn't figure in sloppy conditions at this trip in the Gunsynd Classic. First time here. Give her another chance.

OwnerName: Mrs C L Munce, M P Rafter Et Al
Spotlight: Responded well from back half to get the prize at Gold Coast. Soft proven. Capable of figuring up in class with even luck.

OwnerName: J D Sayeg, M N Lindenmayer & B L Wheeler
Spotlight: Ran on okay without troubling at Doomben. Sterner task awaits.

OwnerName: Miss V L Thompson, Mrs J H Kemp & Ms C Kowalchuk
Spotlight: Great run at Toowoomba 13 Jun. Led, stuck on for third in the 3yo (Bm65) at Gold Coast. Tougher alley. Strikes a harder race.
Settled back and didn't figure in the 3yo (Open) SW at Ipswich. Has to lift considerably.

RaceName: Hadley's "Hello My Wee China" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Proved more settled when finally gaining a first win in 7f handicap at Carlisle 11 days ago; up 2lb but not taken lightly in her current mood.

RaceName: Hadley's "Hello My Wee China" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Dual course winner who failed to build on earlier promise when seventh of eight in 5f Hamilton handicap ten days ago; handily weighted if back on her A-game here though.

RaceName: Hadley's "Hello My Wee China" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
OwnerName: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner; returned from a break with a solid fifth in 5f Hamilton handicap last month; this reliable sort is a player off a 2lb lower mark here with Eireann Cagney up.

RaceName: Hadley's "Hello My Wee China" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: It's now 21 starts since his last success but he comes here in good form, fourth of ten in 5f Thirsk handicap 19 days ago; well in the mix.

RaceName: Hadley's "Hello My Wee China" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Ripon (5f) in May but just sixth of ten in 5f Thirsk handicap 19 days ago; needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Hadley's "Hello My Wee China" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: On a long losing run and he ended 2025 below par in 5f handicaps at Hamilton and Haydock last autumn; needs to hit the ground running after eight months off.

RaceName: Hadley's "Hello My Wee China" Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Course scorer who got back on track with third of five in 5f Catterick handicap 13 days ago; needs considering from an easing mark.

RaceName: Ayrshire Magazine Community EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Belied his 50-1 odds with third to odds-on Godolphin newcomer at York (6f, good) last time, improving on debut effort; the only runner with experience; much will depend on whether any of the debutants are up to scratch.

RaceName: Ayrshire Magazine Community EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 21,000euros yearling, 75,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; Unfortunately half-brother to 7f AW 2yo winner Sanaam (RPR 85); dam maiden (79), out of 1m2f/11.4f Listed winner; yard landed this race with newcomers in 2019 and 2022; interesting.

RaceName: Ayrshire Magazine Community EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 62,000euros foal, £28,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; by Churchill out of once-raced half-sister to smart 6f/7f winner Find and daughter of US 8.5f 2yo Grade 1 winner; stable has modest strike-rate with 2yos.

RaceName: Ayrshire Magazine Community EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 80,000euros yearling, £50,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; Starman half-brother to four winners, notably Rage Of Thunder (6f; RPR 81); dam Listed-placed 5f/6f 2yo winner (97); suitably bred debutant.
Low-mileage 4yo who shaped well with third of 14 in 1m handicap at Carlisle 11 days ago, nearest finish; bold showing is on the cards off an unchanged mark with this return to 1m2f a likely plus.
Scored over 1m for Andrew Balding in 2024; off 22 months and came in last of 16 for his new yard in 1m1f York handicap 22 days ago; blinkers replace usual visor now.
It's now 20 outings since his last victory; his run of good form came to an end when just third of five in 1m2f Beverley handicap 12 days ago; the sort to bounce back though.
A dual 1m3f/1m4f scorer in 2025; underwent wind surgery before only fifth of six in 1m4f handicap at Doncaster three weeks ago; needs to take a step forward.

Fair 1m winner in 2024 for Marco Botti; took a big step back in the right direction for his current yard with sixth of 20 in 1m handicap at the Curragh nine days ago, staying on well having been set a lot to do; very much one to consider.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Lope De Vega gelding who took a big step forward on his handicap debut when second of four at Yarmouth (1m6f) nine days ago; drops back in trip with cheekpieces added and he's respected after just four starts.
Gained a third win of 2026 at Beverley (1m2f) in May; comes here on the back of a good second of five in another 1m2f handicap there 12 days ago so must enter calculations.
Ended a losing run in 1m2f handicap at Lingfield (on soft) last month; up 4lb here and any rain would be a help given he goes well on soft ground.

Fair form when a 1m4f scorer in France in 2025; yet to show that level after three runs for his current handler, seventh of 11 in 1m6f handicap at Carlisle 20 days ago; more is needed despite a falling mark.
Ended 2025 when completing a hat-trick over C&D (third C&D success) in October but he's yet to fire this summer, soon ridden when only seventh of ten over C&D 16 days ago; has something to prove now.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo who tends to start slowly but has respectable recent handicap form at Musselburgh, namely a 1m win and two efforts in the frame; reasonably solid.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Has failed to figure in maiden/novice events but may take a big step forward at this weaker level with Billy Loughnane booked; similar type to Midnight Steppers; market support would heighten interest.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
OwnerName: N J Dalgarno & W R Ellis
Spotlight: Placed in similar event on AW two starts ago (1m2f) but that is flanked by lesser efforts on turf; remains a longstanding maiden.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Prevailed narrowly in Beverley handicap (7.5f, good) last time, bouncing back to form and showing that his old legs can still operate successfully; 1-1 at classified level; shortlisted.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Veteran who is a C&D winner but has question marks over current form and whether he's still capable of cutting any mustard aged ten; gives classified grade a whirl.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Oldest contender in this field; gained last five wins on Newcastle AW and hasn't scored on turf for three years; brings mixed recent handicap form to classified debut.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Inconsistent on AW this year and record is only 1-27 but the Ayr factor makes him interesting on classified debut; gained his win in sole visit to this course (May 2023).

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Inconsistent 3yo maiden who finished last in the Beverley race won by veteran Langholm most recently; has possibilities on best form but isn't a solid option; classified debut.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
Spotlight: AW winner for previous stable; far from solid on his form for current yard; drops back to 1m after attempting 1m5f at Ayr last time.

RaceName: Book The Sounds Of Summer Racenight Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Newmarket raider; has achieved little at maiden/novice level but wouldn't be the first horse of this nature to improve sharply for a drop to classified grade; addition of headgear may also help; interesting.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: Good second at Doncaster (1m4f) in May but scuppered by a slow start when a never-nearer fifth of 11 at Hamilton (1m4f) last time; no surprise to see her play a prominent role here.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
OwnerName: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
Spotlight: Is 0-16 but he arrives in good form, not seen to ideal effect when a running-on third of nine over C&D 16 days ago; well in the mix from the same mark.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: C&D scorer in 2024; back in good form of late, runner-up in nine-runner handicap over C&D 16 days ago; a likely player again, nudged up 1lb.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a first success when prevailing at Hamilton (1m4f) in June and backed it up with a solid fifth in 1m3f handicap there 18 days ago; not out of things.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who last May gained a ninth C&D win; his more recent form is not so encouraging, though, just fifth of eight over C&D 16 days ago; needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to go on after a good third at Newcastle (1m2f) last July, signing off for Paul Robson with a fading seventh of 11 in 1m4f handicap there last month; starts out for new yard here off a reduced mark and not without interest, especially if the market vibes are positive.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't offered much in his six runs at up to 1m1f, beating only one in 1m Musselburgh handicap 13 days ago; in good hands but lots more is required; seventh 6:39 Carlisle Saturday.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: Modest form at 2yrs; has failed to kick on sent handicapping this season, fourth of six at Southwell (1m4f) 17 days ago; this Kodi Bear gelding needs to take a step forward in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: Scored over C&D last July and she got back on track with fourth of nine over C&D 16 days ago, despite going very freely; one to consider.

RaceName: Glasgow Fair Family Raceday 20 July Handicap
Spotlight: Has cut little ice in a trio of runs in 2026, ninth of 13 in 6f Leicester novice in April; since gelded (cheekpieces also added) and this son of Sergei Prokofiev could still do better now going into handicaps upped markedly in trip, especially if the market vibes are positive.
Odds-on in a pair of novice events this term, winning over 1m at Ripon then clear second over 1m1f at Hamilton; still plausibly open to progress and could do well in handicaps; respected.

OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Scored six times, mostly at Ayr, last season; beaten only a neck, despite being out 4lb of the weights, at Musselburgh five days ago; holds a fighting chance, back off proper mark.
Ex-French 4yo who won well from the front at Hamilton (1m) in May but has failed to beat a rival in his other two British starts; not entirely sure what to expect from him.
In-form 6yo who scored at Nottingham in May/June and ran well when favourite for the Carlisle Bell most recently; revised mark has kicked in but he defied it last summer; solid chance.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who didn't have things go entirely smoothly last time when beaten favourite in his attempt to take Epsom record to 2-2; may still have more to offer and remains of interest.
3yo filly; respectable tenth in the Sandringham at Royal Ascot (handicap debut) with reappearance under her belt; now 2lb lower and Jack Nicholls takes off a useful 5lb; not ruled out.
In a consistent vein of form over 1m until below par over 1m1f last time; went very close over C&D on penultimate start and could go well returned to his usual trip.
Has produced his peak RPR of 102 in both C&D attempts, including a win off 3lb higher in this race last year; Conor Whiteley takes off a bonus 5lb; interesting returned to this scenario.
Low-mileage 3yo; Newmarket winner for previous yard and ran well at Musselburgh on stable/seasonal debut; took a step backwards last time so needs to rebound.
Has form at a variety of tracks but seems ideally suited by Pontefract, being 3-4 there compared to just 1-16 elsewhere; drops back in class for this first visit to Ayr.

Gained four of his eight wins over this C&D; made the frame off today's mark on consecutive starts at Carlisle last month (7f, soft/1m, good) and is sure to be suited by the return to this track; respected.
Two wins this season, the latter when making all in C&D (soft) classified a fortnight ago; faces tougher assignment in this handicap but won't mind if the ground is quicker.
Safely held on consecutive 6f starts this spring but looked back in good nick when close third at Musselburgh (7f, good; just behind Monhammer) on Tuesday and remains on a good mark; shortlisted.
Without a win since spring 2024 but was placed behind Lope Y Linda over C&D (soft) last month and lost out only by a neck off today's mark at Musselburgh (7f, good) on Tuesday; must be considered.
Largely consistent mare who was third over 6f at Hamilton on her last two appearances; also suited by this longer trip and won't mind if the ground is slower than good; probably won't be far away.

Irish raider who comes here after three good efforts, looking a shade unlucky when close second of 15 in first-time visor at Roscommon (7.5f, good) on Tuesday the latest time; big player of the same mark.

Eight-race maiden; made encouraging seasonal debut when third of seven here (6f, good to firm) in May but ran as though something was amiss over that trip last month; cheekpieces removed after one use; others have more persuasive profiles.

Six-time AW winner but not since March 2024, and his turf strike-rate is 0-28; probably worth another crack at 7f but has been out of form over sprint trips for a good while now; considered only if lively in betting.
Unplaced all seven starts and absent since low-key stable debut off today's basement mark in October (1m, AW); needs December's wind operation to have made a big difference.
Has achieved little in five runs (7f-1m).
Recorded 40-1 win over 6f here (good to soft) a year ago but has lost her way since and refused to race on latest appearance 25 days ago; others are much less risky.

RaceName: Tom Halliday Memorial Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Lightly raced and dug deep to win over 2m2f at Newton Abbot (good); probably similar form when third back there off this 5lb higher mark; tongue-tie now added; in the mix.

RaceName: Tom Halliday Memorial Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a bumper in Ireland; doesn't look that well handicapped on his British form and tried a visor when last seen (cheekpieces did nothing when tried once before); what may help is the drying ground.

RaceName: Tom Halliday Memorial Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: A third win over fences at Stratford on Tuesday and he's 9lb lower for this return to hurdling (0-9); has to be of interest with trip/ground ideal.

RaceName: Tom Halliday Memorial Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Only 1-38 but was second at Southwell last time and should encounter a similar scenario here; likely to run well.

RaceName: Tom Halliday Memorial Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Consistent in defeat last autumn and winter but has been pulled up (2m4f) and tailed off (2m7f) here this season.

RaceName: Tom Halliday Memorial Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Without a win since September 2024 and needs to be at full strength to win this back from a break.

RaceName: Tom Halliday Memorial Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Third in a bumper but no worthwhile form as yet over hurdles; however, now goes handicapping after a break and tries cheekpieces; market could be helpful.

RaceName: Tom Halliday Memorial Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Regressive 10yo who ran no race last time at Southwell when behind Gone In Sixty; hard to trust these days.
Runner-up in three bumpers last summer when trained by Ryan Potter; jumped moderately and finished tailed off on hurdle/stable debut at Worcester (2m).
Flat/hurdle form for Dermot Weld earns respect but left that yard for £4,000 and was down the field on yard debut in last week's Northumberland Vase (2m, AW).

Dual bumper winner who has been placed in both completions over hurdles; 9-4 for a Uttoxeter novice last time when he swerved and unseated mid-race; hopefully there will be no repeat of that here with cheekpieces added.
Useful on the Flat and ran in last year's Derby; hasn't won a race since but he ran okay to be second on his hurdle debut at Worcester (2m, good) and going up in trip here could suit.
Similar sort of form in defeat in bumpers and in some of her hurdle races; beginning to look a shade exposed and may need handicaps.
Questionable what he achieved in points and he was completely tailed off on his hurdle debut; returns from wind surgery.
Took seven goes to win an Irish point and fetched £28,000 soon after; should have some future under rules but probably best watched on his hurdle debut.

RaceName: ECIS Say Yes To Solar Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Made all to win C&D (good) maiden in May, his fourth hurdling start, and was beaten only about 1l when third to Crown Of India on handicap debut at Worcester (2m, good) later that month; may still have more to offer but has shown a tendency to hang and/or race too freely (ran out three starts ago), so comes with risk.

RaceName: ECIS Say Yes To Solar Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Exploited much-reduced mark at Worcester (2m, good) in May, his second run after a break, and backed up that performance with good second behind unexposed rival there last month; can make presence felt again.

RaceName: ECIS Say Yes To Solar Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late G Kennington
Spotlight: Progressive mare who kept on well at Stratford (2m, good) in April to complete a hat-trick; absent for 11 weeks since but latest 6lb rise does not look excessive and the return to a right-handed track should be no issue; respected.

RaceName: ECIS Say Yes To Solar Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles for Philip Dempsey in Ireland but signed off with pretty good fifth of 16 at Fairyhouse (2m, yielding) in October; has hood refitted for stable debut; worth a glance in the betting.

RaceName: ECIS Say Yes To Solar Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed on first four hurdling starts, including close second on handicap debut at Taunton (2m, good) in March; absent since disappointing run one month later but returns with Sean Bowen enlisted and remains of interest.

RaceName: ECIS Say Yes To Solar Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Had modest strike-rate when based in Ireland but belied 40-1 odds when very respectable third on recent stable debut at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) and has possibilities if able to build on that performance.

RaceName: ECIS Say Yes To Solar Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won twice at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last summer and was subsequently placed off higher marks than today's; made just a satisfactory reappearance here in April and fell heavily when last in action but will be a danger to all if back in prime form.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Both wins here, the latest 16 days ago when holding on grimly off a 6lb lower mark; can be inconsistent.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran fairly well for fourth at Hexham (2m7f, good) but that made it 0-35; not hard to take on.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: AW winner who is 0-15 over hurdles; latest third over C&D was one of his better efforts but he looks worth taking on from 3lb out of the weights.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has won two of his last three, the latest over 3m2f at Fontwell when Sean Bowen was at his strongest; probably has little up his sleeve after the 5lb rise.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: In a new visor when showing more in a C&D maiden 16 days ago and that gives him some momentum ahead of this handicap debut.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three wins on good ground at about 3m; good run at Southwell last time as the winner got first run; went up 3lb but he's in the mix.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Only 3-32 and without a win since 2024; however, he has added some consistency to his game this year with four successive placed efforts and his mark hasn't really gone anywhere.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Left previous yard for £1,400 in January but new trainer added a tongue-tie at Cartmel (2m6f, good) and he stayed on well for a close second; up 2lb but thereabouts with a repeat.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten 9l and 5l when twice runner-up in May but didn't appear to give his true running when a beaten favourite at Southwell (behind Indian Sunbird); could be given another chance.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has stronger form over fences and uncompetitive in two races on his return to hurdling; reservations for now.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Back-to-back wins (2m4f/2m7f) last summer and went in again at Southwell a month ago (3m, good; made most); didn't win with all that much in hand but only went up 4lb.

RaceName: Stay At The Market Rasen Racecourse Caravan Park Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won over hurdles at Hexham (2m4f, good) and Musselburgh (3m, soft) last season; below par last two outings and was due to go chasing last week when withdrawn due to the ground.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Did well over hurdles and fences for Henry Daly prior to £22,000 sale; a 5lb rise for her clearcut win on yard debut at Uttoxeter (3m handicap chase, good) could have been worse; very solid claims.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Multiple winner but his first success beyond about 2m when making all for a 9l victory over C&D 23 days ago; back up 7lb but had dropped to a good mark.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Successful stable debut at Stratford (2m5f, good to firm) but that was a weak race and he was a remote third over C&D a fortnight ago; that was perhaps too bad to be true but it still leaves him with a bit to prove.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Ran out a 5l winner on his chase debut at Bangor in April (3m, good) and next time couldn't lay a glove on an all-the-way winner; another good run when 4l behind at Southwell having travelled best throughout; should run well.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 1-5 over fences and went close in December on final chase start; another second over hurdles next time and he just never went a yard in his final race; has a big effort in him if bouncing back.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Four-time chase winner and has been in good form since May barring his penultimate race; never far away when runner-up at Bangor last week (3m, good) and must have every chance once more.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Two wins last season included this race but he hasn't been in such good form this year; an 11yo now.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Around 8l off the winner at Bangor (3m, good) when returning from 562 days off in a race he won two years earlier; on a useful mark and entitled to be all the sharper this time.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner who is 0-12 over fences but he has competitive form and although no match for the winner at Uttoxeter (3m, good) that run was probably needed after an absence.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Five wins include two over fences; he'll need to run a lot better than he did in a handicap chase at Warwick a month ago (after 101 days away) and in any case he could be vulnerable to stronger stayers over this far.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 1-6 as a chaser; returned to form when runner-up at Ffos Las (3m, good to soft) but wasn't on her game at all when a remote third at Newton Abbot; can be hard to predict.

RaceName: Wild West Beer Fest Saturday 26th September Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Point/hurdle/chase winner; has her off days but ran pretty well last time at Uttoxeter (3m, good) behind Regal Renaissance who runs in the previous race; respected.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: 9,000euros foal; half-brother to two Flat winners and out of a 1m6f winner (RPR 64); from a yard that wins bumpers and the market should be useful.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: 2m4f point winner; left behind by the front two at Worcester and the Skelton-trained winner has since finished second with a penalty; probably needs to find something on that form but that's feasible; yard won this last year.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: From a good jumping family; bought for £30,000 after finishing second in a 3m Irish point; 5-1 when going down narrowly to a previous winner here 16 days ago and may well go one better.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
OwnerName: H J Jarvis & Mrs P Jarvis
Spotlight: Kept on to finish third at Plumpton (good), finishing 4l behind stablemate Zandahar; could improve on that.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Runner-up in his fourth and final point; future is probably over longer trips.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
OwnerName: H J Jarvis & Mrs P Jarvis
Spotlight: Good Flat pedigree; didn't make her debut until May when second to a James Owen horse who has won again since, narrowly beating Masterius; should run well.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Finished last in his two point bumpers when trained by Lily Bradstock; however, new trainer Toby Bulgin is always respected in races of this nature.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Brought up the rear at Southwell when an 18-1 chance.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Cost 240,000euros as a yearling but never raced for Godolphin and was sold on for 1,500gns in February; rare bumper runner for the yard.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Finished tailed off at Worcester last month but she did go off the 11-4 favourite; better was presumably expected of her.

RaceName: Boxing Day At The Races On Sale Now! Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Cat 3 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: First foal out of a 1m2f-11.6f Flat (RPR 64) and 2m1f-2m5f hurdle winner; tongue tied for debut; yard 0-6 in bumpers recent seasons.
Has form figures of 2142312 (turf/AW) since handicapping, the wins over 1m and 1m2f; ran well from the front at Nottingham most recently; solid claims back down in trip.

OwnerName: Duaij Al Khalifa & Ahmad Al Qassimi
Spotlight: Bought for 165,000euros at 2yo breeze-up sale (unraced that season); has made the frame in three AW events this year; open to progress in handicaps and warrants respect.
Has performed well in two races at Nottingham (1m) since switched to handicaps, making all latest; may build on that win and show further progress, provided she takes to AW.
Largely consistent having been placed in four of her six starts, including over C&D; has a solid chance on handicap debut, albeit likely needs progress to defy top weight.
Probably best to forgive her turf effort last time; has shown promise in two AW maidens otherwise, including success at Wolverhampton; still open to further improvement.

OwnerName: G H Thompson &  S P C Woods
Spotlight: Suited by AW but has shown best form over 6f, including a close second at Southwell; modest fourth returned to 7f last time; something to prove over this new trip.
Suited by AW and looks attractively handicapped, having gained his March win off 2lb higher, but appears more exposed than most of today's rivals; overall record is 1-10.
Has broadly encouraging form, including a couple of fourth-place finishes this term; better than bare result last time (met traffic issues, eased once beaten); could go well.
Best novice effort when fourth at Nottingham on penultimate start; from a family that has served his yard well; may be capable of improvement now handicapping.
Has shown promise; never landed a blow last time (handicap debut) but is a half-brother to seven winners and may do better still; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear.
Broadly consistent having made the frame in four of his five runs, latest at Newbury; chance of getting off the mark partly depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
Promising second at Newcastle (AW) on debut; has regressed in three starts since, beaten long way over 1m2f on turf latest; big turnaround is required.

TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: Shaped well over 1m on handicap debut; seemed a non-stayer over 1m1f since; may improve back down in trip, provided he responds well to new headgear and added tongue-tie.
Has consistent form figures in maiden/novice events on turf but looked wayward last time; remains to be seen whether he can show progress on AW/handicap debut.

Tapeta winner who has dropped down the weights and is one to watch in the betting on his third start for this yard; however, he's been soundly beaten on both runs this season and his losing sequence is up to 14.

Won at Musselburgh (7f, good) last August and also effective on Tapeta; he may have needed his sole run this year (well beaten at Catterick in April) but his profile is very up and down and claims aren't compelling.
He's not consistent but he won over C&D last November and ran really well when second of 18 at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) three weeks ago; major player if he's once more on song.

Went close over C&D in March and won at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) 13 days later; not at the same level on his three starts since, particularly on turf on last two outings, but the return to Tapeta could prompt a revival.

6f Tapeta winner who scored at Newmarket (6f, good to soft) last August on final run of last season; hasn't shone on either outing this term but last time was a step back in the right direction and he could be working his way back into form; the return to 7f is worth a go; not ruled out.
Made debut last July and there was promise among his three novice starts, but he's finished down the field this year in his first two handicaps (1m/7f, good to firm); needs to get back on track.

Lightly raced 4yo who has a patchy profile and finished last at Carlisle (6f, soft) four weeks ago; however, the ground may not have suited and prior to that she kept on well for close third there (6f, good to soft), hinting this return to 7f could be a positive; not discounted on AW debut.

He's dropped to a mark fully 15lb lower than when a close fourth over C&D last August; however, this regressive 5yo has struggled to get competitive this year; a first-time visor replaces a tongue-tie.

Won Kempton classified (7f, Polytrack) in March and, after five unplaced runs, he returned to form with convincing handicap win at 20-1 at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) last week; bang there if backing up that latest effort.

Made it 0-9 when well beaten at Salisbury (1m2f, good) three weeks ago and the drop back to 7f doesn't seem an obvious plus given how well she kept on for second over 9.4f at Wolverhampton (Tapeta) two starts ago; however, she could still run well back on Tapeta in first-time cheekpieces.

OwnerName: Mrs K S Pratt & M Dods
Spotlight: Ran well in fourth at Carlisle (1m1f, soft) two starts ago then failed to settle when well beaten at Ripon (1m2f, good) last time; he has some versatility trip-wise, having won over C&D last March, and he's capable of making an impact back down in distance.

Won over C&D in April; not at the same level on her two runs on turf last month but she had a wide trip when fifth at Carlisle on the second occasion and could build on that effort today; stable is in fine form.

He needs to improve upon his last few showings and was held in fifth over C&D most recently; however, this two-time course winner is 6lb lower than when second at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) in March and is not written off.

Slowly away on his last three starts; runner-up over 6f here in April following a break; hasn't built on that on turf on his two starts since (6f) but he made late headway at Chester last time and the return to 7f may be a good move; could be involved back here.

Remains a maiden after 13 starts and comes with a risk attached having been very slowly away at Kempton last time; however, he was in good form previously, finishing third at Kempton (7f) then second over this C&D, and he's a leading contender if breaking on terms.

Promising start to career early this year when third on his first two runs; hasn't kicked on but he was beaten only about 2l when eighth of nine on handicap debut at Lingfield in April (7f, Polytrack), and perhaps he'll take a step forward in a first-time tongue-tie; not discounted.

Mixed form this year but ran okay when third at Newmarket (7f, good) recently and has dropped below his last winning mark; unraced on Tapeta but seemed fine on Polytrack on his two previous AW runs; might not be far away.
Attractively handicapped on form as recent as last December but she needs to improve upon what she showed on her three outings this spring if she's to get off the mark this afternoon (0-11).
13yo who reappeared with a C&D win in May before a good third at Wolverhampton (7f, Tapeta) last month; most of these have something to prove whereas he arrives with very solid credentials.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: She has about 4l to find with runner-up Hamda's Joy on her debut fourth at Kempton in May (7f, Polytrack; 5-1) but she showed signs of inexperience and this half-sister to two useful winners may be able to take a fair step forward this afternoon; the hood is retained; she's one to consider.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Third at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) last autumn on first two starts and got closer to the target when filling the same position on reappearance at Bath (5.7f, good) three weeks ago; there is every chance that 7f will suit and she's on the shortlist.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Remains a maiden after four starts but she was third over 6f on her first three outings (including on Tapeta) before an improved showing when second at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) last time; leading claims.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Tailed off last July on sole 2yo start; took a step forward when fifth of 14 on her comeback at Kempton in May (7f, Polytrack; Cixi third) and looks to have a future, but this Sea The Moon filly may be of greater interest when tackling longer distances.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Hinted at promise when down the field at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) in May on debut and built on that run when fifth of 11 at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) last month; pedigree provides optimism that she will continue to progress and she's entitled to respect.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fifth foal; Invincible Army half-sister to winners Liangel Hope (7f/1m; RPR 83) and Hasten Slowly (7f-11.5f Flat including AW, 79; 2m hurdle); dam 7f 2yo/1m winner (69); may be best watched on debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 60,000euros yearling; eighth foal; Starman half-sister to five winners including Big Time Baby (5f-6f including Listed), Charline Royale (Italian 5f/6f including Group 3) and Royal Mehmas (Italian Group-placed 5f/6f); represents powerful stable and could be a major player on debut, especially if strong in the betting.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Her dam was useful and she's open to improvement on this second start, but she was well beaten at 80-1 on debut at Musselburgh (7f, soft) last November and a watching brief is advised on her comeback.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Inexperience evident on debut at Bath (5.7f, good) last month, when she was 16-1 and kept on well from the rear to finish fifth (the experienced Clementines Star was third); this half-sister to seven winners could deliver a chunk of improvement today, with the step up in trip a possible plus.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 0-5 but she ran well when third of 14 in a maiden at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) in May and didn't see out 1m2f at Goodwood later that month; she could have a big part to play back down in trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 4yo who has been well beaten at big odds this spring on her first two starts (1m2f, good to firm); drops in trip today; others preferred.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Big odds and unable to beat a rival on first two starts.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive last year and reappeared with a win on stable debut at Yarmouth (5f, good to soft) in May; respectable fourth at Doncaster (5f, good to firm) since and this 4yo may yet have more to offer; ran well in 2024 on sole previous encounter with Tapeta.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Won C&D novice last September before a stint in Meydan (some good efforts), and he returned to action with a midfield finish in a competitive handicap at Newcastle (6f, Tapeta) eight days ago; could improve for that run and the drop back to 5f; unexposed and he's an interesting contender.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
OwnerName: M McHale, J Munroe, M Guy & D Walshe
Spotlight: Four wins last season, including competitive C&D handicap off the same mark as today; in and out this season but he gave it a good go from the front when close sixth of 14 at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta) last week, and this less demanding track should play to his strengths; respected.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Beaten 13 times since his last win in March 2025 but he returned to form with close fourth at Chester three weeks ago and he has a good record over this C&D in the main; each-way claims.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't been at his best since a Listed win at York last May but he's on a reduced mark and showed some spark when third at Goodwood in May; he's had wind surgery since a midfield finish at York later in May and it will be no surprise if he runs well on AW debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell
Spotlight: His form has taken a downturn but he's dropped down the weights and there was some promise in his eighth of 14 at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta) last week in reapplied blinkers (retained), when not beaten far despite being denied a clear run; drops in grade here and could have a part to play.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: This is his first outing since last December but he's gone well fresh in the past and ran some good races in the second half of last year; his C&D form figures read 12772 and he can be in the shake-up if fully tuned up.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
OwnerName: R J H Limited And Ruth Carr
Spotlight: 9yo who was well beaten at 33-1 at Redcar a fortnight ago; however, he may have needed that first run since January and he completed an AW hat-trick late last year, the first leg coming over this C&D; he's won here three times and could bounce back with a bold show returned to Southwell.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Handicap
Spotlight: Won early in 2025 on final two runs for Bryan Smart, both at Newcastle (5f, Tapeta), and he returned from an absence with an encouraging fourth on stable debut there this January (5f); he's back from some more time off today but with his trainer among the winners; could be a contender.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Sent off favourite when good third on stable debut at Lingfield (2m, Polytrack) in March; also effective over C&D and he's one to watch in the betting, but he's been well beaten on his last two starts and needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: She's hasn't progressed and she's been soundly beaten in her first two handicaps (1m4f, good to firm/AW); however, this 3yo is from a talented family and is in good hands, and things may click at some point.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Won over hurdles last December (2m); reverted to the Flat in May and was beaten just a neck at Ffos Las (1m4f, good to soft) last month on his second start back, when 7l clear of the third; there's every chance that this trip will be within range and he should again be involved.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Made all in a big field here (2m) last July and he's on a handy mark on last season's best efforts; however, he's been well beaten on both outings this year and needs to turn things around.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who went close over C&D in April and won at Leicester (1m4f, good to firm) next time; appeared to be stretched by 2m at Newbury last time and could go well back down in trip.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Won over C&D in October 2024 off 9lb higher; however, he's run just five times since and has been disappointing in the main, particularly on both starts this season.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up on handicap debut at Ayr (1m7f, soft) last September and good third at Musselburgh (2m, good) in October; this lightly raced 4yo is not written off today but it's his first outing since a heavy defeat at Musselburgh (2m, soft) in November, so there are questions to answer.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Came second in a low-grade handicap hurdle in May but his two Flat wins for William Haggas last year came in plating company and he needs to prove he's worth his current mark in this sphere.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Consistent filly who was nicely clear of third when second to unexposed rival at Kempton (1m4f) last month; has to take 2lb rise on the chin but has big role to play here if first-time cheekpieces have any positive effect.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: The Ridgeway Racing For Fun Partnership
Spotlight: Well beaten after racing too freely on turf last month but has the hood refitted today and is now only 1lb higher than for narrow C&D win on handicap debut in April; could have a say.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Three wins in 2025, the last two over this trip at Kempton; yet to match that form after three starts this year, though, and didn't find that much after travelling well at Kempton (1m3f) on Monday.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has worked his way back to form since disappointing stable debut in April and posted career-best performance when second of 18 at York (1m2f, good) three weeks ago; should be fine back on Tapeta; good chance.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Signed off for Ralph Beckett in good form in autumn 2024 but, following a long absence, was last of four on last month's stable debut (1m4f, turf); might need a few more runs before he's of interest again.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 11-race maiden; raced too freely in first-time cheekpieces (now removed) and flopped badly when warm favourite at Chester two months ago but the form of his close fourth at Newbury (1m2f, good) in April worked out well and he'll be dangerous here if all goes to plan after his short break.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 5-18 when based in Australia but absent since winning 1m3f turf handicap in January 2025 and very difficult to weigh up on this British/AW debut; the market may guide.

OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: Has some good form on turf but latest defeat took his record in that sphere to 0-8; two attempts on AW comprise a win at Lingfield (1m2f) on debut and keeping-on third at Kempton (1m4f) in May; unexposed on artificial surfaces and looks interesting assuming he stays the new trip.

Ran poorly last time, taking turf record to 0-6; broadly consistent on AW otherwise for current yard and he is 2-2 over C&D, the wins gained last autumn; 3-8 overall in this sphere; likely to be played late by Jamie Spencer (up for first time) and has possibilities back here.

Eight-time AW winner, including thrice at Southwell; close third in the Northumberland Vase at Newcastle last weekend when returned to this sphere; back up 2lb but takes a drop back in grade; solid chance.

Has done well over 1m4f-1m6f on turf this term, winning three times from five starts; scored easily at Newmarket latest; better than ever and may improve further in his current form; respected assuming he's as effective back at 2m and returned to AW.

Record of 5-15 on AW, including two wins at Southwell, for Gay Kelleway; far from solid on his form since last success but market support should be heeded on debut for new stable; still in same ownership.

RaceName: Hugo Pfohe Horsepower Trophy - Hamburger Stutenpreis (Group 3) (Fillies) (Turf)

Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud - World Pool By PMU (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Highest-rated racehorse of 2025 following four consecutive Group 1 wins, including the King George and the Japan Cup; didn't have to match pick of efforts to win the Sheema Classic at Meydan on reappearance and heavy rain scuppered his chance when tailed off in last month's Coronation Cup; beat Aventure in this last year and, with a fine weather forecast in the area, will be hard to beat if bouncing back.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud - World Pool By PMU (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Did well to beat subsequent dual Group 1 winner Bay City Roller in last year's Prix Niel but never figured in the Arc; has run well on both outings this season but is closely matched with Eydon on Chantilly form in May and he's another who will have to raise his game.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud - World Pool By PMU (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Unexposed sort who turned in an improved effort when winning the Group 3 Allez France at this venue on her reappearance in May; close second to Aventure (won with more in hand than the official margin suggested) in the Corrida later that month and, although back up in trip, she'll have  to fare better if she's to reverse placings on the same terms.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud - World Pool By PMU (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Respectable second to Calandagan in this last year before going on to win the Group 1 Vermeille; down the field in the Arc and, although she returned to winning ways when beating Sunly in the Corrida, she hasn't matched the pick of last year's form; that'll be needed if she's to follow up in this company, though the return to 1m4f could suit; claims.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud - World Pool By PMU (Group 1) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Last year's Derby/Irish Derby winner but has still to match either piece of form in five starts since his Curragh success; ran in the Hardwicke as though he wasn't over his Coronation Cup exertions on unsuitably soft ground but, even if that is overlooked, he'll have to return to his best if he's going to win or go close.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud - World Pool By PMU (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Last season's Grand Prix de Paris winner (over this trip) who ran to his best when eighth in the Arc; posted two solid efforts in good company at Longchamp in spring and not inconceivable he'll improve for the return to this trip.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud - World Pool By PMU (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Group 2 & 3 winner over this trip in 2025 and this fine physical specimen has shaped as though retaining all his ability this time round; closely matched with Cualificar on most recent Chantilly form but he'll need several of the others to underperform if he's to register a first win at this level.

RaceName: Grand Prix de Saint-Cloud - World Pool By PMU (Group 1) (Turf)
Spotlight: Disappointing in the Derbies at both Epsom and the Curragh last year but bagged a pair of Group 2 events at York, the Dante in May and the Voltigeur at the Ebor meeting; found 1m2f on the sharp side when last seen in a strong renewal of the Bahrain International in November; delayed start to 2026 due to a bruised foot but looks the sort to raise his game this time round; interesting back up in trip for a yard that won this in 2023.

RaceName: Prix des Reservoirs de l'Avre - World Pool By PMU (Handicap) (Turf)
