OwnerName: Serio Racing Stables, USA Steeplechase Et Al

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a 1m2f Listed winner and led close home to make winning debut at Carlisle (6f, good) last month; seems sure to be suited by the extra furlong here and has obvious scope for progress; leading contender.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 35,000euros yearling who shaped with considerable promise before fading into fourth of seven at York (6f, good) last month, form that has recently been well advertised by the runner-up; bred to be better suited by this longer trip; good chance.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Left inauspicious debut behind when respectable fifth of ten behind Redline at Carlisle (6f, good) three weeks ago but needs to take another step forward over this longer trip.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bettered low-key debut when very close second at Pontefract (5f, good) in April and was not discredited when well-held second behind useful rival there (6f, good to soft) in May; up in trip again here and has another big role to play.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 25,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 6f 2yo winner (RPR 95), half-sister to winners Graft (5f 2yo Listed) and Ribenska (7f), out of a well-connected 7f winner; stable runs two other newcomers in this race.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Quite stoutly bred colt who kept on for second (no threat to winner) on debut at Redcar (7f, good) in May; probably won't be seen to best effect until moving up in trip but still considered.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 30,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Fanjove (7f AW 2yo; RPR 66), Wise Men Say (5f/7f including 2yo) and an Italian 6f/7.5f winner; dam once-raced half-sister to winners Nidhaal (6f 2yo Listed) and Maslak (prolific 11.6f-1m6f); already gelding and has pretty good standard to reach on debut but represents a respected stable.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £24,000 yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Mearall (6f 2yo; RPR 89) and Love Lies (6f; 78); dam unraced half-sister to a French middle-distance winner; already gelded; stable has only occasional first-time-out 2yo winners.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 30,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to winners Hot Cash (7f 2yo/1m; RPR 93) and Spirit Of Athene (1m AW 2yo; 66); dam 1m2f/1m4f winner (including Group 3; 109), half-sister to winners Hatta Fort (7f 2yo Group 2) and Blue Bayou (7f 2yo Group 3); already gelded; one of three newcomers from the stable.

RaceName: EBF Stallions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 7,500gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to three winners including Plegastell (French 1m 2yo including Listed; RPR 100) and a French 10.5f winner; dam placed 1m1f-1m3f in France, half-sister to US 8.5f Grade 2 winner Pleuven; considered only if lively in the betting.

Needs to rebound from a substandard AW run ten days ago but made the frame on two of three turf starts in the spring (both times over 1m1f) and this longer trip probably suits better; can't be ignored off current mark.
A maiden after 12 runs and not yet proven over this fair but gave good account when third at Hamilton (8.3f, good to soft) last month and enters calculations.
Now 6b lower than when coming from off the pace to open account at Hamilton (1m1f, soft) in September but has been out of form this year (four runs) and needs to turn a corner.

Runner-up three consecutive times in recent weeks, perhaps most notably when losing out only by a head in higher-grade company at Carlisle (1m3f, good to soft) two starts ago; also suited by this C&D; likely contender.

Dual course winner (1m/1m2f); not the force of old but returned from break with respectable runs of late, most recently when a never-nearer third over C&D (good) yesterday; should be in the thick of it if turning out again.
Did well last year, winning three times over 1m2f; yet to hit top gear after three starts this season (1m3f-1m4f) but is back on a good mark and won't mind what the weather does; might be the answer.

Became disappointing for Marcus Tregoning last year and has been safely held on all three starts for Jim Goldie this season but continues to slip down the weights and will be favoured by the forecast rain; can't be written off just yet.
Unplaced all nine starts; took step in right direction when fourth of six at Musselburgh (1m4f, good) on Tuesday but moves up from 0-55 grade here and needs to build on that recent effort.

Belatedly came good with two 1m1f wins last summer and looked back in prime form when close second at Pontefract (1m2f, good) last month but his latest run was slightly disappointing and others in the field have more persuasive profiles.
Weakened into fourth of five after making the running at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) a fortnight ago and is now 0-7; needs to find something extra here.
Ran quite well in AW nursery on stable debut in November but was well beaten twice on turf in the spring (1m/8.3f); moves up in trip with cheekpieces tried here; others have much less to prove.

OwnerName: Gordon Bulloch & S Hardcastle D Szepler
Spotlight: Won twice on ground slower than good last season (1m1f/1m2f) and is still quite lightly raced but looked out of sorts on both appearances this spring and needs recent wind op to have made a difference.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Down the field over 6f and then in a higher grade than this on last two starts but he has more going for him back over C&D, having won his first attempt last August (good) and then quite readily in May (good to firm); solid claims for last year's winning trainer-jockey combination.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Successful in 7f AW maiden in May 2025 for previous yard; has see-sawed in handicaps since but has been back in better form over 1m off a reduced mark of late and the return to 7f should suit; thereabouts.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple wins include three over C&D earlier in his career; has run well on occasions since a narrow Musselburgh win (7f, good) in April off this mark and a recent never-nearer third of eight there under Jake Dickson puts him in this picture; yard runs three.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Had just two 7f runs on turf for this yard, winning over C&D (good to firm) in May, then third to The Gay Blade here (soft) 17 days ago; weights chance of turning things round with that one but Paul Mulrennan switches to Native Honey; a good run is still on the cards.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Three AW wins; 0-9 on turf but has a 7f race in him on grass on best form in the spring and had another troubled run at Beverley on Friday; has worn various aids on last two starts, goes without this time.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
OwnerName: The Old Etonian Racing Syndicate II
Spotlight: Ahead of his mark when a ready winner over 7f on Lingfield AW in May and he had more to do when running with credit behind an improver at Newbury (1m, good to soft) since; this looks his trip for now and much respected for in-form yard.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Usually leads, as when getting off the mark in a 7f Musselburgh maiden last August; had had some disappointments since, though, and he found zero back over that C&D in June; hard to fancy just now.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Down the weights since some ordinary 7f runs in the middle months of 2025 and looked as if he might take advantage shortly when a close second at Thirsk (1m, good) in April following a break; however, he hasn't run to the same level since and needs a revival returned to 7f.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden who ran some fair races at sprint trips for previous yard but she finished remote over 6f on this track for new yard in June and can only watch over new trip.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Has risen to new heights this year, winning six times, the most recent three over 7f on turf (good and soft), but his charmed run ended at Musselburgh last week; runs off the same mark today and has ground to make up on Abduction; may need weight off his back now.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Won three in a row over 1m and 7f at Musselburgh (all on good) last June and July but she wasn't seen after August and finished down the field on her recent reappearance; dropped just 1lb and likely other days.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
HorseName: Ey Up He's A Star right
Spotlight: Campaigned mainly over 6f/7f but looked suited by step up to 1m when keeping-on third of 18 at Doncaster (good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) three weeks ago; could still have more to offer over this trip; high on the list.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who was seventh of eight behind Hunterian over C&D (soft) on recent stable/handicap debut; may fare better on the forecast faster ground today but is not the most obvious answer.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Soundly beaten all three 2yo starts (6f-7f) but placed behind Hunterian over C&D (soft) when upped in trip for recent seasonal/stable/handicap debut; still unexposed and will be dangerous if that run brought him on.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time C&D winner last year, making all on two occasions and ridden prominently on the other; much less effective when ridden very patiently on all four appearances this season but her keeping-on fifth here (1m2f, soft) last month was a big step back in the right direction; well handicapped now and needs close attention in the betting.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Clear second at Redcar (7f, good) last month, despite racing too freely in first-time cheekpieces (now removed), and has now performed well on all three starts for new stable this year; still unexposed over 1m; respected.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Placed on first two starts this season (7f/8.3f) before opening account over C&D (good to soft) 17 days ago; has scope for further progress and recent 3lb rise does not look excessive; likely contender.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
OwnerName: Making Headway Racing And K Scott
Spotlight: Well handicapped on pick of this year's AW form but finished well down the field on both starts last month (1m1f/7f) and is now 0-23 on turf.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
OwnerName: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt
Spotlight: Just held on to score over C&D (good to firm) in May and looked unlucky at Hamilton (8.3f, good to firm) two starts ago (persistently denied clear run) but has become rather inconsistent and it's hard to predict whether the first-time cheekpieces will get the best out out him.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: 11-race maiden; seemingly aided by first-time tongue-tie when respectable fifth of eight at Carlisle (7f, good) three weeks ago and ought to be suited by this return to 1m but others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to make any further progress since winning 1m Ripon maiden a year ago and has been safely held on all three starts this season; might be aided by new tongue-tie and/or cheekpieces, though.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Close up on consecutive outings in May (5f/6f) and also ran well when sixth of 21 at York (6f, good; behind Woven) last month; still on a workable mark and ought to be in the thick of things again.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Fifth over C&D (soft) in Bronze Cup in September and filled same position in 19-runner Doncaster handicap (6f, good to soft) on reappearance in March; slightly disappointing on good to firm ground last time but conditions ought to be suitable for him today; one to consider.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner; finished well for fourth at Carlisle (6f, good to firm) in May but has rather mixed record since returned to turf in April and never really got involved at York last time.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Placed twice off higher marks on Tapeta (6f/5f) at start of this year and may not have been suited by the soft ground when safely held over C&D last month, after a break; others appeal more all the same, though.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Readily won modest four-runner Ripon maiden (6f, good) for George Boughey in September but was unable to land a telling blow in two 5f handicaps for new stable this season; remains lightly raced and ought to be suited by this move back up in trip but others have less to prove.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't run badly when midfield at York (7f, good) last month, having been dropped in from stall 20 of 20, and is back on a good mark now; not ruled out, particularly if there's significant rain about.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-French 4yo who is yet to add to his 2yo Group 3 win; safely held on all three British starts this year but positives can be drawn from his keeping-on sixth at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) last month and he might be seen to better effect if the ground is on the slow side of good here.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
OwnerName: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell
Spotlight: Regressive 10yo who has not won since 2024; ran well on consecutive 7f starts in April/May and is well handicapped on those efforts but recent sprint form has been below par.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
OwnerName: John Sagar And Dunham Trading Ltd
Spotlight: Veteran who still retains most of his ability and gave a good account when keeping-on second of 21 at York (6f, good) last month; still only 1lb higher than for C&D (heavy) win in October and will be favoured by any rain.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Made all over 5f on seasonal debut and finished second on both starts since, latterly under Cara Tuke in 14-runner C&D (soft) contest 16 days ago; evidently in good nick; ought to give another good account.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent during the AW season and made nothing better than a satisfactory reappearance when midfield over C&D (soft) last month; on another career-low mark today but needs that recent run to have brought him on.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
OwnerName: John Murphy & Miss L A Perratt
Spotlight: Belied 100-1 odds when respectable fifth of 14 off his much higher AW mark at Newcastle (6f) last week but has 0-20 strike-rate on turf; others have more persuasive profiles.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Gained all four wins over 7f+ and is yet to hit top gear this season but attracted stewards' attention when keeping on for sixth under tender handling over C&D (soft) last month and remains well handicapped; one to keep a close eye on in the betting.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Seemed to respond well to first-time cheekpieces when fourth at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) in May but ran poorly when dropped back to 6f later that month and now goes without headgear; others look safer.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Ended last season with a 7f win on soft ground but made a slow start to this campaign; not the percentage call.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to form at Thirsk (6f, good) last month, making all to win quite cosily, and is now 2-12; 5lb higher in a higher-grade race today but his other win was gained over C&D and he won't mind if there's rain about.

RaceName: Ladbrokes Get More With Racing Bet Builder Ayr Silver Cup Trial Handicap
Spotlight: Smart in his prime and a multiple winner over the years but became disappointing after last season's winning reappearance and was last of 20 when back in action at York in May; needs to turn things around.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Has led; 0-9 but he only just failed to hang on in an AW handicap (6f) on final 2yo start and he's run two good races over 7f (good and soft) for new connections this year; very likely to go well back over 6f.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Has remained in form since breaking his duck at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) in April, albeit he didn't find much back at 7f on Newcastle AW recently; likely to give his running but others may have more scope off their current marks.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: All seven AW wins at 5f but was never involved on Newcastle Tapeta ten days ago; 0-21 on turf and something new is needed back at 6f on grass.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Has course form at 5f; losing run goes back to Thirsk (6f, good to firm) in May 2025 and he's been through troubled times since, despite taking a healthy slide down the weights; risky.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Can lead; came to the end of his tether over 7f on soft ground here recently and he's more favourably treated now than when close up over 6f at Hamilton (good) in May; does need a bit more for a first win, though.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: A good spell in 2025 included a Redcar win (6f, good to firm) and a close second in this race (good to soft); slow to find her form this year but things started to look up when she was second of nine at Carlisle (6f) in June; primed to go well.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins at 5f on good ground; not quite nailing it this year and it's up in the air as to whether this is his trip, having been well held in sole previous attempt, over C&D in April 2025.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on the AW and he's won just once on turf in the last three years; however, he only just lost out over 5f at Catterick (good) in May and has a decent run in him if on song.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Not yet off the mark and he's raced only at 5f since handicapping but he did look worth another go at 6f when keeping on well in fourth here (soft) last month, when first-time blinkers replaced cheekpieces; thereabouts.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden who has finished close up in two of three 6f handicaps for new yard, notably when finding Carlisle's stiff test at the trip a bit much for her in May; lesser efforts at 5f and 6f since, though, the fitting of cheekpieces at Catterick last time not an obvious pointer for this.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced filly who couldn't get going quickly enough on handicap debut over 6f at Carlisle but was struggling from a fair way out when upped to 1m at Musselburgh in June; others more likely for the win.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: One useful 5f run as 2yo; well held in two handicaps last autumn following an absence but has dropped 7lb for those defeats and remains one to be interested in for yard that's won this three times (including two divisions) since 2021, all for Paul Mulrennan; market can guide.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Belated debut in April; slowly away in three of just four starts, including on handicap debut at Carlisle (6f, good), but there was definite promise in her second of eight there; capable of better off the same mark.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner; on a losing run of 17 but was seemingly perked up by refitted cheekpieces when third here (1m7f, soft; just behind La Tosca) last month and competes off another career-low mark today.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Seemed to benefit from combination of drop back in trip and change of headgear when clearcut winner at Wolverhampton (1m4f, AW; first-time blinkers) nine days ago; 6lb higher here and too inconsistent to rely upon heavily but the return to turf won't be a problem.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Gained three of his nine wins over this C&D; disappointing favourite when well-held third of six at Hamilton (1m5f, good to firm) 11 days ago but won over 1m3f there a week earlier and has claims if judged on that form.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Well beaten in a low-grade Irish handicap hurdle in May but 2-2 for new stable since returning to the Flat last month, the wins gained over C&D (soft) and at Musselburgh (1m4f, good); probably still on a good mark under 4lb penalty for latter success, and commands respect in current mood.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Ran as though something was amiss on seasonal/handicap debut in May (7f) and finished well down the field at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) last month; hiked up in trip here; big leap of faith required to support him.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice on slow ground here in the autumn (1m7f/1m5f) but was behind La Tosca and Ebony Maw when keeping-on fourth here (1m7f, soft) last month and the forecast quicker ground today is not ideal.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Well handicapped on old form but hasn't won on the Flat since 2021 and, following a 406-day absence, he finished last in recent 1m3f Hamilton race won by Annandale; has too much to prove.

RaceName: Dream Weddings At Western House Hotel Handicap
OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: Runner-up in all three handicaps; it's questionable what she achieved when beaten 16l in three-runner contest at Doncaster (1m6f, good to firm) last time but she still features prominently in calculations here.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Dual turf winner at 1m2f and 1m3f for Charlie Johnston last spring but career has gone wrong since and was picked up for 1,000gns last autumn; again finished last on his latest run but wasn't beaten far and the handicapper's proving quick to relent; this is easier.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to win in Ireland (5f-1m); bit of hope for his new yard at Yarmouth (1m2f) but that was a poor race, the winner aside (runner-up bombed since), and he has stamina to prove upped in trip again.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins on turf, mainly in the spring months, and she's 0-12 on the AW; better effort at Windsor in May but never went a yard without the usual cheekpieces (back today) as favourite last time; Polytrack record wouldn't inspire.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Won when cheekpieces were first fitted and again when they were reapplied early in the year, so it's a plus point that they are back on today; not shown much since leaving Ian Williams but soft ground was no good to him last time and he'll be happier back on the AW.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Said to have taken a false step and briefly lost her action last time; she again started a big price, though, and first-time headgear will need to help for a trainer who looks to have stronger claims with Moonlit Cloud.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Inclined to pull hard in early runs and has got into the habit of blowing the start lately; that's what's cost him the past twice and this mark is surely within range under Oisin Murphy (0-3 for the yard) if it falls right now switched to a visor.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who hasn't won since 2023, stays a deal further and is on the downgrade now; patchy for his new yard this year and others make greater appeal back from seven weeks off.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who was well backed on turf here last time, when coming away with two subsequent winners; a player on that, having since been picked up from Ross Burdon for 1,800gns, but he's never been quite this far and will need to settle better.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Selling Handicap
Spotlight: Regressed from a fair Kempton debut and has run poorly in two starts since handicapping; the return to Polytrack and a first-time tongue-tie will need to make a big difference.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Bought for 100,000euros at a breeze-up; by Sioux Nation; promising 3.5l second in a Windsor maiden last month (6f, good to firm; 6-4 favourite); that form is nothing special but he should improve for the experience; a possible.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Home-bred by New Bay; half-brother to a 7f AW winner out of a 7f-1m2f winner; interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Colt by Minzaal; beaten 2.75l when sixth of nine on his debut at Newbury last month (6.5f, good to soft; 4-1 joint-favourite); should improve; chance.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: American-bred by Breeders' Cup Dirt Mile winner Liam's Map; half-brother to four dirt winners and a 2m1f jumps winner; market a guide to what is expected on debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Colt by Territories out of a useful 9.5f winner; likely sort who is worth a market check.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 65,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; dam a useful 7f/1m winner; could go well on debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Colt by Palace Pier; £70,000 yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to four winners at up to 1m (one on the AW); dam 7f winner; worth a market check on debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
OwnerName: J F Dean, Mrs J Scott & Lady Trenchard
Spotlight: Well beaten on both starts so far, last time finishing 14.5l fourth of five in a Newbury novice (7f, good to firm; 100-1); plenty to prove.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: £40,000 breeze-up buy who was beaten 20l in a 5f maiden at Hamilton on his debut in May (good to firm; 16-1); takes a marked step up in trip; should do better.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: First foal of an unraced mare by Mastercraftsman; likely to improve for the experience.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Solid Ascot debut from the front in May, when having Romanza and Thunder Home behind, but well beaten at odds-on next time before never involved at Royal Ascot; has something to prove now.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Decent Ascot debut behind Victory Gold (6f) before running as well as could have been expected in the Listed Chesham Stakes (7f) latest; back to calmer waters today for a trainer whose strike-rate with 2yos is up this season (0-26 with them here).

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 400,000gns foal, 600,000gns yearling who's a half-brother to Group 3-placed 1m4f Tapeta winner Nightime Dancer; stable going well and has made a bright start with 2yos (3-8, including a winning newcomer for the same connections); interesting debutant.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Hard to know what to make of his debut second in a small-field Tapeta race in May, with them finishing in a heap; there was promise in it, though, with him coming home nicely at a big price and he's entitled to improve.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: May foal who finished behind Victory Gold and Romanza (since improved) when all three made their debuts at Ascot (6f) a couple of months ago; will need a big step forward if he's to take this.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 100,000gns yearling who made a pleasing debut at Newbury (6.5f, good to soft) when fluffing the start but coming home nicely in a race in which the leaders didn't come back; will have learned from that and is shortlisted.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: £135,000 yearling; inclined to pull hard and failed to see it out on recent 6f Windsor debut; trainer has a much better record with 2yos on the AW than turf, including this season, and this one should fare better.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Big prices for two runs on turf, not looking best suited by the drop to 6f last week despite his speedy pedigree; perhaps nurseries will be his time to shine.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: $22,000 yearling, £62,000 breeze-up 2yo who's a half-brother to four winners in the US; the market will be the ultimate guide on debut.
Has improved since going handicapping, but was 8l behind Worlington at Ffos Las last week (1m4f, good); needs to benefit from new headgear and this step up to 2m.
Unexposed 3yo; bred to stay well and may do better up in trip on AW debut after finishing tailed off over 1m6f on turf (soft) last time.
Ex-Mike Murphy; shaped as though she would stay further when second on stable debut at Brighton last month (1m4f, good to firm); could go well back on AW.
Winning hurdler; off the mark on the Flat at Kempton last week (2m) and although penalised 5lb, is a proven stayer and a possible.
Modest form over 1m for Jack Channon; needs to improve for marked step up in trip on stable debut.

OwnerName: G E Bassett & P R Chamings
Spotlight: Fair third at Chepstow last time (1m2f, good to soft); yet to win in nine runs on AW and, although he stays 1m4f, has stamina to prove at this trip.
Veteran; seven-time C&D winner, but latest success was back in 2024 and he hasn't been at his best on the Flat this year.
Winning hurdler/chaser but maiden on the Flat; fair third over 1m4f at Kempton two runs back, but tailed off at Bath last time (1m4f, good); others appeal more.
Off the mark when tried in cheekpieces/tongue-tie at Ffos Las last week (1m4f, good); same headgear here and a chance back on AW and up in trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Has won on the AW but off a much lower mark and all his best efforts have come on turf, including two wins in the spring; looked like the handicapper may have caught up again when third to Michaela's Boy last time and he's drawn wide today.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Consistent since returning from 18 months off; easy to argue he's happier on the AW than turf, so he's at least entitled to close the gap on Michaela's Boy from Yarmouth on 5lb better terms; the hood comes off today.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Poor effort last time will need forgiving but he's always been capable of the odd stinker; better drawn than for two runs here early in the year and it wouldn't be a shock to see him bounce back.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Unbeaten (2-2) under Oisin Murphy until finding traffic in the Windsor race he'd won last summer a fortnight ago; unlikely he'd have won there but he's consistent, fine round here and on a winning mark, so is one to consider.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div I)
OwnerName: Ged Mason, Sir Alex Ferguson, Jim McGoff
Spotlight: First crack at shy of 6f when well held at Brighton (5.3f) last time; still only has a Tapeta maiden win to his name and has had his moments at the stalls; another furlong would have looked optimal round here.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Three-time winner last year, twice on soft ground, who's proved tough to place this season; better effort last time but will need to better that on her AW debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Dundalk regular who's done most racing over further, usually ridden prominently, and been off for three months; would want to see some market confidence for his new yard.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Two wins and a close second to his name in four runs over C&D; tended to be hot and cold in the past, twice winning back-to-back, which bodes well after bouncing back off 4lb lower at Yarmouth last time; goes well for Luke Morris too.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Chelmsford winner off 5lb higher this time last year; boasts a fair record overall at this trip on the AW and a return to form wouldn't surprise; may well be dropped in and ridden for luck from stall 9.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Won over 5f early in his career but has raced exclusively over 6f for the past two years, latterly when winning here for Harry Vigors (then claiming 7lb) off 3lb lower in April; goes well fresh but another furlong would have been ideal round here.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Front-runner who's drawn to attack and boasts a solid record over C&D; not much room for manoeuvre off this mark, though, for a yard also responsible for Ziggy's Missile.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Best over 5f and currently 1-1 on the AW (Tapeta); run in Listed company twice since then and once over 6f, and is best judged on her latest Epsom fourth off the same mark; holds claims on that into a less-competitive handicap.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Lost his way and is now 10lb lower than when narrowly beaten over C&D early in the year; needs a revival but hasn't had conditions to suit on turf lately and ought to fare much better.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Ten-time C&D winner, mainly from the front, including twice earlier in the year, latterly off this mark; this company a bit warmer than he's used to and there's other pace in here, so he'll need something close to a career-best effort.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Front-runner who's resumed better than ever, backing up his Bath win with a Windsor near-miss off 1lb lower (inclined to hang left); might have been better drawn but can't be discounted for a yard that's won with four of its last five runners.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Irish wins came on turf and his record on Polytrack at Dundalk wasn't great; poor this spring and goes without the usual blinkers for his new yard; would want to see support before factoring him in.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Come to hand early in previous seasons but hasn't offered much in two runs back this time; now 11lb lower than when winning at Chelmsford last summer, which shows how things have gone since; needs a major revival.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won his maiden over C&D on debut, in the spring; ran to a similar level under a penalty on turf six weeks ago, but didn't look to see out a stiff 1m2f (stamina was a question on pedigree) that day and will be happier back down in trip.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Pulled hard on her turf debut here (soft) a month ago and again on Tapeta a week ago; those were two weak maidens, latterly against her own sex, but the fitting of a hood and drop in trip ought to help.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well held on his AW debut but much better here last time (turf), when having Santiago Boy back in fourth; the extra furlong will suit on both pedigree and that evidence, and he's high on the shortlist under reigning Bahrain champion jockey Ebrahim Nader.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 1,000gns half-sister to useful 1m2f winner Approval; raced wide on her debut here (turf) a month ago, when a long way off the front trio; likely she'll need more time.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Goodwood second two months ago solid enough; below that level last time down to 7f at Ffos Las (dam won over 1m6f for the yard) under Billy Loughnane (rides Rogue Defence today); still one of the likelier winners.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Big prices and well held in two 7f starts earlier in the year; pulled hard at Doncaster and is now tried in a hood; likely she'll find things easier once handicapping.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Just a fair debut (Tapeta) for Andrew Balding last summer before moving yards this spring; pulled hard from a wide stall on her comeback and will need to settle much better upped in trip; low-grade handicaps probably next.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Taking a while for the penny to drop and will need to settle better than over 7f (turf) here last time (some way off Mythological Star); room for improvement at the start too but he still has a bit of potential.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Pulled hard in two runs on turf last month (1m2f, 1m) and finished well beaten each time; from a decent family but will need to settle down if he's to progress and will likely find things easier once handicapping.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Booth, Silver, Steed And Fittocks Stud
Spotlight: Gelded 28,000gns yearling whose half-brother Waistcoat won four 6f handicaps at an ordinary level; hooded on debut and the market will show what's anticipated.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sole AW effort (Tapeta), when hitting traffic, much better than his two turf runs; may well be dropped in again from a wide stall, in which case he'll need the breaks for a yard in decent form of late.

RaceName: Expert Entertainment & Event Solutions Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to four winners, including three on the AW; no surprise were she one for handicaps down the line, so may be best watched unless there's good money around.
Bumped into one at Kempton early in the year and again on Tapeta in May; things went wrong over C&D last time and he deserves a change of luck off the same mark; his claims are obvious.
Didn't look to see out 1m2f in a 0-50 classified event here last time and is tried in first-time headgear back down in trip; not easily recommended.

OwnerName: Mpr Xxi, S Marris & Rathmoy Racing Vii
Spotlight: Latest run, back from a break in a turf sprint, is best overlooked; holds claims on a few of his placed efforts last winter but did look to find this trip stretching him in one previous go (C&D).
Didn't progress in maidens and ran poorly on his handicap debut over this trip on Friday, when a remote last of seven; hard to fancy on his Polytrack debut.
Tailed-off last behind Fallacious Promise over C&D a month ago; didn't see out 1m2f on turf last time but is seemingly exposed as moderate.
Bit better behind Fallacious Promise over C&D a month ago (always near the front; hung right late on); ran to a similar level when well off the winner on turf last time and needs to find more.

Hung left last time (may have been down to soft ground); had previously offered more in first-time cheekpieces, without quite seeing out a stiff 1m in better company, and a case could be made for this half-sister to a three-time Kempton winner.
Longer trip not assured on pedigree but she offered a bit more last time (6f) and this is more realistic now handicapping; worth tracking in the market.

Half-sister to numerous winners, notably Culturati and Valiant; has not achieved much in three sprints but starts handicap life at a basement level, upped in trip on her Polytrack debut, and market confidence would see her enter calculations.
Had issues at the stalls and is yet to get close to being involved in a finish; the first-time tongue-tie and first-time blinkers will need to make a massive difference.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Represents major stable; started second favourite in Yarmouth maiden (6f) but ended up running as if the outing/experience was needed; open to improvement.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Twice-raced filly; kept on well for close second, despite still seeming green, at Carlisle (6f, good) last time; the form pick and looks open to further progress to boot.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Has shown ability in a couple of 6f contests, finishing fourth at Ripon then third at Thirsk; latter effort was five days ago; again has frame possibilities.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 16,000gns yearling; by Lucky Vega; closely related to Hungarian 1m winner Almos Vezer, out of well-related 6f 2yo winner (RPR 83); stable is 3-4 with 2yos on turf this term; respected debutante.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Hinted at ability, without landing a blow, in 5f event at York where she finished sixth of nine; may do better over this extra furlong with the experience under her belt.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 32,000gns yearling; Caturra half-sister to two winners, notably Clearpoint (Listed-placed 5f/6f including 2yo; RPR 105); stable is 0-14 with 2yos this season.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Both starts in 7f sellers, better effort when rallying fourth of five at Musselburgh latest; doesn't appear to be crying out for this drop in distance.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Only sixth of seven over the minimum trip at Hamilton but ran on in the final furlong and may fare better in this 6f race.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Stayed on for modest fifth over 7f at Carlisle last time; drop back to 6f is far from certain to prompt the necessary further progress.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: £25,000 yearling; by Blackbeard; dam unraced half-sister to 6f/7f Group 3 winner Majestic Queen, out of 1m2f winner; one of two newcomers for her yard; market instructive.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D)(GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 25,000gns foal, 24,000euros yearling; Lope Y Fernandez half-sister to two winners, notably Heavenly Holly (Listed-placed 7f AW; RPR 98); yard also runs Sweet Iris; check the betting.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Front-running, thrice-raced filly whose RPRs are moving in the right direction; good third at Beverley (8.5f, good) most recently; the pick on form and should have her own way at a track that is likely to suit.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Filly by Gleneagles; sibling to five winners, notably Hoodwinker (1m-1m2f; 92); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winner Pongee; represents major yard and starts off in an uncompetitive race; interesting.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Form dipped sharply on final AW start early last year for Sir Mark Prescott; seemed to need the outing when a 33-1 shot on stable/seasonal debut; the market signals may again provide guidance.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Twice-raced filly who brings the worst form and should find handicaps more suitable shortly.

RaceName: Merit Estates Chartered Surveyors Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B-D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 30,000gns yearling; Mehmas half-sister to 7.5f 2yo winner Neardown (RPR 76); dam 7f winner (76), half-sister to 9.5f Listed winner Rainbow Crossing; stable isn't associated with debut scorers.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Respectable fifth at Beverley three days ago; largely solid record at Ripon features a success in 2024 and peak effort this season (close second in early June); likely player returned to this course.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Prevailed narrowly in cheekpieces at Musselburgh (1m, good) last time, registering a first win since 2024; back up 3lb but remains well treated on peak form, which includes a Ripon success; headgear removed; should remain competitive.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran whose 2026 wins at Ripon (1m2f) and Musselburgh (1m1f) show that he retains plenty of vitality; form dipped when back at this venue last time, so needs to rebound.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: In reasonably good form having made the frame in last three starts; however, still has a better strike-rate on Newcastle AW than on turf and this is a step back up in grade; 2lb out of weights.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Five-race maiden who has failed to progress in two handicaps this season; perhaps best watched unless the market suggests a bigger effort is likely.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Front-running filly who ran creditably at Ayr last time when bidding for a hat-trick (maiden previously); remains in form and has a strong record (2112) under Lewis Chalkley; solid chance.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Dual AW winner for previous yard; far from solid on his form for current stable, clear best effort when 80-1 second at Wetherby on penultimate start; 0-7 in this sphere.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
Spotlight: Never dangerous over 7f in sole outing this term; has a record of 3-3 over C&D (all on good ground last term; 0-17 elsewhere) and looks very interesting returned to this scenario; favourably treated off 1lb below last winning mark.

RaceName: Napoleons Casino And Restaurant Leeds Handicap
OwnerName: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown
Spotlight: Posted duck eggs at York the last twice but this is a drop back in class and he's on last winning mark; however, still has a much better strike-rate on AW than on turf and his losing run is mounting up.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Markedly more exposed than Goyard but is similar in one respect, being 2-2 at Ripon; recent form is respectable enough to put him in calculations off a workable mark back at this course.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Has a record of 2-4 this term and scored over C&D (good) most recently; creeping back up the weights but remains well handicapped on historical data (peak mark was 15lb higher); major contender.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran who is a ten-time winner at Ripon, most recently last September; led for long way in small field over 6f here most recently and appears to retain a sizeable chunk of ability.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Has mostly duck eggs in his form figures this term but won at Ripon (6f) in April and the return to this venue, combined with first-time visor, may have positive effect; scored over 5f last year.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Productive mare who has been successful three times this term and finished a creditable second in last two C&D attempts; came up short last time, however, and may be in the assessor's grip now.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Failed to beat a rival last time, as well as failing to maintain signs of a revival; by no means certain to take advantage of a reduced mark.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
OwnerName: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League
Spotlight: Prevailed in small field at Catterick (5f) last time; however, no certainty to follow up off revised mark in this deeper race against mostly rivals who already have winning form at Ripon; 0-3 here.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who ran creditably (1l third) at Pontefract on reappearance and may well build on that effort; 2-2 at Ripon (maiden/novice events) during his first season last year and warrants respect back here.

RaceName: Mark's Choice, Ripon's Most-Winning Horse Handicap
Spotlight: Far from consistent this season but scored off just 1lb lower over C&D in May and has a fighting chance provided he's back in the same form.
Seven-race maiden who is in a consistent vein of form, having finished fourth in last three starts (1m-1m6f); should give her running but still needs to prove she's capable of progress.
Holds weak claims on his 7f/1m efforts but will be suited by tonight's new trip if taking after his parents; improvement looks possible on handicap debut.
Eight-race maiden who is proving inconsistent but has possibilities on peak effort, namely short-head second off current mark at Haydock (11.5f) three starts ago; tongue-tie replaces cheekpieces.
Eight-race maiden whose handicap form, latterly over 1m6f, isn't particularly convincing; may benefit from the drop back in trip but others have stronger claims.

Record of 3-3 since being gelded includes 1m2f wins at Ripon/Beverley that represent solid form and suggest he'll be suited by 1m4f; transferred the ability to hurdles last time; respected with further improvement plausible.

Ten-race maiden; rallied for respectable third upped to 1m2f at Beverley most recently, leaving the impression she'll benefit from tonight's even longer distance and return of headgear (namely first-time cheekpieces); shortlisted.
Four-race maiden; weakened into fourth in Yarmouth handicap (11.5f, soft) most recently, having made the running in first-time cheekpieces, but may improve returned to forecast faster ground.
Only a modest fourth at Catterick (1m4f) on handicap/turf debut but that effort wasn't completely devoid of promise; soft ground may explain a lesser effort in visor since; now goes in blinkers.
Four-race maiden who is showing consistency, respectable fifth in Doncaster handicap (6f) most recently; may still do better at this level; not dismissed.

Thrice-raced gelding who ran well in Ayr maiden (6f) most recently, without quite seeing out his race as well as the first two on the soft ground; may take another step forward now handicapping; shortlisted.

OwnerName: D Jenkins J Glover R O'Donnell J Short
Spotlight: Ended his 2yo campaign with 40-1 success at Redcar (6f) on handicap debut; has failed to back up the form in two outings this season; one of only two previous winners in this field but isn't particularly solid.

Nine-race maiden who is developing into a consistent sort, having made the frame in most starts this term; beaten only a head when favourite at Catterick (6f, good to firm) most recently; holds solid claims and his turn may be near.

OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And M Dods
Spotlight: Lacks consistency but it's best to forgive his latest effort (met major traffic issues); showed best handicap form when third over C&D in May; now 4lb lower and Rhys Elliott takes off a bonus 5lb; interesting back here.

OwnerName: Ursa Major & Mark Walford Racing Club
Spotlight: Six-race maiden last year for George Scott, going closest when 1l second in a seller; sold for 5,000gns after final start; market may prove best guide on stable/seasonal debut.
Thrice-raced filly who holds weak claims on form but may be capable of improvement now handicapping with Catterick reappearance under her belt; check the betting for further guidance.

Nine-race maiden; ran respectably in first-time blinkers at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) most recently; possibilities off a reduced mark dropped in class with the headgear retained; sole runner on the card for his Newmarket yard.
AW winner on final 2yo start for previous yard; has achieved little in turf handicaps this term and is perhaps best watched unless the market suggests a resurgence is likely; drops to Class 6.

Has been making hay in claiming hurdles this year and has won four of his last six starts; dropped back in trip to 2m4f to complete a trio of claimers at Clonmel on penultimate (good to yielding); found to be blowing hard post-race and had a graze when tailed off over fences here last time; of obvious interest back in claimer company, although best form is on softer.

OwnerName: Pat Tennyson & N P Morrissey & Raymond Micheal Cody
Spotlight: 5-39 under rules; three of those have come in chases, the latest last August at Tramore (2m6f, good); moderate efforts in that sphere in his opening two runs this year and even worse back over hurdles at Ballinrobe, although badly hampered at the second; has O'Keeffe aboard now; not ruled out.

Won a Galway novice handicap in October (2m7f, yielding); but out of form since; has youth on his side but form is off-putting and carries 6lb more than Howdoyalikeherhome but would receive 9lb from that rival in a handicap.
On a long losing run since winning a Punchestown handicap in 2023; heavy defeats in two hurdle runs this year; others preferred.

Not much to shout about in two point-to-point runs and moderate efforts under rules as well, including in opening two handicap runs; makes stable debut in first-time cheekpieces but work to do at these weights.
Weak chase form over the past 12 months and holds a modest hurdles rating; beaten 15l in a Sligo claimer last time (2m2f, good); veteran faces a stiff task once again.

One of two for trainer; best of nine hurdle runs came when runner-up in a Clonmel maiden over 2m4f last autumn on good ground; beaten when fell next time and has been tailed off in two runs this season; doesn't come out the best at the weights and hasn't run anywhere near his rating recently.

One of two for trainer; won a novice handicap hurdle at Down Royal in April last year and posted some good placed efforts subsequently; form tailed off in August and his two chase runs this year leave a lot to be desired; best treated at these weights but must return to somewhere near his best after just a six day break since his last run.
Poor on debut at Cork but was much better there next time and improved again when runner-up over 2m at this track since; this step up in trip looks as if it will suit and looks a major contender.
Point winner has run some solid races in defeat in maiden hurdles behind decent sorts; handled good ground when second after a break at Punchestown last time; a big player.

Point winner in April ran a cracker on hurdles debut when third in a 3m Cork maiden hurdle; surprising move to go back to 2m1f at Sligo since but caught they eye staying on; this trip is more suitable and a chance.
Put up his best effort at Punchestown last time with a hood and tongue-tie added; the tongue-tie is left off and could get involved with further progress.
Has shown some ability in points, bumpers and maiden hurdles without winning; well beaten at Wexford three days ago and has to do much better.
Raced keenly when beaten 43l in a Limerick bumper on debut and can only be watched with hood retained.

OwnerName: A Cahill, M Dowd, P Glancy Partnership
Spotlight: Bought for 40,000GBP after winning a point in 2025; off for over a year before finishing tailed-off in a Sligo bumper; has to leave that run well behind.
Tailed-off in a Punchestown bumper on debut and can only be watched today.
Dual-purpose maiden improved hugely on previous form when runner-up in a Downpatrick 2m6f maiden hurdle; needs to show that wasn't a fluke but can be involved again if repeating that.

Third foal; half-sister to 2m-2m4f hurdle winner Morricone; dam bumper winner, half-sister to useful bumper and 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Encore Champs; cost E19,000 as a 3yo; debutante has to be taken seriously from this source, more so if strong in the market.
Third in a Clonmel bumper last year before an 11l runner-up in this race; not as good in three starts since and has to find improvement on his best.
Beaten 42l in a Punchestown bumper on debut and can only be watched for now.

First foal; dam bumper and 2m3f/2m4f hurdle winner, half-sister to useful winners Mount Pelier (bumper/2m2f chase/2m3f hurdle) and Legends Gold (2m-3m hurdle/chase); cost E5,000 as a foal; worth a market check coming from a top yard.
Well beaten in a bumper and maiden hurdle on heavy ground but much better on good at Tramore last time; can be a player with further progress likely.
Has been runner-up in six of her eight hurdle starts but there was no improvement tackling a first handicap at Ballinrobe last time; needs to find a bit extra to get the job done.

Useful but frustrating Flat maiden for Ger Lyons; solid efforts in maiden hurdles at Sligo and Limerick; was ahead of Luker's Tipple on latter occasion and should confirm that form on 2lb worse terms.

Flat maiden has run well in defeat lots of times over hurdles (13 starts) including at Limerick last time; ground-versatile and has the highest published rating in the field so should be bang there again.

His sole Flat win came at this track in 2024 and is now rated 71 in that sphere; lots of good efforts in 10 maiden hurdle runs including on comeback at Punchestown last time; penalised 8lb makes it difficult for him but should run well.
Showed ability in a Galway bumper on debut last October but was well below that level on soft at Tramore next time; has to do more on return/hurdling debut.

First foal; dam point/3m hurdle winner, out of unraced half-sister to 2m2f hurdle winner Long Walk Home (dam of smart 2m1f-3m hurdle/chase winner Rocky's Diamond); by a promising young sire and a decent damline so could run well on debut.
Fell and pulled-up in points; beaten 21l in a Hexham bumper since; one to watch for now.

Seventh foal; half-sister to winners Marshalled (useful 2m-2m5f hurdle/chase), Hands On (2m hurdle) and Teenage Kiss (3m hurdle); dam maiden hurdler, half-sister to useful 2m hurdle winner Miss Pronunciation; nicely-bred and Harry Cobden takes the mount; watch for market strength.

Fifth foal; half-brother to four winners inc Norathir (Listed-placed French 9.7f AW/11.7f Flat, RPR 95; 2m2f hurdle) and Louvetot (French 1m2f AW Flat/2m1f hurdle); dam Listed-placed French 1m3f/1m4f winner; nicely-bred sort cost £55,000 as a 2yo; could go well on debut.
Finished in the frame in three bumpers but has been well beaten in three maiden hurdles and can only be watched for now.
Beaten a long way in both maiden hurdle starts and can't be fancied.
Regressive Flat maiden was well beaten on hurdling debut and can't be fancied.
Beaten 28l in a Cork bumper on debut and will have to do much better with a hood added.
Started at 200/1 and pulled-up in a Limerick maiden hurdle on debut; can't be fancied.

Third foal; half-sister to 2m hurdle winner Likealightswitch; dam unraced half-sister to useful 2m3f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Dorking Lad; by a decent young sire and related to winners; could run well on debut.
Was in the process of running a big race on point debut in 2024 when falling; his absence since is a concern but if market support came then it would make him interesting.
French Flat winner ran a cracker on hurdle/stable debut at Cork but it was a lesser run at Punchestown since so has to resume progress; penalised 8lb.
Promise in a bumper but has been well beaten in two maiden hurdles; better ground may help but others preferred.
Put up decent efforts in defeat in Clonmel and Kilbeggan bumpers, though was favourite both times; quickly sent over hurdles and has to be considered from this source.
Showed improvement on his latest two point runs but was well beaten on hurdles debut so others preferred.
French bumper winner has some good maiden hurdle runs for this yard after falling at Cork on debut; was below form last time at Ballinrobe and is penalised 8lb so needs his best.
Soundly beaten in a bumper and two maiden hurdles; brought down on return; best watched for now.
Pulled-up on hurdles debut; better last time but still one to watch for now.
Well beaten in two maiden hurdles so best wait for handicaps.

Fairyhouse bumper winner last year for Martin Fitzgerald; burst a blood vessel on return/hurdles debut for this yard in a Down Royal maiden hurdle in March; given time to get over that so can feature even though penalised 8lb.
Some promise in bumpers but not much in two maiden hurdles so best watched for now.
Beaten 46l on debut over C&D last year and can only be watched on return.

Second foal; sister to point winner Sound As A Bell; dam point winner, closely related to winners incl' Follow The Plan (high-class 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase); home-bred comes from a decent damline; inexperienced rider aboard so best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Down Royal debut bumper winner didn't build on that in Listed company next time; however, bounced back to win nicely on hurdles debut at Punchestown; this is tougher and she needs more but could find it so is considered.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a 2m3f Limerick maiden hurdle and unseated at the first in a Punchestown handicap next time; didn't jump that well and idled badly when just denied in a 3m Wexford novice last time; down massively in trip; highest-rated here but far from bomb-proof.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed improved form to win a Ballinrobe maiden hurdle last time with the minimum of fuss; needs to find more again but is going the right way and has to be considered.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Was poor in a bumper last year at Ballinrobe but was running well in a maiden hurdle at Clonmel on return when a bit unlucky to fall; this is a step up in class but he could give a good account.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Just a hint of promise in five bumpers; however, it wasn't a bad hurdling debut at Listowel but this demands a lot more.

RaceName: SuperValu Co. Roscommon Ladies Day Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden over hurdles is rated only 74 so is out of his depth in this company; blinkers added.
No impact in three maidens and not much encouragement at Wexford on handicap debut (2m1f, good to yielding); tongue tie goes on now.

Winless from nine but her best effort came in a maiden hurdle here last August when a 3l third (2m, good to yielding); quiet since, including when beaten 17l on handicap debut at Listowel (2m, yielding); needs more but maybe this track can bring it out of her.

Ended a terrible run of form when a surprise winner on the AW in November (1m4f, 50-1); decent efforts in two runs back there afterwards; his previous efforts over hurdles were dire but he has found some form on the Flat since then.

Looked limited until a much better effort at Kilbeggan on his second handicap start when a 1.7l runner-up (2m, good to yielding); the first-time cheekpieces worn that day were retained at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) but didn't have the same effect; a bit hard to trust.

OwnerName: Conor O'Connor & Colum Currie & Mark Devenny
Spotlight: Flat winner on heavy ground; 0-4 over hurdles but some decent efforts all the same; proved she handles better ground when not too far away in fourth at Downpatrick in a rated novice hurdle last time (2m2f, good); can't be dismissed.
Much improved to make a successful stable debut in a 2m handicap at Punchestown in May (2m, good to yielding); back over hurdles off a 10lb higher mark since two respectable runs over fences; player.

OwnerName: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club
Spotlight: Veteran bagged a fourth win at Wexford last summer off this mark (2m1f, good); not disgraced in three runs this year, but more needed to win.
Gained a first win at Wexford last summer off 4lb lower (2m1f, good); he may improve for this return but market check advised.
