Spotlight: Sole success came here in 1m maiden in 2024 when trained by Hughie Morrison; has been below his best for current yard this year and although his mark continues to fall he doesn't look a betting proposition at present.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing On fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Fair third in a classified over C&D (good to firm) last month but has yet to win on turf with all three wins coming at Wolverhampton; best to leave until he returns to his favoured track.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing On fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Sole win from 20 starts came off this mark at Lingfield (7f, AW) in January; turf form not so hot; fell at Lingfield last time; others more persuasive.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing On fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Continues to run with credit and recorded his eighth career win at Lingfield in February; has made the frame on all four starts since and shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Watch Live Racing On fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: No improvement for the switch to handicaps when fourth of nine at Brighton (5.3f, good to firm) in May in first-time visor (retained); not easily recommended.

RaceName: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap
Spotlight: Can race freely and has done a lot of his racing over shorter, often in headgear, but proved this trip is within range on his Windsor comeback in May; below that level twice since though.

RaceName: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to score this year but has run well a few times, latest when third of five at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm) ten days ago; should be thereabouts but would prefer some give in the ground.

RaceName: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap
Spotlight: Got back on track for first success with this yard at Epsom (1m2f, good) last Wednesday; runs off the same mark here as that was a race confined to apprentices and he should be in the shake-up once more.

RaceName: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap
Spotlight: Has an inconsistent profile but both wins have been off higher marks so cannot be discounted.

RaceName: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark over 1m4f at Kempton AW in April but below par in handicaps at Windsor and back at Kempton since; drops back in trip and needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins came in 2025 over C&D; not in the best of form at present and needs to show improvement.

RaceName: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap
Spotlight: Has yet to score this year but is getting close, finishing second of eight over C&D (good to firm) last Tuesday; 8lb lower than for his two wins last autumn.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Classified Stakes
Spotlight: It's been a while since he got his head in front on the line; was beaten less than 3l at Windsor last month and drops into a classified; needs to be considered.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Has won twice this year and did well after a slow start to claim fourth in a similar race at Lingfield (1m2f, good) last month; if he breaks on terms then he holds strong claims.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Had shown little until finishing fourth in a similar event at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) 11 days ago; needs to build on that but may do so and has place claims at least.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 0-14 under rules and has been out of sorts over hurdles and on the Flat this year for Joe Ponting; makes his debut for shrewd yard but hard to make a case for.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Yet to make the frame in six starts; first go in a classified but others are more compelling.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Hasn't won since 2023 but went close when beaten a neck over C&D (good to firm) last month; first run in a classified and should go close.

RaceName: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 3yo who's not finished closer than 8l to a winner and has finished last in her last two contests with cheekpieces worn (removed here); sports first-time tongue-tie and steps up in trip but has plenty to prove.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Scored on the AW in March and recorded his first win on turf over C&D (good) four weeks ago; tends to be slowly away but finishes strongly; holds strong claims to go in again off 4lb higher.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Trailblazer who is not the force he once was, but was runner-up at Windsor (5f, good to firm) last month; should give his supporters a run for their money but vulnerable to a late closer.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Has three AW wins at Wolverhampton and got off the mark on turf over C&D in May off 7lb lower; placed twice since and should go close.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
Spotlight: Has six wins to his name but yet to score for his current trainer; best recent effort when runner-up at Lingfield (5f, AW) last month; not out of this.

RaceName: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap
OwnerName: K Hunter, D Leon And Hunscote Stud Ltd
Spotlight: 0-9 but ran his best race this year when third of eight at Goodwood (5f, good) last month in first-time cheekpieces (retained); just needs a bit more to shed his maiden tag.

RaceName: Wakefield Thornes Cricket Club Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown signs of promise, notably when 7l third over C&D on debut (good to soft); not quite so good since; looks to have stiffish task on handicap debut.

RaceName: Wakefield Thornes Cricket Club Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Chester seller last month (7f, good to soft) and ran to a similar level of form when fifth in a Salisbury novice last week (6f, good to firm); a chance on handicap debut.

RaceName: Wakefield Thornes Cricket Club Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Signs of promise, including when fourth in a Beverley claimer last time (5f, good to firm); an interesting contender off this basement mark on her handicap debut.

RaceName: Wakefield Thornes Cricket Club Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Fourth in maidens over 5f/6f on good ground here and merits consideration off her low mark on her handicap debut.

RaceName: Wakefield Thornes Cricket Club Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Best run when 4.25l fifth of nine in a Redcar maiden on third start (6f, good to firm); chance on that form, but was disappointing in a Nottingham novice last time (5f, good to soft); today's better ground should suit; handicap debut.

RaceName: Wakefield Thornes Cricket Club Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Creditable fifth in a 5f novice here on debut (good); fair run on AW next time, but not so good in a Chester seller when last seen out in May (6f, good); not obviously well treated on handicap debut.

RaceName: Wakefield Thornes Cricket Club Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: T&t Racing Ltd And Keith West
Spotlight: Best run when 4.25l fourth in a maiden at Redcar on second start (6f, good); not so good on other two starts (well beaten in a York seller last time).

RaceName: Wakefield Thornes Cricket Club Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Shaped with plenty of promise when fifth in a Beverley maiden on her debut (5f, good to soft; not the best of runs, flashed tail); disappointing since; a bit to prove on her handicap debut.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has finished third in both starts, firstly at Carlisle (1m1f, good to soft) last month and at Thirsk (1m, good to firm) six days ago; shapes as though this longer trip may suit even if he isn't bred for it; shortlisted.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Third of seven in Listed event at Hamilton (1m3f, good; 150-1) a year ago, but turned over at 1-5 there the following month and has beaten just two rivals in three starts in warm handicaps since; this is easier, but still too many doubts for comfort.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Made a promising start when third of six behind two susbsequent winners on her Redcar debut (1m2f, good to firm) in May, but took a step backwards when only fourth of five at Thirsk (1m4f, good) three weeks ago; still a player if bouncing back to her debut form.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Set the pace before dropping away to finish last of four on her Doncaster debut (1m2f, good to firm; 9-1) 17 days ago; improvement required.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has shown promise in both starts over this trip, finishing fourth of eight on his Chepstow debut (good to firm) in May and third of six at Sandown (good) last month; that's among the best form on offer in this contest; major chance.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won four times over hurdles between July and September last year, but lost his way in that sphere and showed nothing in two starts on the Flat (11.4f, 1m6f) last month; look elsewhere.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Never threatened when fourth of seven on last month's Leicester debut (1m, good; 100-1); may be one for handicaps in due course.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Sent off 11-1 when a well-held sixth of seven on last month's Lingfield debut (11.6f, good); has a taking pedigree and likely to do better in due course, but he will need plenty of improvement on that initial effort.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 150-1 when a tailed-off last of 11 on her Wetherby debut (7f, good to firm) last month.

RaceName: Noel John Curtin Memorial Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Third in two novices (1m2f, good to soft) for Brian Meehan as a 2yo, but has shown nothing in four starts over hurdles for this yard; return to the Flat may help, but risky all the same.

6-19, her latest success when making all to win a handicap at Ascot (1m, good) in May, but she finished behind three of these in the Kensington Palace back there (1m, good to firm) last month; more needed.

OwnerName: Castle Racing Co Ltd & Mps Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Placed just once in 13 starts since her latest success and finished behind a couple of these on her third start for the yard in the Kensington Palace at Ascot (1m, good to firm) last month; down the list.

Losing run up to 15 but she has run some fine races in that period, not least when beaten less than a length into third of six in the Group 2 Celebration Mile at Goodwood (1m, good to firm) last August (entered again this year); respected on these terms and Jamie Spencer knows her well; still holds a Sussex Stakes entry.

OwnerName: J Fyffe, A Edmondson & L McGurty
Spotlight: Won a Newcastle handicap (1m, AW) in January and was returning from an absence when beaten a nose at Carlisle (7f, good to firm) 13 days ago; she will need a lot more in order to figure in this company.

Won a Dieppe maiden (1m3f, heavy) a year ago, but not seen again until well held on her stable debut in the Kensington Palace at Ascot (1m, good to firm) last month (behind four of these); hard to recommend.

Could hardly be coming into this in better form, winning four of her last five starts including another Listed event at Chester (7f, good) ten days ago; still favoured on these terms despite her 3lb penalty and although the stiffer mile is one small question mark, she is a major player nonetheless.
Looked promising when making a winning debut at Newbury (7f, good) in April last year, but hasn't built on it in her six outings since; needs the application of cheekpieces to make a difference.

Made a belated winning debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) in March, but probably put up a better performance when second of six (well clear of the third) behind a subsequent winner under her penalty in a Bath novice (1m, firm) in May; has plenty more on her plate here, but she is an unexposed filly from a major stable.

All eight wins have come on the AW and is 0-8 on turf, including when just under 3l behind Bellarchi at Chester (7f, good) ten days ago; 3lb pull not enough to tempt, especially as she has never attempted this far before.

Won a Listed race at Chantilly (1m, good) to soft in May last year and beaten a neck by Cheshire Dancer in a Group 3 at Ascot (1m, good) two months later; out of the frame in her last five starts, though, and others make more appeal; stable also runs Never Let Go.

Runner-up in this race last year before going on to beat American Gal by a neck in a Group 3 at Ascot (1m, good) 18 days later; not at the same level in three starts this year and although not beaten that far in the Kensington Palace at Ascot (1m, good to firm) last month (four of these behind), still more will be needed.

Twice runner-up in Listed company, the latest at Kempton (1m, AW) in March; has failed to beat a rival in two starts since, though the very soft ground may have counted against her at Longchamp (1m) last time; can be given a chance on these terms if rediscovering her best form.

RaceName: Pontefract Park Silver Sprint Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Has performed well in both starts over C&D, including a game success 16 days ago (good); much more exposed than his rivals, but still high on list from 3lb higher.

RaceName: Pontefract Park Silver Sprint Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Useful for Richard Fahey, winning five times the latest of which was a 7f handicap in Qatar in November 2024; makes his debut for his new yard (gelded in the meantime) and, like his stablemate The Strikin Viking, whether he is on a favourable mark remains to be seen; the choice of James Doyle, though, so interesting to see what the market says.

RaceName: Pontefract Park Silver Sprint Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Bounced back from some lesser efforts when making all and scoring comfortably from a subsequent winner at Ripon (6f, good) 19 days ago; the visor may have helped and although similar tactics may not be so easy in this field, he doesn't have to lead; much respected from 6lb higher.

RaceName: Pontefract Park Silver Sprint Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Responded well to first-time cheekpieces and a drop in the weights when winning at Ripon (6f, good) in May and beaten less than a length into fifth of 12 off this mark at York (6f, good) last month; shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Pontefract Park Silver Sprint Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to his useful 2yo form when winning twice at Kempton (6f, AW) in the winter, but hasn't built on it and failed to beat a rival at Windsor (6f, good to firm) eight days ago; application of cheekpieces will need to bring about a resurgence.

RaceName: Pontefract Park Silver Sprint Trophy Handicap
Spotlight: Has been on his travels, winning a Group 3 at Baden-Baden (6f, soft) last August but finishing unplaced at Woodbine, Meydan and Doha in the meantime; returns from another five months off and hard to know how well handicapped he is; stable also runs Garfield Shadow.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Not at his best in a 0-70 at Carlisle (1m3f, good to soft) four weeks ago, but likely to do better especially if the ground continues to dry out, with his two wins last year (including one over C&D) coming on good to firm; big player down in grade.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Was having his second start for the yard and first since wind surgery when fifth of 11 at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) five weeks ago; 2lb lower than when winning at Ffos Las (2m, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) last August and worth a second look, especially if backed.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Often makes the frame and would have found 1m6f too far at Nottingham 12 days ago, but he is now 0-25; others preferred.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Made it 2-40 when winning by a neck at Beverley (1m2f, good) three weeks ago; wouldn't normally be an obvious one to follow up, but she is only 2lb higher and the runner-up has won since; shortlisted.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: 0-15 since winning a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) as a 2yo, but was beaten less than a length into third of eight at Leicester (1m4f, good to firm) 12 days ago; could play a part if building on that.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Made it 2-28 when successful at Leicester (1m4f, good to firm) in May and although a fair fourth of eight back there 12 days ago (Coverbridge third), it does suggest he needs a bit more off this mark.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Often a front runner who has gained six of his seven wins on the AW at Southwell; Elle-May Croot knows him especially well, but a record of 1-20 on turf suggests others are more likely.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Ran with credit when fourth of nine at Yarmouth (1m6f, soft) last month and does handle testing ground, but looks better on a faster surface; 5lb lower than for the latest of two wins around this time last year and one for the shortlist.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Easily landed an amateur riders' event at Southwell (1m4f, AW) in March and wasn't beaten far into fourth of 12 in a similar contest at Ripon (1m4f, good) last time, but he still needs more in order to come out best here.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: Has made the frame several times this year including when third of seven over C&D (good to soft) in May, but a record of 0-22 is hard to ignore.

RaceName: Pontefract Squash And Leisure Club Handicap
Spotlight: 0-11, but has shown some ability and didn't seem to lack for stamina when a close fourth of six at Beverley (1m4f, good) a fortnight ago; could make the frame again.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
Spotlight: Has run to a similar level in three turf handicaps since winning a Southwell novice (1m, AW) in March; still unexposed and again holds each-way claims at least.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
Spotlight: Blew the start at Carlisle 13 days ago, so possible to forgive that, and is just 1lb higher than when winning at Thirsk (1m, good) in April; this stiff mile should suit her well and one of the more interesting ones.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
Spotlight: Back off the same mark as when making a successful stable debut/reappearance over C&D (good to soft; first-time tongue-tie) in April; possible to forgive his latest effort on the AW and holds good claims otherwise, albeit good to firm ground may pose a small question.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
Spotlight: Has made the frame a few times this year including off higher marks, but is just 1-24 since joining this yard; others more compelling.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
Spotlight: Three of his four wins have come over C&D and is 5lb lower than for the latest a year ago; well beaten in his last two visits, though, so has something to prove.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
Spotlight: Looked a nice prospect this time last year with wins at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) and Goodwood (1m, good to firm), but she has become disappointing; down another 2lb and needs the cheekpieces to help halt the slide.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
Spotlight: Had dropped to his last winning mark when third of seven at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last time, but he is now 0-14 on turf; another placing may be his best hope.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
Spotlight: Nicely bred and has shown ability in his last two starts, albeit the trip appeared to stretch him when third of seven at Thirsk (1m, good) last time; makes his handicap debut in a first-time hood after three months off having been gelded in the meantime; market support would be interesting.

RaceName: Go Racing Bet With Jayne Handicap
OwnerName: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam
Spotlight: Won twice over 7f this time last year for Mick Appleby and is 8lb lower than when successful at Ascot on the second occasion, but six starts for this yard so far this year leave plenty to be desired; others safer.

RaceName: Tallbaldybloke Three Score And Ten Birthday Handicap
Spotlight: Made the most of dropping 2lb below his previous winning mark when beating the dead-heaters Betweenthesticks and Dream Deal by a length over C&D (good) eight days ago; 5lb penalty shouldn't preclude another big effort given he has twice won from 1lb higher.

RaceName: Tallbaldybloke Three Score And Ten Birthday Handicap
Spotlight: 1lb below his previous winning mark when successful at Beverley (5f, good) last month before dead-heating for second with Betweenthesticks behind Hover On The Wind over C&D eight days ago; enjoyed anything but a clear run then and he may emerge best of the trio this time granted more luck.

RaceName: Tallbaldybloke Three Score And Ten Birthday Handicap
OwnerName: J A Swinburne & Mrs Ruth A Carr
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner and again ran well when dead-heating for second with Dream Deal behind Hover On The Wind here eight days ago; likely to be bang there again.

RaceName: Tallbaldybloke Three Score And Ten Birthday Handicap
Spotlight: Won her first two starts for the yard in May last year, but her form since then has been uneven; well held on last month's Thirsk return (5f, good; first start since a second wind operation) and she needs to improve on that.

RaceName: Tallbaldybloke Three Score And Ten Birthday Handicap
Spotlight: Back off the same mark as when winning narrowly at Beverley (5f, good) in May, but he hasn't proved very consistent so far this year so not quite sure what to expect.

RaceName: Tallbaldybloke Three Score And Ten Birthday Handicap
Spotlight: Reponded well to the fitting of blinkers when winning at Redcar (5f, good) last October, but not matched that form since; also has something to prove over this stiffer 5f.

RaceName: Tallbaldybloke Three Score And Ten Birthday Handicap
Spotlight: Losing run up to 21, but he ran well to finish a close fourth of 16 at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) last month; a few winners have come out of that race and a hood is given a go, but the drop to 5f may not be ideal even on a stiff track like this.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Claiming Race
OwnerName: Mrs N K Griffin & T Brosnan
Spotlight: 0-14 overall (0-7 on turf) but wasn't beaten far at all in a 1m4f (gd-yld) claimer at Limerick on latest when an unconsidered 25-1 shot; also went close enough in a 1m4f Dundalk claimer in December; poorly treated on the figures but that was the case last time so could outrun likely odds again.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Claiming Race
Spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Sligo for Willie Mullins in August 2024; failed to win on the Flat for that stable (no Flat win since 2022) but far from disgraced in hot handicaps at the Curragh and Galway on last two runs on the level last season; has joined a new yard and worth checking in the market after a 208-day break.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Claiming Race
Spotlight: Ran okay on second start in a Dundalk maiden in January (10.5f) and again on turf debut when a 4.5l fifth of 15 at Bellewstown (11.5f, soft); struggled on better ground back there latest so has to prove he acts on this sort of surface; cheekpieces on.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Claiming Race
Spotlight: Showed little in four runs for Ger Lyons and similar story on the Flat and over hurdles for this stable; rating of 66 could flatter him; hard to make a case for.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Claiming Race
Spotlight: 1m Thirsk novice winner for James Ferguson last season; poor form over hurdles so far for this stable and after a slightly better run at Dundalk, was well behind Taj Crown and Timandi in a Limerick claimer latest; chance at the weights (rated 70) but risks attached.

RaceName: Follow Tote On X Claiming Race
Spotlight: Fine record of 3-5 on the AW for Andrew Balding; 0-3 on turf on the Flat for that yard and hasn't troubled the judge in three runs over hurdles since joining this stable; pulled up over timber at Listowel after break latest; questions to answer.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: In rear in this event last year; showed ability at Dundalk later in the campaign, notably when second over this trip; failed to reproduce that form over the same course and trip in February.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: Has won only one of her 25 races over hurdles, placed six times, currently rated 99; never a threat in a 1m7f maiden at Leopardstown; marginally better over this trip at Fairyhouse last week; could be of interest with a handicap mark after this.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: Smart hurdles form since last seen on the Flat; won Listed event at Kempton (2m5f, good) last November; third to Woodhoh in Grade 3 at Leopardstown, second in a 3m Grade 2 at Doncaster; out of her depth in Grade 1 at Cheltenham; runner-up in all three starts in Flat maidens last season, the first over C&D; likely Galway-bound, but hard to oppose here.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: First foal; English Channel filly out of a 5f Listed winner by Choisir; sire was top-class over 1m4f, more speed than stamina on the dam's side.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: Point/maiden hurdle/beginners' chase winner; placed in two handicap hurdles before a below-par run at Listowel in May; no Flat experience.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: Six-time winner over jumps in Britain, best known for his 2023 win in the Old Roan Handicap Chase at Aintree; ordinary form in points and hunter chases for this stable before finding a new lease of life when winning a handicap hurdle at Ballinrobe in May; asking a lot here on belated Flat debut at the age of 11.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: In rear in a Kilbeggan bumper in April; remote ninth in a 1m4f Roscommon maiden last week.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: Lost ground at the start and never a danger on debut at Thurles; huge price then.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: Never looked like getting into serious contention in a 1m4f maiden at Fairyhouse last month.

RaceName: Waterford Castle And Resort Style Evening August 15th Maiden
Spotlight: Struggled after winning a bumper on debut in 2023; placed in three maiden hurdles last year; made the frame twice over hurdles in May; fair sixth of 16 behind subsequent Royal Ascot handicap winner Enceladus at Cork on his first run on the Flat; in rear at Ballinrobe on second attempt.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Gained second turf win on latest at Ballinrobe (1m5f, yielding) under 5lb claimer; raised 7lb for that 3l success so effectively 12lb higher here with Keane taking over but clearly in good fettle and should run well again dropped slightly in trip (has won over 1m4f on good).

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden winner in May (9.5f, gd-yld); stayed on well for a 2.25l third of 10 on handicap debut back there on latest (9.5f, yielding); good ground fine and this step up in trip looks the right move; big chance.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: 1m4f (good) Bellewstown maiden winner last year has finished down the field in better races than this in two runs this term; drops in grade now and tongue tie replaces the hood worn last time.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time turf winner but hasn't got her head in front since scoring off 12lb higher at Ballinrobe (1m5f, soft) in July 2024; three runs this term poor but acts on good ground and was second in this last season off 12lb higher; could revive.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins on the Flat (both 9.5f) last season for Joseph O'Brien; heavy defeats all four starts over timber since joining this stable; remains 7lb above last success and stamina to prove for this trip.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Sandown (1m2f, gd-fm) when trained by Daniel and Claire Kubler in 2024; heavy defeats in first two runs over hurdles for this stable; never raced over this far on the level and likely best watched on return to the Flat after short break.

Flat maiden; won a Kilbeggan handicap hurdle in 2024 when with John McConnell; down the field in two runs for this stable; seemed to be well fancied for a similar race to this at Roscommon on the first occasion; blinkered now after a poor hurdle run.

Promising fourth on his stable debut/seasonal return at Dundalk over 1m4f; met some trouble in running when sixth at Fairyhouse; fair chance of turning the tables on Chopsticks (fourth) and of upholding form with Magna Gee Gee (sixth).
Sole Flat win came on AW at Wolverhampton in December 2024 over an extended 1m1f; one of his better turf efforts when sixth at Fairyhouse last month, not far behind Chopsticks and Highway Sixty One.
Winner over this trip on good ground at Cork last summer for Joe Murphy; lacks a recent run and has changed stable; tongue-tie on for the first time.
Almost two years since the most recent of his three wins; lack of a recent outing is a drawback.

114-race veteran, winner of 11 races; best known as a four-time course jumps winner; also C&D winner; most recent success came over fences here last October; well below best on his last three visits, years may be catching up, but premature to write off.
No sign of winning potential on the Flat or over hurdles; never closer than eighth place.
Has failed to show much in three handicap attempts; may improve from recent stable debut when seventh of 14 at Down Royal.
Weak form in maidens; towards the rear in two soft-ground handicaps; hard to fancy unless first-time cheekpieces make a big difference.
Sole win from 44 starts came in a hurdle race here last August; Flat form is unconvincing; may have needed the run over hurdles here in May.
1-27, placed nine times; hard to be confident given his overall profile, but closely matched with Highway Sixty One and Magna Gee Gee on Fairyhouse running.
Has won twice over hurdles; nothing of much note in his Flat form; getting on in years; modest hurdles form since down the field at Dundalk in February.

Won off 9lb lower at Navan last year; had been struggling off revised mark but step in right direction when an unlucky fourth at Naas (1m3f, good) 13 days ago; Colin Keane returns (on board for both previous wins); enters calculations.

3yo made all at Ballinrobe in May (9.5f, gd-yld); beaten off revised mark twice since and is 6lb wrong here (though that is negated by rider's claim); unproven over this far and needs to find more off this mark.

Promise on first two runs of the season for John O'Donoghue on yielding and soft to heavy; another decent effort when a 4.25l fourth on debut for this yard at Ballinrobe (1m5f, yld); good ground is a query, should go well if acts on it.

1m2f (gd-fm) winner for Tom Clover at Newmarket off 2lb lower last August; not beaten far on the latest run for this yard in March at Dundalk (10.5f); cheekpieces left off and worth a market check on return to turf after 109 days off.
Very modest form so far over hurdles and on the level; slow away (not for the first time) and beaten 27l at Gowran (1m6f, gd-yld) on seasonal return latest; visor tried now.

Standout effort a 3l third in a 9.5f (heavy) maiden at Gowran last October; weakened over 1m5f on seasonal return at Ballinrobe; has had issues in the starting stalls before; cheekpieces added to tongue tie; needs more.

3yo; promising efforts all three starts in handicaps; staying on sixth at Naas latest (1m3f, good) but only beaten 2.75l and wasn't far behind Forlio; this trip could suit and he's a leading contender.

Won at Dundalk in January (1m4f) off 2lb lower; 0-4 on turf on varying ground and yet to trouble the judge, but three of those runs were in maidens; should be sharper for Leopardstown handicap run latest and not discounted.

Maiden has finished in the first three in five of his nine Flat starts; not beaten that far in a Naas handicap latest (1m3f, good) when a shade keen early; eased 1lb and now wears a hood and tongue tie; could go well if settling better.
Best run for Adrian Murray came in a 2yo maiden at Tipperary (7f, soft) in May 2024; tailed off on debut for this stable at the Curragh last month; others appeal more.
Maiden is 0-6 on turf but has shown enough to suggest she can win a race; only beaten 4l when third at Ballinrobe latest (1m5f, yielding); eased 1lb and place claims again at the very least.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Much better record on AW (three wins from nine starts, placed four times); 1-12 on turf and needs to improve on recent efforts.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success achieved at odds-on in a maiden hurdle at Kilbeggan last autumn; placed twice from ten starts on the Flat; could feature if reacting well to blinkers.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Bumper winner in 2020; best known as a three-time chase scorer, runner-up five times on the Flat (three times at Killarney, twice at this venue); probably ran close to his handicap mark when third in a 1m7f maiden at Leopardstown last month.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Dual-purpose performer; has won three of his last five races, two over hurdles and on the Flat at Gowran before finishing in mid-division in a handicap hurdle at the Punchestown festival; best Flat form at around 1m2f.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Beat today's rival The Banger Doyle in a 1m6f maiden at Killarney in 2023; a long time since his last Flat outing; however, he comes here in good shape after an emphatic win in a 2m handicap hurdle at this venue in May.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Both Flat wins last season were gained on soft ground; tailed off at the Curragh in May; no impression over hurdles last time; not one of the stronger contenders.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Some promise in maidens; first four handicap runs have revealed limitations; visored now and dropped in class after contesting a strong contest at the Curragh.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Won a mares' bumper at Roscommon last July (good); fair effort on Flat handicap debut at Down Royal (1m5f, yielding); 1m2f too sharp on seasonal return; Roscommon sixth was satisfactory; had a valid excuse (almost slipped up) when fancied at Gowran on latest.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m2f on soft or heavy, latest over two years ago; fair form this season and proved he can stay when second at 33-1 over 2m1f at Limerick last month.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time winning hurdler, including in a claiming event at this venue; not at his best for some time; well beaten in three Flat maidens this term.

RaceName: August Festival Early Bird Packages On tramoreraces.ie Handicap
Spotlight: Gained the most recent of two 2m AW wins in February; turf credentials are relatively weak, placed only once from ten attempts; however, suited by a sound surface.

Three-time hurdles winner (also chase winner); 0-22 on Flat; fair placed form last season, though beaten 11l when third in this race 12 months ago; needs to step up on this term's outings at Gowran and Limerick.

Veteran with 2-39 Flat turf record; most recent success was in 2022; often placed, including over hurdles; 33-1 when putting in a satisfactory display at Sligo a month ago on his first Flat outing this year.

Soundly beaten at big odds in handicap hurdles at this venue (11th of 12 finishers) and Sligo (last of 16) before showing dramatic improvement to win at Wexford last Friday; trainer suggested that the removal of a hood might have been a major factor; hard to find any positives in last season's form in Flat maidens; good Flat pedigree, unwise to discount.

Runner-up in maiden hurdles at Clonmel in May and Punchestown last month; failed to match that form at Wexford last Friday; interesting that he makes a quick reappearance with Colin Keane booked; turf Flat form is modest but not without a chance if reproducing last winter's best AW form.

Well held in AW handicaps; improved in first-time hood on turf return in late May when chasing home hot favourite Ballyadam over this trip at Listowel (good); moderate run over 1m4f at Bellewstown last week; return to longer trip should help.

Maiden gelding by Frankel; started his career with the Gosdens; has shown potential for a belated breakthrough in his two most recent races; recovered from interference at the start to finish fourth over this trip at Down Royal in May; leading contender based on his second to solid yardstick Jurality over 1m5f at Navan; same good 7lb claimer aboard now.
Won three hurdle races and a 1m6f Flat handicap for her previous trainer; suited by good ground; made a bright start for this stable when fourth in a handicap hurdle at Listowel last month.

OwnerName: T & R Hennessy & A Fahy Partnership
Spotlight: Four wins last year, two on the Flat, two over hurdles; placed twice on the Flat this season, beaten 7l when favourite for a 1m6f handicap at Sligo in May; good run when second in a mares' handicap hurdle at Downpatrick last month, well clear of the remainder.
Three-time Flat winner; last success was in 2021; hard to take any encouragement from his Flat/hurdles form this year.
Won two handicap hurdles for Gavin Cromwell in May 2023, both on good-to-yielding ground; has struggled over hurdles in recent seasons; has changed stable and now has his first Flat run since 2022.

Point winner; won a Down Royal maiden hurdle in 2025; varied campaign this year; ran well up to the point of a fall two out in a chase at this venue in April; maiden on the Flat, runner-up three times; in rear at Dundalk in March.
Back-to-back winner in staying handicaps at Gowran and Listowel for Emmet Mullins in May/June 2023; has run infrequently in recent years; tailed off at Listowel in May.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Promising in defeat for Nicky Henderson before a stint in Ireland; has made a positive start for Tom Lacey, winning at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm) and running just as well in defeat when penalised here last month (good); has raced freely and now goes in a hood.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Awr Ltd Sperling Snowden Morley & Stacey
Spotlight: First run after wind surgery and in a new tongue-tie when cosily justifying favouritism in a 2m handicap at Huntingdon (good to firm; second a winner since); has good form claims for last year's winning yard.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Left Karl Burke for 7,000gns and as a seven-race maiden; 150-1 when never a factor on hurdle debut at Worcester.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: He was very smart on the Flat when in his prime but left Karl Burke under a cloud and has yet to get anything going for current yard, on the Flat and in two runs over hurdles; looks on a slippery slope.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well related but went off 20-1 at Bangor and unseated at the very first flight.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Flat winner in France and added a 1m6f handicap at Haydock last August; no impact in two runs over hurdles, though reportedly finished lame after the latest.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten 28l and 37l in two good-ground maidens at Worcester (hooded for both); in a top yard but the positives stop there and probably one for handicaps.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Mostly well held in all his Flat races, especially in the two for current yard (1m6f, cheekpieces; 1m4f, visored); limited appeal on hurdling debut.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Tailed off in two bumpers and suffered the same fate on hurdling debut at Bangor.

RaceName: CopyBet Proudly Backs UK Horse Racing Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
OwnerName: The Soldiers Of Fortune Iv & Partner
Spotlight: Fifth in two Flat maidens in Ireland (1m/1m4f); always behind and tailed off on stable/hurdle debut at Worcester (10-1); hood added; has a good bit to prove now.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won easily over 2m5f here (good) and then made all at Worcester (2m4f, good) where it was harder work; now has to deal with a 6lb rise but he's on a roll.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 5lb well in under a double penalty when completing a ten-day hat-trick over hurdles at Worcester (2m4f, good); 7lb higher this time but this 4yo is improving and seems game, so could well continue the good work as a chaser.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Didn't hang about over hurdles; best chase efforts (0-5) have been over shorter but should stay this far and he's better than he showed last time at Ffos Las; can run well if back on song.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Generally disappointing since her two wins over hurdles in the 2024/25 season and she's now 0-6 as a chaser, from 2m3f to 3m; needs a lift from this new headgear combination.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Value Racing - Back In The Bay
Spotlight: Second hurdle win for this yard when taking a mares' handicap at Stratford (2m3f, good) last July but very disappointing in her last two runs after lengthy breaks; makes chase debut and she was second in a point.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Point winner who ran well enough in defeat at times over hurdles for former yard; unseated over hurdles and well beaten over fences for this yard; had three months off.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Hasn't really kicked on since making the breakthrough over fences at Newcastle (2m4f, good) in April and Hexham last time was a backward step; previous efforts weren't too bad, so if forgiven that last run a case could be made off this mark.

RaceName: CopyBet Daily World Cup Profit Boost Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Miss Jayne Brace & Mrs Karen Smith
Spotlight: Competitive in handicap hurdles but he's not bred for fences and unseated early on his first attempt at Bangor (2m1f, good; 8-1); likely best watched on the back of that.

RaceName: Peter Smith Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has done most of his racing in France but two good runs for David Pipe, winning at Worcester on stable debut (2m4f, good) before a good third off 9lb higher here since (2m5f); up another 2lb and in trip (won over 2m7f in France); interesting.

RaceName: Peter Smith Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Prolific winning pointer; off the mark on second start for Stuart Edmunds when winning over C&D in November (soft; off 6lb lower), but pulled up here next time (jockey reported that he never travelled); goes well on good ground; interesting.

RaceName: Peter Smith Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has won ten of his 30 chases, four of them last year; hasn't been in quite such good form this term, last time finishing 8l fourth of six at Cartmel (3m1f, good), but he's on a good mark now.

RaceName: Peter Smith Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Winning hurdler but yet to score after four runs over fences; good second at Hexham two runs back (3m, good to soft; first run after wind op and in cheekpieces) but poor last time; could bounce back with visor now tried.

RaceName: Peter Smith Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Three chase wins for Harry Derham (2m3f/2m4f); yet to hit form for new yard following 309 days off.

RaceName: Peter Smith Birthday Celebration Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has won two of his 12 races over fences, most recently over 3m5f at Sedgefield last October (good), but not in top form on last three starts.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Looking very progressive and impressed in how he came through to beat a subsequent winner at Hexham one month ago on his first try over this far (good ground); could well take the 7lb rise in his stride.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Fell in her final chase run after a career-best win in a 3m1f handicap at Cheltenham; she's good over hurdles as well, making it 4-8 in this discipline when beating one other finisher by 30l at Fontwell last time out (2m5f, good); the handicapper didn't go overboard with a 5lb rise.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: S T Racing - The Founders' Syndicate
Spotlight: Bids for a four-timer after two wins over hurdles and a chase success in between; had nothing in hand last time at Worcester (2m7f, good) and he'll need another career best off 4lb higher.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won two handicaps over similar trips to this last season, both on good ground; latest run in a Class 2 wasn't too bad and he's capable when fresh; this mark isn't beyond him.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a novice and a handicap over C&D (both on good) two years ago but struggled in a couple of handicap hurdles after a long absence and has since left Olly Murphy.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won two hunter chases in April on good ground before gifted a point walkover; can't be ruled out after those exploits and his hurdle figure is 10lb beneath his chase mark; had win and placed form for Olly Murphy in this discipline.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2024 EBF Final winner off 4lb higher so on a good mark and he rediscovered some form when runner-up at Warwick (2m5f, good) in May; considered.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Made a winning start for this yard in a novice handicap chase last November but pulled up in his final chase and has finished last in two subsequent Flat starts; capable hurdler but hardly returns to this discipline at the top of his game.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won three 2m7f handicaps (all on good) in early part of last season, two over C&D; has failed to fire in two runs after a break but could be dangerous if all the better for a wind operation and new tongue-tie.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner but beaten about 14l in two handicaps after three months off, and he was pulled up in his race prior to those; very opposable; first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: CopyBet Support Safer Gambling Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Useful hurdler back in the day but those times have probably gone for good; no form in two runs for this yard.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Proven winner who had lost his way but got back on track with a near miss at Worcester (2m, good) 20 days ago; just 2lb higher but the new blinkers (replacing cheekpieces) cast some doubts.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Respectable efforts since switching to handicaps, notably a fourth at Aintree on Boxing Day (2m4f, good to soft; fine at 2m and on good); had a break since her no-show at Ludlow the following month; not dismissed.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three wins on the Flat; 0-8 as a jumper but was clear of the rest when a 7l runner-up at Stratford (2m, good) recently and something similar will see him on the scene.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual hurdle winner whose latest success was over fences on Boxing Day; ran well in that scene afterwards but, following a break of 110 days, he finished well behind over hurdles at Hexham.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Made all for a narrow success here in May and only 3lb higher now; has remained in fairly good form since; each-way claims.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Made all for a narrow verdict at Warwick in May of last year; neither run since has been quite so good and current wellbeing is an issue.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: All three defeats over hurdles have been in maidens; has since gone 5-9 over fences, last time making all at Stratford (2m1f, good to firm) after wind surgery; off the same mark for his handicap debut as a hurdler.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: No fireworks in his first three races and on RPRs this initial mark isn't obviously lenient; however, he is clearly open to improvement on handicap debut representing the Murphy/Bowen team.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 11-race maiden under both codes and only recently changed hands for 1,200gns after pulling up on his handicap debut over hurdles.

RaceName: CopyBet Pay First Past The Post Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Eight-time winner over hurdles who enjoys good ground but he's been quiet for a while now.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won over fences in Ireland last August (2m3f); his respectable stable debut was over hurdles (2m) and he was keeping on at the finish; wants further ideally but one to consider under Harry Cobden.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Five wins include four over fences; ran well but no match for Most Wanted at Fakenham in May on his return from a break; chance on that form even from 2lb out of the weights, and 2m3f looked too far last time.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Low-grade Flat winner; 0-6 over hurdles but made a successful chase debut at Fakenham when too good for Getaway Jewel and one other finisher; this is deeper and he's up 5lb.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
OwnerName: G C Myddelton & V E C Taylor
Spotlight: Was going okay when whacking the third last at Southwell (2m, good; beaten 15l) in June, a race he won the previous year off 11lb lower; he does look a shade high in the weights but can run well.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 0-16 over hurdles but came good over fences at the third attempt, making most at Hereford (2m, good); jumped to her left that day but to her right when beaten 4l at Worcester since; still ran well and she's in the mix.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 1-11 over hurdles and 0-4 as a chaser; runner-up in his first chase last November but hasn't kicked on and was well behind Alshadhian at Southwell last time.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 0-12 over hurdles but often placed; chase debut at Worcester was encouraging (2m, good) but she was lacklustre on her return visit when the 2m4f trip was no excuse; not entirely sure what to expect.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Won his third chase start at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March, edging home by a short head; hasn't reached those heights since, last time finishing behind two of these at Southwell.

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Had lots of racing over hurdles and without a win since late 2023; comfortably beaten in two runs after a break and has steered clear of fences since trying them once before over two years ago (tailed off).

RaceName: Read Johnny Burke's Blog At CopyBet Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Low-grade Flat winner in Ireland back in 2023 (sole career success); unplaced in six races over hurdles and beaten 16.5l on chase debut at Southwell (behind Alshadhian) when a mid-race mistake didn't help.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: 55,000euros 3yo; sixth foal; half-brother to four winners including Raffles Quattro, Quadrille Succes (both French 2m2f/2m3f hurdle) and Raffles Tour (French 2m1f chase); dam 2m3f hurdle winner; likely contender for powerful yard.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Beaten 4l in 3m Irish point in April 2025; afterwards she was unraced for Joseph O'Brien; worth a market check.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Clear of the rest when chasing home the favourite in a 3m Irish point two months ago (good to yielding); now with Dan Skelton who adds a tongue-tie; he's of clear interest.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: 22-1 at Worcester (good) in May, where he led 3f out and came home just over 4l to the good; there are doubts over what the form is worth and he has a penalty but still commands much respect.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 2m4f hurdle winner Isle Of Aron; dam placed 1m6f and 2m Flat (RPR 53) and bumper/2m4f hurdle winner; stable 0-17 in bumpers the last five seasons, which rates a negative.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-brother to 3m hurdle winner Hazy Dream; dam 2m hurdle winner; yard 0-9 in bumpers in recent seasons.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB)
Spotlight: Ninth foal from a prolific family; bred to have a future but perhaps not in bumpers (yard 0-12 in this scene in recent seasons).

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten a nose on his return to action in a similar contest at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) last month; looks sure to progress and should take all the beating.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Interesting that connections have persevered with this 4yo having been absent since making a winning debut at Newmarket in August 2024; has been gelded in the interim and commands plenty of respect, especially if the market signals are favourable.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed improvement on his second start when runner-up in a similar contest at Kempton (7f) in May with first-time tongue-tie applied (retained); should not be far away.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to winners Mutawaffer (6f 2yo; RPR 87), Ray Vonn (6f; 80), Harswell (5f-1m; 72) and Golden Bear (Qatari 6f 2yo); dam 5f AW winner (79), sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Habaayib; handler also trained the sire Starman; must be of interest if he is ready to roll on debut.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has shown just modest form in a couple of similar contests on turf; makes AW debut and needs to show improvement.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50,000gns yearlings; ninth foal; by Zoustar; brother to US 6.5f/1m turf winner Lunar Impact (including Listed; RPR 107), half-brother to two winners including Lehoogg (1m AW; 81); dam Listed-placed 6f winner (90), half-sister to dam of outstanding 5f performer Battaash; makes debut and likely to need more time.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fair third for Jamie Osborne on debut at Southwell last September; subsequently sold for 7,000gns; beaten 23l on stable debut at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) last month.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in 5f-6f maidens last August/September; steps up in trip and swaps hood for first-time cheekpieces; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Seventh foal; by Massaat; half-sister to winners Ffion (7f including AW; RPR 98) and Dolly Gray (5f/6f; 81); dam 5f AW 2yo winner (75), closely related to 7f winner Call Me Grumpy, out of 1m2f winner; rare Flat runner for NH yard and best watched on debut.

RaceName: Book Hotel & Conferencing At Wolverhampton Classified Stakes

Five of his six wins have come on AW; didn't look straightforward without usual hood or tongue-tie (back on here) when fourth of seven at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago; two things in his favour here are that he is the only distance winner in the field and this represents a drop in grade, so he needs to be considered seriously.
Placed on plenty of occasions here and last month's overdue first win was also registered at this track (6f, soft); 1-32 win record can't be forgotten but he holds place prospects.
12-race maiden making his seasonal/stable debut following nine-month absence; down to a career-low mark but probably best watched for now.

Fourth at Cork (10.5f, soft) in May in the last of three maidens for Jessica Harrington; sold for 22,000gns but beaten 52l on stable/handicap debut at Ffos Las (1m4f, good to soft) last month; best left alone at present.

OwnerName: J O'Mulloy And W R Muir
Spotlight: Sole success came at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last October; made a positive return to action when runner-up at Leicester (1m2f, good) last month but raced too freely when upped to 1m4f at Ffos Las nine days ago; needs to settle in order to get home.

Has run some solid races in handicaps at Bath this year, latest when runner-up (1m2f, good) last month; there is a good chance that this step up in trip will eke out more improvement in the search of her first win.
Ran his best race when beaten a nose at Ffos Las (upped to 1m4f, good) nine days ago on his handicap debut; there should be better to come and he's an obvious contender here off the same mark.
Hasn't finished within 12l of the winner in five starts and has trailed in last in a couple of handicaps since headgear was introduced; difficult to fancy.

TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: 0-8 but running well in defeat and crying out for a step up to this distance; serious player in bid to break her duck.
Has shown little in five runs between 6f-1m2f; steps up further in trip and hard to make a case for.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Ballinrobe maiden at just shy of this trip was okay but stamina test (2m1f) may have been too much at Limerick latest; back in trip with visor now replacing cheekpieces, can go well.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Ballinrobe claiming third probably her best effort; safely held in handicaps since; seemingly tough task at the weights.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in all five starts, last twice in handicaps; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Hasn't counted in three maidens, showing mild promise; upped in trip now.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Fourth in similar Ballinrobe contest recently; basement 40 mark leaves her with lots to find.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Shaped creditably on handicap debut last month over just shy of this trip at Gowran, staying on third after a slow start; big chance with a repeat of that run.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in four maidens over shorter trips; blinkers tried latest now replaced by tongue-tie and cheekpieces and steps up in trip.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Has had plenty of chances, pick being Gowran handicap third (1m), lost action at Ballinrobe latest; weighted to go well.

RaceName: Dino Family Day @ fairyhouse Optional Claiming Race
Spotlight: Three maiden runs all on soft/heavy, taking a backward step at Limerick last time; tongue-tie fitted and contrasting ground conditions.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Promise in both starts; beaten 2.5l into third of six on debut by subsequent Queen Mary winner Victorious at Naas (6f, good) in April; not as good next time upped to 7f at the Curragh when beaten 8.25l; drop back in trip here a plus and has a chance on that debut effort.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: State Of Rest filly; E85,000 yearling; half-sister to winners Yaahobby (5f-7f AW; RPR 70) and Aussie Nation (Australian 1m2f/10.5f); dam 6f/7f 2yo winner (inc Group 3; 104), closely related to useful 6f-1m winner Band Width; in good hands; check the market.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Saxon Warrior filly; dam Listed-placed 7f winner (RPR 99), half-sister to winners Bella Isabella (Group-placed 7f) and Sara Valentina (6.6f), out of 1m1f Group 3 winning half-sister to useful 7f winners Joailliere and True Solitaire; market best guide on debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Sent off at 12-1 on debut over C&D in median sires series race a week ago and caught the eye finishing a 3l second after meeting trouble in running; enters calculations now with improvement likely to be forthcoming.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith
Spotlight: Siyouni filly; dam 1m winner (inc 2yo/Group 3; RPR 104), half-sister to winners Propose (1m2f) and Minerva (7f 2yo), out of US 6.5f/1m Grade 2 winning sister to 1m Group 1 winner Mohaather; Lordan prefers stablemate but still worth a market check.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 33-1 and well held on debut at Cork in the race in which Argentarius finished second; much more needed.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly; half-sister to four winners inc Armory (7f-1m2f inc 2yo Group 2; RPR 119), Subsequent (11.6f-14.5f inc Listed; 111) and HMS Seahorse (1m4f Flat, 100; useful 2m/2m4f hurdle); dam Group-placed 6f 2yo winner (108); looks top yard's first string on rider arrangements.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Soldier's Call filly; dam unraced half-sister to Italian 5f winner Isolina, out of maiden sister to 6f 2yo/2m hurdle winner Freedom Square, is granddaughter of sister to 1m Listed winner Speirbhean (dam of Dewhurst winner Teofilo); likely best watched on debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Huge odds and no impression in 6f maidens at the Curragh and Cork; others preferred.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: No Nay Never colt; 80,000gns yearling; half-brother to 5f 2yo winner Dinamo (RPR 52); dam 1m AW winner (70), half-sister to winners Go Angellica (1m 2yo Listed) and Southern Belle (useful 5f/6f inc 2yo); one of two for yard and market can guide on debut.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: No signs yet of winning potential in maidens over C&D (good) and at Roscommon (7.5f, good); may need more time and experience.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Starman colt; 400,000gns yearling; half-brother to Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Letherfly (RPR 93); dam 5f AW winner (83), half-sister to 5f 2yo Listed winner Orlaith, out of unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Toormore; likely type on paper from respected source, with some big-race entries; can be a player.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Mehmas colt sent off at 12-1 on debut at the Curragh (6f, gd-yld) and wasn't knocked about when keeping on well from the back in the closing stages for an 8.5l sixth of 13 after a sluggish start; likely to know more now and could go well in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; E400,000 yearling; dam French 1m/1m1f winner (inc Listed; RPR 106), out of maiden sister to 1m3f Listed winner Parthenon, half-sister to Group 3 winners Magna Graecia (15.5f) and Graikos (1m1f); preferred by Lordan and should go well on debut for top yard.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
OwnerName: Philip Antonacci & AC Crawford Farms LLC
Spotlight: Kingman colt; 160,000 yearling; closely related to French 1m4f winner Senguun; dam unraced half-sister to French 1m2f winner Frankish, out of 1,000 Guineas winner Miss France; showed ability when second in Naas barrier trial; interesting.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Blackbeard colt one of two for top yard; the choice of Moore when 100-30 on debut at the Curragh last month but beat just one rival home; will need to improve plenty on that and looks the stable's second string on rider arrangements here.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF (C & G) Maiden
Spotlight: Ten Sovereigns colt; dam 1m AW 2yo/Qatari 1m2f winner (RPR 86), closely related to Scandinavian 1m 2yo-1m7f winner Zakopane, half-sister to winners Admiral De Vega (Swedish 1m1f Group 3) and Zeeyara (French 1m2f Listed) behind King's Fury when seventh in Naas barrier trial; probably best watched.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Talented hurdler whose three Flat wins include 1m4f Tramore handicap last summer; not seen since midfield in 2m Naas premier handicap in October; another likely Galway-bound, jockey booking encouraging.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Sole win came on AW (1m4f maiden); well beaten both turf outings this term and looks to have a fair bit to find.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Four Flat wins at up to 1m4f, including Leopardstown handicap last season off 78; yet to hit form this campaign though, twice not appearing to stay today's trip; bit to prove now.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Two bumper wins and was gaining a second win on the Flat when readily taking 1m6f Gowran handicap in April (soft); 8lb higher and quicker ground to contend with (has won on good).

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Capable under both codes, both Flat wins coming in 1m6f Killarney handicaps, last summer off 72 before good second at Newmarket (2m2f); Curragh comeback run in late May was poor but more expected of here.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: Talented dual-purpose performer is only 1-8 on the Flat but brought her mark to a career-high at Ballinrobe last month when just denied close home in fillies' handicap over 1m5f; quick ground a slight concern, big player if handling it.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
OwnerName: E Lynch & T C Quinn & John J McGrath
Spotlight: Smart staying hurdler/chaser, midfield in BetMGM Handicap at Cheltenham in March when last seen; lightly raced on Flat, winning 1m5f Ballinrobe maiden last season, well held in Leopardstown handicap subsequently; may just need this comeback run with Galway on the horizon.

RaceName: Fairyhouse Winter Festival 28th & 29th November Rated Race
Spotlight: In fine form over 1m4f on the AW since return from a break, winning off 72 in early April and close second off revised mark later that month; didn't count on turf return at Gowran last month and hood now added to usual tongue-tie.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Progressive last season, two wins included Listed contest at Leopardstown (1m6f, good) and signed off with very creditable fifth in the Group 2 Park Hill at Doncaster; has to bounce back from a poor comeback run in Gowran Listed in early May and cheekpieces tried.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Last year's winner who went on to post a career-best when second of 24 in Group 1 Melbourne Cup (2m, good-to-soft); last month's Limerick comeback run well below her best but entitled to come on for that and major chance on ratings.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Winner of three of her six races for Ralph Beckett; purchased for 300,000gns and cracking effort on seasonal return/stable debut when just denied in 1m2f Curragh premier handicap in May; didn't land a blow but not beaten far in Group 3 Munster Oaks latest (first-time cheekpieces) so not one to dismiss lightly.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Maiden has been highly tried at times to no avail but did pick up some black type when third behind Floresta in Leopardstown Listed last term; not a bad effort when fourth in Gowran Listed in May but this is a stronger race.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Maiden twice runner-up in good-ground maidens last year and career-best when beaten 5.5l in 1m6f Gowran Listed in May; never sighted in Group 3 Munster Oaks at Cork on latest; again has plenty on her plate here.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Cork 2yo maiden winner highly-tried in three runs this term when tried in pacemaking role, notably third behind Thundering On in 1m2f Navan Group 3; blinkers had no effect when down the field in Munster Oaks latest and now replaced by cheekpieces and upped in trip.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Unlimited
Spotlight: Dual-purpose mare, whose only Flat win came in Leopardstown 3yo maiden (1m2f); in good form however this term in very competitive handicaps, second at Navan, close fourth of 15 in 2m2f Chester Cup and 4.5l off stablemate Kizlyar when seventh of 20 in 2m4f Ascot Stakes last month; bit to find with some of these now entering Group company but don't rule out.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Mrs H Shanahan & Mrs Jane Shanahan
Spotlight: Ran away with 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden (good) last month but limitations seemingly exposed 18 days later in Roscommon Listed over same trip; upped in distance here.

RaceName: Darley Irish EBF Stanerra Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Group-placed 2yo winner last year who has finished last in all three runs this term, most recently Group 3 Munster Oaks (two of today's rivals ahead).

Narrowly denied on first two starts in 7f maidens at Naas (yld-sft) and Gowran (yld); not disgraced when pitched into Group 1 company at the Curragh last time (1m, good); rated 102 and the one to beat back in these calmer waters.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: In the first three in three of his four starts (all on easy ground) and rated 83; attracted the attention of the stewards when eased up late for third at Gowran (7f, soft) last time in first-time cheekpieces (discarded); should be in the mix again if handling these quicker conditions (dam related to good ground winners).

Rated 72; fine effort on debut in June of last year at Leopardstown (7.5f, good) but nowhere near that level in three subsequent runs; off since a moderate run on testing ground over 6f at the Curragh in March; quicker ground and step back up in trip here a plus but more needed.

Dark Angel gelding; E220,000 yearling; brother to 7f winner Dark Tornado (RPR 99), half-brother to three winners inc Raintree (7f; 77) and Marie Paradis (1m2f/1m4f; 59); dam 9.4f AW winner (69); bred to be decent but beat just one home in Naas barrier trial.

Well-bred Into Mischief colt ran a pleasing race over 6f on debut at Dundalk in January when sent off at 16-5; absence since a bit of a concern and drawn wide but pedigree suggests this trip should suit and worth monitoring in the market.

River Boyne gelding; second foal; brother to 5f winner Boyne Lady (RPR 74); dam twice-raced (60) half-sister to winners Centennial (1m4f Group 2) and Siren's Song (1m2f Listed), out of half-sister to 1m1f/1m2f Group 1 winner Croco Rouge; worth a market check on debut.

OwnerName: One Eight Seven Four Syndicate/Paul J Cahill
Spotlight: Modest efforts all three starts over 5f-6f on yielding ground and on the AW; will probably find his level when tackling handicaps.

Rated 74; standout effort in three previous maiden runs a 0.75l third of 9 at Leopardstown (7f, good); disappointing run off 77 on handicap debut at the Curragh (1m, good), weakening into last of 12; dropping back to 7f is a plus but drawn wide and probably place claims at best.
80-1 and soundly beaten over 1m4f here on debut; can only watched down markedly in distance.
Triple figure odds for both starts and ran accordingly; look elsewhere.
Soundly beaten in maidens at Dundalk last November and on turf at Limerick in June; will qualify for handicaps after this.
Down the field at big odds in maidens over C&D and at Limerick on good and soft ground; may need more time.
150-1 and beaten 22l on debut at Naas (1m, good); seems safe to rule out on the back of that tame effort.
Neither run devoid of promise, over 5f at Dundalk and when keeping on over 6f at the Curragh last time; should appreciate the step up to 7f; might outrun likely odds.
Very modest efforts at big odds in 1m maidens at Gowran and Leopardstown; unlikely winner.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
Spotlight: Generally consistent filly; won for a second time at Leopardstown in April (7f, yld-sft); almost added to her tally over 6f (good) here on latest when denied by a head; the step back up to 7f is fine and ground versatile so she has to enter calculations off just 2lb higher.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner has been running well this season but yet to get his head in front; all his victories have come over 6f at this venue; 3lb wrong here but he ran well in this race last season to finish a 2.5l second off 1lb higher; thereabouts.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
OwnerName: Thomas J O'Connor & Ms Hazel McGuinness
Spotlight: Eight-time winner on the AW but just 1-31 on turf; beaten 3l when third of 6 in this last season and just 1lb lower now; finished a couple of places ahead of Tolebi when a 4.5l seventh of 17 at the Curragh on latest; place shout.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
Spotlight: A winner in France for Andre Fabre; not bad efforts the last twice, including over C&D on penultimate; eased 1lb for a 5.75l ninth of 17 at the Curragh 12 days ago when racing a touch keenly after a slowish start; not discounted.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
OwnerName: W Jones/D W Sloan/M C Mocchetti/Kabin Racing Syndicate
Spotlight: 2-7 on the AW but is 1-13 on turf (win came on yielding); won at Dundalk in March off 3lb higher; the best of three turf runs since then came over 7f at Cork on penultimate; not as good dropped back to 6f at the Curragh on latest; moves back up in trip now and might prefer an ease in the ground.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
OwnerName: Richard McNally & Leo Walsh & L Murrin
Spotlight: Four-time winner, including two premier handicaps; below best on first two runs in Listed company this term and failed to beat a rival back in handicap company latest; is now 1lb below his last win but a revival is needed for last year's winning stable.

RaceName: Winter Festival Early Bird Tickets From 20 Euros Handicap
Spotlight: AW maiden winner has yet to get her head in front on turf (0-10) but has run some fine races in defeat; not a bad effort when she had two of these behind at the Curragh latest (7f, good); eased 2lb and can go close if the new tongue tie ekes out a bit more.
Glimpses of ability in all four maiden runs, both Curragh efforts this term when staying on late over 6f; return to 7f to suit and potential improver entering handicaps.
