Last five wins have all been gained over 2m4f, but three defeats since May's latest Uttoxeter success (her fifth there) have taken her score to 0-4 racing off marks in the 120s; rivalry for early lead.

Bidding to emulate stablemate Handlethekettle, who took the same C&D 0-140 as him last year before going on to add this race; 4lb rise for that latest win leaves him with nothing in hand on peak RPRs and he could still jump better, but there's nothing halfway as progressive in opposition.

2m maiden win here last August (good) remains his only success in 11 over hurdles; debuted hood (retained here) possibly behind a better round of jumping than he sometimes manages around sharp tracks when going close off today's mark at Newton Abbot last month; place claims in same mood.

OwnerName: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson
Spotlight: Worcester record of 3122U1 includes last month's 2m7f win off 6lb lower (good); no margin for error back on his highest mark, but place claims if now able to return to the consistency of his 2024-25 campaign.

Front-runner whose 2024 six-timer came over longer trips than today's; no evidence in three runs since a 14-month absence that he retains sufficient ability to figure here; gains cheekpieces and a tongue-tie.

Generally fast-weakening efforts since returning from a winter break, so the recourse to wind surgery is unsurprising; mark never lower, but effectiveness down at 2m4f hasn't been tested since March 2024.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Pulled up on first two hurdling starts for Paul Nicholls but seemed to be getting his act together when close third of five at Ffos Las (2m4f, good) in May; subsequently changed hands for £21,000; open to significant further progress in handicaps for new stable.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Opened British account under strong ride over C&D (good) in May and has obvious claims if judged on that performance but already looked beaten when badly hampered at third-last hurdle at Market Rasen last month and needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Returned from break with two low-key runs this spring but looked back in good nick when third here (2m, good) last month and will be fine over today's longer trip; one to consider.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Well beaten here (2m, good) last month, after a break, and has now been unplaced all nine hurdle starts; might come on for the run but others have much more obvious claims; 1lb out of the weights.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles but seemingly aided by tongue-tie on last two appearances, more recently when clear second at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last month; competes off same mark here and enters calculations.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div I)
Spotlight: Unexposed after only five starts (one in a point) but was soundly beaten on recent stable/handicap debut at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm) and needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Tailed off on handicap debut (2m4f; wore cheekpieces) in February and probably didn't achieve a great deal when sixth at Southwell (3m, good; blinkered) last month but remains lightly raced and may yet burst into life.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Dual 2m course winner in early part of last season; has mixed record since and is not yet proven over this far but seemed to have benefited from a wind operation when close third of five here (2m, good) last month.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Without a win since 2023 and was out of form when last seen over hurdles in the autumn; this year's Flat form has been fairly respectable but he's on the downgrade and makes only limited appeal back in this sphere.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Ex-French 5yo; began British career with some disappointing hurdle/chase runs this spring but drew clear to score by 7l here (2m7f hurdle, good) nine days ago; escapes a penalty for that and is 8lb ahead of the handicapper; makes obvious appeal.

RaceName: Martin Keighley Racing Club Handicap Hurdle (Div II)
Spotlight: Won twice last summer (2m7f/3m2f) but returned from a break with two disappointing runs last month (both 2m7f) and this drop back in trip is not an obvious move.

Won against own age group on soft ground in the autumn and close second on handicap debut at Kempton (2m, good) in March; below that form behind Best Night at Stratford recently but might just have needed that outing and will be a threat if back on song in first-time cheekpieces.
Unexposed after only four hurdle runs but was safely held when upped to 2m4f for last month's handicap debut; drops back in trip in need of improvement.

Well held when third at Warwick (2m, good) last month, after a break, but gained her wins in the last two editions of this race and is now 2lb lower than last year; likely to have been primed for a bold defence.

Not one to rely upon that heavily but scored off today's mark at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March and creditable third of 14 at Stratford (2m, good to firm) 11 days ago; features prominently in calculations.

1-11 over hurdles; returned from break with a quite encouraging fourth at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) last month and subsequent run may be best overlooked (pulled up after losing action in early stages) but others appeal more all the same.

Won a bumper in spring 2025 but has made a disappointing start to her jumping career; all three handicap runs were over fences, with two non-completions sandwiching a 33l defeat; reverts to hurdling with quite a bit to prove.
Unplaced all seven hurdle starts and absent since pulled up twice here (2m4f/2m) for Ryan Potter last June; 14lb wrong for stable debut; can't be recommended.

OwnerName: John Murray & Exor Of Late Niall Farrell
Spotlight: Well beaten after racing too freely at Perth (2m4f, good to soft) in May and now 0-9 over hurdles; hasn't been with current stable long and may benefit from this drop back in trip but others look safer.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won three times under Sean Bowen (once over C&D) in 2025; well below best on all four appearances this year but has slipped to a good mark and the champion jockey evidently still has faith in him; revival possible.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but returned from a break with a pretty good second at Market Rasen (2m4f, good; refitted cheekpieces) on Sunday and is quickly back in action before handicapper has chance to nudge him up the weights; unexposed as a stayer; likely contender.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-6 under rules; struggled on chasing debut in March but made a promising stable debut when fourth over hurdles at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last month and will probably be suited by this move back up in trip; a possible.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2-13 over hurdles, the latter win gained off this mark over C&D (good) last summer; has lost his way a bit since, though, and was safely held off his lower chase mark last month; others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seven-time chase winner who developed into a useful performer last season; below form at Uttoxeter recently and tricky to weigh up over hurdles but still looks tempting, given he's 27lb lower in this sphere.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seemingly aided by cheekpieces when making the frame at Warwick twice this season (3m1f/3m2f, good) but his finishing was disappointing in those races and he's patently not as good as he was; others preferred.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but was making first appearance for 13 months when keeping on for third at Southwell (2m4f, good) in May and is sure to be suited by this move back up in trip; shortlisted.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Came good at Warwick (3m1f, good) in May, his seventh start over hurdles, but beaten about 11l when fourth on next two outings (one over C&D) and needs to kick on again.

RaceName: CopyBet Overnight Best Odds Guaranteed Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson
Spotlight: Won over 2m3f on hurdle debut in April 2025 but lightly raced and disappointing since; positives can be drawn from last month's reappearance sixth at Southwell (3m, good) but he needs to have come on for the run.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: High-class handicapper at 7f/1m; winless since landing a valuable 7f handicap at Ascot in July 2024 but he is now 7lb lower and he retains plenty of ability; should be sharper with last month's reappearance behind him; bold bid expected as he attempts to win this race for a second time.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 7f conditions event at Newmarket last April and paid the price with the handicapper as a result; left James Owen for 52,000gns last October; well beaten in both runs for David O'Meara but in ultra-competitive Ascot handicaps; sneaking down the weights and the type to pop up at some point soon.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: His progress for James Owen has been quite a story, winning a 6f handicap at Newcastle in December off a 40lb lower mark; fine second-place finishes on his last two runs and still seems to be improving; each-way shout.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 1m handicap at Lingfield on Good Friday off this mark; another solid effort when a close sixth at Newmarket (1m, good) three weeks ago; more needed to come out on top here.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with a pair of heavy-ground wins at Doncaster (7f); better form in defeat this year, placed in good C&D handicaps (good ground) in consecutive days last month; has won on fast ground and his connections took this race in 2024; needs serious consideration.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Improved for a step up to 7f last season; made a slow start to this campaign but he had a wind operation in June and returned to form with a win at Chepstow (7f, good to firm; blinkered first time) last week; penalty in a much stronger race asks a stiffer question.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs J Woods And K Lawrence
Spotlight: In fine form last spring, winning three times including over C&D; cheekpieces returned at Goodwood last month and he recorded a narrow soft-ground success; 2lb rise shouldn't handicap him out of things and faster ground should be beneficial.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Both his wins have come on AW but he handles turf, albeit best grass runs have come on good going; two disappointing efforts since his Southwell win in April and others are more convincing.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Beaten 16 times since his winning 2yo debut but there have been several good runs along the way; two midfield finishes in good handicaps this summer; edging down the weights and he should be popping up soon.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to win for new connections but he has run stormers here (7f/1m, both good ground) on his last two starts, bumping into a handicap blot on the first occasion and going down by just a neck two weeks ago; slow starts are a problem though and he will need things to go his way; cheekpieces now join the tongue tie.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Still seeking a first turf win; fair mark judged on his AW best but neither start this year suggests he is the answer today.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Two-time C&D winner and has also run with distinction in defeat here on numerous occasions; retains plenty of ability despite his advancing years but he would be better suited by slower conditions.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Did best of the prominent racers when third of 19 over C&D last month, splitting Andesite and Lir Speciale in the process; not so good dropped into a Class 4 at Musselburgh ten days ago; minor placing perhaps his best hope.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer; just 1lb higher than for last August's win at Chester (7.6f, good to firm); effective over C&D and last month's reappearance should have sharpened him up; suspicion he will prove vulnerable to younger rivals here though.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Top handicap form for the Crisfords in 2023, including at York; absent for 1038 days prior to his Haydock return in April and duly well beaten; better run here at the Dante meeting and he should be at concert pitch now.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2025 quietly but that resulted in a drop in the weights and he bounced back to form when pipping Lir Speciale over C&D four weeks ago; that was his first run after a wind operation and he retains handicapping scope despite a 3lb rise; drawn widest.

RaceName: William Hill Extra Place Races Daily Handicap
OwnerName: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner
Spotlight: None too consistent for Hugo Palmer last year but he is well treated on his best form; sold for 24,000gns last October; likely best watched back at 7f after 287 days off.

Won this on good to soft in 2024 and also close second in C&D Group 2 on good to firm last year; reappeared with Listed win at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) in May; eased off as if amiss (got upset in stalls) in Royal Ascot Group 1 since but turns out again three weeks later and will have a big say if back near her best.

Won a Cork Listed race (5.5f, good) and Newbury Group 3 (5f, good to soft) in 2025 but hasn't got close to that form in two 5f starts this year, albeit it was a Royal Ascot Group 1 latterly; won over 6f on good to firm at the start of her career.

Starman filly who took her record to 3-4 when seeing off eight rivals in 6f Goodwood Listed race (good) ten weeks ago; this is harder but it'll be a surprise if this lightly raced filly from a leading yard has reached her limit after only four starts.

Second to Royal Fixation in last year's Lowther over C&D (good to firm); made an encouraging return when beaten a neck in Group 3 Nell Gwyn at Newmarket (7f, good) in April; faded to finish eighth of 16 in the Jersey at Royal Ascot (7f, good to firm) but should find life a bit easier back among her own sex and the return to 6f could also suit; owner/trainer won this last year.

Leading 2yo, seeing off America Queen by 1l in the Group 2 Lowther over C&D (good to firm) at last year's Ebor meeting; also had that reopposing rival and Fitzella behind her when third to True Love in the Cheveley Park at Newmarket in the autumn (final start for Ed Walker); raced keenly and faded into a below-form seventh in 6f Newbury Listed race on reappearance for new connections; fitted with a first-time cheekpieces and tongue-tie combination as she bids to bounce back to form with a bang.

Listed runner-up (6f, heavy) last October; also fourth in similar races at Haydock (6f, good to soft) and Carlisle (7f, good to firm) in recent months but her form is more useful than smart and she'll need a big career best to reach the frame in this higher grade.

Listed winner at Newmarket (6f, good) last October; beaten 4.5l into third by Venetian Sun in the Sandy Lane at Haydock on good to soft in May but got a lot closer that rival on quicker ground in the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot, going down by only a head for a big career best, with reopposing Fitzella fifth; the one to beat.

Progressive sprinter in 2025; promising reappearance second in Yarmouth handicap (5f, good to soft) but hasn't built on it twice since, finishing well held tackling Listed company for the first time at Ayr three weeks ago; her claims aren't obvious.

Lightly raced and progressive filly; ran a cracker on what was effectively her seasonal return (unseated her rider soon after start on reappearance) when fourth of 15 in 5f Ayr Listed race (soft) three weeks ago, nearest finish on that occasion so this return to 6f could suit; something to find on form but could have bigger performances in her.

Placed four times in Listed grade but below par in two comeback outings this spring (behind Flora Of Bermuda at Newmarket latterly); off ten weeks; even a return to her best is highly unlikely to be enough.

Made all in the Group 3 Princess Margaret Stakes at Ascot (6f, good) last July; finished behind reopposing pair America Queen and Soul Love when below par on first two outings this year but showed she's trained on when very good fifth of 22 in Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm), albeit Spicy Marg was just under 3l ahead in second.

0-7 since winning the 2024 Lowther over C&D (good to firm) but her third to Sayidah Dariyan in this race last year is her peak RPR; hasn't fired in two outings this year (including fifth to Flora Of Bermuda at Newmarket) but this C&D does seem to bring out the best in her.

Had form figures of 211 last year, culminating with 6f Ayr Listed success on soft (other win on good to firm); well held in the Fred Darling on 7f Newbury reappearance in April, although she wasn't helped by a hefty bump at a crucial stage; off again since.

RaceName: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Caught the eye after a slow start when running on to finish third of six on her debut at Lingfield (5f, good to firm) 13 days ago; the runner-up at Lingfield wasn't disgraced in a Sandown Listed event last Friday, particularly as she met trouble; this daughter of Mehmas should go well with improvement on the cards now moving up to 6f.

RaceName: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Showcasing filly who is closely related to 5f winner Fashion Queen (including 2yo/Listed; RPR 106) and half-sister to five other winners; a newcomer to note in the betting.

RaceName: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 70,000gns daughter of Dark Angel who is closely related to 5f 2yo winner Proverb (including French Listed; RPR 102); dam 6f 2yo winner (78); one of two newcomers in this for the stable and they are both bred to go a bit; betting informative.

RaceName: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Hinted at ability when fifth of ten at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) nine days ago but she's probably more one for nurseries after this.

RaceName: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: 200,000gns half-sister to French 1m2f winner Borcano; dam 6f AW winner (RPR 78); stable 2yos are operating at a healthy 20% strike-rate on turf this year; market confidence would look significant.

RaceName: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Speedy pedigree but her inexperience was evident when a well-held fourth of five on Bath debut (5f, good) 27 days ago; will do better but a good chunk of improvement will be needed to go close.

RaceName: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 80,000gns Showcasing filly who has winners in her family and is with a top yard; would firmly enter the reckoning if the betting suggests she's fancied.

RaceName: Irish Thoroughbred Marketing EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 52,000gns yearling; closely related to 6.5f/7f winner Chorlton Lane (including 2yo/AW/Australia; RPR 110) and half-sister to 6f winner The Strikin Viking (including 2yo/Group 3; 114); makes paper appeal and interesting to see how she compares in the betting to the yard's other debutante.

All three wins have come over 7f and latest was at Southwell in March; creditable third back there next time but has found it harder on turf since; has returned to the mark he defied at Southwell but has too much too prove, including stamina for 1m (remote last of four previous attempt).

Pulled too hard under restraint when a beaten favourite at Haydock (1m, good to soft) in May and a return to front running worked a treat at Cork (1m, good) since, making all by over 3l; the handicapper hasn't missed him but this course often suits similar tactics and David Marnane had a winner here last month.

Progressive; won a shade cosily on handicap debut at Lingfield (7.5f, good to firm) in May and went down only narrowly to a next-time-out scorer at Goodwood (1m, good) since, despite not enjoying the clearest of runs towards the rail; leading claims.

Bettered his low-key debut when second (albeit no match for useful winner) at Redcar (1m, good to firm) last month; well below that level at Beverley since but still brings unexposed potential to this handicap debut with the yard going well.

Much improved under a more positive ride when easily gaining her first win in 7f Redcar handicap last month and backed it up with a narrow success over 1m at Musselburgh three days later, despite her jockey dropping his whip; only fifth of six off this mark at Catterick on Wednesday, though.

Knocking at the door, finishing runner-up over 6f/7f (good to soft/soft) on his last three outings (handicaps last twice); the step up to 1m ought to be within range but today's likely ground is an unknown.

Ex Irish-trained gelding who improved to make a winning start for Roger Fell in 7f Catterick novice in May; has shaped quite well in a couple of handicaps over that trip since (here first occasion); the way he finished off for third at Thirsk last time suggests this step up to 1m is well worth a go.

Got up late to win a 5f Catterick novice (good to firm) in May but beaten upwards of 10l in his four other starts, including a 5f handicap here four weeks ago; hard to know what to expect now taking a marked step back up in trip (first two outings over 7f).

Won a Southwell handicap (1m, AW) last September and he's held his form with five creditable efforts since; ridden more prominently than previously when going down only narrowly at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) under Cieren Fallon a fortnight ago; should remain competitive after a 2lb nudge.

New Bay colt who has shown fair form across his three starts (trained first two by Jessica Harrington); went from the front and faded late as if his recent yard debut after nine months off was just needed; blinkers are added for handicap debut.

Stepped up from 6f to turnover a long odds-on favourite in a match race over 1m at Leicester (good to firm) 15 days ago; hard to be certain of the worth of that form but his rival had been in form with two wins prior to that and this son of Kodiac is unexposed at the new trip.
His 19 course starts have yielded five places and a win over 1m4f back in 2023 but he hasn't been at his best on turf here the last twice; back on a good mark but need to see more.

Steadily progressive; had to work hard to land the odds in 1m1f Hamilton maiden (good to firm) 15 days ago but found plenty for pressure and there should be more to come in handicaps for his top stable, particularly over this slightly longer trip (dam stayed 1m4f).
Beaten on his last 14 starts but he was a good second of 17 over 1m4f here (good) at the Dante meeting in May; freshened up since a disappointing effort at Thirsk six weeks ago.

OwnerName: John & Peter Seabrook And Tim Fisher
Spotlight: Has done well with cheekpieces, winning twice at 1m2f at Sandown (good/soft) last summer; started 2026 with three creditable in-frame efforts (including good to firm) but needs to shrug off a lesser effort over C&D four weeks ago.

Has won handicaps around 1m on good at Newmarket and Hamilton in recent months; advanced her form again when second of eight at Chester (extended 1m2f, good) 13 days ago; one of two solid contenders for Tim Easterby.

Has landed 1m2f fast-ground handicaps at Beverley and Wetherby in recent months; those were Class 6 events so this is a step up in grade but he had a fair bit in hand last time and there is likely more to come.

Course winner who is in a consistent vein of form this term, placing in handicaps over 1m1f and 1m here (both good) on his last two outings; this longer trip asks a different question but he's likely to be on the premises again if his stamina holds.
Five-time winner but recent C&D second of 14 is as good as anything he's produced, leading until the final 110yds; a 3lb nudge shouldn't prevent a prominent showing if in similar form.

Narrow winner of a novice at Thirsk (7f, soft) last September and has been placed in all five handicaps since, including C&D; went down only to a progressive sort at Redcar (1m2f, good) last time and should give another good account.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: The winner of five of his 26 races, including on fast turf; suited by a sharp 5f and this is weaker than the two C&D handicaps he has been well held in this year; others may still have his measure today.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: Enjoyed a productive summer last year and he returned to action with a career-best effort at Southwell in January; solid runs in defeat on his last two starts; another big run on the cards.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: Ready win in a Class 5 at Thirsk last month; similar form when second in a Class 3 at Doncaster last week; she will need more to come out on top today but she's arriving in top form.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: Advanced her form with her C&D win last month and when in midfield back here 13 days ago it was in Class 2 company; others appeal as being better treated though.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: His form has scaled new heights since an eyeshield was added, running away with a 6f handicap at Newmarket last month and following up in a small field at Lingfield 13 days ago; a 5lb higher mark against stronger opposition demands another step forward but he's progressive.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: In fine form last summer, culminating in her Portland win off a 2lb higher mark; slow to come to hand this year but much better dropped back to Class 4 company on her last two starts; this is tougher than the fillies' handicap in which she finished second last week but she is a major player all the same.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: He has turned in a string of good runs this year, winning a 17-runner event at Thirsk in May; while he should give his running it would be a surprise if one or two weren't better handicapped.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: 4lb higher than for his win in this race 12 months ago, after which he went on to score at Glorious Goodwood; fine return to action (after wind surgery) at Thirsk in May but no progress in two subsequent runs; Warren Fentiman back on board now dropping in class; high on the list.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW but he has won on fast turf; good fourth at Ripon in April but he could only manage a midfield finish at Thirsk in May; 2lb lower than when short-headed by Brazen Bolt in this race 12 months ago but he arrived in top form then.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner; won two handicaps last season and he is 10lb lower than for the last of them; not beaten far at Nottingham eight days ago and he's likely to pop up soon.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: 0-15 on turf as opposed to 6-24 on AW; on a winning mark and promising apprentice takes off 5lb but he does need to rebound from a poor run at Doncaster five weeks ago.

RaceName: William Hill Keep Your Raceday Positive Handicap
Spotlight: Firmly on the up for his new stable, making all at Catterick before narrowly denied by Rotokura Belle over C&D; back to winning ways with a Newcastle success 15 days ago (much more convincing than the neck margin might suggest); up 6lb in a better race but he's a speedy and progressive 3yo who commands major respect.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has done most of her recent racing over hurdles (0-15) but returned to the Flat to land a second career success easily in 1m4f Chepstow handicap (good to firm) last Friday, her first outing for Rod Millman; 3lb well in under her penalty and likely to be popular with Toby Moore able to claim 7lb against fellow apprentices.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: David Kilburn, Mrs Corbett, Chris Wright
Spotlight: Largely progressive sort in 2025 (when a two-time 1m2f winner) and she's continued on the up stepped up to 1m4f this season, finishing runner-up twice; the Ed Bethell team is in good form, so she should go well back from a seven-week break.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Quite useful on the Flat at the start of his career; similar standard over hurdles for Harry Fry in more recent times; sold for £15,000 in July and joined a stable with excels with new recruits.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Two 1m4f wins for Ben Brookhouse last year, including this race on good to firm; hasn't seriously threatened back on the Flat for current yard and it remains to be seen whether a return to York sparks a full-scale revival.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Five-time AW winner who made it sixteenth time lucky on turf when seeing off five rivals over 1m4f at Doncaster (good to soft) under  Conor Whiteley 26 days ago; 3lb higher in a deeper race now but the Michael Herrington stable continues in good form.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Slow starter whose losing run goes back 17 races to March 2025; however, he went down by only a nose over C&D (good) last month and was caught too far back in a Class 2 at Ayr since; bold show likely with his sights lowered.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Unexposed 4yo; just second handicap when going close at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) 22 days ago; stuck at it well, so this longer trip could be within range.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Ended a lengthy losing run when making all at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) last month but found out off 3lb higher at Yarmouth since, albeit another creditable effort.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Promising return from absence when fourth at Chester (10.5f, good to soft) for new yard last month and he was best of the rest behind a handicap blot in the Cumberland Plate at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm) since; enters calculations.

RaceName: William Hill Best Odds Guaranteed Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won three at up to 2m on the Flat last season, including on good to firm; winner over hurdles in March (2m1f, soft) but well held over 1m2f here back on the Flat four weeks ago; this trip is more suitable but she needs to leave that latest run well behind.

6f win in Bahrain in April; shaped well at Chester (5f, good) on his return to Britain and again when tenth in the Wokingham, a race where he would have finished closer with a clear run; looks the type to win a nice prize this year but his low draw may be a disadvantage.

Promise on 2yo debut (7f, soft) here last September; returned from 227 days off to win a 6f AW Lingfield maiden by daylight in April and followed up at the same track one month later; found things happening a bit too quickly for him when thrown into the lion's den in the King Charles III here last month but he was drawn lower than ideal; this should be more manageable and he remains a sprinter of interest.

Two good efforts for his new yard in May, beating 13 rivals in a Class 2 handicap at Thirsk (6f, good) off 3lb lower; only midfield in the Wokingham next time and he was left trailing by King Of Light at Windsor 12 days ago; others appeal more.

Made all to win the Ayr Gold Cup (6f, soft) last September; good form in Dubai earlier in the year, winning a 5f handicap before a Group 3 second (6f) in February; 40-1 and well held in the Wokingham latest; others preferred for win purposes.
Yet to win at Ascot but she has run some good races over C&D, including a close fifth in this race 12 months ago; better than ever in defeat this season; another big run is on the cards.

Making good progress for his new stable, gaining a deserved success at Redcar last month and following it with a fine second of 13 at York a fortnight ago; a 5lb rise for that defeat does ask an even stiffer question of him but he's not taken lightly.

Six runs at Ascot have yielded two Group 3 wins (6f) as well as a third in a Group 1 over C&D; back to form with a good third in a competitive AW handicap (6f) two weeks ago; 1lb rise not insurmountable; respected.
The winner of 14 of his 48 starts, including two 5f handicaps this season; dropped away when seventh to King Of Light over 6f at Windsor 12 days ago; others look better handicapped.

Useful 2yo, winning the Windsor Castle over C&D last June and running creditably in Group 2 company on a couple of occasions; two sound efforts this year, including in first-time cheekpieces at Sandown (5f, good) eight days ago; this test should suit and he's a possible.

Well handicapped on his 6f form last season, including a course win off 4lb higher, but he hasn't got it going yet this year; 5f on fast ground perhaps sharp enough and stall 1 unlikely to be a positive.

Saw off 23 rivals when winning last season's Palace of Holyroodhouse Stakes at the Royal meeting (C&D; good to firm); better form when pipped at Goodwood last July; this season yet to take off but she may have needed her return and then there were blindfold issues at Redcar three weeks ago; Colin Keane booked and a revival isn't out of the question but her low draw may be an issue.

OwnerName: L Bond & Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi
Spotlight: Exploited his reduced mark in fine style at Doncaster (5f, good to firm) last month; came up short off an 11lb higher mark in Ireland 13 days ago, hanging left; minor placing perhaps his best hope this time.
Well drawn when third in this race 12 months ago and he is 5lb lower this time around; arrives on the back of some good runs and another big run is expected.

OwnerName: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner
Spotlight: Only 1lb higher than for his win at York's Ebor meeting (5.4f, good to firm) last August; Windsor fourth in May was promising and the Wokingham was always going to be a tough task latest; not dismissed.

Did well for Stuart Williams in the second half of 2025; solid efforts on his first two runs this year before taking his form to new heights with a runaway win at Windsor (6f, good to firm); hit with an 8lb rise for that but it was a good time and there is no suggestion he was flattered; promising apprentice takes off 5lb; one to consider.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: 4-4 in his first season last year, culminating in Group 2 win at Newmarket (1m, good to firm); back to somewhere approaching that form when third to Notable Speech in the Lockinge at Newbury (1m, good) and respectable fourth to Ten Bob Tony in the Queen Anne here (straight 1m, good to firm) since, keeping on again after getting outpaced; likely to go well but More Thunder was ahead of him on those last two starts.

OwnerName: N White, S White, T White & E Burke
Spotlight: Made a solid return to Britain when chasing home Jonquil in a Listed race over the straight 1m here (good to firm) in May but comfortably held in the Royal Hunt Cup back here since; has yet to win above Group 3 level and it's likely that will remain the case.

OwnerName: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four
Spotlight: This prominent racer was pipped by No Lunch (who has to give him 3lb now) in Saint-Cloud Group 2 on reappearance but has built on that with wins in Listed race at Longchamp and Group 3 at Epsom (both 1m, good to soft) in recent months; while his best RPRS have been achieved on good or softer his second on good to firm at last year's Newmarket July meeting was as good as anything he'd achieved at that stage; should go well.

Usually held up; did very well over 6f/7f last term, including summer wins in a major handicap and Group 2; made a cracking first attempt at 1m when 2l second to Notable Speech in the Lockinge at Newbury on reappearance and backed that up by filling the same position in the Queen Anne at the Royal meeting (straight 1m, good to firm); the reopposing Zeus Olympios was behind him on both occasions; sets a good standard.

Won a 1m2f Listed race at Goodwood (good to soft) last September; built on reappearance fifth to Jonquil and Holloway Boy in a Listed race over the straight 1m here when narrowly denied a second 1m2f Goodwood Listed success seven weeks ago; more needed at this level and his peak RPRs have been achieved over longer trips.

Has an excellent record over the straight 1m here, including a Queen Anne win at the 2025 Royal meeting; below par in the latest edition of that race but a Doncaster Listed win and Sha Tin Group 1 third in the spring suggest he's still just about as good as ever.

Won the Group 2 Celebration Mile at Goodwood (good to firm) last August; mixed results in international races on next three starts but back on the domestic scene with a defeat of Holloway Boy in a Listed race over the straight 1m here (good to firm) in May; too keen and never involved back in Group 1 company at Newbury since, with reopposing pair More Thunder and Zeus Olympios comfortably ahead.

Got on a roll back on AW over the winter, including three Listed wins, and slightly belatedly showed he's equally as good on turf when getting up late to deny Seagulls Eleven in 1m Saint-Cloud Group 2 (good) ten weeks ago; very much in the winning habit but suspicion he might prove vulnerable under the penalty.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sottsass colt who picked up well to lead in the closing stages on his 7f Leicester debut (good) last month; the fact the third has won since gives the form a bit of substance; open to progress.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Greenness in front (wandered around) is probably the only thing which cost him victory on his 6f Salisbury debut (good) four weeks ago; that form has been boosted by the third, fourth and fifth winning since; leading claims upped to 7f (bred to suit).

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looks another useful juvenile for his stable this year judged on the way he ran on from off the pace to land the spoils on his 7f Carlisle debut (good to firm) 17 days ago; sure to improve.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Placed in 7f AW novices at Southwell and Kempton in recent months but she'll need to step up on those efforts if she's to get off the mark now switching to turf.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Dean De Zoete Inglett Morris Palmer Rees
Spotlight: Sent off at 25-1 when well behind Rising Tiger on 6f Salisbury debut (good) 27 days ago; needs to leave that well behind.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 72,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to 1m 2yo winner Sugar Goodson (RPR 75); newcomer from a top stable and would enter the reckoning if the betting suggests he's fancied.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 48,000gns Harry Angel brother to 1m2f winner A Major Payne (RPR 84) and half-brother to six other winners; bred to have a future but he'll need to be very useful to take this on debut.

RaceName: Juddmonte EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands A, B, C and D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 48,000gns brother/half-brother to four French winners, notably Panjaman (10.5f-1m4f including AW; RPR 105); leading stable's 2yos have been operating at a healthy strike-rate this year; market informative.

Has contested four 1m4f handicaps this season and won twice in small fields on the AW, latterly despite veering badly right; 22-1 for red-hot race at Royal Ascot (good to firm) on latest outing but finished strongly for sixth of 19, which looked promising for today's test off 2lb higher.

OwnerName: Elb Bloodstock Ltd And R L Banks
Spotlight: Made good strides last season and scored twice at up to 2m, latterly at this track (good to firm); shaped encouragingly on 2026 return (1m2f); failed to build on that at Windsor (11.4f) nearly ten weeks later but takes another step back up in trip today; well handicapped if he returns to his best.

5yo now who won for Sean Woods on belated debut last July; no joy since but he kept on well for a good third of 12 off this mark at Ayr (upped markedly to 1m5f, soft) three weeks ago on second start for new yard, suggesting potential over staying trips; probably acts on good to firm; one to note.

Won from the front in handicaps at Redcar (1m2f, good to firm) in April and Thirsk (1m4f, good) in June; only third when 8-13 for a four-runner race at Sandown (upped to 1m6f, good) on latest outing but he was close up and that represented further progress in form terms; a more strongly run race at the trip would probably suit him.

Not proven on firmer than good (one attempt); second of nine at Salisbury (1m6f, good) in May was his closest finish since 2yo, making most and rallying well; disappointing latest start but that Salisbury run was his only appearance this season over this trip (the rest were 2m); not totally ruled out in his search for first win since debut.

Industrious and usually dependable sort at 1m6f-2m2f; turf strike-rate is 1-20, however, and he was tailed off at Thirsk (good) on penultimate start, before he failed to shine at Newcastle's Northumberland Plate meeting (AW) for the third year in a row; not the percentage call.

RaceName: Side Glance Story Book Launch Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Better on AW but he did win on good to firm last May; placed in two of his AW starts last winter but tailed off in a hot 2m race at Easter (hooded) and well beaten when returned to turf at Salisbury in May; now tries cheekpieces and a lower turf mark is largely negated by being 4lb out of the handicap.

RaceName: Side Glance Story Book Launch Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Improved on second and third starts, latterly when front-running second in a novice at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm); handicap debut saw him thrown in at the deep end at Royal Ascot (1m4f, good to firm; 80-1) and he sank almost without trace having led to 3f out; could still resume progress but he looks best watched for now.

RaceName: Side Glance Story Book Launch Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 3yo maiden winner; running on well into a close third of 12 at Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm) this May was his first significant impact in handicaps and perhaps the ground was too soft at Epsom (1m2f) on Oaks day five weeks ago; this new trip gives him a bit more to prove, though.

RaceName: Side Glance Story Book Launch Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 2-5 in France; also scored in December in Bahrain for new connections; managed only seventh in his three subsequent runs there and, after 11 weeks off, same again on latest outing but that was at the Royal meeting here (1m4f, good to firm; 100-1) and probably his best form; needs to build on that.

RaceName: Side Glance Story Book Launch Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fourth at Newmarket (1m4f, good) on seasonal/stable debut gave encouragement again, followed by a decisive win at Kempton (1m4f, AW) in May; 14-1 but it looked as if 1m6f stretched him when seventh of 16 at Royal Ascot (good to firm; every chance 2f out) and he probably remained in form; needs a close look back at 1m4f.

RaceName: Side Glance Story Book Launch Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Career-best form on her first three starts (1m4f, 1m5f and 1m3f handicaps) this season, two of them on good to firm, before she found a Listed race far too tough on latest outing; thereabouts if she bounces straight back.

RaceName: Side Glance Story Book Launch Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Contested three Group races last summer; 1m4f handicaps (good to firm) this season, registering his highest rating when fourth of eight at Newmarket in May but backpedalling from over 3f out when just 9-1 at Royal Ascot; needs to bounce back in first-time headgear but his sights are lowered.

RaceName: Side Glance Story Book Launch Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 1-10 for Andre Fabre, the win over 1m3f last June; has had three races for current stable and seemingly showed useful form from the front in a Abu Dhabi Listed race over 1m4f in February on second occasion, but a heavy defeat at Royal Ascot (1m6f, good to firm; 50-1) suggests he is best watched.

First attempt at around 1m when winning handicap at Nottingham (good to soft) in May and backed it up with a very good third (Blingy's Sister fourth) of 30 in the Sandringham over C&D (good to firm) 22 days ago, impressing with the move she made from off the pace to hit the front in the penultimate furlong; another prominent showing is on the cards.

Prescott filly who ended her 2yo campaign by making all in 1m Newcastle novice and returned to win a Salisbury handicap over the same trip (good to firm) 11 days ago; a 4lb rise may not stop her with further progress looking assured.
0-5 since debut win but made a good start to her handicap career when fourth (Rosa Inglesa third) in Sandringham over C&D at the Royal meeting (good to firm); claims if she can build on that.

1m2f winner for the Kublers last season and struck at the second time of asking for her new stable in 1m2f Newbury handicap (good to firm) in June, edging out a next-time-out winner with the pair clear; raised 7lb and needs to prove her effectiveness at a shorter trip now.
Has started her handicap career with solid placed efforts at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) and Thirsk (1m, good); likely to go well again

Won Salisbury novice (7f, good to firm) and Epsom handicap (extended 1m, good to soft) last year; only seventh of 14 on 1m Kempton reappearance but the fact she went off 15-8 favourite suggests better was anticipated.
Came good in a small-field novice at Leicester (1m, good to firm) in May but finished well behind a couple of these in the Sandringham here since; others make more appeal.

Turned over at odds on on first two starts this season but made no mistake with cheekpieces added (retained) in maiden at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) 23 days ago, making all; not obviously progressive but still no surprise were she to kick on now she's got her head in front.
Had plenty of chances from marks in the mid 70s last season, ending it with two below-par runs; her mark has eased as a result but it's not enough to tempt back from nine months off.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner from the front last summer but has shown very little this season; tumbling in the weights but carries clear risks.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins at around 7f on good or good to firm ground; possibly unsuited by 1m on soft at Goodwood last time; 7lb below last winning mark and could be dangerous under optimum conditions.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: 40-1 winner of novice at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on reappearance; down the field on handicap debut at York four weeks later (subsequently gelded); has to prove he's as effective on turf as the AW but yard won this with a lightly raced 3yo in 2024.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Won two from five last season and made an encouraging reappearance/stable debut when fourth of nine at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago; stepping back down to 7f looks a positive move and the booking of promising claimer Jake Dickson is a plus; high on the list.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Best form (and both wins) on the AW; bled from nose back on turf when only eighth of 11 at Nottingham two months ago; needs to bounce back after a break.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Arrives in top form after a 7f win at Kempton and two second places over 6f; hard to knock and should be thereabouts.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: On an advancing losing run but he's well handicapped and bubbling under; too free (not for the first time) for a 7lb claimer last time and far from disgraced to finish fourth of eight at Yarmouth; interesting with Cieren Fallon taking over.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than turf but she wasn't disgraced after a break at Carlisle (1m, good to firm; 40-1) 17 days ago; may come on for that outing and she's not ruled out.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
OwnerName: Al Shaqab Racing, Miller And Day
Spotlight: Has gone the wrong way since making the frame in novices at Windsor (1m) and Newbury (7f) in the spring; may come good at some point but the handicapper is dropping him only gradually.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
OwnerName: Kevin Frost Racing Club & Mike Humphries
Spotlight: Admirable teenager with 12 wins to his name, including one over C&D; best recent effort when second of 12 at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last month; should be on the premises again.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: 7f winner on the AW early in the year but seems to have lost his form and finished last at Yarmouth two weeks ago; fair bit to prove.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins on AW but below par at Kempton last time; fifth of ten at Epsom on sole turf outing last spring; bit to prove back on grass.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: 8lb below his last winning mark and ran respectably when third at Newbury 18 days ago, finishing one place behind Documenting; more appealing than some.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Won over this trip at Newmarket last autumn for Mick Appleby but three turf runs for current yard have been well below par; bit to prove.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to form in first-time cheekpieces when winning at Chelmsford last October; unplaced all four starts since and now returns from five months off.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Unplaced all four starts but he shaped a bit better than the bare result when sixth of ten on handicap debut at Windsor (6f, good to soft; stayed on after being short of room) five weeks ago; hard to pin down and may have untapped potential.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Most recent win came over this trip at Chepstow in May on final run for Thomas Faulkner; not disgraced over 6f twice since and looks ready for a return to 7f; gets that here and should be thereabouts.

RaceName: Excell Supply Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Hot To Trot Racing 2 & J Fill
Spotlight: Showed promise when fourth on Salisbury debut (7f, good to firm) in May and she built on that when going close behind her main market rival at Doncaster (7f, good) last month; sets a good standard on that form and she could continue to improve with experience; big player.

RaceName: Excell Supply Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Cost 550,000euros as a yearling; promising second on Nottingham debut (5f, good) for Charlie Appleby last June but she was well held at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) later that month; been on the sidelines since then but it's still early days and she needs a close look on return for new yard.

RaceName: Excell Supply Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Closely related to 7f/1m winner Treasure Time (RPR 98), half-sister to five winners including Equality (5f including Group 3; 117), Badri (prolific 5f-7f; 109) and Outgate (6f-1m including HK; 105); dam Listed-placed 6f AW winner (98); plenty to like on paper and she needs checking in market on debut.

RaceName: Excell Supply Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Big prices and she's been well held in two maidens over sprint trips this season; has plenty of stamina in her pedigree but she needs a transformation on this step up to 7f.

RaceName: Excell Supply Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Four-race maiden who resumed her progress when a 2l fourth of 12 in a Doncaster novice (7f, good; cheekpieces added) last month; was only around 2l behind Dream Vega in that race and she should have more to offer for top yard; in the mix.

RaceName: Excell Supply Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Massive prices and she's struggled in five runs this season, with a best RPR of 50.

RaceName: Excell Supply Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to French/Australian 1m1f-1m2f winner Barazin, out of 1m4f Listed winning half-sister to Australian 1m4f Group 3 winner Qewy, family of top miler/sire Dubawi; interesting newcomer and market should be informative.
Six-time winner; effective at this track and she arrives on the back of a good second under Myla Coppins at Nottingham (5f, good) three weeks ago; has a low draw and looks set for another big run.
Went close in a C&D handicap last July when rated 8lb higher; he has run some fair races in defeat this summer and shouldn't be too far away.
Three handicap wins this year, the latest at Beverley (5f, good to soft) in May; solid efforts twice since and she is well drawn to attack; should put up a bold show back down in class.
In good order on AW this winter; two turf runs this summer well short of that level though and others bring more pressing claims.

OwnerName: R M Brilley, A E Townsend & Partner
Spotlight: Zipped around Wolverhampton when recording a couple of impressive 5f wins this winter so this track should suit; two solid placed efforts since returned to turf; might find a few better treated though.
Drawn wide when winning this race 12 months ago off a 1lb higher mark; not hit top gear yet in 2026 but this should be run to suit him and a revival would come as no surprise.
On a good mark, fully 14lb lower than when winning last season's Great St Wilfrid, but 0-14 at 5f and he is drawn out wide.

Ran out the impressive winner of a 5f fillies' novice at Lingfield (AW) on her debut last August; not come close to repeating it in four runs this year; tumbling down the weights but not a solid option.
Only run to form once in four runs back on turf this summer and that was over 6f; still 1lb higher than for his AW win in December; others appeal more.

OwnerName: Two Plus Three Two Plus Four
Spotlight: Won a 6f maiden here in June of his 2yo season but that remains his only win; unlikely to be breaking his handicap duck from stall 12.
Expertly placed to reel off a seven-timer on AW earlier in the year; winning spree came to an end at Wolverhampton in March and he returns to turf (1-13) off a tough mark.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has been out of sorts in his three runs this season; continues to drop down the weights but he needs a big turnaround back at this trip; visor added.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Won four times last year including twice at Chester (7f/7.6f, good); only eighth of 14 on Carlisle reappearance last month but she had some major traffic issues and was only beaten around 4l; should be sharper this time and has claims if she can get back near best.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: All three wins have been on AW (7f/1m); good second over 6f at Windsor (good to firm) last month but that was a Class 6 event and this looks tough from 6lb out of the weights; entered in 8.50 here on Friday.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Overall record of 3-11 but he's not been easy to predict; finished down the field here when last seen in May but he's now only 1lb higher than when completing a double in this race last year; cheekpieces are reapplied after a short break and he needs a close look on this drop back in grade.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho
Spotlight: Got back on the scoresheet at Wetherby (7f, good to firm) last month and he backed that up with a 2l fifth from off the pace at Doncaster three weeks ago; should get a good tow into this race and has possibilities if he gets some luck.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: All six wins have been on turf and last two were in Class 2 events over C&D (good/good to firm); struggled in his first three runs after a long absence this spring but he got back on track with a front-running second at Kempton (1m, AW) last Monday; 2lb higher back on turf but he remains well treated on old form and is an interesting contender back at this track.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Five-time AW winner who was knocking on the door at Wolverhampton (8.6f/7f) in his final two starts in the spring; only 1lb higher than for his last run but this is tougher back up in grade and he's 1-25 on turf; has some work to do from high draw after a break.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
OwnerName: Nbr30, Mike Owen & Rasio Cymru Racing
Spotlight: Won a C&D novice on debut last September but he's 0-7 since; has run respectably at Carlisle (1m, good to firm) and Chester (7f, good to soft) in his last two runs but he needs to find more with eyeshields now tried.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Six-time course winner who is versatile trip-wise but all of his wins have been on good or slower and last two have been in the mud; on a dangerous mark but he didn't fire in his three runs this season; has often worn cheekpieces but is now blinkered first time; non-runner here (7.35) on Friday.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 3yo who got off the mark at the ninth attempt when swooping late for a last-gasp win over C&D (soft) last month; up 3lb but that was a personal best last time and this return to quicker ground is no problem; respected.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Two wins (1m1f/1m, good) for Dermot Weld in Ireland last year; runner-up at Chester (7f, good) in his second run for current yard but he's posted three duck eggs since; well handicapped on his best form but he needs to get back on track with blinkers added.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Well handicapped on his best 2025 form but he's struggled in his last five starts including two runs for new yard this summer; overall record is now 1-18 and he has plenty to prove.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Improved form since being gelded and he made it 2-2 at Chester when completing a double in a 7f handicap (good) two weeks ago; up another 4lb but he finished well to beat a clear second last time and is open to more progress at this new trip; big player.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: Four-time turf winner who scored off 1lb higher over C&D last autumn; took a while to get going this season but he's been placed at with cheekpieces added in his last two runs (1m, good to firm); usually comes from off the pace and has possibilities if he gets some luck.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo, who won an AW maiden (1m) for George Boughey last autumn but he's been tailed off in two runs for new yard this season; has lots to prove and needs a major revival back in trip.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Ended a long losing run when scoring over 7f here (good to soft) last month but he wasn't in the same form at Epsom last week; has bit to prove again after that and he hasn't had much luck with the draw.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
OwnerName: Mrs Tabor, Mrs Mv Magnier, Mrs Shanahan
Spotlight: Narrow winner of a Beverley maiden before finishing a fair fifth on her handicap debut at Thirsk (1m, good); found things tough in the Sandringham at Royal Ascot last time but she's back in calmer waters now and still has potential after only five starts; not ruled out.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Racecourse Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Front-runner who has won four times since blinkers have been added and latest was over 7f here (good) in May; well held in his last two runs (1m2f/1m) but he's now only 2lb higher than for his last win and is well drawn back at this trip; not ruled out.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won two conditions races at Meydan (7f/6f, good) early this year and he's had tough tasks in Group 1 events in last two starts including the Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot; still lightly raced but he needs to find more progress back up in trip and down in class.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Both wins have been in handicaps but she finished well to go close in a Listed event over C&D (good) two weeks ago; hard to rule out anther good run but this is tougher than last time and she's not easy to predict.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won this race in 2023 and 2025, posting a personal best when narrowly beating Witness Stand in the latter; didn't shine back from a lengthy absence at Haydock in May, but he shaped as though he needed that run and has leading claims if he can get back near his best here; key player in his bid to make it 3-4 at Chester.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won 6f Group 3 on AW last September and fourth in Group 1 Middle Park at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) three weeks later; that rounded off his 2yo campaign but he's been regressive in three runs this season and was down the field in a York handicap last time; has some questions to answer and he's untried at this trip.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won Group 3 John of Gaunt at Haydock (7f, good) in June 2024 and returned to form to land Group 2 Hungerford at Newbury (7f, good to firm) two months later; has had only two runs since then but latest was a creditable second in a Listed event at Haydock last May; now returns after another lengthy absence but he has a good strike-rate and needs a close look for in-form yard.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: Went close behind Holguin in this race last year before winning the Group 2 Lennox Stakes at Goodwood (7f, good); had some struggles last autumn/winter, but he returned after wind surgery with a near-miss in a Group 3 at Epsom (tongue-tie added) last month; that was much more like it and he's a major player if he can back that up.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Likeable 5yo who has a strike-rate of 7-19 and was runner-up behind an impressive winner in a Listed race at Haydock (7f, good) in May; should be in the mix again and his only previous visit to Chester resulted in a C&D win.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Comfortable winner of a C&D handicap (good to firm) last August; better than ever when a close fourth in a Listed event at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in March but he's been well held in both subsequent runs and latest was in a C&D; has bit to prove again and this looks a tough task on the figures.

RaceName: Raymond & Kathleen Corbett Memorial City Plate Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Has won in Bahrain but all eight British wins have come at Chester (5f/6f); his last success was in May but he's gone backwards since and was beaten 14l in the Wokingham last time; now returns to Chester but he's drawn out wide and has something to prove back up in trip.

Started off at 7f but showed no lack of pace when emphatic 6f winner at Ripon in June; the experience of coping with that unconventional track should stand him in good stead round this tight track on handicap debut; can progress.

Impressive when winning 6f maiden at Ffos Las (good to soft) and although his handicap runs came to nothing in the end, a troubled run ended his hopes at Doncaster (6f, good) last September; gelded since; the market should show what's expected on his return.

Has found new reserves of late, gaining back-to-back wins over 6f (good to firm) and 7f (AW) before building up a head of steam to take a late third against the older fillies at Newmarket (6f) recently; might prefer a stiffer test than this sharp 6f but still hard to leave out.

Has acquitted himself well in all four starts, winning over 5f on Ripon debut as 2yo, but most significantly when keeping on well against smart sprinting rivals at York (6f, good) four weeks ago; in the thick of it at this easier level for yard that has won two of the last eight runnings.
Ended 2yo season with soft-ground win over 6f at Pontefract; fared better back over this trip at Hamilton (good) in May but he's another for whom this easy 6f may be on the sharp side.

Had some difficult assignments after 5f Newbury maiden win on debut; well behind on handicap debut at Leicester (6f, good to firm; 28-1) on return in May; needs too much more to make him of strong interest this time.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Seems in form having posted a couple of creditable efforts over fences this year (May/June); close second over C&D when last seen on the Flat ten months ago; capable of taking a serious hand.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Drops back in trip/grade having never landed a blow in the Northumberland Vase last time; broadly consistent otherwise this term and won at Musselburgh (12.5f) in April; 4lb higher now, though.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Has a much better strike-rate on AW than on grass; soundly beaten off his lower turf mark returned to this sphere last time; opposed.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Registered his last win off 1lb higher at Windsor a year ago; consistent since, mostly making the frame; successful in two of his last three attempts in Class 5; solid claims back down in grade.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Well suited by about 1m4f; form over C&D features a success (off current mark) in this race last year and resurgent effort, doing well to finish third having been slowly away then come from a difficult position, in latest start; respected.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Productive on Flat and over hurdles since early last summer; creditable second, one place in front of L'Eagle Aid who admittedly came from a poorer position, over C&D most recently; major contender.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Has a modest strike-rate for current yard but is technically unexposed over today's trip; creditable second at Musselburgh (12.5f) in May in sole attempt beyond 1m2f; could go well off bottom weight.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Finished last of six at Ripon on stable/seasonal debut but is interesting assuming he's back in better form with the outing under his belt; dual winner off higher marks at Chester; check the betting,

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Dual-purpose performer but his last Flat win was in December 2023; some creditable efforts off a falling mark on AW (2m) and turf (1m6f) this season but he was tailed off at Newbury last time and needs to bounce back after that; cheekpieces are removed and visor returns.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: No wins since an odds-on debut success for Karl Burke back in 2023; well handicapped on his old form but he's been well held in his last three runs including over 1m6f at York (good) two weeks ago; down the list.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Second start was a five-runner maiden win at Ripon (1m2f, good) in April but she's been held in two handicaps since; still unexposed but she needs to find more to defy this mark and not sure this new trip will suit on pedigree.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won a small-field maiden last August and he went close in 1m4f handicaps in his final two runs for Ralph Beckett; left that yard for 90,000gns last autumn; struggled on stable debut here (2m, good) in May but he was a fair fourth over the same C&D last month; has possibilities if he can build on that and he's in the mix back in trip.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has a patchy profile but he hasn't been with current yard for long and he won at Bath (1m6f, good) last month; only sixth of seven here (2m)  last time but he ran well for a long way and was beaten less than 4l; still looks on a workable mark and he could make a bold bid back in trip.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin
Spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who has won two handicaps (1m4f/1m6f) on turf this season but his form nosedived at Carlisle 17 days ago; still unexposed at this trip but he comes with risks attached after that heavy defeat.
Resumed winning ways at Pontefract (5f) in June and posted a good second of five over C&D 16 days ago; this C&D winner can go well again nudged up 1lb.

OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at Ayr (5f) last summer; built on earlier promise this season when a very good third of 12 at Newcastle (5f) 15 days ago; big shout off a 2lb lower mark here.
Gained a second win of 2026 at Carlisle (5f) in May but he's been well below par over C&D and at Newcastle since; needs to take a big step forward.
Ended a losing run over 6f here in June and not disgraced when a staying-on third of five over C&D last time; not ruled out if getting a strong gallop to aim at.
It's now 13 starts since he scored over C&D last July and he's largely been below par this season, seventh of ten at Musselburgh (5f) 11 days ago; others are preferred.
Scored five times (including over C&D) in 2025; in very good nick lately too, second for the third consecutive start in 6f handicap here last month; a likely player once more off an unchanged mark.
Ex-Irish maiden who has yet to make the frame in five runs for her current yard, a never-dangerous sixth of ten at Musselburgh (5f) 19 days ago; not easy to make a case for.
A dual 5f scorer in 2025; right back on track of late when placed in handicaps (around 5f) at Wetherby and Ayr so she ought to be thereabouts nudged up 1lb.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: 9-1, made a promising start with second of six in 5f novice at Newcastle two weeks ago, pulling clear of rest; this son of Naval Crown seems sure to build on it; big player.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: Cotai Glory colt who shaped well when a debut second over 6f here but failed to build on it with sixth of 11 in 6f York novice two weeks ago, fading late; remains with potential now dropping to 5f.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: 12-1 and very green when last of six in novice at Pontefract (6f, good) on his debut 20 days ago; lots more is needed from this son of Minzaal.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: Blue Point filly who has progressed with each of her three runs, posting a good third of eight in 5f Catterick maiden 19 days ago; she may do better still so is not out of things.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: 14-1, hinted at promise when a debut seventh of ten in maiden at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) in April; since gelded and likely to take a step forward.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: 18-1 and green when a debut fourth of five in maiden here (6f, good) last month; this No Nay Never colt can improve a fair bit for the initial experience.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Bagged a remarkable 11th C&D success in June; only sixth of eight in 7f Musselburgh handicap 11 days ago but no surprise to see her bounce back here.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a first win for his current yard at Ayr (7f) in May and has continued in good nick, fourth of 11 back there five days ago; drops back in trip here but he's well in the mix again.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Ended a long losing run when cosily making all in five-runner handicap at Nottingham (6f) three weeks ago; up 2lb but not taken lightly in his current mood.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a breakthrough success on his yard debut (formerly with Ivan Furtado) in 7.5f handicap at Chester last month; needs to back it up dropped to 6f here off a 4lb higher mark.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: A fair 5.5f winner as a 2yo but she offered little in three runs last summer; needs to take a major step forward after 11 months off the track.
Scored at Ayr (1m2f) last April in a light campaign in 2025; back from 12 months off with an encouraging Ripon fifth but beat only one at Chester (1m4f) since; needs to bounce back.

A fairly useful 1m4f winner for Ralph Beckett in 2025; has steadily found his form for his current yard this season, posting a good third of eight in 1m2f handicap at Doncaster eight days ago; firmly in the picture stepping back up in trip.

A dual winner (at up to 1m2f) last season; recorded a very good second over C&D in June and didn't enjoy a smooth run when fifth of 12 at Newcastle (1m4f) two weeks ago; remains one to be interested in.
Gained a second success of the season on her first go over 1m4f in nine-runner York handicap four weeks ago; up 2lb but she must enter calculations if her stamina holds out over this longer trip.
A four-time winner (at up to 1m5f) in 2025; easily best run this season when a clear second of six in 1m4f handicap at Ripon 24 days ago; a likely player despite taking a 2lb rise here.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: Shaped well when runner-up in 1m novices at Nottingham on his first two runs but failed to land the odds when third of seven in 1m Leicester maiden four weeks ago; since gelded and this son of Lucky Vega remains with potential on his handicap debut.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: A fair 7f winner for John and Thady Gosden in 2025; back on song for his current yard with a clear second of seven in 1m Pontefract handicap 12 days ago; a likely player nudged up 2lb.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: Fair ex-German 1m1f winner; got off the mark for his current yard over C&D in May but beat only one at Ayr (1m2f) on soft ground since; no surprise if he bounced back here.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: Ended a long losing sequence (on his yard debut) over C&D in May but comes here on the back of a below-form sixth at Newcastle (1m) last month; needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up on his first three starts in 1m AW maidens but he's failed to kick on sent handicapping, only 12th at Kempton (1m) 12 days ago; has a bit to prove.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Saxon Warrior filly who took a step forward for new yard (formerly with George Boughey) when a neck second of seven in 8.5f handicap at Beverley eight days ago; a player nudged up 1lb.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Enjoyed a remarkable 2025 when a six-time winner, including over C&D; below par this season until third of seven in 1m1f handicap at Musselburgh 11 days ago but very much one to consider off a reduced mark now.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Modest maiden who wasn't discredited in first-time cheekpieces when fifth of nine in 1m Carlisle handicap last month; needs to build on it if she's to gain a first win at the seventh attempt.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
