Well beaten in Bahrain in February when last seen and a wide draw could prove tough for this front-runner; however, he won two C&D handicaps last summer (good/good to firm) and was back on the scoresheet in Bahrain in January; unbeaten at York and could make a bold bid at a big price.

Lightly raced 4yo who was an eye-catching third from the rear behind Warrant Holder over C&D (good) in May on his stable/handicap debut, following a year off, and four runs later he got off the mark at Ayr (1m2f, good) last Sunday when making most of the running; back up in grade under a 5lb penalty but he may well have more left in the tank.

Some good C&D form, beaten just a neck in a big field last August (good to firm) and fourth of 16 two starts ago (good); he has an each-way squeak if he's on song but that wasn't the case at Newcastle last time (slowly away) and he's won just one of his last 22 starts.

Easy win at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) in May before solid third of 16 over this C&D (good), and he improved again to win at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm) last Saturday; today's assignment is tougher still but this thriving 5yo could be in the each-way mix under a 5lb penalty.

Two wins on soft going last season and he's been placed in big-field handicaps at Cork (10.4f, good to yielding) and the Curragh (1m2f, good) on his last two starts; each-way shout if fast ground isn't an issue.

Beaten a nose in a 3m1f hurdle race at Carlisle in April 2025 and the same margin over 1m6f at Goodwood (good; unraced on firmer) this May on his return to Flat turf; second again over Goodwood's 1m6f four weeks ago, this time on soft, and both runs there suggested that a return to 2m should be okay.

Won handicap debut for Ed Walker off 4lb higher last September (extended 1m3f, heavy); got closer to that level in on his last two runs for his new connections and seemed to stay 2m at Chester last time, but he was only fifth of seven in that race and others have more pressing claims.

Probably best known for Musselburgh exploits but last two wins were at Nottingham last October and Carlisle last month; below form on sole start here (last October) but probably best forgiven being well beaten 17 days ago as that was just 1m3f; chief concern is that he needs another career best.

Five-runner race at Carlisle (1m3f) last Saturday was first win since 2023 and she's a dependable sort who finished a C&D second (twice) and fourth in July 2024 and 2025 on her only three appearances at York; going back up 5lb since Carlisle seems to pose the difficulty but she should be on the premises.

Four months off before winning a six-runner race at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) early last month; disappointing in a maiden hurdle (fourth at 10-11) 12 days later but remember also that he raised his game markedly when upped to 1m6f on Flat last autumn; this first 2m Flat assignment is interesting.

4yo who's relatively lightly raced and has found his niche in 2m races this season, winning at Ripon (good; made all) in April and Catterick (good to firm) last month; didn't show it in the Northumberland Vase at Newcastle two weeks ago but that was on AW and after just a five-day break; 3lb lower today and still has more to offer with stamina tests.

Sole run on good to firm was last July when tailed off in a novice race; staying-on third here (1m4f) this June and he knuckled down well to score over 2m2f at Pontefract (also on good) eight days later; that win was the first time he'd worn this headgear combination and his first go at a staying trip on the Flat; up 4lb but probably has more to offer and he's a big player if he goes on the ground.

Won this race last year (good to firm) in good style and has a 1lb lower mark today; 2026 build-up has been far less auspicious than last season's, however, and the first-time visor of latest start is now replaced with a first-time tongue-tie.

Three wins last summer included two over C&D; started this season in good form but well beaten on last two outings when back to York for a Group 3 and, more worryingly, a 1m6f handicap; off 2lb higher than her last winning mark, she'll have a shout if returning to her best.
5yo who is an improved performer this season and registered career-best form when a close third in C&D Listed race last time, a neck behind Tabletalk; should be bang there once again.

Won the Melrose Handicap over C&D in August 2024; 0-9 since but he was runner-up in five of those races, including when beaten a neck in C&D Listed event four weeks ago, narrowly in front of Mount Atlas; the lack of a recent win is a touch frustrating but he's clearly a leading contender.

Progressed very well last term, culminating in his close third in Group 2 Great Voltigeur here (1m4f, good to firm) in August, until not so good trying 1m6f on final outing; rallied when close second in 1m4f French Listed event on reappearance but needs to bounce back from a tame show in Newbury Group 3; tongue tied first time on this second attempt at 1m6f.

Won at Hexham last August in the first of her three bumpers but a Grade 2 at Aintree (125-1) proved far too tough in April; will build at some point on the modest form shown in a 9.4f maiden on her recent first venture under Flat rules, but she's impossible to fancy today.

7yo who is 0-12 since winning at Meydan in January 2025; has run really well in plenty of Group races in the interim, including the last two runnings of the Yorkshire Cup over C&D, but he ran a stinker in C&D Listed event four weeks ago; bang there if he bounces straight back.

OwnerName: A Cope, B Roberts And J Biggane
Spotlight: Eight AW wins, two last winter in 2m handicaps; however, he failed to beat a rival in Group 3 at Sandown (2m, good) on turf return, has tons to prove on turf record (0-5) and even best AW form does not look good enough.
7f winner at Redcar last November; two solid efforts (6f/7f, good ground) last month but he will need more if he is to defy this mark.

Looked as good as ever when second of nine at Catterick (6f, good) on his reappearance in April but that hasn't been a springboard to better things; conditions no problem and he's on a manageable mark but this is a warm race in which to try to bounce back.
Ended 2025 in good form on AW; some solid efforts back on turf this year without suggesting he had much in hand of his mark; minor money perhaps his best hope.
Ran his best race for new connections when third of 21 in a 0-85 over C&D four weeks ago; mark left unchanged but he does need to prove himself on ground firmer than good.
Thrown in on last season's best; yet to get going for David O'Meara this time around but he has dropped 10lb for his troubles and the addition of a visor could help; stable also runs Yes I'm Mali.
The handicapper has been in command since his runaway win at Leicester (6f, good) in April; today's return to sprinting is in his favour but he's likely to be vulnerable at this level.

Rejuvenated with James Owen, reeling off an AW hat-trick in January and making it four on the bounce when scoring at Catterick (6f, good) in May; only just missed out at Doncaster last month and it was 5f when beaten favourite at Sandown last time; now 3lb higher but she did look promising early in her career and Mason Paetel's claim helps too.
No problem with fast turf but he has a much better strike-rate on AW; midfield finish at Doncaster last month didn't suggest he is the answer here.

Hold-up sprinter who ended a near two-year losing run at Pontefract (6f, good) last month; better form when second there three weeks ago; has plenty of C&D form in big fields and he has each-way claims.

Not the force of old but that is reflected in his handicap mark; has undergone wind surgery since his latest run but it isn't enough to earn him the vote in this competitive race; usual headgear is absent today.
Both wins have come on good to soft ground but he does have one good run on good to firm to his name; disappointed at Windsor last month but this is an ease in grade.
He has been threatening to add to his six wins, with a third at Pontefract and second at Thirsk on his last two starts; on the downside he has been unplaced in all seven visits to York.

String of good runs since returning in May from a 167-day absence, latterly keeping on for second at Redcar (5f, good) three weeks ago; steps back up to 6f for the first time this year and it could suit.
Won two 6f events (good/good to soft) for Michael Dods as a 2yo; drew a blank in 2025; makes stable debut in a competitive race after 259 days off; likely best watched.
Arrives on the crest of a wave having won four of her last five starts; a 4lb rise for her small-field Class 5 win at Ripon last month could just find her out here, though.
Good run of form ground to a halt at Thirsk ten days ago when he wasn't on his best behaviour in the stalls; has the ability to feature off this mark but is not without risk.

OwnerName: S Thompson, Mrs R Carr & G Shields
Spotlight: Picking up his share of prize money this season, making the frame five times either side of his Pontefract (6f, good) win last month; despite that he is still 5lb lower than when a close fourth in this race 12 months ago; each-way shout.
Won this race in 2022 as a 5yo; no sign of his enthusiasm waning this year, winning three 6f handicaps, and stepping back up in  trip will suit after two good 5f efforts last month; chance.

OwnerName: Alfa Site Services & Alan Gove Racing
Spotlight: Made a winning handicap debut (6f, AW) in March and a perfect start to her turf career when making all at Pontefract (6f, good) in June; Doncaster third on latest outing was another good run and this track should prove more to her liking; still capable of better.

OwnerName: R A Holburt, B R Holburt Et Al
Spotlight: Strong win from just off the leaders this course at this journey. Unbeaten at track and distance. Can challenge again.
Resuming (32-weeks). Has a good first up record. Recent Cranbourne jump-out winner. Right in the mix fresh.

OwnerName: D L Hewitt & Ms J C Preston
Spotlight: Looked likely but had a real fight on his hands and just missed at Morphettville Parks. Tougher here but draws to do no work. Each way hope.

OwnerName: D J Oliphant, P J Salter Et Al
Spotlight: First-up (35-weeks). In the minors at Moonee Valley first-up last campaign while building fitness through 2 trials and a jump-out win. Should mean business first-up.

OwnerName: A J Hehir, D W Rasheed Et Al
Spotlight: Let-up (63-days). Kept finding the line without threatening in the Goodwood on a soft deck at Morphettville. At optimum distance. Looks a key contender.

OwnerName: N S Psaltis, Mrs G C Psaltis Et Al
Spotlight: Just missed in a crackerjack finish in the Open Hcp at Flemington and over this trip when drifting in the betting. Previous winner in wet here. Capable of making an impact.

OwnerName: G N Peters, J Baker Et Al
Spotlight: Ears scrubbed off early to cross and lead but kept on bravely in rain affected going for the minors at this trip. Can play a role.

TrainerName: Mick Price & Michael Kent Jnr right
OwnerName: Werrett Bloodstock Pty Ltd, S Tsalikidis Et Al
Spotlight: Placed Bel Esprit Stakes at Caulfield 30 May. Battled home okay at Randwick. Both efforts here stack up. Worth a look.

OwnerName: N Morgan, J F Naffine & Bula Bula
Spotlight: Resuming (23-weeks). Nabbed on the wire in Benchmark 100 company at Caulfield first-up last campaign while cruising through a winning trial at Murray Bridge Gh. One for multiples fresh in this company.
Let-up (42-days). Had all before him at turn and went home tamely this track at this distance. Others more persuasive.

OwnerName: Best Bloodstock Syndicate, Broady Cats Syndicate Et Al
Spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Made running, no answer when challenged but held on for second in the (Bm84) at Sandown-Hillside. Strips fitter second run back. Place claims.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Career wins-runs record of 7-20 and he was in the money in a stakes last August; close up second at Churchill Downs when dropped to 1m 1/2f 51 days ago in an allowance optional claimer; leading contender; absence is no concern as has previously gone well off a break and he can take this step up in class in his stride.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Steadily progressive sort who has taken well to Pattern company in best effort when second to prolific scorer Rebel's Romance in the Group 2 City Of Gold (1m4f) at Meydan in February having previously won the Group 3 Dubail Millennium (1m2f); not disgraced when sixth to Ombudsmen in Group 1 Dubai Turf (1m2f); handlwes quick ground and a major player here.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Not beaten far in an allowance Grade 3 at Churchill Downs six weeks ago; has been consistent of late and has made an impressive start to his career with three wins from six starts; up in class but ought to be on the premises.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
OwnerName: Stonelea Stable LLC & Bonnie Rye Stable
Spotlight: Admirable career strike-rate (four from 19 to date); recent efforts have generally been solid; close up behind Soleil Volant when fourth 28 days ago beaten just over 4l at Delaware Park in an allowance listed; faces a tougher assignment this time so he can be taken on.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Finished fourth, beaten a fair way at Belmont At The Big A upped to 1m1 1/2f in May in an allowance optional claimer; useful at this level; possesses winning form at this track; tackling an extra 1 1/2f now.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
OwnerName: Jacques Dupuis Jr, Luke Bourque & Bobby Sutton
Spotlight: Successful twice in his last five starts; displayed a fair level of form when 3 1/2l runner-up in an allowance listed at Delaware Park four weeks ago; more on his plate but not dismissed.

OwnerName: Michael Scaife/Liam Ferguson/Simon Murphy/H S Khunkhuna
Spotlight: Won four for Clive Cox, including the Group 2 Temple Stakes at Haydock in 2024, his last victory; yet to hit those heights for this yard and has been soundly beaten on his last three starts; 0-2 on the AW but did run well here behind Valiant Force in February; blinkers replace cheekpieces worn last time.

Speedy filly has an excellent strike rate over C&D (4-6) including this race last year off 2lb lower; went close behind Howd'yadoit at Navan in May but has failed to fire in three runs since; return to this track offers hope of a revival and cheekpieces are added; bold bid likely from plum draw.

OwnerName: Exors of the Late Mrs S Power
Spotlight: Six-time course winner, including twice over C&D, but his last four wins here have come over 6f; however, he did go close off this mark over C&D in May and should get a strong pace to aim at; not out of this.

Won over C&D in October off 13lb lower; poor on first two starts this season but roared back to form to make all at Bellewstown 10 days ago (5f, good); career-high mark now but this 3yo could have more to offer and top rider booked; player.

Won his maiden over C&D last December and overall form figures here read 2313; not as consistent on turf this season; ran well at Cork in June but down the field on latest at the Curragh; another one that could potentially improve now returned to the AW, but he's 4lb wrong here.

Back on song when just failing to reel in the winner at Navan on latest; four C&D wins to his name, including a Group 3, and is 5lb better off for a 1.75l defeat behind Valiant Force in a rated race here in February; third in this last year and should go well again.

OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: Struggling on turf this season but won his last start over C&D in February and, overall, he has won five times at this venue over a variety of trips; second behind Eclairage in this last season; a return to form would come as no surprise but David Egan prefers Ipanema Queen.

3yo landed the third win of his career when beating Eclairage by a head at Navan in May (5f, yielding); has been beaten in conditions races twice since and is 7lb higher on return to handicap company; sole previous AW run wasn't his best, but his yard is red-hot so can't discount.

OwnerName: Gary Devlin & Kevin Osborne & N W T Dalzell & Tracey Wylie
Spotlight: C&D winner; finished off last year in fine form at this venue, backing up her C&D handicap win with a short-head second in a conditions race over C&D when last seen in December; is 4lb wrong here, however, and lacks a recent run so is probably best watched unless the market speaks.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
OwnerName: David Daly & Gerard Shevlin & D Battersby
Spotlight: Twelve-race maiden ran one of her better races when runner-up over 6f here in April off 56; soundly beaten on turf in four runs since, but now 4lb lower than she was for that April run and not dismissed back at this venue.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden was a close third in a fillies' maiden over C&D in December on final start for Sheila Lavery; mixed bag on turf in three starts for this stable; ran well at Gowran on penultimate outing but never figured in visor (retained) at Fairyhouse on latest.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: 0-7 overall and 0-4 on AW; ran okay when a 2.75l third over 1m here in December but two runs this season have not been her best; tongue-tie now added to usual hood; more needed and is 5lb wrong.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: 6f Windsor novice winner for former yard; three turf runs for this yard have all been at that trip and yet to get closer than 4l to the winner; mark sliding but unproven on this surface, and others make more appeal.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: Won on handicap debut here over 6f off 1lb higher, but that is starting to look like a flash in the pan; poor form otherwise and soundly beaten all three starts this season; others appeal more.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: Won fillies' maiden over C&D in December; back to form with a 1l third of seven at Down Royal on latest (5f, soft); return to 7f and this surface are positives; player.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: Pretty modest form in maidens and no sign of progress in first two handicaps; never figured on seasonal return at the Curragh and now makes AW debut; not easy to fancy.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: Has held form well since sent handicapping; won off 6lb lower over 6f here in December; beaten 0.5l off this mark over 1m here in March; solid efforts over 7f and 8.5f at Fairyhouse the last twice; place claims at the very least.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
OwnerName: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell
Spotlight: Best effort came on debut over C&D in January; five starts since (four on turf) have not been at the same level and never involved when 33-1 on handicap debut at Gowran last month; blinkers tried that day are left off here.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner in February off 8lb lower; beaten less than 2l in a big field at the Curragh on latest (6f, good), staying on well, and eased 1lb for that effort; return to 7f on the AW could enable him to get his head back in front; leading claims.

RaceName: RACING TV FREE ONE MONTH TRIAL Handicap
Spotlight: Didn't achieve much in three turf maidens at 6f-7f on varying ground; could do better now tackling a handicap, and worth checking in the market.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Never worse than third in six starts, over this trip last twice; tongue-tie returns for AW debut and plenty of horses by Mehmas win on synthetic surfaces.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Placed four times from six starts on turf, including when nailed on the line at Bellewstown having tried to make all; reckon he'll be in the front rank again with cheekpieces now fitted, after another solid run at the Curragh; sire was classy turf horse in the US, yet to sire AW winner from limited runners; dam did win on AW.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Profitable gelding with an interesting pedigree; closely related to a Godolphin Group 1-winning 2yo and half-brother to Heavy Metal, a prolific winner on the dirt at Meydan; 16s, got tired late on when fifth over 1m at The Curragh, 5l off third-placed Karrygrant.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Mark of 78 after three C&D runs, but remains to be seen if he merits that rating because it's winter form; hung right the first day and missed the break in a hood last time, so he might have a few quirks, but right man on board to sort him out.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Well-bred son of Palace Pier; 18s for Curragh debut over extended 6f, trailed home in rear after a slow start.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Phoenix Of Spain filly cost E13,000 as a yearling and sports a tongue-tie for debut; related to three prolific and useful winners, two of them on AW.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Boiled over, bolted to post and unseated rider before Curragh debut, where he ran keen and dropped out to finish in rear (Karrygrant third); hood now on.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Didn't make it to the breeze-ups after costing 40,000gns as a yearling; half-brother to 7f Group 3 winner Breathtaking Look (also won on AW) and to two other winners; dam Listed-placed 1m-9.4f AW winner; not hard to see him running well.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Starman filly had three turf runs over 5f last year, modest enough form; dam was placed on AW in France; 10 months off and McAteer on stablemate.

RaceName: Anglo Printers - Award Winning Print And Packaging Maiden
Spotlight: Homebred son of The Gurkha; half-brother to 8.6f/1m4f winner Space Kid (inc AW/Belgium); unraced dam out of Group-placed 1m1f winning half-sister to 1m1f/1m2f Listed winner Livadiya; trainer doesn't have debut winners.
Winner over 6f here and twice placed over this trip; been off 14 months and new stable (same owners)
Hold-up performer by necessity, really, as he can be tardy from the gates; C&D winner and just failed to get up here in April; only 1lb higher now and if they go hard, it will be a big help.
Defied a similarly-wide draw when winning over C&D in March; only 1lb higher now and little wrong with her turf form since.
Lightly raced and back from over a year off with creditable mid-field run over this trip at Leoprdstown, keeping on steadily from off the pace; first start on AW.
Well beaten in two turf handicaps over 6f; first run here and dam is related to AW winners; visor on and stamina to prove.

Went close from a wide draw over C&D last August; should come on from fair seasonal debut over 1m1f at Leopardstown where she was disputing the lead a furlong down; this is only her fifth start in handicaps.

OwnerName: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney
Spotlight: Won a division of this last year off a mark of 60, but has been out of form on turf this season and only mid-field when favourite over C&D in April on sole AW start in 2026.

OwnerName: Charles Kerwin & Brendan Kerwin & Liam Kehoe
Spotlight: C&D winner in the winter and has added two wins on turf; likes a bit of space, so draw is okay, but she might be high enough in the handicap now.

OwnerName: P E Ryan & J G Shanahan
Spotlight: Has winning and placed form over C&D, but has a habit of starting slowly which has been evident on turf lately; placed on last two visits here earlier this year.
Maiden was second over a longer trip on soft last year; slowly away before late headway behind Micasso at Bellewstown latest; unplaced in four runs here, albeit three of them were non-handicaps.

Second over 1m4f here in November, but that's her only time to place in 10 starts here and 1m likely too sharp; another headgear combo tried, but even the champion jockey will struggle to get this one involved
One good run last year and popped up at 22-1 on return at Bellewstown; burst here on a previous visit and making a quick return to action, so the 'bounce factor' sirens are sounding.
Maiden not beaten far a few times here earlier in the year, over trips between 6f-1m; likely to find a few too strong but minor place claims.
Four wins here, three of them over this trip, and may step up on recent turf efforts which were probably with a view to getting him fit for AW action.
Two wins here in the spring, including over this trip, and last couple of turf runs have been fine, 2m looking too far last time.
Back-to-back C&D wins under this rider in the spring and really good third in higher grade at the Curragh last time; lots to like.
Been racing prominently before weakening in her races over longer trips; cheekpieces added for first outing here and half-sister won on AW.
Twice placed over 10.5f here in November; off the mark over 1m1f at Ballinrobe and fair runs since; hasn't gone this far before but is by a St Leger winner.
Fair fifth over C&D in May on sole AW run; did nothing in a hurry at Leopardstown (1m1f) on Thursday, but stuck to his task and not beaten far; big horse has one in him, but maybe not quite yet.
AW winner at two in Britain, but has never raced here; won a claiming hurdle two runs back, but trip looks too sharp as well as the query about him on Polytrack.

Nice run here over 10.5f for previous yard; having been gelded, encouraging fourth (posted wide) over same trip at Down Royal on stable debut (strong form); half-sister won three handicaps here, two of them over this trip, and Elzaams have a great record here.
Forget his two recent turf runs as they were probably preps for this; useful record here with two wins over 10.5f and some good runs over this trip.
Sole win came at Sligo last summer; placed loads of times here, including over this trip in May, but burst only five days ago which makes him too risky.
Dual turf winner and thrice runner-up here, including in last year's renewal; form tailed off later last year, but better for his return to action.
Famous colours and he won 10.5f maiden here in February, but that's weak form and he was lapped on handicap debut at Down Royal; steps up in trip and bred for it.
Both starts have come here; soundly beaten on debut over 1m in April; didn't quite get home upped to 1m4f on latest when beaten 9l into third; new headgear goes on and he drops in trip; more needed.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Progressive without winning in three maidens on turf; best effort yet over 1m2f (yielding) on latest at Navan 57 days ago; has since been gelded and cheekpieces now go on for AW debut; rated 85 and the one to beat.

Sent off at 22-1 here on debut (1m) in December and found just one too good, beaten a neck into second; off since, which is slightly concerning, but pedigree suggests that this longer trip might suit and should run well again if ready after his break.

Almanzor gelding was a 15,000gns foal; half-brother to 7f/1m winner Beagle Bay (inc AW; RPR 101); dam Listed-placed 6f-1m winner (inc 2yo/AW; 103), half-sister to 5f 2yo winner Limetree Lady; a bit of appeal on paper and worth a market check.
Heavy defeats all four starts, including twice here over 7f; no improvement for step up in trip at Ballinrobe latest; hard to make a case for.
Modest form in bumpers and maiden hurdles; makes Flat and AW debut; best watched.

RaceName: Family Funday At Dundalk Saturday 15th August Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Promise in all three maiden runs at 1m2f-1m5f; the latter trip on soft ground probably stretched her on latest at Down Royal; form of previous second at Roscommon (1m4f, good) looks solid in the context of this race; rated 74 and big chance if taking to this surface (half-sister won on AW).

RaceName: Family Funday At Dundalk Saturday 15th August Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Standout effort a head second at Gowran on third start last October (9.5f, heavy); has failed to build on that and soundly beaten in handicaps on last three starts; rated 65 and a fair bit to find on AW debut.

RaceName: Family Funday At Dundalk Saturday 15th August Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: All four turf runs have come on soft and heavy ground at 1m3f-1m4f; beaten favourite when second at Killarney in October and at the Curragh in November; well below best on seasonal return at Bellewstown in April and off since; claims on best form if handling the AW.

RaceName: Family Funday At Dundalk Saturday 15th August Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Showed enough to suggest she could win a race when trained in Britain; below best when last seen at Pontefract in September; has joined yard that can ready one and has run well on the AW before, so monitor the market.

RaceName: Family Funday At Dundalk Saturday 15th August Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Heavy defeats at huge odds in maidens at Down Royal and Bellewstown; seems safe to rule out.

RaceName: Family Funday At Dundalk Saturday 15th August Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Very modest form so far in turf maidens; beaten 29l on latest; can only be watched in first-time cheekpieces on AW debut.

RaceName: Book Your Christmas Party Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Making headway when unseating at Downpatrick (2m3f) and then made no mistake at the expense of a subsequent winner at Clonmel (2m, good); sets the standard on RPRs and Rian Corcoran's claim takes away 5lb of his penalty.

RaceName: Book Your Christmas Party Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50-1 when scraping home in a modest 2m4f maiden at Hexham and she has more on her plate here, especially with a penalty.

RaceName: Book Your Christmas Party Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looks limited and was 125-1 when tailed off behind Dolly Dior at Hexham.

RaceName: Book Your Christmas Party Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in good-ground bumpers in France and at Southwell (good; tongue tied); should make a hurdler but this is no easy starter.

RaceName: Book Your Christmas Party Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Worcester winner who was found wanting under a penalty at Uttoxeter (2m, good), though that wasn't too bad a race and the second has won since; should also improve for this longer trip with his pointing background.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: All four wins have been at Hexham and it was never in much doubt there three weeks ago (1m7f, good); hard to grumble with the 8lb rise but now has to continue his good work on a very different track, and he was a lot lower in the weights when second to Garde Des Champs in this 12 months ago.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Better at this trip than the 2m4f over which he was third here ten days ago, but even when on a good day he's become extremely hard to win with (1-24 as a chaser and 2-39 overall); the clear plus is that he won this last year off 5lb higher.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Free-goer who often makes the running and ultimately paid for giving chase to an improving winner when behind Garde Des Champs here a month ago; ran better behind the same winner the time before when he finished ahead of the Russell/Scudamore runner; a soft lead would help matters.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won all sorts in France, following Flat wins on turf and AW with two wins over fences and four as a hurdler (the latest in May on good ground over 2m2f); might not be on too bad a mark for this British debut; has won from the front and when held up.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won two handicap hurdles for Stuart Crawford but he's not looking as good over fences and was behind Garde Des Champs here last time.

RaceName: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sole win came in a three-runner maiden hurdle; this mark should be manageable on the balance of his hurdle form but a forgettable chase debut in January needs forgiving; absent since.

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won her maiden over C&D on good ground last May (four ran); hasn't looked on that good a mark since sent into handicaps and has been ridden by Jack Power in all bar one of those; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: All good in three runs for Olly Murphy, and after winning over C&D (good), she wasn't far from defying a 5lb rise at Aintree; just 2lb higher this time and looks sure to put up another bold show.

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing
Spotlight: C&D winner who hasn't found her form in three starts following a break and there are stamina concerns to overcome at this trip.

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hasn't had that much racing for a 10yo; enjoys this ground and has a solid record over C&D, so one to consider as he ran quite well last time at Bangor in a higher grade (finished last but weakened only late on).

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Should be well handicapped but has been below par for a good while now and more new headgear is tried after flirting with a tongue-tie last time; capable but risky.

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Former smart Flat horse who is 0-6 over hurdles and was beaten a little under 10l on handicap debut here a month ago; he was boxing on over 2m and this longer trip could bring him on (stayed well on the Flat).

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner who is finding her feet after a long absence and she's an assured stayer over this far; looks handicapped about right but has each-way claims.

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Struggled with consistency towards the end of his time in Ireland, and failed to stay 3m6f on April's stable debut; more in his favour here, but others more persuasive.

RaceName: Saints In The Community Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Regressed in Ireland, including in two handicaps, after finishing runner-up in a maiden hurdle; could have needed the run when beaten 9l in a 2m novice at Kelso (good) in May but it's hard to make a compelling case.

RaceName: Sport, Education & Wellbeing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Had long career in France before four British runs this season; won by 17l at Uttoxeter in May and has posted another two good efforts since, more recently when battling on for fourth in valuable contest back at Uttoxeter (also 3m, good) a fortnight ago; respected.

RaceName: Sport, Education & Wellbeing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won three-runner contest at Kelso (2m7f, good) in May and is still on a workable mark but never really got competitive in last month's Perth Gold Cup and needs to put that run behind him.

RaceName: Sport, Education & Wellbeing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Exploited much-reduced mark over C&D (good) in April and finished second over C&D on both subsequent appearances (good to soft/good) but is no longer the force of old and others in this field have more appealing profiles.

RaceName: Sport, Education & Wellbeing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has modest 2-30 strike-rate under rules but kept on well for second at Aintree (2m4f, good) in May and has now filled that position twice since his wind operation in March; could plausibly still have untapped potential over 3m; no surprise if he features.

RaceName: Sport, Education & Wellbeing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Miss D Sellars & Exors Of Mrs Ic Sellars
Spotlight: Gained both chase wins over 2m4f (one here); no match for progressive rival when second at Hexham (1m7f, good; first run after wind surgery) last month but that was still a good effort; a possible if suited by this new trip.

RaceName: Sport, Education & Wellbeing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Gained all seven chase wins at the track, the latest a dead-heat over this trip in April (good ground); on fairly tough mark now but last month's Perth Gold Cup fourth was respectable and he probably won't be far away.

RaceName: Sport, Education & Wellbeing Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Abbadis Racing & D A Harrison Racing
Spotlight: Four-time C&D winner; returned from break with two low-key runs this spring (hurdle/chase) but may have been aided by refitted cheekpieces when running big race in defeat here (2m4f, good) ten days ago; up in grade today but still on a good mark and this return to 3m is sure to suit.

RaceName: Saints Kidz Community Football Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Thp Racing & D A Harrison Racing
Spotlight: Course regular who looked as good as ever when keeping on strongly from off the pace for clearcut C&D (good) win ten days ago; 6lb higher in a stronger race today but can make his presence felt again.

RaceName: Saints Kidz Community Football Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Six-time hurdle winner who was placed off today's mark on his last four appearances, most recently when second at Uttoxeter (2m7f, good) last month; seems very likely to be on the premises again.

RaceName: Saints Kidz Community Football Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Disappointed at Huntingdon (a course he likes) in May, after a break, and didn't find much at business end when last of four finishers at Worcester (2m7f, good) last month; on a good mark but arrives here with questions to answer.

RaceName: Saints Kidz Community Football Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Made all in 2m4f maiden here (good) in May, after a break, and also ran well when second on handicap return at Cartmel (2m6f, good) a fortnight later; upped further in trip today and remains lightly raced for her age; could still have more to offer; highly respected.

RaceName: Saints Kidz Community Football Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 2-4 for new stable this year, with both wins gained from the front here (3m/3m2f); below form at Hexham last month but sure to be suited by the return to this track and could take some catching; cheekpieces refitted.

RaceName: Saints Kidz Community Football Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two hurdles wins last season, the latter when upped to 2m7f at Bangor (good) in April; still unexposed over this sort of trip but was tailed off when dropped back to 2m4f last month; hard to predict.

RaceName: Saints Kidz Community Football Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner last autumn (good/soft); ended last season with some disappointing efforts, though, and looked badly out of sorts on both Flat appearances this spring; leap of faith required to support her today.

RaceName: Scone Palace International Horse Trials Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has slipped to a tempting mark but needs to up his game after some below-par recent efforts; gained all four wins at about 2m4f and is probably better suited by ground slower than good; others preferred.

RaceName: Scone Palace International Horse Trials Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Fair Flat racer who kept on from the rear for second of 14 over C&D (good) on handicap debut in April; didn't really build on that run when fifth of 12 here last month but still considered; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: Scone Palace International Horse Trials Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: In the frame all six hurdling starts, most recently when clear second of four on last month's handicap debut at Bangor (2m, good); competes off unchanged mark here and features prominently in calculations.

RaceName: Scone Palace International Horse Trials Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: T Hunter & D A Harrison Racing
Spotlight: Veteran who has gained four of his ten hurdle wins at this track and often builds up a big lead; needs to rebound from a disappointing effort here (2m4f) ten days ago but was in a consistent vein of form beforehand and is still on a workable mark.

RaceName: Scone Palace International Horse Trials Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Safely held when sixth over C&D (good) on both recent outings and has now been unplaced all six hurdling starts; not written off just yet but clearly needs to take a step forward.

RaceName: Scone Palace International Horse Trials Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed on bumper debut in 2024 for Fergal O'Brien; struggled in four novice/maiden hurdles for Max Young last year but makes stable/handicap debut off a lowly mark and it will be very interesting to see what Ben Pauling can do with him.

RaceName: Scone Palace International Horse Trials Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Resurgent 10yo who led close home over C&D (good) last month and has now won three of his last four; probably doesn't have much margin for error after latest 5lb rise but must be considered in current mood.

French bumper winner has run some fine races in defeat over hurdles; has been below form on his last two outings but was badly hampered at Roscommon six days ago; a major player if bouncing back, even penalised 8lb.

Flat winner has run well in defeat several times over hurdles, though has run out a couple of times too, including at Clonmel two starts ago; decent run at Bellewstown since and is a player even penalised 8lb.,
Some promise in Flat maidens last year but has been disappointing in handicaps this term; tongue-tie goes on for hurdles debut; best watched.
Modest Flat maiden ran his best race over hurdles when fourth in a Bellewstown novice last time; has a chance if backing up that run.

First foal; dam unraced sister to 2m4f hurdle/chase winner Yabo, out of 2m chase-winning half-sister to useful bumper and 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Valley Ride; by a young sire and from a decent damline; best watched on debut.
Beaten 18l in a Clonmel maiden hurdle that fell apart early on; can do better, but others preferred today.
Flat winner was pulled-up on hurdles debut in testing ground last November, but a much better effort in a Downpatrick novice last time; penalised 8lb and still needs a fair bit more.
Dual Flat winner over approximately 1m2f but has been hit and miss this season; penalised 8lb on hurdles debut, so will have to put up a big effort to defy that.
Third runner for yard; was well beaten in a Ballinrobe bumper on debut and will have to make enormous improvement.

Eleventh foal; half-sister to 2m/2m6f hurdle winner Roman Gold; dam 2m hurdle winner, sister to 2m hurdle winners Burdens Boy and Burdens Girl; by a useful young sire and from a decent damline ,but best watched on debut.

OwnerName: A G Beatty & P McBride & Mrs Peter Casey & Mrs Paula Casey
Spotlight: First foal; dam 1m5f Flat (RPR 58) and 2m hurdle/chase winner, sister to 2m4f hurdle winner Give Us A Swig, half-sister to 2m chase winner Trooblue; unsold for E6,200 as a yearling; has a decent pedigree, but best watched on debut.
Late starter showed some improvement on second hurdle run at Kilbeggan, but plenty more is needed.
Not a bad run in sole completed point start and in two maiden hurdles since, at Punchestown and over C&D; this is a weaker race, so could be more involved.
Some promise in maidens; best handicap run came at Kilbeggan last time; needs to find more, but could be involved.
The best of four maiden hurdle runs came at Wexford last time over 2m4f but was still beaten 23l; however, there should be improvement to come and shouldn't be far away.
Soundly beaten in three maiden hurdles and will have to find plenty more, even in a weaker race.
Second runner for yard; maiden in points and chases doesn't make much appeal, even in this weak contest; cheekpieces added.

The best of his three bumper starts was on debut last year behind a useful rival; not as good in two starts after an absence, but Punchestown last time was a slowly-run race; looks a player in this weak maiden hurdle.
Maiden in all disciplines ran his best race over hurdles at Tramore last winter on soft; recent point form is off-putting.

OwnerName: Brian M Keenan & P W Mullins
Spotlight: Started 13-8 favourite for a Roscommon bumper last August, but was found to be blowing hard after being well beaten; raced keenly when again a beaten favourite at Tramore in October, but a better effort; hood added for jumps debut and half-brother to pair of useful hurdlers has to considered.
Second runner for yard; tailed-off on hurdles debut at Roscommon last time and not easy to make a case for.
Little sign of promise in three starts and can't be fancied.
Well beaten in two maiden hurdles, including over 2m4f here last time; needs loads more, even in this weak race.

RaceName: Racing TV Irish EBF Mares Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: M R D Fleming & G Bruce & J P Ryan & Miss G Ryan
Spotlight: Has shown improved form in her three maiden hurdle runs this season, two of them over C&D on good; third here and at Ballinrobe before a 10.5l sixth of 16 here last time; needs to find more on handicap bow.

RaceName: Racing TV Irish EBF Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Off the mark on the Flat with a bang at Down Royal last month when pulverising the field by 12l (10.5f, soft); attractive hurdles mark based on that performance, but this good ground is a significant question mark.

RaceName: Racing TV Irish EBF Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner has scored twice over hurdles, one of them coming at this venue over 2m4f; middling Flat efforts on her first two runs this year, but better at Downpatrick back over hurdles last month when a distant fourth (2m6f, good); others preferred; 1lb wrong.

RaceName: Racing TV Irish EBF Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won this race last year off 6lb lower (good) under O'Keeffe; made the frame a couple of times on the Flat this year; unseated at the first when joint-favourite at Downpatrick last month; didn't improve for 3m1f trip at Bellewstown last time; remains of interest.

RaceName: Racing TV Irish EBF Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 1-21 on the Flat, although placed on several occasions; her C&D maiden hurdle run here (good) last time wasn't too encouraging; makes handicap hurdle debut now but others make more appeal.

RaceName: Racing TV Irish EBF Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Downpatrick bumper (2m2f, good) and Kilbeggan maiden hurdle winner (2m, good) was turned over at short odds in a Perth mares' novice when last seen in September; lacks a recent run, so market can guide as to her fitness.

RaceName: Racing TV Irish EBF Mares Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Bumper and hurdles winner has been disappointing overall over fences and in one hurdle run this year, except for when second of six over fences at Tramore (2m, good) on penultimate; others are more appealing.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Last hurdles win came in April 2024 when trained by Fergal O'Brien; came back from a long absence for a new yard in the spring and not a bad Flat run last time, so looks a player with cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hasn't added to his maiden hurdle success for Liam O'Brien, but some good runs in defeat lately, including at Limerick last time when the winner raced on a faster strip of ground; a player.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden in all spheres ran well enough in a C&D claimer last time; needs a fair bit more from 4lb out of the handicap; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner in July 2024; best effort after return from an absence was last time at Downpatrick; can be involved with a repeat raised 1lb.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a Tramore claimer comfortably in April and was a decent third in a Downpatrick handicap since for this yard; raised 1lb and can go well again.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Six-time winner has won over fences here and has run well in defeat over hurdles here too; solid runs lately over hurdles and fences, so can be a player.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner was mostly chasing without winning last term; posted a decent hurdling return at Listowel and the third home that day since won a nice pot at Bellewstown; can go well.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: The last of three hurdle wins came in 2023; some good runs over fences in the last two seasons without winning; needs best hurdles form, but is off a low mark.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Synd'/D O'Leary/J McNally
Spotlight: Best hurdle run came when third at Downpatrick last time in this sphere, but was poor on the Flat since, so has to put that effort behind him.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Was third over 2m at Ballinrobe but might have found 2m3f too far at Punchestown since; record of 2-23 over hurdles, so others preferred.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden over fences and just 1-21 over hurdles; well beaten over fences lately and others make more appeal.

RaceName: Foley's Bar & Off Licence Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner for Wilie Mullins ran well in some handicaps, but was below form when last seen; needs his best on return for a new yard.
Won a maiden hurdle here in 2024 (2m2f, good); okay return at Clonmel in May when fifth of 16 (2m4f, good to yielding); 2m6f perhaps too far at Ballinrobe and Downpatrick recently.

OwnerName: Anthony Vaughan & Mark Phillips & D P Gaffney
Spotlight: Best effort over hurdles came at Punchestown in May when a 7.25l fifth of 19 (2m, good to yielding); below that level at Tramore next time (2m, good); cheekpieces are added to the tongue tie now.

Won a Punchestown handicap hurdle (2m4f, good) in October off 87; decent third on handicap chase debut at Punchestown in June (3m1f, good to yielding), outstayed late on; brought down at the sixth back over hurdles at Bellewstown earlier this month; chance if none the worse eight days later.
Won a 2m2f beginners' chase at Downpatrick (yielding) last autumn; placed over hurdles on either side of that success; might need this on his first run back after a lengthy break.
Third in a C&D maiden last year for Gavin Cromwell; fair recent form for this yard, twice over 2m6f at Downpatrick.

Off the mark over C&D on seasonal return here in May (good); up 5lb in the ratings, while his 5lb claimer has also been replaced; nonetheless, he took a couple of bumps in the course of his win and may improve for that first run back too; big player.
No show in maidens and similar story in three handicaps, the latest of which at Fairyhouse in November where he was tailed off; may need more time.
Impossible to fancy based on his three maiden hurdle runs, all coming on softer; tongue tie goes on now.
Won at Tramore (2m5f, soft to heavy) on final start in 2024; off for a year before three poor runs in December/January; tough task against that background.
1-22 over hurdles with the win coming at Downpatrick last August off 3lb higher (2m2f, good); poor in four runs this season; blinkers go on but he's opposable.
Only one win from 27 starts over hurdles, at Ballinrobe last year off 9lb higher (2m6f, good); well below his best in three runs this season, including over C&D in May.

Won a bumper and a maiden hurdle at Ballinrobe, but that latest win came three years ago; was in poor form over hurdles last autumn and was tailed off on his chasing debut at Downpatrick back from a break; the blinkers are new.

OwnerName: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club
Spotlight: Ordinary form; beaten 32l and 44l since entering handicap company.
No impact in maidens and his three handicap outings haven't been anything to shout about; down 4lb compared to his opening mark, but could probably do with a bit more clemency.

Flat maiden has run well at times over hurdles, particularly at this track; most recently was fourth in maiden hurdle company (2m2f, good); tongue-tie goes on and she should give a good account of herself.

Standout run over hurdles when a good second in a novice handicap at Wexford in March (2m, soft) on handicap debut; beaten when a faller over 2m4f at Tramore next time and was a distant fourth at Listowel last time; cheekpieces are added to the tongue tie but he may want it softer.
Unplaced in 26 starts; ninth of 13 at Limerick last time (2m, soft, 33-1); unlikely.

Posted some okay efforts in maiden hurdles last summer; tailed off on handicap/stable debut in May, but much better at Clonmel when a 3.5l fourth (2m1f, good); shaped like a return to this sort of trip would suit better that day.
Three-time British Flat winner for James Ferguson; no wins since 2024; enters handicap hurdle company for the first time now following moderate efforts in four maidens; best watched.
Modest form in bumpers and maiden hurdles for her previous trainer; not much better for her new yard, beaten 19l at Clonmel last month.
Outran 80-1 odds at Down Royal (2m2f, good) on handicap debut in May when fourth of 20, staying on well; a bit below that when a 19l fifth of 18 at Listowel (2m5f, good to yielding); place chance.

Narrowly got off the mark at Limerick (2m4f, soft) and continued the winning thread over C&D (good) next time when 2.75l ahead of the rest, keeping on well; up 7lb more and riders' claim reduced since, but big chance of making it a three-timer.
Lost form after finishing second in a maiden hurdle at this venue last summer; continued poor run of form when well beaten over 2m2f here on seasonal return (good) last month.
Hard to win with but not in bad form by any means, over hurdles and fences; 2m6f at Roscommon (yielding to soft) was likely too far for her last time.

Surprise winner of a 2m2f maiden (good, 28-1) here last month having failed to complete her three previous point-to-point starts; the form probably doesn't amount to much, but entitled to respect all the same.
Dual AW winner was fourth behind an easy winner on his second handicap hurdle start at Wexford in May (2m1f, good to yielding); never better than midfield on the Flat at Down Royal later that month.
Much better, compared to her return, at Bellewstown last time when a 12.5l fifth of 15; others more likely.
Has never reached the first three, but two okay hurdling runs recently sandwich a poor Limerick effort; others preferred.
Very modest form in maidens and pulled up in both handicaps; hard to fancy.

One of three for trainer; placed in four bumpers and took the runner-up spot over 2m2f here on handicap debut in August (good); weakened into fifth of 12 on return at Downpatrick (2m3f, good) last month; questions at this trip as she was well beaten over 2m5f at Limerick in October (yielding).

OwnerName: John J Cullen & Michael C Barrett
Spotlight: All three hurdling wins have come here (two over 2m4f and one at this trip); well backed on the Flat here in May but was well beaten (1m6f, yielding to soft); 8lb above last winning mark, but not ruled out back over hurdles.

Unbeaten in two point-to-points for Michael Goff; disappointing since, including on handicap debut at Naas in January, but first try at this trip and the addition of a tongue-tie could unlock improvement; interesting if the market speaks in his favour.
Dual hurdles winner reverts to this sphere after not distinguishing himself over fences most recently; others preferred.
Point winner ran her best race under rules when a 3.5l third of 16 over 3m2f here last month (good, 40-1); the form of that race might not be trustworthy, though, as the race turned into a dash.

One of three for trainer; got a first hurdles win at Kilbeggan in April last year off 9lb lower (3m1f, yielding); well beaten on return at Punchestown in May, but that was his first run in nine months, so entitled to come on for it.

Lost his form badly after winning the Cork National in 2024, but bounced back over 3m2f here (good) last time in first-time blinkers when runner-up after idling when leading (1.75l ahead of Je Suis); form of that race is questionable, but he has a notably low hurdles mark if he can repeat that effort.
One of two for trainer; sole success in 26 hurdle starts came over C&D in August off 6lb lower when winning by 15l (good); should come on for creditable fifth on return at Kilbeggan.

Both his chase wins have come at this track over 3m2f on better ground; two runs since returning to action have not been inspiring, tailed off over hurdles at Punchestown and pulled up over fences at Roscommon.
Dual hurdles winner and has won a chase; never better than midfield here last month (3m2f, good), but much better at Limerick (2m7f, good) last time when a 2.75l runner-up; could build on that.
Seven-time winner but his best years are behind him; beaten 21l on stable debut last month.

Bounced back to form when adding a fourth chase win at Punchestown in June (3m1f, good to yielding); another good run at Cartmel last time when beaten a neck; reverts to hurdles now off a much lower mark, but hasn't been able to take advantage of it in the past; 2lb wrong.
One of two for trainer; maiden winner over 2m6f over two years ago; has only run three times since then and the two most recent of those have seen him pull up.

One of three for trainer; off the mark when winning this race last year (good); nearly made it back-to-back C&D victories when beaten a neck on seasonal return in May (good, 11-1); up 2lb but he should come on for that run and O'Keeffe now rides for the first time.

Has been showing a fair standard over hurdles, making the frame in three of his last four outings in this sphere; his chasing debut didn't go to plan when last seen in January; in the mix if picking up where he left off over hurdles.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
OwnerName: Dermot J Kelly & E J Burns & Sandra C Kelly
Spotlight: Represents a local yard and her best runs have come over C&D, notably last time when was 2.25l in front of Moyneard; should uphold that form and doesn't need much more to take this.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Has a decent pedigree and caught the eye when staying on to be 5l behind Circus Hill over C&D on debut; there should be a decent bit of improvement to come and is in the mix.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Solid fourth in a 2m Clonmel maiden hurdle on debut and might not have stayed 2m4f at Limerick since; of some interest down in trip, reverting to a bumper.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Solid efforts all six bumper starts including over C&D last time; has 2.25l to find on Circus Hill from that run, but is likely to be involved again.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Well beaten in two bumpers, including in a visor at Bellewstown last week; this is a lesser race, but still needs loads more.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Fine runs in defeat in three out of her four bumper starts; her poor run came in Listed company on testing ground last November; the tongue-tie worn on return at Downpatrick is retained; solid chance.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Well beaten in a Limerick bumper on debut and has to make a lot of improvement.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Well beaten in three bumpers, including twice over C&D; the addition of a hood will need to make a major impact.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to point winner Syr Maffos; dam 2m4f-3m hunter chase/prolific point winner, out of unraced half-sister to prolific 7f-1m4f winner Rashay; likely one to watch for future reference on debut.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to winners Yeats Star (useful for this yard; 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase) and Chosen Thyme (bumper); dam fell in a point, is granddaughter of 2m5f-3m1f chase winner; related to some decent sorts and is one to consider, with the market a help.

RaceName: Clayton Hotel Mares INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to useful bumper and 2m1f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Tesseract; dam unraced half-sister to Italian 1m3f Listed winner Apprimus; by a useful sire and from a dual-purpose pedigree; could go well on debut.

RaceName: Welcome To Family Day Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner (albeit at a lowly level) in cheekpieces this spring (1m/1m2f) and made pretty good start to hurdling career when clear second of five over C&D (good to firm; no headgear) a fortnight ago; now has cheekpieces refitted; good chance.

RaceName: Welcome To Family Day Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Put in place by main market rival when second of four at Market Rasen (2m, good; raced too freely) last month but beat a next-time-out winner when scoring on hurdle debut at Hexham (2m, good) a few days earlier and has obvious claims here.

RaceName: Welcome To Family Day Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Irish raider; 0-5 on the Flat but kept on for second in low-grade Chepstow handicap (1m2f, good to firm) in first-time cheekpieces a fortnight ago; can feature if he takes well to hurdling.

RaceName: Welcome To Family Day Juvenile Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Unplaced all six Flat runs for Joseph Parr, showing no significant promise (including off lowly marks); changed hands for 1,000gns in May; cheekpieces removed after one use; best watched on stable/hurdle debut.

RaceName: Next Sunday Is Ladies Day Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed best form when third of seven over C&D (good to firm) 13 days ago on handicap debut; needs to improve again but that is not out of the question.

RaceName: Next Sunday Is Ladies Day Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Third over hurdles for the second time at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last month; this step up in trip needs to eke out more improvement.

RaceName: Next Sunday Is Ladies Day Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed improvement up in distance when runner-up at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) on her handicap debut last month; on a workable mark and should run well.

RaceName: Next Sunday Is Ladies Day Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Struggled to get competitive off basement Flat marks in 2023 and, following a long absence, has made an inauspicious start to her hurdling career for new stable this year; 8lb out of the weights.

RaceName: Next Sunday Is Ladies Day Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hasn't stood much racing and returns from a 671-day break following wind surgery with a new trainer; lacks the scope of his rivals and best watched unless the market speaks in his favour.

RaceName: Next Sunday Is Ladies Day Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Got off the mark when stepping up in trip at Ludlow (3m, good) in May; not in the same form when seventh of ten at Worcester (2m7f, good) in race won by Brionywells last time; first-time blinkers need to have an impact following a short break.

RaceName: Gary Hubball's 60th Celebration Year Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won the second of two points in April before being sold for £10,000; got off the mark over hurdles at Southwell (3m, good) last month when making all; well held at Worcester subsequently but must be of interest on chasing debut.

RaceName: Gary Hubball's 60th Celebration Year Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-17 under rules and plodded on to grab third at Worcester (2m4f, good) last month; sports first-time tongue-tie but lacks the scope of her younger rivals.

RaceName: Gary Hubball's 60th Celebration Year Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner; 0-12 over fences but placed a good few times but took a backward step last time when beaten 45l at Fontwell (3m2f, good); first-time visor replaces cheekpieces with tongue-tie retained; has a bit to prove.

RaceName: Gary Hubball's 60th Celebration Year Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Landed her third point in May; finished a modest second on her chase/stable debut here (2m3f, good to firm) 13 days ago; runs without hood worn on last four starts; has more on her plate here.

RaceName: Gary Hubball's 60th Celebration Year Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Made a pleasing start over fences when runner-up at Worcester (2m4f, good) last month; her performance when pulled up at Hexham subsequently was too bad to be true; first-time cheekpieces are now applied and she still has time on her side.

RaceName: Gary Hubball's 60th Celebration Year Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won a point last year and is still unexposed under rules but was pulled up at Bangor (3m, good) on handicap/chase debut in April; difficult to enthuse over.

RaceName: Gary Hubball's 60th Celebration Year Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Has shown little under rules so far; best watched on chase debut.

RaceName: Gary Hubball's 60th Celebration Year Novices' Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Made the frame for the second time over fences when fourth of eight finishers at Bangor (3m, good) last month in first-time tongue-tie (retained); holds place claims.

RaceName: University Hospitals Birmingham Charity Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Had wind surgery since failing to land the odds at Worcester (2m; bled from nose) last time on debut for Faye Bramley; form for Irish yards featured a success on the Flat and good second over hurdles; major contender provided he's back on song.

RaceName: University Hospitals Birmingham Charity Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Not the force of old (one-time useful Flat handicapper) but has made the frame in two Stratford events since rejoining Shark Hanlon, albeit proved no match for a Skelton-trained 11-10 favourite last time; likely to be a similar story today.

RaceName: University Hospitals Birmingham Charity Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has failed to transfer the pick of his bumper form to hurdles and ran out, possibly not entirely due to a slipped saddle, at Stratford most recently.

RaceName: University Hospitals Birmingham Charity Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Point bumper whose rules form, which includes a thoroughly remote second at Stratford, is far from convincing; had wind surgery since last run, which was her hurdles debut.

RaceName: University Hospitals Birmingham Charity Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Brings the worst form having achieved virtually nothing (Flat/hurdles); moreover, has failed to complete in three attempts under this code.

RaceName: Visit Zoolab In The Centre Course Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Is better over fences and got off the mark at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) in May; produced another solid effort when second at Market Rasen (2m3f, good) last month; first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces and looks sure to be thereabouts.

RaceName: Visit Zoolab In The Centre Course Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Scored over fences for Shark Hanlon in August 2025 but lost his way subsequently; back to his best on his second start for his current yard when winning at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last Tuesday; shouldered with a 7lb penalty but will be suited by the step up in trip and holds claims.

RaceName: Visit Zoolab In The Centre Course Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Won this contest last year off the same mark on the back of another win here, but has pulled up on his last three starts; not in the same form as last year so difficult to enthuse over.

RaceName: Visit Zoolab In The Centre Course Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Has a modest strike rate of 1-30 under rules; not disgraced in two runs over hurdles for this yard and has another go over fences after failing to take to this discipline previously; others preferred.

RaceName: Visit Zoolab In The Centre Course Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-11 under rules but made an encouraging start over fences when beaten a short-head here (2m1f, good to firm) in May; fourth of five finishers at Worcester (2m, good) last month but may be worth another chance back here.

RaceName: Visit Zoolab In The Centre Course Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Longstanding maiden who has been making the frame on a regular basis until finishing last of five at Warwick (2m4f, good) last month; hard to fancy for win purposes.

RaceName: Visit Zoolab In The Centre Course Handicap Chase
OwnerName: M Enticott / Ella Pickard Racing Club
Spotlight: Sole success came here in May last year but she has been out of sorts since; 4lb wrong in the weights and hard to fancy.

RaceName: Try Our Picnic Package Handicap Hurdle (For The Stratford Summer Salver)
Spotlight: Two of her three wins have been here and the other was at Plumpton, another sharp left-handed track; on a handy mark and her record when fresh is eye-catching; considered.

RaceName: Try Our Picnic Package Handicap Hurdle (For The Stratford Summer Salver)
OwnerName: A Walk In The Park Partnership
Spotlight: A sixth win over hurdles when beating three rivals with a bit in hand at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good); got off lightly with just a 1lb nudge; respected.

RaceName: Try Our Picnic Package Handicap Hurdle (For The Stratford Summer Salver)
Spotlight: Ludlow maiden winner in November; mark remains the same after respectable efforts in his first two handicaps (2m4f/2m6f); should have some say but perhaps vulnerable once more.

RaceName: Try Our Picnic Package Handicap Hurdle (For The Stratford Summer Salver)
Spotlight: Fourth in a claiming hurdle before having a complete off-day over fences five weeks ago, when he lost lengths at the start and jumped poorly before ultimately unseating; hard to win with at the best of times and now makes debut for a new Irish yard.

RaceName: Try Our Picnic Package Handicap Hurdle (For The Stratford Summer Salver)
Spotlight: Flat winner in Ireland; now 3-5 over hurdles, though it's questionable what he's achieved in the wins as the maiden came at odds of 2-9 and the handicaps were desperately uncompetitive, the latest a gift after market rival had relinquished lengths at the start; competes here off an untouched mark; this should reveal plenty more about him.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran creditably switched back to hurdles from the Flat last time, taking Stratford record to 1423214; seems weighted to the hilt but holds a solid chance kept to this venue and back up in trip; 1-1 over C&D.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Still a maiden and has unconvincing form for current yard; weakened into modest fifth over C&D most recently; best watched unless the betting suggests a resurgent effort is likely.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who is on a reduced mark and returns to the scene of her best effort for current stable but has been absent for 12 months; the market signals may offer further guidance.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ties in with Basilette on latest effort but that rival holds stronger claims kept to Stratford; has never won away from Plumpton and, despite a falling mark, her losing run is mounting up to boot.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Showed improvement in the autumn when last seen, winning at Luldlow (2m5f) then running well under a penalty at Huntingdon (2m3f), both times under James Bowen; may do better still; interesting handicap debutante.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Irish maiden who has been back in better form the last twice, respectable third at Limerick (2m4f; handicap debut) latest occasion; this drop back in distance looks ideal; first success is not out of the question.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Gained last two wins over fences; has refused to race; ties in with two of today's rivals on C&D form last time (third start for new yard) but isn't certain to back up that effort, having become unreliable.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Favourite Pullout Every Day Mares' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seems versatile as regards trip; good second at Hexham (2m7f) and successful at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good) since joining Dan Skelton; better than ever and may improve further at this sort of level; respected.

Haras de Fresnay-Le-Buffard Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
OwnerName: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Most progressive keen-going colt, who followed a reappearance Group 3 win in April over 7f with comfortably held efforts in the English and Irish Guineas; improved on those with a drop in class and trip, landing the Jersey Stakes on good to firm; shapes as a solid sort who should give his running back up to Group 1 company and Christophe Soumillon on for first time.

RaceName: Haras de Fresnay-Le-Buffard Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Had plenty of racing, pairing seven top-class sprinting performances as a 2yo, with mostly better ones this year over 7f and a mile; followed 1,000 Guineas win with two more solid ones and the drop to 7f looks something she could well enjoy; receives the sex allowance and a leading chance.

RaceName: Haras de Fresnay-Le-Buffard Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Top-level performer; second in last year's Group 1 Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere, finishing ahead of Rayif, but 3l behind that rival from a wide draw in the Poule d'Essai Des Poulains during May; confirmed his potential for better when beating a couple of these in the 7f Prix Paul de Moussac on good to soft last month, and should be fighting for a place at least.

RaceName: Haras de Fresnay-Le-Buffard Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Stable/ownermate of Rayif; had plenty of decent form last year, but hasn't progressed as of yet as a 3yo, although wasn't far short of his best in the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup (6f, good to firm) 23 days ago; cheekpieces go on but Mickael Barzalona unsurprisingly prefers Rayif.

RaceName: Haras de Fresnay-Le-Buffard Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Group 3 winner here as a 2yo and Group 1 placed (albeit behind Nighttime), both times at 7f, and won the Poule d'Essai Des Poulains on his first try over a mile when making his seasonal return in May, Nighttime back in sixth; that form hasn't been hugely advertised since and a bit surprising he's not been kept to a mile, but drop in trip holds few concerns and big player considering he's lightly raced.

RaceName: Haras de Fresnay-Le-Buffard Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Only once out of the first two in a busy juvenile campaign, latterly over 7f, which saw him behind the second-placed Nighttime in the Group 1 Prix Jean-Luc Lagardere before winning the Group 3 Horris Hill Stakes (7f, soft) in October; made a good return to action when runner-up to highly regarded stablemate Talk Of New York in late May at a mile, and should have more to come as a 3yo.

RaceName: Haras de Fresnay-Le-Buffard Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
OwnerName: Christian Faure, J-M Lefebvre et al
Spotlight: Consistent French performer, who finished less than a length behind Nighttime 35 days ago in the 7f Group 2 Prix Paul de Moussac on good to soft; may still do better but this looks a tough task to add another win to his tally.

RaceName: Haras de Fresnay-Le-Buffard Prix Jean Prat (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Finished well behind Rayif in the Poule d'Essai Des Poulains and then had Nighttime and Go Man in front of him in the Prix Paul de Moussac when last seen; would need a big career best to land this.

OwnerName: Bag Racing Stables, Miller Racing Et Al
By Talla Racing LLC, St Elias Stables LLC Et Al
L and N Racing LLC & Clark O Brewster
Arnold Bennewith, Rick Wiest Et Al
Baccari Racing Stable, Dream Walkin Farms Inc Et Al
Andrew Farm, Mountmellick Farm Et Al

Spotlight: Has definite form claims on this year's best efforts but last run wasn't one of those and that was three months ago; since well held in two trials and he's drawn in 14.

OwnerName: Sunny Wong Chi Tat & Wong Tsz Wah
Spotlight: Longstanding maiden but finished upsides Golden Fortune last time at Happy Valley when trying 1m3f for the first time; has form here and enters the reckoning.

OwnerName: Graham Kot Li Heng & Amy Chau
Spotlight: Infrequent winner whose best form is on dirt, having been unplaced in all 11 turf races.

OwnerName: Robert Chung Wai Shu & Jeanette Chung Siu Wah
Spotlight: In reapplied blinkers when beaten just over 2l over 1m here 11 days ago, staying on well from off the pace; has a better draw this time and 1m1f suits him well; he's 1-40 but goes on the shortlist.
Dead-heated for second with Hailtothevictors on dirt here last month (1m1f) and again runner-up on turf 18 days ago when 1m3f seemed to stretch him; possibilities.
Yet to be even placed (0-10) and there's no clear light at the end of the tunnel; very opposable drawn so wide.
Won twice in Britain; has a poor strike-rate in Hong Kong but he's not been running at all badly and the race didn't go to plan here last time (beaten 3.5l); not without hope.
Only 1-24 but has been running consistently well in defeat; a nagging concern is that he's a better horse at Happy Valley but he has to rate a contender from stall 1.
0-18 in Hong Kong and the drop to Class 5 company has made little difference; difficult to warm to.
Maiden winner for Aidan O'Brien but has got nothing going in Hong Kong and remains opposable.
0-16 but some of this season's form makes him a player and he wasn't beaten too far into seventh here last time; he's never been lower in the weights.
Behind some of these at Happy Valley last time and the draw was no excuse, but the fact he's a five-time winner here has to heighten interest.
7f winner who stays this far but his recent efforts have been pretty ordinary.
Should be well handicapped but has looked out of sorts for a while now, in Class 5s the last two races.

OwnerName: Charmaine Wong Kam Mei & Chan Fu Ho
Spotlight: Only 1-26 but would have won in another stride over 7f here three weeks back when Draco was fourth; consistent in the main and Purton was also aboard last time.
His trial win was over 6f but he was runner-up over C&D late last year and latest effort wasn't too shoddy; however, stall 14 poses problems.
No wins in 19 but excellent runs in his last two races over C&D, latterly finishing second after finding heaps of trouble up the straight.
20-race maiden with a mixed record and sprinting is probably more his scene.

OwnerName: Steve Chan Chi Fung & Terry Ng Ting Chi
