Spotlight: 0-10 but has been running on in his last two races over 6f which seems too sharp; a strong pace up front would help as he's often strong at the finish.
A winner in Ireland who has been threatening a breakthrough in Hong Kong but over longer trips; back over 7f he was behind Double Bingo and Draco three weeks ago, admittedly from a poor draw.
Hadn't been in the best of form but he was nearest at the finish from a bad draw over C&D 11 days ago; exposed but has dropped to a very realistic mark.

OwnerName: The Hon & Mrs Kenneth Lau Ip Keung
Spotlight: Sole win came over 1m here in January and that seems more his trip, over which he was placed 18 days ago; vulnerable back at 7f.
0-21 but has competitive form and didn't get a clear shot at it when a close fourth here last time over 7f, finishing just behind Double Bingo.
Hasn't been getting much luck but there were no apparent excuses last time and he's since performed moderately in a trial.

OwnerName: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho
Spotlight: Exposed four-time winner but all those victories, and his best form, came on dirt; good fourth on that surface over 6f last week.

OwnerName: Kenneth Kong Siu Chee & Elaine Kong Yick Ning
Spotlight: Yet to make the frame in seven starts but not beaten far when seventh over C&D last month and this is a drop in grade; considered despite an awkward draw.
Had plenty of chances now in Hong Kong and he's been running over longer trips; opposable from stall 13.
Midfield over 6f on final run in April, since when he's finished down the field in a trial; interesting back at 7f over which he has placed form this year at a higher level.
Well held in first two races over 6f but the step up to 7f in first-time cheekpieces brought about an improved third over C&D three weeks ago; he finished that race out well.
Was threatening to go the wrong way but has turned things around in winning two trials and might now be on a handy mark.

OwnerName: Tommy Ching Tsun Wah, Fujisawa Tasayoshi Et Al
Spotlight: Has got himself well handicapped and from a good draw (similar to today's) he was a front-running third under Nichola Yuen here a month ago; likely to run well again.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs George Chang And George Hing Chang
Spotlight: Some promise last year over 6f but well beaten on his return at that trip; steps up to 7f from stall 14; risky.
All three wins have been here but over 6f and he has poor form in three attempts at 7f, which were a long time ago.

OwnerName: Eden Wong Chi Hong & Anita Chu Suk Ching
Spotlight: Ran on well to be just behind Grand Patch here recently and his previous run on yard debut was also a good one; should be winning soon (0-24) but stall 12 is a negative.
Went very close off 6lb higher in a Class 3 over C&D in January but disappointing since, including on relegation to this level last time.

OwnerName: Raymond Tsang Heung Tak & Kenneth Tsang Heung Kan
Spotlight: Didn't always get the breaks over 6f and again on first run over 7f he got behind before staying on well; might be able to hold a handier position here from a lower draw and Purton rides again; very tempting.

OwnerName: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate
Spotlight: Lightly raced 3yo but struggled in Class 3s and again on Class 4 debut, admittedly from a wide draw.
Suggested his turn might be near when second over 1m here in March but lesser efforts have followed.
Hasn't been pulling up any trees in his trials and readily opposed on this official debut.
Early days and this is a welcome drop in grade from Class 3s, but he's not been showing much.

OwnerName: Yuki Kong Yuk Ki & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen
Spotlight: The winner of three barrier trials but uncompetitive in four official races from 5f to 1m.
Ordinary in trials and was down the field on last month's debut here over 6f.

OwnerName: James Kwan Wai Ming & Leung Kam Chuen
Spotlight: Dual 7f Class 4 winner this season and not disgraced in a higher grade since those wins, notably three starts back; now returns to a Class 4 on the back of a trial win and he's well drawn; player.

OwnerName: Gibson Lau Wai Chi, Wu Kai Char Et Al
Spotlight: May need further assistance from the handicapper; placed in trials since his run here in May.

Beneath his last winning mark and ran really well for second from a good draw over C&D two weeks ago; drawn nothing like so well this time stuck out in box 14, but he's in better form than many in here.
Has struggled this year and his fourth over 6f here in May proved a false dawn.
Positives to take from all three races, though no closer than 3.75l; more needed.
Won here in November but has lost his way big time and recent trials weren't overly encouraging.

OwnerName: Kelvin Chan Kam Kei & Eddie Chan Kam Kong
Spotlight: Consistent in the main but at 1m, and 7f caught him out last time; others might be too sharp at this trip.
Has gone the wrong way since securing 6f honours here in February and was readily outpaced over 7f here a month ago (beaten just under 4l); others preferred.
Regressive form on the track and finished last in two trials during June.
A disappointing last run but had been first and second (both over C&D) on initial runs for this yard and he's still on a good mark; well drawn for one who can race up with the pace.
Twice down the field over 6f but gave it a good go up front when beaten 2l over 7f a month ago; drawn lower now; only sixth in a recent trial but perhaps best not to read too much into that.
Makes official debut after several defeats in trials; easy to turn elsewhere.
Three quiet runs since his C&D win in April and there were no draw excuses last time; needs to rebound.

OwnerName: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho
Spotlight: Made promising debut when fourth over C&D in March but has finished down the field three times since (6f-1m); did finish second in a recent trial and eyeshields are fitted today.
Safely held on all three starts (6f-7f) and wouldn't be the obvious answer despite finishing second in a trial since the last of those.

It was a Class 5 in which he made all by 2l here in May and he has been found wanting in Class 4s since, though was by no means disgraced; Britney Wong claims 7lb, replacing Nichola Yuen who took 10lb off last time.

Trip winner at Happy Valley off 4lb lower and has shown he can deal with this mark; dropped unlucky with the draw when back in a Class 4 last time but now in stall 1; likely contender under Zac Purton.
Opposable on his trial runs and most definitely so on what he's shown on the track (over C&D).
Has form figures of 41479 in trials since April; likely best watched on debut.
Won two trials before ridden out from a prominent position to score over C&D 15 days ago; has been competitive before off this 6lb higher mark; player.
Has shown some ability in his six defeats, including last time when fourth from a bad draw; however, he's got another one here and was dropped only 2lb.
Consistent in his five races, beaten between 2l and 4l; would have been worth considering off a sliding mark had he not been drawn so wide.
Three-time winner in Australia but well down the field in all his Hong Kong runs; did finish third in a recent trial and has changed trainers, but far from solid.
Won a 6f dirt trial before finishing well behind on his official debut here a month ago; drawn wide.
On a dangerous mark even before Nichola Yuen's 10lb claim is considered and they combined for a front-running second over C&D two weeks ago; has to go well.
Given the promise of his third run when he didn't enjoy the clearest of passages, his last effort was bitterly underwhelming; if forgiven that then wouldn't be without hope.
Tried 7f last time to no avail; the 2l defeat over C&D previously brings him into this off 3lb lower.

OwnerName: Dr Fu Chi Wing, Dr Lau Siu Fai, Lau Park Yung & Fu Florence
Spotlight: Underwhelming trial runs on either side of his 4l defeat here in January.

OwnerName: Eric Yeung Ting Fung & Angel Wong Sau Kuen
Spotlight: Ran respectably from wide stalls on consecutive 6f Class 3 starts at Happy Valley in April/May but ought to have been suited by return to 7f when only tenth in this lower grade six weeks ago; now has a point to prove.
Launched career with two low-key 6f runs; probably one for further down the line.
0-16 including three runs in Australia but recent 7f efforts have been respectable and he should be okay back at 6f; considered each-way.

A maiden after eight starts but placed twice over C&D this season and shaped very much as though this return to 6f will suit when keeping-on fifth over 5f last month; could have a say, despite wide draw.
Unplaced all eight starts but has shown improvement to finish fifth on last two appearances and has same favourable draw in stall 2 today; enters calculations.
Came good for Mark Newnham at third attempt, with C&D win in May, and ran at least as well when second here last month; drawn wide today but still looks the one to beat in this uncompetitive Class 4.
0-10 in Hong Kong; suggested he might be building up to something when fourth at Happy Valley in April but three lesser efforts have followed and he's drawn wide here.
Runner-up here on dirt in January, his second run after a layoff, but this regressive 6yo finished down the field on his next three appearances; needs to have been refreshed by latest break.

OwnerName: Jameson Leung Tai Lin & Rene Leung Yan Ting
Spotlight: Made encouraging late headway over 5f on debut here in May but was safely held over C&D a fortnight ago; remains best watched.

OwnerName: Jimmy Tang Kwok Leung, Ho Kai Man & Ricky Lee Wing Ngai
Spotlight: Unplaced all six Hong Kong starts; only beaten about 3l when sixth from stall 11 over C&D a fortnight ago but drawn even wider this time; may be one for another day.
Made just a mildly encouraging debut when ninth of 14 here (5f; just behind Prime Winister) three weeks ago; favourable draw not enough to make him of interest.
Made all over C&D from stall 10 last week but has the same awkward draw today and his record this year is very mixed; back up to career-high mark; not easy to predict.
Not devoid of ability but yet to be placed after six runs (6f-7f) and will probably be of greater interest when eligible for Class 5.

OwnerName: 25/26 Danny Shum Chap Shing Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Not disgraced when eighth on debut here (5f) three weeks ago but needs to take a good step forward today.
Found one just too strong when odds-on for stable debut over C&D three weeks ago but that was still a good effort and he commands respect, despite another awkward draw.
Outran triple-digit odds when respectable fifth here (1m1f) last month but is yet to be placed after nine Hong Kong starts; hood removed today; others have more pressing claims.
In the frame all four starts (7f-1m1f) for new stable since March, most notably when winning over C&D on second occasion; each-way claims at least.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Admund Au Chi Hoe
Spotlight: Placed four times here (1m-1m1f) this season and also ran well when fourth here (1m1f) eight days ago; eyeshields removed from headgear collection today; probably won't be far away.
Placed on dirt (1m1f) in May but was safely held when back on turf recently and has become far too inconsistent to rely upon.
Came good with emphatic win at Happy Valley in May, his 11th start, and was placed off new mark there last month; deserted by Zac Purton today but ought to make his presence felt again.

Just 1-24 in Hong Kong but put a poor run behind him when creditable second from stall 12 at Happy Valley 18 days ago and has now filled that position on three of his last four appearances; respected from stall 3 here.

OwnerName: Stephanie Fung Wing Man, Kelly Fung Et Al
Spotlight: 0-9 in Hong Kong; posted sound effort when fourth to Packing Fighter over C&D three weeks ago but needs to find something extra today; finished last the only previous time he wore cheekpieces (in November).
Soundly beaten on first two starts but in the frame three consecutive times since (6f-7f); not out of this if suited by the step up in trip.
Came good with narrow C&D win in Class 5 in May, his ninth start, but struggled when back up in this grade three weeks ago and looks vulnerable again from stall 14.

OwnerName: Keith Poon Pak Kit, Henry Cheng Wai Hong Et Al
Spotlight: Launched career with three fairly encouraging runs (6f-1m) and benefits from Nichola Yuen's useful 10lb claim today; not ruled out each-way.
Looked suited by step up in trip when sixth over 7f last month, his fifth start, but needs to kick on again over this extra furlong.
Ended last season with a C&D win and was placed on first two starts this term but seems badly to have lost his way since.
Well handicapped if judged on Happy Valley second in February but weakened out of contention on last three outings and now 0-8 in Hong Kong.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen
Spotlight: Five-time Group winner, most recently over 1m1f here three weeks ago; evidently retains a large chunk of ability as a 9yo but recent 5lb rise might still be enough to prevent a follow up.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Ricky Ng Yu Chiu & To Ngo Fung
Spotlight: Four wins this season (7f-1m); still in good form but, from out of the weights, he's effectively 6lb higher than when fourth behind Soleil Fighter over C&D last month.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Improving 5yo who won more cosily than narrow margin suggests at Happy Valley (1m1f) last month and was persistently denied a clear run when fourth in Group 3 handicap won by Beauty Joy three weeks ago; good chance.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Showed career-best form four weeks ago, making all over C&D at a good pace under Nichola Yuen; might find it tough to fend off this stronger field after recent 6lb rise, though.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Disappointing favourite when only sixth in C&D contest won by Soleil Fighter last month but was in good form beforehand and may yet have more improvement to come; contender if quickly back on song.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Seemingly back in good form when 37-1 third at Happy Valley (1m1f) last month but finished down the field behind Beauty Joy in Group 3 handicap here three weeks ago; 2lb out of the weights; not the percentage call.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won 1m4f Group 1 in Brazil in late 2024 and is a dual C&D scorer this Hong Kong season; put two down-the-field runs behind him when keeping-on third to Soleil Fighter over C&D on last month's stable debut and has possibilities off same mark today.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won twice here (1m/1m1f) in February/March but his improvement has levelled off since; 1lb wrong today; others look stronger.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with two Happy Valley wins (1m1f/1m) and has largely remained in good form since; didn't fire last time, though, and has the outside draw today.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; won here (1m1f) on Hong Kong debut in April and wasn't disgraced in Group races on next two outings but needs to improve to land this prize from 7lb out of the handicap.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won over this trip at Happy Valley in November and was placed there (1m-1m1f) on last four appearances, including close second in Group 3 handicap in January; won't be far away if still in same form after 137-day break.

RaceName: Hong Kong Racehorse Owners Association Trophy (Handicap) (Class 1) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Lightly raced since winning Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) in March 2024 and absent since respectable sixth in 1m1f Group 3 handicap here in November; makes stable debut from workable mark but tricky to weigh up at present.
Dual 6f dirt winner here this season, latterly when leading close home on stable debut four weeks ago; another 7lb higher today, though, and this return to turf may not be ideal.

Yet to run a bad race after five starts this season (all over C&D) and ought to be on the premises again but may not have much wiggle room off this career-high mark; likely to be dropped in from his wide draw.
Low-mileage 4yo who started off for new stable with two C&D wins in April/May and was not discredited when close third here three weeks ago; warrant serious consideration.

OwnerName: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate
Spotlight: Well below best this season but was close third from stall 10 off much-reduced mark over C&D two starts ago; combination of much more favourable draw and Nichola Yuen's useful 10lb claim makes him of major interest.
Three wins this season, two over C&D and the latest on dirt; below form on latest dirt start, though, and has another wide draw today.

OwnerName: 23/24 Cody Mo Wai Kit Trainer Syndicate
Spotlight: Not beaten far from double-digit stalls on last three outings, most recently when fifth over C&D three weeks ago, but his run of poor luck draw-wise has continued.
Progressive 3yo with 3-5 strike-rate (two wins over C&D); ran big race when close third here on latest outing two months ago and makes obvious appeal.
Yet to show any significant promise after five Hong Kong runs (6f-7f) and now eligible for a drop in grade.
Won in Australia in September (6f) but struggled to get seriously competitive on first two Hong Kong starts (5f/6f).
Now 8lb lower than when winning at Happy Valley in early part of this season but recent efforts have been substandard and he's poorly drawn.
Safely held on first two Hong Kong starts but put in good front-running shift when fourth over C&D six weeks ago; well drawn again today and now has something to build on.
Ex-Australian 3yo; well down the field over 5f here on first two Hong Kong starts; difficult to enthuse over.

OwnerName: Fung Koon Yat Ching, Kenneth Fung King Keung Et Al
Spotlight: Made winning debut in 5.5f Australian maiden on Christmas Eve; unraced since but well drawn for this Hong Kong debut; market may guide.
Always behind when a big-priced outsider for Hong Kong debut three weeks ago.
0-5 in Hong Kong but close third over C&D on both starts since upped in trip and fitted with cheekpieces in May; features prominently in calculations.
Showed career-best form when clinging on for C&D success 11 days ago and has now won five times this season; evidently in excellent nick but another 6lb higher today.
Won three in a row over C&D in April/May and proved himself in this higher grade when close second to Endued here last month; needs to be taken very seriously in current mood.

OwnerName: P Chan Pak Hou, J Chan Chi Kwong & J Chan Chon San
Spotlight: Late-maturing 6yo who posted career-best performance when close second over C&D in April; safely held twice since, though, and may remain vulnerable off mark this high.
Gained both win this season over 1m on dirt; also suited by turf but is drawn wide and doesn't have much margin for error off current mark.

Began year with three down-the-field runs over C&D but returned from three-month break with two 7f seconds in May/June; still well handicapped on older form and this move back up in trip should suit; might be the answer.

OwnerName: Lo Yu Sai, Jasmine Lo Siu Yu Et Al
Spotlight: Largely consistent 4yo who scored over 7f here in May and arguably bettered that performance when close second over that trip last month; stays 1m and could plausibly still have untapped potential over this far; high on the list.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung
Spotlight: Quite useful when trained in Britain and was placed for first time in five-race Hong Kong career when close third to Lucky Sam Gor over C&D 11 days ago; obvious contender if in similar form again.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Chan Chung Yin & Mr & Mrs Clevin Got Chong Key
Spotlight: Returned to form with C&D win in first-time visor in May and encountered traffic before keeping on well for fifth at Happy Valley on next outing; Zac Purton keeps the ride; may still be on the upgrade.

OwnerName: Gwenny Wu Pui Kwan & Daniel Yeung Ngai
Spotlight: Won consecutive 7f races here in February/March and has remained in good form since; not yet proven over 1m and Zac Purton evidently prefers Smart Avenue today but this fellow can still feature.
Progressive 4yo who has collected three C&D wins this season, the latest four weeks ago; not so well drawn today and latest 6lb rise demands more from him but he ought to give another good account.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Lam Ngai And Emily Lam Huen
Spotlight: Dual 6f course winner during this first Hong Kong season and twice in the frame since upped to 7f in May; not ideally drawn for first attempt at 1m but likely to be on the premises again if stamina holds out.

OwnerName: Ricky Wong Kin Chat & Helen Leung Yee Wan
Spotlight: Drops in grade after contesting 1m4f Group 3 handicap in May and has a workable mark but would appeal more over further.
Sixth at big odds over C&D in April but subsequent efforts have been underwhelming and he now has it all to prove again.

OwnerName: Choi Yui Hin & Daniel Choi Yui Fai
Spotlight: Didn't really fire here four weeks ago but this generally progressive 4yo scored over C&D in May and, after only ten starts, is not yet fully exposed.
Bang-in-form 4yo who has won his last two over C&D; combination of rise in grade and outside draw makes this a daunting assignment, though.

2-3 as a 2yo in Australia, the latter win gained in 5.5f Group 3; denied clear run on both Hong Kong starts but made headway into midfield once in the clear here (6f) last time and looks worth a crack at 7f.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Hamen Fan Shi Hoo, Alex Fan Chen Yen Et Al
Spotlight: Posted career-best performance when coming from off the pace to win easily over C&D in May and didn't get a clear run when fifth in this higher grade last month but might find others in this field better handicapped.
Consistent and steadily progressive 4yo who has run ten times over C&D, winning twice and finishing second on other eight occasions; likely to be right in the thick of things again.
Won comfortably over C&D from stall 14 when upped in trip three weeks ago and is now 2-5; 8lb higher today but much better drawn this time and, with further progress likely, commands respect.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Colin Lo Chor Cheong
Spotlight: Returned from break with encouraging C&D fourth in May but was well below that form last month and has become hard to predict.

OwnerName: Richard Yue Wah Chuk & David Pao
Spotlight: 2-3 over 6f in Class 4 but has found things a bit tougher since upped to this grade for last three runs; has the services of Zac Purton today but needs the extra furlong to unlock more improvement; blinkers removed; tongue-tie added.

Largely in good form over 1m for new stable this season and coped well with drop back in trip when fourth (just behind Super Express) from wide stall over C&D last month; not ideally drawn today either but still considered.

OwnerName: Peter Chu Ka Lok, Maria Teresa Chu & Dr C Chu Ping Wing
Spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who battled well for narrow C&D wins on his last two appearances; could still have more to offer but this rise in grade asks quite a lot of him.

OwnerName: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen
Spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; held on narrowly for C&D win on Hong Kong debut in April but two down-the-field runs have followed and he's drawn wide again today; needs help from new visor.
Took big step back in right direction when fourth over C&D three weeks ago but has not been at his best this year and others have much more appealing profiles.
Quite useful when trained in Britain but well down the field on first three Hong Kong starts (7f-1m).

OwnerName: Ching Chi Fai, Tommy Ching Tsun Wah & Mandy Ching Tsin Wai
Spotlight: Made winning debut over an extended 7f in Australia in December; remains an unknown quantity but makes Hong Kong debut after seven-month break and in a competitive race.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Cost 145,000gns as a yearling and is out of a fairly useful 7f AW winner; shaped well before fading into third of seven on debut at Beverley (7.4f, good) four weeks ago and will be a tough nut to crack if able to build on that performance here.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to four winners, two of them placed in Group races; beaten nearly 8l but still displayed significant promise when third of five in Newmarket novice (7f, good to firm) won by a good prospect last month; needs to be taken very seriously.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Closely related to a fairly useful 6f 2yo winner; beaten about 11l when keeping-on third of six on last month's debut at Musselburgh (7f, good), showing only minor promise, but ought to have learnt from that experience.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 68,000euros yearling; first foal; dam once-raced half-sister to winners Stormy Antarctic (1m/1m1f Group 2) and Al Johrah (Group-placed 5f 2yo), out of Listed-placed 1m/1m2f winner; with a good stable; no surprise if he features on debut.

RaceName: Supporting Ayr Racecourse, The John Cocking Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50,000gns yearling; seventh foal; half-brother to winners Lauderdale (French/KSA/Qatari 1m-10.5f including 2yo; RPR 102), Firelight (6f 2yo; 100) and Antiphon (5f-6f; 87); dam 1m4f winner (AW/Listed; 107), sister to 12.5f Listed winner Oh Beautiful; good market support will bring him into the equation.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Won off an 11lb mark at Musselburgh last August (5f, good); hasn't run as well since and below-par over same C&D last time (good); dropped another 3lb so no surprise if he were to go well.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Three of her four wins last season (5f/6f) were on fast ground, so conditions suit here; creditable second over C&D last week (good); a leading chance.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Hamilton off 3lb lower in May (5f, good); close third over same C&D next time; each-way chance.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to win on turf, but is now 1lb lower than when winning at Newcastle in February (5f); could go well, although was beaten 10l here last time (6f, soft).

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: All six wins, three on AW, have been over 6f; best run this season was when second over 5f at Ffos Las last month (good); chance on that run but this faster going not sure to suit.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish; five-race maiden; well beaten over 6f on both starts for Mike Smith; best watched for now.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Only turf win came off a 7lb lower mark at Musselburgh in June (5f, good); two decent runs since then, goes well on fast ground and one to consider.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Back to form in first-time cheekpieces when winning a selling handicap at Hamilton last time (5f, good to firm); only 2lb higher so not out of it with headgear on again.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Best at 5f, but all his wins have come on AW or good/soft ground on turf; fair third on fast ground at Catterick two runs back (5f) but well beaten after being slowly away and losing a shoe over C&D last week.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Now 3lb lower than when winning on AW at Newcastle (5f) in January; fair form on turf last twice, latest when 2l fourth at Thirsk (5f, good); has won on fast ground; each-way chance.

RaceName: 25% Off Lunch At Western House Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark in a maiden here in May (6f, soft; 200-1); not so good back in handicap company at Carlisle last time (6f, good); others stronger.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Three wins last June/July, the third over 5f here (good to soft); just as effective over 6f on fast ground and AW; neck second at Hamilton (5f, good) on Saturday and one to consider.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Has won four times, including twice here (5f/6f), but ideally suited by some give in the ground; proving hard to win with this season, although has run creditably on last four starts (on good to firm on first occasion); each-way chance.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-George Scott; eight-race maiden and yet to hit form for Mike Smith (well beaten over 7f here last week).

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark in a C&D handicap last week (good); has stiffer task with his penalty and was withdrawn on account of fast ground last month, but worth considering.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins have been over 5f on good ground, latest at Musselburgh last August; hasn't been in top form this term, best run when third at Musselburgh in June (5f); bit to prove, including his stamina.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
OwnerName: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing
Spotlight: 12-time winner, latest on AW in March (6f); fair running-on third over 5f here last time (good; Woohoo, who runs in the 2.30, was second); contender back up in trip.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Only win in 19 turf runs was at Hamilton in June (6f, good to soft); close third to Jm Jhingree (runs in the 2.30) in a 5f Hamilton seller last month (good) and close fourth in a 5f handicap there on Saturday; each-way chance.

RaceName: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed; well beaten on both starts in handicaps; best watched for now.

Won maiden claimer for Tony Mullins in August 2024 (1m, good); hasn't won since but a few fair runs for new yard, including when third at Carlisle in June (1m1f, soft); blinkers now tried; fast ground is a concern.

OwnerName: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt
Spotlight: C&D winner in May (good to firm) and decent third at Hamilton later in the month (8.5f, good to firm); disappointing in two runs since, first-time cheekpieces latest (retained); could go well back on good to firm ground.
Veteran who has won three of his 58 races on turf, the latest over this trip at Musselburgh last September (good); has been a bit disappointing this season.

OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner
Spotlight: Dual turf winner in 2024 (6f/7.5f); has also won twice on the AW, most recently last December (7f), but rather out of form since and others are stronger.
Two wins over 1m at Ripon last season (good/good to firm); a bit disappointing this term (4l fourth of seven at Pontefract latest) but on a good mark now.
Two wins (7f/1m) in August 2024; best run last season was when second over C&D last May (good to firm); could bounce back from a below-par reappearance run last month.

OwnerName: N J Dalgarno & W R Ellis
Spotlight: 27-race maiden; creditable third in a C&D classified event eight days ago (good); this is tougher.
Ex-Irish; 0-11; well beaten on stable debut at Carlisle nine days ago (1m1f, good) after nearly a year off but better can be expected today.
Only win in 23 runs was a 1m nursery at York in October 2023 for Ralph Beckett; badly out of form this season and cheekpieces now tried.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Has modest 1-22 strike-rate and is better known as a sprinter but kept on for respectable fifth at Thirsk (7f, good to firm) recently; drops in grade here and is well worth another crack at this trip; interesting.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Safely held on consecutive 6f starts this spring but placed twice since returned to 7f, latterly in C&D (good) contest won by Jkr Cobbler three weeks ago; 2lb higher today but probably won't be far away.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to strike form after four runs for new stable this year (1m-1m2f) and her only win was gained over 1m2f on heavy ground; continues to slip down the weights but drops back to 7f with quite a bit to prove.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Gained five of his nine wins over C&D, the latest eight days ago (good ground); still on workable mark under 4lb penalty and ought to be right in the thick of things again.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Just 1-22 but scores good marks for her consistency since 6f AW win in January; denied clear run before keeping on for fifth in recent C&D contest won by Jkr Cobbler; each-way claims at least.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: In the frame three consecutive times in recent weeks, most recently when fourth behind Jkr Cobbler over C&D (good) eight days ago, but remains without a win since spring 2024 and recent 2lb rise is not helpful.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Well handicapped on some of last year's form for Gemma Tutty but left that stable after some disappointing runs in the autumn and there has been no upturn after three starts for new trainer this year.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: Belied 33-1 odds when fourth at Musselburgh (7f, good) three weeks ago and is now 2lb lower than for last summer's win at Redcar (7f, good to soft) but her overall profile remains unconvincing.

RaceName: Try Racing TV For Free Now Handicap
Spotlight: 2-4 since returned to turf in April, the latter win gained at Catterick (7f, good for firm) three weeks ago; effectively 6lb higher here if Conor Whiteley's reduced claim is considered but ought to go well again.

Came good for Jim Goldie at third attempt, in refitted cheekpieces at Hamilton last month, and bettered that form when close third in competitive race at York (also 1m1f, good) soon afterwards; not quite at same level over the extended 1m2f at York last time but still a major player here.

0-14 overall but has performed respectably for new stable this year, most recently when fourth at Carlisle (1m3f, good to soft) last month; continues to edge down the weights and ought to be suited by the reduced emphasis on stamina here; not out of this.
Low-mileage filly who kept on for clear second behind unexposed rival on last month's handicap debut at Hamilton (8.3f, good); moves back up in trip and competes off same mark here; commands respect.
Yet to add to his two C&D wins in spring 2024 but got back in the groove with two placed runs last month, the latter in Class 4 C&D contest; ought to pose a potent threat at this lower level.
Safely held over 7f recently and now 0-7 but made the frame on consecutive 8.3f starts at Nottingham in April, and the dam's side of her pedigree suggests she's well worth a crack at this new trip.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Long in the tooth now and needs to better his recent efforts but was close third off 4lb higher at Hamilton (1m1f, good to firm) in May and is probably better suited by today's trip; can't rule out.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Placed over hurdles this season and was third (albeit no match for wide-margin winner) in Cumberland Plate at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) when back on the Flat for new stable last month; respected off same mark in this easier race.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins this spring, latterly when making all to score by over 6l at Musselburgh (2m, good) last month; safely held when making the frame on next two outings (2m/2m1f) but still enters calculations.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Gained three of his nine wins over this C&D and gave good account when staying-on third over C&D (good) a week ago; still on the same workable mark and has another big role to play.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Took step back in right direction when second of five at Musselburgh (2m, good) last month but was unable to lay a glove on Cascade Hall and will need to build on that performance here.

RaceName: Every Race Live On Racing TV Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Made winning handicap debut for Ed Walker at Ffos Las (1m4f, soft) last summer but was sold for 13,000gns after a lesser effort in September and this stable debut comes after a 292-day break; market may guide.

Mehmas colt; E260,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Bear Story (1m-10.6f AW inc 2yo/Group 3/Australia; RPR 110) and Slieve Bearnagh (7f-1m2f Flat, 71; 2m/2m1f hurdle); dam 1m2f winner (96), half-sister to HK/Singapore 1m-1m2f Group 1 winner Dunboyne Express; respected source and lots of appeal on paper.

Frankel colt; dam 6f-8.5f winner (inc 2yo/US Grade 1; RPR 110), sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Sacred Bridge, closely related to 1m2f Listed winner Crossed Baton, out of 9.4f/1m2f winner from fine family; attractive pedigree and yard in red-hot form.

OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Regally-bred Wootton Bassett colt was sent off 6-1 on debut at the Curragh 16 days ago and found just stablemate Giant Sequoia too good; pulled nicely clear of the third that day and likely to improve plenty for that initial experience; preferred by Lordan; the one to beat.

Zarak colt; E65,000 yearling; bought for 75,000gns at the Breeze Ups in April; unraced dam closely related to Australian 1m2f Listed winner Astronomos, half-sister to Gold Cup winner Courage Mon Ami; preferred by Browne-McMonagle.

Churchill colt; E40,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Spirit Of Saxony (7f AW; RPR 89), Coolmeen Vega (Spanish 7.5f-8.5f) and Pure Circle (Italian 6f); dam unraced half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Jessica's Dream (dam of 1m Group 1 winner Havana Gold); looks yard's second string on rider arrangements.

OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Dubawi colt; half-brother to winners Saxon Warrior (1m inc 2yo/2,000 Guineas; RPR 122), Drumroll (1m/1m2f inc Group 3; 110), More Beautiful (Listed-placed 5f 2yo; 102) and Pavlenko (7f; 91); dam 6f/7f 2yo winner (inc Group 1; 114); top pedigree but Lordan prefers stablemate.

OwnerName: Flaxman Stables, Mrs Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith
Spotlight: Won on debut at Listowel last September (7f, heavy); two of three subsequent starts have come in Group 3s over 1m at Newmarket (sixth of 7 beaten 3.75l) and on latest in first-time hood (retained) over 1m2f at Leopardstown (last of 5 beaten 5.5l); rated 99, so obvious chance on the figures in this lesser race.

Disappointed when a beaten favourite on sole start at two but much better form this year; close second over this C&D (good) on return in May; justified short odds to win an ordinary maiden at Limerick by 6l on latest (1m, good); rated 89 so a fair bit to find with Straight Up, but going the right way and his yard remains in terrific form.

AW maiden winner and Listed second for Charlie Appleby last year; didn't cut much ice in three starts at Meydan for Bhupat Seemar and now makes debut for Andy Slattery; yard looks to have stronger claims with Loughrea, so likely best watched.

Enjoying a good season with wins in handicap at Cork (1m, soft) in May and a rated race at Leopardstown (1m, good) last month; beat Glitter And Glory by half a length the last day and 5lb worse off here, so may not uphold that form.
Definite promise in maidens on the AW over 7f-1m and on latest at Naas (1m, good); could be on a decent mark for her first handicap and worth checking in the market.

Consistent filly rarely runs a bad race; won a maiden at Dundalk in March; not beaten far in Curragh handicap in May; 0.5l behind Johnny Soda in rated race at Leopardstown latest and weighted to turn that around now; big chance.

Won off 13lb lower at Gowran (7f, soft) in April; let backers down when beaten favourite at Leopardstown (7f, sft-hvy) but bounced back last time when a good second at Cork (1m, good); raised 2lb for that and this is a deeper race, so more needed.
Dundalk maiden winner; 0-2 on turf; over 4.25l behind Johnny Soda (and Glitter And Glory) in rated race at Leopardstown on latest; 7lb swing could enable him to close that gap on his handicap debut.

All three handicap runs on good ground have been fine efforts; runner up at Down Royal (7f), won at Fairyhouse (6f, good) and kept on for a 0.5l second back there over 7f on latest; nudged up another 3lb and this is harder, but the step up to 1m could eke out more improvement, so not discounted.

Won a Bellewstown maiden last year (1m, good); okay return in the Madrid at Naas (7f, soft) in March; not as good upped to 1m1f on latest at Leopardstown (soft); drops back in trip now and visor goes on; could rebound on this sounder surface.
Didn't show a whole lot in maidens at 7f-9.5f on varying ground; gelded after mid-field finish at Gowran in May and now makes handicap debut; yard also runs Vishaya; the market can guide.

Maiden has finished second five times, twice at Ballinrobe and three times at Leopardstown; on second start of the season, was backed at latter venue when front-running second over 1m1f; that was on soft to heavy, but he's fine on good; doesn't have to lead, but mightn't be much competition for it here.

Wonderfully durable handicapper with 11 wins to his name, the latest being on his favoured soft ground at Roscommon in September; returned to form at that track two starts back, where he handled good ground, and fine run at Limerick (yielding) last month when narrowly beaten by an in-form 3yo; easier ground would be ideal, but still the one to beat.

OwnerName: Just The Two Of Us Partnership
Spotlight: Latest of five wins was in the 2023 renewal of this event, after which he ran well in a Group 3 at Leopardstown and a Listed contest over C&D; not the same force nowadays and has come down a good bit in the ratings; was still capable of good form last year; not at his best on AW in the spring and back from a break here.

Three wins from eight AW starts, but 0-20 on turf; did well at Dundalk over the winter and carried that form through to soft-ground contests in the spring; second over 7f at Naas and fourth over 1m2f at Leopardstown in April (strong form); not so good lately; likes to come from off the pace.
Won in Poland at two and over 1m1f in France last year; picked up for 9,000 euros in November; 80-1, never a factor over 10.5f on Irish debut at Roscommon.

Had placed form over 6f/7f at three, but sole win came when making all over 1m7f in amateur riders' handicap at Leopardstown last summer; twice runner-up in maiden hurdles as well; this will be too sharp a test on first run since October.

Five wins for Joe Murphy, latest of them over 1m1f at Tipperary in October; big prices and has shown little in four starts for this yard; mark tumbling, but prefers it soft and will be of more interest when the rain returns.

Often made the frame in France, but just a 1m maiden win to his name; picked up out of a claimer in August, a frequent source of recruits to this yard; almost always wore hood/tongue-tie combination; goes without any aids here; long absence and surprise if he's ready to be involved.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Rated 77 after three starts at 1m-9.5f; got to within 4.5l of the smart Sparan Nua on second start at Gowran (9.5f, good); found just one too good when ridden positively in Down Royal maiden on latest (10.5f, soft); may be more to come at this longer trip and this track could suit; chance.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: 0-8 and rated 82; all starts over 1m this season; 1.75l third in Naas maiden on seasonal return on soft; no impact in two stakes races and a handicap subsequently, but back on song in first time blinkers (retained) returned to maiden company at Naas (1m, good); worth a go at this trip and should go well again.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Camelot filly; half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Promo Queen (RPR 72); dam 1m/1m2f AW winner (RPR 73), half-sister to winners Erhaaf (1m2f), Ruzeiz (French 1m3f) and Jasaara (1m2f), out of unraced sister to Arc winner Sakhee; yard in red-hot form; check market.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: 28-1 but far from disgraced on debut at Down Royal (10.5f, soft); open to improvement over this slightly longer trip and dam acted on good ground, so should handle conditions.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Both runs on soft ground; kept on nicely enough on debut at Gowran (9.5f); another solid effort upped to 1m4f at Cork latest when she had Benaki over 5l behind; pedigree suggests she could take another step forward on this quicker ground.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Barnane Stud & Hollywood Stud Ireland Limited
Spotlight: Sent off at 10-1 on debut at Leopardstown (1m5f, gd-fm) and kept on well for a 5.25l fifth of 10; not sure this drop in trip will suit on that evidence, but yard continues in terrific form, so can't be ruled out.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Huge odds and well beaten in both starts; seems safe to rule out.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: No impact at big odds in maidens at Naas and Fairyhouse; will qualify for handicaps after this.

RaceName: Kerry Publicans Summer Race Day Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Promising start when runner up on debut at Limerick (1m4f, yielding); upset in the stalls before running moderately on soft to heavy at Cork (1m4f); could do better back on a sounder surface, but more needed.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: The fact that he was 250-1 for his latest run over 1m at Naas tells you how little he had accomplished prior to that; came from the rear and was going on well in the final furlong when beaten 4.5l; mark looks in the right ballpark on that evidence and half-brother won over this trip in France.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Superbly bred (sibling of three Group 1 winners); 14s for 1m4f maiden at Dundalk, green and off the bridle a long way out, beaten 11l by winner who was placed in a Royal Ascot handicap next time; blinkers added.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Third to Causeway (unbeaten in four starts since, including a Group 2 at Royal Ascot) in 7f Curragh maiden last year; hasn't built on that; mark was put back up 5lb after fair run in valuable event at Gowran last month; all his form is with an ease and scratched because of fast ground last time; steps up in trip by two furlongs.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Third to a smart stablemate over 1m at Limerick (Highwayman well in arrears) and strong at the finish when second over 9.5f at Ballinrobe (third won handicap off 72 since); bred to stay and will take beating.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Third to a pair of mid-80s rivals over 1m2f at Navan, beaten 5l having travelled well; looked a non-stayer over 1m4f last time; headgear.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: U S Navy Flag half-brother to five winners including Preamble (6f 2yo; RPR 88), Sonoran (7f; 85) and Never Rains (1m; 67); family of Goldikova; nice page but best watched.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Sands Of Mali brother to 7.3f winner Sofian, half-brother to four other winners at distances from 7f to 1m6f; dam placed 5f-1m as 2yo.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal of unraced half-sister to winners Primo Lady (5f 2yo Listed), Zhiggy's Stardust (6f) and Lucky Mellor (5f 2yo), out of 6f 2yo winner; certainly not bred for this trip.

OwnerName: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit
Spotlight: Handicapped on AW maiden runs; only beaten 2l over C&D on handicap debut in May; that form is only ordinary, but headgear might help.
Ran well in a nursery and fair runs behind the same horse, Rodeo Drive, in last two outings; seems safely held by Point Cartwright on latest outing at Naas.

Going the right way in handicaps; second over 1m4f at Limerick and looked in need of farther when third to good yardstick Rodeo Blues over this trip at Naas; the long straight here will suit him as he needs time to hit full stride.
Didn't have the best of runs up the straight when keeping on to fair effect at Fairyhouse (1m4f), but still has fair bit to find with both the winner Wipeawayyourtears and third Sir Trancealot.

Only 3-1 for handicap debut at Sligo in May; ran poorly but maybe soft ground was to blame; gelded since and change of headgear; with the stable so powerful in the 3yo category, plenty will afford him another chance.
Placed over 9.5f at Gowran; hard to see him reversing Naas 1m3f form with Point Cartwright.
Finished a head in front of Sir Trancealot when fifth against older horses at Bellewstown (1m4f); that was her best effort in handicaps and might be capable of better.
Creditable sixth in Gowran maiden, but dismal run when 9-1 for handicap debut over 9.5f at Ballinrobe; some middle-distance performers in family.
Placed on easy ground in first two handicaps and then handled good ground when winning well over 1m4f at Fairyhouse; Cleary's claim means she's effectively off the same mark.
Big prices all starts; ran okay over 5f on handicap debut, but well beaten since and going half-a-mile farther today; not bred for this trip either.
Couple of creditable handicap runs over 7f/1m, placed over latter trip behind easy winner at Bellewstown; big step up in trip; dam won over almost this far in France.

Fourth in a Gowran maiden and back to form on second handicap start when staying on to be runner-up to subsequent winner Rodeo Drive at Ballinrobe (9.5f); stayers on dam's side of pedigree, but her best runs have been on easy ground.
Quiet in maidens; half-sister to a pair of winners over 1mf/1m3f; yard has had a couple run well lately.

Third to Wipeawayyourtears over 1m4f at Fairyhouse where he battled on well; 7-2 fav for second handicap start over same trip at Bellewstown, faded out of it in the final furlong; cheekpieces swapped for blinkers.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Did best of the newcomers when an encouraging third on her Goodwood debut (6f, soft) last month; 5-2 for a fast-ground Newbury novice 11 days later and she went down fighting in a close second of ten; looks on a fair mark now switched to a nursery and she's an interesting contender at this new trip.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: Sarah Fern, Patricia James And Partner
Spotlight: Left his debut form well behind when runner-up at Leicester (7f, good) last month and he took another step forward when scoring at Newcastle (7f, AW) 18 days ago; rallied well to beat a clear second in that race and he's open to more progress now switched to a nursery; respected.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: 120,000gns yearling; looked in trouble at the halfway stage at Nottingham (6f, good) in May but she finished well to snatch an unlikely victory at 12-1; found things tough in the Albany at Royal Ascot but she's back in calmer waters here and is open to progress on this step up to 7f; not ruled out on nursery debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Finished down the field in his first two runs but he found improvement when runner-up in a Windsor maiden (6f, good to firm) in May; needs to build on that but he should have more to offer and his dam was a 7f winner as a 2yo.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Nursery Handicap
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: Showed promise in three novice events, most recently when runner-up over an extended 7f at Beverley (good to firm) in May; should have a future in handicaps but opening mark is no gift and she needs to find more after a short break.

Has a patchy record but he turned things around with an 5l win over 1m6f at Southwell (AW; 7-4 favourite) last Sunday; looks well treated under a penalty for that success and his penultimate win was at this track; strong contender.

Dual turf winner at 1m2f and 1m3f for Charlie Johnston last spring; has had some struggles since but he got back to form with a bang when winning by 7l here (1m4f, AW) last Monday; that was off a tumbling mark and he's a major player if he can repeat that form under a penalty.
His last win was 14 months ago but he made some good late headway when runner-up at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) last month; only 1lb higher here and he looks interesting back up in trip.

All four wins have been at Wolverhampton (1m4f-2m) and latest was 11 months ago; finished a close third off 1lb higher at Bath (1m6f, good) last month but he's not easy to predict and was well held with cheekpieces tried at Chester last time; the headgear is quickly discarded but he comes with risks attached.

Made the breakthrough at the 12th attempt when justifying support at Ffos Las (1m4f, good to soft) last month; 4lb higher in different conditions here but he pulled well clear with the runner-up last time and his best form has come on Polytrack; respected back up in trip.

Six-time winner, with four at Windsor (11.4f) and two on Polytrack (1m2f/2m); her last success was off 2lb higher last autumn but she's been disappointing in last two runs and needs to get back on track.

Her first four wins were on turf but she made the breakthrough on AW when finishing well to land an apprentice handicap at Kempton (1m3f) last Wednesday; now steps back up in trip but she beat an odds-on rival last time and escapes a penalty for that success; dangerous.

In and out since winning at Leicester (1m4f, good) in April and he was beaten 8l after a slow start at Newbury last time; now only 1lb higher than for that last success but he needs to raise his game back up in trip.
Well treated on his old form for the Johnstons but he returned after a mammoth absence last month and has been tailed off at big prices in both runs (1m4f/2m) for current yard; lots to prove.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Ran into some trouble before keeping on well when fourth on Yarmouth debut (6f, good) in May; never-dangerous sixth in the Woodcote at Epsom last month but that was a respectable effort from a wide draw and he's open to progress on this step up in trip; in the mix for in-form yard.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 8,000euros yearling; out of a sister to a 7f Group 3 juvenile winner in Germany but he made a low-key start when sixth of eight here (7f, good to firm) in May; should be more streetwise this time but he needs to leave that debut form well behind.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Gelded son of Sea The Moon; 20-1 and he ran green throughout when a well-held fourth at Kempton (7f, AW) two weeks ago; should know more this time but he needs a major step forward on his second start.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Half-sister to French winners Sandon (1m2f 2yo) and Betty Nelson (10.5f); made a promising start when a clear third at Salisbury (6f, soft) and she built on that with a near-miss over 7f at Newcastle (AW) 18 days ago; sets the standard on that form and she's open to more progress; strong contender.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 15,000euros foal, 27,000gns yearling; third foal; half-brother to Italian winners Sayidah Poets (7.5f/1m 2yo) and Siguanaba (6f-1m including 2yo); dam maiden (third 10.5f in France) sister to useful 7f/1m winner Brave Hero, half-sister to US 1m stakes winner Double Touch; yard 1-7 with 2yos this year and market should guide on debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: RPR of 46 when well held in 6f events at Goodwood (soft) and Newbury (good to firm); has a lot to find upped in trip and nurseries will be more suitable.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 19,000euros foal, 24,000euros yearling; half-brother to 1m/1m3f winner Lennox (including AW/Australia; RPR 83); dam 13.3f AW winner (82), half-sister to winners James Garfield (6f 2yo Group 2) and Hallelujah U (useful 1m 2yo/1m4f); drawn out wide on debut and he could be one for later on.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 36,000gns foal, 27,000gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Tannaaf (7f 2yo/1m2f), Danjeu (1m4f) and Waheebah (10.5f-1m6f), out of 1m4f winner; yard 4-10 with 2yos this season and she needs a close look on debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C-D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Tailed off at big prices in his three runs and latest was over this trip at Kempton (AW); hood is now added but he can only be watched.

16-race maiden but he was runner-up three times last season and wasn't beaten far when fifth in new headgear at Kempton (6f, AW) 11 days ago; that was eye-catching from off the pace and he's in the mix back up in trip.

Won 6f maiden at Navan as 2yo; promising stable debut over 6f on this track in February 2025 but he's mostly struggled since; now 27lb below his peak mark but he was laboured over C&D last time and others are preferred.

Two 1m wins on Polytrack this spring, in a Lingfield classified event and a Kempton handicap; finished runner-up three times since her last success including at Wolverhampton (7f) and Ffos Las (1m) in last two runs; up another 2lb but she still looks feasibly treated and has leading claims.

Turf and dual Tapeta winner who justified favouritism with a narrow success at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in March; still looks feasibly treated on that form but he flopped at Bath (1m, firm) last time and needs to bounce back after that heavy defeat.

Can miss the break but he's on a dangerous mark and was runner-up here (7f, good) in May before a respectable fourth at Chepstow last month; this return to AW is no problem and he has each-way claims.
Looks interesting on some runner-up efforts (6f/7f) last summer but he was quiet during the winter including over 7f on Polytrack; record is now 0-12 and he has something to prove after 155 days off.

Seven-time AW winner and his last success was over 6f here last October; now 2lb lower than for that win but has a mixed record since and was never involved at Wolverhampton last Monday; comes with risks attached and he needs to raise his game back up in trip; cheekpieces reapplied.

OwnerName: G E Bassett & P R Chamings
Spotlight: Defied this mark at Salisbury (6f, heavy) last September but she's had only two runs since and has struggled in both of them; still unexposed on AW but she needs a major revival on her second run after break.

Triple turf winner who is 1-19 on AW; perked up with a close call at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in December but he's not come close to that form in three subsequent runs; may have needed his Nottingham reappearance last month but his tactical speed will be tested back at this trip and others are more convincing.

All wins at 6f on ground ranging from good to soft to heavy, the last three in 2024; well handicapped on his old form but he's been tailed off in three runs (1m, AW/good to firm) for new yard this year and has a lot to prove

Showed similar level of fair form on all four 2yo starts, most recently when fourth on nursery debut at Chelmsford (7f) in December; might have more to offer back at 6f this year but has fitness to prove after seven-month break.
Unexposed 3yo who displayed improvement when close second in 5f novice here in December; makes handicap debut after seven-month absence but solid market support will bring her into the equation.
Flopped badly when odds-on at Wolverhampton (7f) in May and now has a point to prove but scored over C&D on handicap debut in March and could still have a lot more to offer if back on song.
Scored off reduced mark at Yarmouth (6f, soft) last month and also ran well when third at Newmarket (5f, good) ten days later; suited by his C&D and can make her presence felt again.

OwnerName: Mildmay Racing Pl & Only Fillies Rc
Spotlight: Seemed to benefit from combination of drop back in trip and new cheekpieces when scoring at Goodwood (6f, soft) last month; recent 5lb rise asks quite a lot of her but this return to Polytrack won't be a problem.

Six-race maiden who ended last season with a disappointing 7f run and made a low-key return over that trip last month; might have needed that recent outing but others in this line-up have much less to prove.

Runner-up over 7f on all three 2yo starts but was last of ten when upped to 1m for last month's seasonal/handicap debut; tries sprinting with a tongue-tie added today; not an easy one to weigh up at the moment.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: 0-13 but she went close at Nottingham (8.3f, good) in April and wasn't beaten far after missing the break in a Windsor handicap (1m, good to firm) last month; has claims if things go more to plan this time and she's respected back on AW.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Went close at Wolverhampton (8.6f) and Chepstow (1m) before getting off the mark (13th attempt) in a classified event at Wolverhampton last Tuesday; pulled 5l clear with his main market rival in that race and he hit a personal best with that brave success; key player.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: In-form 4yo who has been placed at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in his last two starts including when runner-up in a handicap last Monday; usually comes from off the pace and has claims if he gets the breaks.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Dual AW winner who was placed on turf here (1m2f) last month but he flopped on his drop back to 1m at Bath (started slowly) 12 days ago; needs to bounce back after that and he's probably more potent over longer trips these days.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Both wins have come over C&D but latest was in January 2025; finished third over 7f here in March but he's struggled in both subsequent runs and others are preferred.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Won a classified event over C&D in January but he's not hit the same level since and was out the back at Brighton (1m2f) last month; now drops back in trip but he's not easy to predict and overall record is 1-18; others are more convincing.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Two respectable efforts on AW (1m2f/9.4f) this year but he struggled in a classified event here (1m4f) when last seen in March; gelded since but he's now 0-9 and needs a major revival back in trip after a break.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: 12-race maiden who has been struggling to get competitive this year and has been well held in both runs for current yard; down the list.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Beaten 14l or more in three novice events (6f/7f) last season, with a best RPR of 29; now switches to a classified event but she needs lots of improvement upped to 1m on her return.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div I)
Spotlight: Disappointing favourite on debut last May and she's finished tailed off again in both subsequent runs; now drops into a 0-50 event but she needs a transformation back on AW.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: 3yo filly who posted a better effort with cheekpieces added when runner-up in a C&D handicap last Monday; was beaten around 4l in that race but she's still unexposed and has claims if she can build on that.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a 7f handicap here (AW) for Michael Attwater last summer but that is his sole win from 26 starts and he's generally struggled since; comes with risks attached after 165 days off but he needs checking in market for new yard.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Off the mark at the 13th attempt when landing a Bath handicap (1m, good) last month; that was a surprise at 33-1 but she responded well to new equipment and hit a personal best with that comfortable success; big player again if she can back that up.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: 15-race maiden who has been out of sorts over hurdles and on the Flat this year; hasn't been with Tony Carroll for long but he was beaten 8l in a classified event at Brighton (1m2f) last week and others are preferred; stablemate of Masqool.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has yet to be placed and he was tailed off in an Epsom handicap (1m2f, good) 12 days ago; visor is now added and he needs a major revival back in trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Five-time AW winner including over C&D; his last success was 17 months ago but he returned after a short break with an eye-catching fourth in a Wolverhampton handicap (6f, Tapeta) last week; didn't get much luck in that race and he's in the mix back up in trip; visor reapplied.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Just three wins from 64 starts but he returned after a short break with a near-miss in a classified at Brighton (7f, good) last month; has possibilities back up in trip and his first two wins came on AW at Lingfield.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: 11-time AW winner including four at this track (7f/1m2f); has a patchy record this year but he came from the rear to finish runner-up in a classified event at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last Tuesday; should get a decent tow into this race and he's respected back on Polytrack.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Virgin 512 Classified Stakes (Div II)
Spotlight: Struggled in maiden/novice events including two tailed-off efforts at massive prices this summer; now drops into a 0-50 event but she needs a transformation at this new trip.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who started off for new stable with two respectable runs; no match for easy winner when second in C&D selling handicap a week ago but has each-way claims again.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Launched handicap career with two thirds off basment mark in April (9.4f/1m4f) and didn't run badly when fifth of eigth at Wolverhampton (1m4f) last month but needs to take a step forward to get a win on the board.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Beaten 19l at Ffos Las (1m2f) three weeks ago but won in this grade at Wolverhampton (1m4f) in March and was placed on turf here (1m2f) two starts ago; a possible if back on song.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden; second in Wolverhampton handicap (9.4f) on seasonal debut in April and put two lesser efforts behind him when sixth there (8.6f) last month but needs to find something extra over this new trip; wore blinkers or cheekpieces for last five races but goes without headgear today.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Has 1-54 career strike-rate, the win gained over hurdles in 2022; this year's Flat handicap form is uninspiring but he'll appreciate this ease in grade and is worth a glance in the betting.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Struggled on turf 12 days ago and remains without a win since September 2024 but this C&D suits him well and the pick of this year's form gives him each-way claims.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Regressive 6yo who has not run on the Flat since midfield finish in 1m6f Wolverhampton handicap in April; ran okay in two handicap hurdles last month but others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Five-race maiden; looked suited by big hike up in trip when staying-on fifth of 13 in this grade at Windsor (11.4f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) on last month's seasonal debut but suffered heavy defeat in 1m4f Epsom handicap 12 days ago; needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
OwnerName: Aura Racing, Dave Brown, Joe Lane
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in 1m Kempton handicap on Wednesday and now 0-12; unexposed over 1m4f but it's questionable whether the refitted cheekpieces will make much difference, and he remains difficult to enthuse over.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Didn't show a great deal on the Flat for Jack Channon last year (unplaced all six runs) but came second in two juvenile hurdles for new stable in December; absent since January but still looks quite interesting in this basement grade on Flat return.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Well beaten on latest Flat start in May (2m, turf) but has been placed in two AW handicaps here this year (1m4f/2m) and didn't run badly over hurdles last month; swaps cheekpieces for first-time visor; more appealing than most.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Unexposed after only four runs but was in rear throughout and beaten over 15l when upped to 11.6f and fitted with cheekpieces (retained) for handicap debut here (turf) in May.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Soundly beaten all four starts, most recently when 80-1 outsider in this grade at Ffos Las (1m2f, good) three weeks ago; needs first-time cheekpieces to have miraculous effect.

RaceName: Racing To School Reaches 250,000 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Probably fortunate when left clear to score at Stratford (2m3f, good to firm) last month but readily followed up in four-runner C&D (good) contest ten days ago and is evidently in prime form.

RaceName: Racing To School Reaches 250,000 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Maiden who is not living up to her pedigree but seemed to enjoy front-running role and ran best race yet when clear second of ten in Worcester handicap chase (2m, good) 12 days ago; must be considered after that effort.

RaceName: Racing To School Reaches 250,000 Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: Made all in mares' maiden hurdle here (2m1f, good) last summer and came second on her next three appearances; absent since October but her 12lb weight-for-age allowance will be handy on this chasing debut.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Useful on the Flat at his best and made the frame in two maiden hurdles in April 2025 (both 2m, good); this is his first run since and he doesn't set a daunting form standard but, nevertheless, he's a big player.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bought for £4,000 after finishing third of nine in good-ground Irish point on debut in April; not an easy one to weigh up but has joined a top stable for rules career; tongue-tie on; market helpful.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of an unraced sister to high-class chaser Nacarat; made low-key rules debut when well-held fourth at Bangor (2m3f, good) last month but was placed in an Irish point in May and is open to improvement.

RaceName: Sun Racing The Home Of Racing Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in both bumpers last year but her fourth of six over hurdles at Fontwell (2m3f, good) last month was more encouraging and further progress is possible.

Still chasing a first British success since his French 2023 hurdle win and, while he has posted a few placed efforts, he has failed to quite see out his races over trips beyond 2m; another 3lb lower but needs to raise his game.
Off the mark at the 25th attempt with a comfortable C&D success ten days ago; a 3lb rise looks fair and a repeat would make him a major player, though his overall profile remains patchy.

Dual Flat winner (1m2f/1m4f) who had shown little over hurdles until stepped up in trip when a near 6l third behind Saucats over C&D ten days ago; meets that rival on 4lb better terms and enters calculations if building on that performance.

Kept on for third on handicap hurdle debut at Ffos Las (2m, good) in May but may not have stayed 2m4f when below that form last month; tongue-tie is fitted and this drop back in trip could see him in a better light.
Two creditable efforts since returning from a short break, latest when a close fourth over 2m1f here last month off this mark; stays this far and enters calculations.

Returned from a break to register a third career success here (2m1f, good) last month, under Eleanor Williams; remains competitively treated with 3lb rise and capable of another bold showing if reproducing that performance.

Best effort when switched to a left-handed track for a 2m5f handicap at Fontwell in May last time and, although this drop back in trip is not an obvious plus, he remains unexposed and open to improvement.

Well treated on peak form but has struggled since returning from wind surgery and finished a well-beaten last of three over 3m2f here in May when he may not have stayed; this much shorter trip and first-time blinkers may help but he arrives with plenty to prove.
Triple hurdle winner who has dropped to an attractive mark but for good reason; tailed off over further here last month and, although this shorter trip is a plus, she has plenty to prove.

18-race maiden; twice a runner-up over C&D last summer; failed to hit those heights since but shaped with more encouragement on last month's return over C&D, as though the run was needed, and remains on a competitive mark if able to build on that effort.

Completed a spring double last year off 8lb higher; struggled for form after returning in December from a break but fared better when back to front-running tactics at Stratford (extended 2m2f; good to firm) a fortnight ago and a repeat of that effort would put him in the mix.
Has struggled outside bumpers; pulled up twice before trailing home last of eight most recently; hard to fancy.

13-race maiden, including three runs over fences, but showed more when travelling well for a long way last time, failing to stay 3m2f; needs to build on that and the drop back in trip is a plus; usually claimer ridden but not today.

Dual chase winner who has been more miss than hit since scoring in May last year; recent hurdle run in a first-time visor (retained) offered little encouragement, but he returns to fences off a handy mark; needs to rediscover his spark.

Only one win from 18 runs over fences but he tends to run his race and has plenty of placed efforts to his name; below par when third off this mark over C&D last month and now wears a refitted tongue-tie; could quickly return to form.

Made it 2-4 for this stable when winning a 2m1f handicap hurdle here in May and followed that with a respectable second off 5lb higher back here a fortnight later; has plenty in his favour on this chase debut.

Irish hunter chase winner who has gone very close on both starts for this stable, beaten a neck at Fontwell (3m2f, good) and by a short head at Market Rasen (2m7f, good) nine days later; up in class but looks to have a strong chance of doubling his chase tally.

Multiple hurdle/chase winner but he's lost his way since a 2m4f success at Ffos Las (good to soft) last June and has offered little in three runs this season; well treated on his best form but with plenty to prove.

Good-ground specialist with nine chase wins to his name, latest off 2lb higher in July last year; solid front-running third at Bangor (2m4f, good) 17 days ago after a break and he holds leading claims.

Showed the benefit of a fourth wind operation when winning over hurdles at Fontwell (2m3f, good) in May and only narrowly denied by an in-form Skelton horse off 3lb higher two weeks later; below par since but he returns to fences on a competitive mark.

Encouraging first two efforts for this stable before a lesser run prompted wind surgery; got back on track to some extent at Bangor (3m, good) last month and, while more is needed, he's likely to have a say.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Hinted at ability in two bumpers last season, most recently when staying on into fifth at Chepstow in April; likely to be seen in a better light over hurdles, particularly when tackling longer trips.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £27,000 purchase after winning her sole Irish point in May and made the perfect start for her new stable when landing a C&D bumper last month (first-time hood; retained); had plenty in hand and she's a leading contender under her penalty.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Justified support when making a winning C&D debut (good to soft) in June, keeping on well despite showing signs of inexperience; more on his plate under a penalty but he's entitled to improve; should be thereabouts.
