RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well-related 4yo who was sent off 8-1 for his debut at Fontwell in May but finished a well-beaten seventh of nine; likely to have benefited from the run though much more is needed.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £5,000 purchase as a 3yo; half-brother to winners Lofgren (useful 2m-3m1f hurdle/chase) and Gatacre Street (2m7f chase); newcomer from a respected stable and worth monitoring in the market.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed more when a 9l sixth of seven at Stratford (2m, good to firm) on second start, though the form is open to question in a steadily run race; improvement is still required.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Second foal; out of an unraced dam closely related to winners Urbanist (2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Lilting Verse (bumper/2m5f hurdle/2m7f chase), likely to benefit from this debut and looks one for the longer term.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to useful winners Perfect Attitude (point/2m hurdle), Dragon Bones (bumper/2m4f-3m hurdle) and Flawless Escape (bumper/2m4f hurdle); pedigree offers encouragement but only of interest if attracting support.

RaceName: Savanna Stevens Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 2 Elimination) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: J L Lightfoot- D V Gardner
Spotlight: Half-sister to winners Bagheera Ginge (2m2f-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Champ Is Real (3m chase); makes debut and bred to have a future but likely one for further down the line.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Close third of seven in Nottingham nursery (5f; first-time visor, now discarded) last time; the most exposed contender in this field, as a record of 1-8 reflects (gained the win in seller); however, he's the solid angle, being a consistent sort.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Thrice-raced colt who made all for 2l success in five-runner maiden at Goodwood (5f, good) most recently; looks all about speed and warrants respect in another sharp scenario with further improvement plausible.

RaceName: Join Fitzdares For A Personal Service Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: 360,000euros breeze-up purchase who has substance to go with her promise; finished third to smart rival (subsequent Group 2 scorer) on debut and the form of her Lingfield success (5f, good to firm) has received a few boosts; looks likely to do well in nurseries.

Too Darn Hot gelding who showed abundant promise with fast-finishing third of 12 under 3lb claimer in 1m AW maiden at Southwell; the form has received a couple of boosts and he is open to progress; major player.

OwnerName: Gestuet Schlenderhan Gmbh U Co Kg
Spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has consistent 1m/1m2f form and managed to run creditably despite traffic issues at Salisbury most recently, albeit couldn't finish the job once finding a gap; has solid place claims back over 1m.

45,000euros foal, 135,000gns yearling; Teofilo half-brother to three winners, notably Alashar (Listed-placed 7f/9.5f; RPR 101); dam 6f-1m winner (including Group 3; 112); wears tongue-tie on debut; market instructive.

OwnerName: J Collins, P Moorhead, C & J Cooley
Spotlight: Showed a plucky attitude to nose ahead in the final strides at Nottingham (1m, good) and build on his debut promise; open to further improvement and warrants respect even with a 5lb penalty.
Placed in sole German start; less solid on his two British efforts and may find better opportunities in handicaps soon.
Has something to find on her 7f efforts and therefore needs improvement for this extra furlong; handicaps may be her ideal scene.
Seems one for handicaps on the bare form of his two runs; however, may show improvement back down in trip, having seemed a non-stayer over 1m2f at Windsor latest.
Always behind in C&D contest having been friendless in the betting; bred to need longer trips.
Recorded only a modest RPR over C&D last time and needs to take another step forward; may be more interesting in handicaps shortly.
Registered only a meagre RPR in 7f AW maiden at Wolverhampton and is probably one for handicaps later on.

£72,000 yearling; Dark Angel sister to three winners, notably Diabolic (US 5f-6.5f turf/dirt 2yo including Listed); dam Group-placed 5f 2yo winner (97); judged solely on breeding, this newcomer has a doubt over the 1m trip.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 100,000gns foal, 160,000euros yearling; by Mehmas and first foal of 6f/7f winner (including Group 3; RPR 113); stable's record of 2-8 with 2yos in 2026 includes a successful newcomer this month; interesting.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 72,000gns foal, 260,000gns yearling; Perfect Power half-brother to three winners, notably Chalk Mountain (multiple 7f AW; RPR 91) and Malvern (6f 2yo; 79); dam 5f winner (95); notable connections; respected debutant.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed some promise with sixth of 11 at Salisbury (6f, soft) despite proving green, losing a shoe and perhaps even finding the ground softer than ideal; interesting with improvement on the cards.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Showed promise with fourth of ten in Newbury event (6f, good to firm) that saw the only runners with experience filled the first two places; open to progress and is one of the main contenders.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 18,000gns yearling; Ardad half-brother to four winners, notably Astronomy's Choice (7f 2yo; RPR 96); dam unplaced 1m (57); wears hood on debut; yard is 1-6 with 2yos this term.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: May benefit from the faster surface but needs to improve plenty on his Salisbury soft-ground effort.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: 3,500euros yearling, 16,000euros breeze-up 2yo; by Space Traveller and first foal of 7f/1m winner (RPR 79); one of several newcomers in the field; market instructive.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Came from further back than the first two in 6f AW maiden at Kempton, finishing third of ten; helps to set the form standard and there is a chance he can show progress to boot.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Has weak claims on form and is likely to be more at home in modest nurseries shortly.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Has plenty to find on Goodwood soft-ground debut and is a similar type to Goodwood Meteor in that she may improve for this quicker surface.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Both starts over C&D, better effort when running-on fifth of 11 latest; difficult to rule out another step forward but he looks the type who'll be more interesting in nurseries shortly.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)
Spotlight: Due Diligence gelded half-brother to 6f 2yo winner Q T Pie (RPR 67), out of 1m2f winner (72); sibling gained the success over C&D, albeit second time out; check the betting.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Mehmas colt; dam 6f AW 2yo winner (RPR 84), out of 7f-winning half-sister to useful winners Kehaar (7f-1m) and All For Laura (5f 2yo; dam of 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Misheer); in good hands; interesting debutant.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Twice-raced colt who ran better than the bare result (seventh of nine) suggests at Newbury (6.5f) last time, albeit unable to make his experience count in a race where all of his rivals were newcomers; the form pick in this field.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: 175,000euros foal, 450,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; Blue Point half-brother to Croupier (7f/1m winner, including 2yo; RPR 108) and two pretty useful winners; dam unraced sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Saamidd; holds a couple of major entries; notable connections; respected newcomer.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Related to several winners for his connections; never landed a blow in 5f event at Salisbury (finished sixth of nine) but may show improvement upped in distance with the experience under his belt.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Has weak claims on form and is likely to be more at home in modest nurseries shortly.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Only eighth of 11 at Salisbury (6f) but seemed to need the run and looks open to improvement; that race has thrown up a few winners since.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Only eighth of 13 in C&D maiden won by useful sort but appeared to need the outing and may be capable of better.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
Spotlight: Only ninth of 12 in C&D maiden but is evidently thought capable of much better, having been backed into favouritism; one to bear in mind, especially if the money again comes for him.

RaceName: Ambrose Loves Reggae EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)
OwnerName: Mr And Mrs B V Sangster
Spotlight: May benefit from the return to turf but seems one for nurseries, having finished out of the money in both starts; debut form has admittedly received a few boosts.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has a record of 2-7 over C&D (both wins on good to firm) and although not in quite the same form this year as she was last summer, such as when going close off 13lb higher on this track, she wasn't far away back here two starts ago; no surprise to see her go well.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Losing turf run goes back over two years but his last three wins on grass have come over C&D, including on good to firm, and he ran another good race on this track two weeks ago; has better claims than many.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Multiple winner but turf successes are thin on the ground now, just one in over three years; however, he's been running up to his best since back on grass, going down narrowly in a higher grade at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) in May and another solid effort there last time; thereabouts.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Win haul includes two C&D wins but he missed all of 2025, was beaten a long way on his return from an absence and then failed to finish at Newbury in June; has it all to prove with refitted blinkers added to regular hood.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Made a successful 2yo debut but he was soon in deep water and his form deteriorated; seen only twice in 2025 but things have stabilised somewhat and he's run two good races over C&D (good to firm), including 16 days ago; not eye-wateringly well handicapped but shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Hooded in three juvenile starts but not as 3yo, returning with a quite convincing win on handicap debut over C&D (good to firm) from one who won either side of that race; unable to improve on that at Newmarket next time when 7lb higher but she was tight for room at one stage and still has potential.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: 5f winner here; she was involved in tight finishes at Bath (extended 5f; good to soft and heavy) last October and over 5f here in May but more often than not she tends to run out of steam over this trip.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Majority of her turf wins have come at Bath and Chepstow, most recently when making all on the former track in June, but she's a C&D winner too (good to firm) and she lost her chance early when back on this course 16 days ago; on a career-high mark but not ruled out.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Two sprint wins in Ireland as 2yo, including when making all at the Curragh (6f, good) last August, but  in poor form on turf and the AW in Britain this year and it's hard to believe he's worth his current mark.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Has run well over C&D in maiden and novice events but her win came over 5f on Southwell AW and she has yet to cut the mustard in handicaps; her mark is sliding slowly but she needs more for the win.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Made all/most to take two 6f races last August/September (on good and heavy) but this year has got off to a terrible start with crushing defeats at Newmarket and Chester; risky for now; yard also runs An Outlaw's Grace.

RaceName: Fitzdares Sprint Series Handicap (Windsor Sprint Series Qualifier)
Spotlight: Unraced as 2yo but her Newbury debut was promising last term and she followed it with an Ayr maiden win (6f, good to firm); seen only twice more that year, ending with a low-key run in Listed event, and she was beaten a long way at Newmarket on her return from eight months off; however, she showed a glimmer of hope before dropping out and Tom Marquand has his first ride of 2026 for the yard (2-9 in the last five years); trainer won this in 2024.

Novice winner for previous yard; 0-6 for current stable but ran encouragingly, despite being left with plenty to do, in last two starts; caught the attention of the stewards at Ffos Las (1m) latest; interesting with Oisin Murphy up for first time, assuming the cards drop right.
Prevailed by a neck at Sandown (1m, good) last time, registering a first success on turf; back up 3lb but remains well treated on the pick of his AW form; gained his other wins back-to-back.

Steadily progressive colt who has form figures of 21423121 (turf/AW) since handicapping; comfortably justified favouritism dropped back to 1m at Southwell eight days ago; 1-1 at Windsor (1m2f win) and holds solid claims under a 6lb penalty returned to this course.

Has a better strike-rate on AW than turf and remains on a career-high mark, so seems up against it; however, consistent record (12233) at Windsor includes a good effort (neck second) in this contest last year; solid contender back here.

Raced only four times, winning 1m AW event then failing to progress over 1m2f on turf; beaten long way last time and needs to derive major benefit from this drop back in trip, combined with first-time headgear.

Made a successful AW debut on final start for previous yard but has failed to back up the form kept to that sphere for new connections; returns from a layoff and reverts to turf; follow the market signals.
Successful for two previous yards and ran creditably at Ayr on debut for current stable; below par in two starts since but likely found 6f too sharp latest; returns to a more suitable distance.

Seems best when fresh and duly ran well (close second) at Bath on seasonal debut; appeared to be a disappointing favourite at Epsom later in April but that effort is excusable with the race likely coming sooner than ideal; only attempt under Tom Marquand resulted in sole win (off current mark to boot); respected on return from 11-week break.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Record is only 1-19 but the success, at Nottingham, is among his recent form and puts him 1-1 under Oisin Murphy; ran creditably a few days later and remains a consistent sort; rested over five weeks since that last run; solid claims.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Prevailed narrowly at Newbury last time, taking record to 5-28 and confirming that he's well suited by fast ground; back up 3lb but remains well handicapped on peak form and is a dual C&D winner; good third in this race last year; one of the main players.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing I and Partner
Spotlight: Has gained both wins on AW but that was the case with stablemate Cristo (declared 7:50) until he recently prevailed on turf; has posted inconsistent results since returned to this sphere, however, and isn't a solid option.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: P Afia, E Hobson, A Pegley, C&r Russell
Spotlight: Creditable third in both starts since making a successful reappearance; remains in form but is getting no respite from the assessor, as another 1lb rise reflects; has done all her winning off marks in the 60s.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Commenced handicap career with a five-timer but a record of 0-4 since reflects that her ceiling has likely been reached; sole effort this term (folded tamely, well beaten) leaves a question mark over her current form.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Low-mileage filly whose solid record features two wins over 1m, including at Windsor; finished nicely for third at Newmarket on seasonal debut and looks ready for a crack at tonight's longer distance; remains open to further progress; highly respected.

RaceName: Download The Fitzdares App Now Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Successful for two previous yards but holds weak claims on his efforts for current stable; campaigned mostly at about 1m and has enough to prove back up in trip; blinkers replace the cheekpieces worn last time.

Yet to make any significant further progress since winning nursery debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in November but recent third of five here (11.4f, good to firm) was perfectly respectable and he may benefit from this drop back in trip.

Three-time AW winner (1m-1m2f) who returned to turf with creditable third in competitive Sandown handicap (1m, good) last month; competes off same mark here and could still have untapped potential over this longer trip; good chance.

Slightly disappointing when well-held fourth at Goodwood (1m4f, good) last month but held on gamely to make winning turf debut here (11.4f, good to firm) in May and might still have a bit more to offer if combination of drop back in trip and first-time cheekpieces aids her cause.

Four-race maiden who was placed at Kempton (7f, AW) last summer; made little impact over that C&D on recent seasonal reappearance but this hike up in trip is an interesting move for her handicap debut, and the booking of Oisin Murphy adds further intrigue.

Absent since safely held in Sandown novice in April but made promising seasonal debut when third at Yarmouth (also 1m2f, good) a fortnight earlier and this well-bred filly is open to considerable improvement in handicaps.

RaceName: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Placed at Goodwood and Nottingham (beaten a short head by last week's Group 2 winner Senorita Bonita) before finishing 6.25l 14th in the Queen Mary; sound chance now down in grade; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
Spotlight: 100,000euros yearling by Blackbeard; first foal; dam French Listed-placed 5.5f/6f winner; has no easy task on debut.

RaceName: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Home-bred filly by Harry Angel; closely related to 6f 2yo winner Deb's Delight and half-sister to three other winners; dam 6f 2yo winner (including a Listed race); interesting newcomer.

RaceName: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Having first run at 5f, but has run two ordinary races so far (6f/7f) and likely to be outclassed.

OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Has found her level in 5f handicaps of late, getting off the mark in quite taking style at Ripon (good; chief victim has won since) before having a troubled run at Musselburgh recently; also has form on a stiff track and should be able to plunder something else off her current mark.

Improved form when second on handicap debut at Catterick but she played up before the start of a C&D race since (joint-favourite; Jack Rabbit Slims second) and failed to come up with much; now has something to prove.

Opening mark suffered from one good run as 2yo and although she's dropped a few pounds this year she didn't find as much as the second Jack Rabbit Slims and third Power Of Chora over C&D recently; shade more needed.

Maiden after 12 starts but he has gone close in two C&D handicaps (good to firm) this year and to his credit that he nearly got back up three weeks ago, in this headgear combination; in the thick of it.

Failed to progress as a 2yo and was well held in sprint handicaps on the AW and turf for previous yard; however, he shaped nicely on his debut for this one when third at Catterick (6f, good to firm) in June so now he's of more interest again.

OwnerName: Mrs Polly Gibson & Miss Pollyanne Gibson
Spotlight: Two siblings won Listed and Group 2 sprints; clear evidence of ability at York (5f, good to firm) last May, behind rivals who are way above this bracket; off for 11 months after and he didn't show much on his return at Doncaster in April but his AW run since (first-time cheekpieces, retained) was better; no surprise if he goes well on handicap debut.

Early promise for previous yard but didn't progress and it's been a struggle off high marks since switched to handicaps; dropping steadily in the weights and down in grade so may do better; has worn a hood, first-time cheekpieces today.

Fairly routine form in her first handicaps but she is more favourably treated now and the drop to 5f seemed to suit when coming from off the pace to finish close up behind Jack Rabbit Slims over C&D (good to firm; in this headgear combination); can take a hand again.

RaceName: Malcolm Greenslade Doncaster LVA Stalwart Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: AW maiden winner in February (8.5f); placed in decent handicaps at York (1m2f, good) and Epsom (1m2f, soft) on last two starts; has made the running; chance now down in grade.

RaceName: Malcolm Greenslade Doncaster LVA Stalwart Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Best of his three runs when neck second to a useful prospect at Hamilton last time (1m1f, good to firm); interesting on handicap debut.

RaceName: Malcolm Greenslade Doncaster LVA Stalwart Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark in an 8.5f maiden here last time (good; third has won since); weakened when tried over 9.5f on AW as a 2yo but may yet improve over this sort of trip (dam won over 1m2f).

RaceName: Malcolm Greenslade Doncaster LVA Stalwart Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 7f Thirsk maiden last July (good); hasn't progressed since going handicapping and was beaten nearly 5l over 1m at Thirsk last time (good); may improve at 1m2f.
Hasn't won for more than two years, but sound effort when length second over C&D in May (good to firm); each-way chance.

Four-time course winner at up to 8.5f, latest success being in April (8.5f, good to firm); fair form since including when 2l fourth here last time (7.5f, good to firm); tries this trip for first time.

Two wins last season, the first a dead-heat over C&D in July (good to firm); rather patchy form this season, best run when third at Pontefract in April (1m, good); a possible on second run back after wind op.
Unexposed 3yo; ordinary form over 7f; likely to improve significantly up in trip (dam a Group-placed 1m2f winner) and should go well on handicap debut.
Has won six races here, from 7.5f to 1m4f, the latest last August (1m4f, good to firm); creditable third here in May (8.5f, good) but below-par last two runs; could go well.
Progressive 3yo; has done well since tried in an eyeshield with wins over this trip at Leicester (good) and Wetherby (good to firm); 4lb higher; one to consider.
Won over 1m2f at Newcastle in March, and creditable third over 8.5f here last time (good); each-way chance
12-race maiden; creditable third to a pair of subsequent winners at Carlisle last time (1m1f, good); conditions suit; should go well.
18-race maiden; second over 8.5f here last month, but most of her form this term is fairly ordinary; others appeal more.
Has run two good races this season, last time getting off the mark on turf at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm); each-way chance.
2-39 on turf, but creditable fourth at Ayr last week (6f, good); worth considering.

3lb lower than when winning at Catterick in April (5f, good); mainly disappointing since, although was only 2l behind Barmyblade (5lb worse off) when third at Thirsk last time (6f, good to firm); well drawn and a possible.
Ex-Ewan Whillans; sound run on stable debut when second at Wetherby last time (5.5f, good to firm); each-way chance.
Has won six times on the AW, but 0-15 on turf; creditable second at Wetherby two runs back (5.5f, good to firm) but well beaten last time (6f).
22-race maiden; third in a Leicester classified two runs ago (cheekpieces on first time) but well beaten last time.
Dual C&D winner last season (soft/good to firm); not been in quite such good form this time, but is on a good mark as a result; wide draw not ideal.
5f Southwell winner in April, but last of 12 at Hamilton on latest start (5f, good); bit to prove.
Winner at Newcastle last November, but disappointing since; blinkers now tried (well beaten in a visor last time); plenty to prove at present.
Four wins over 5f/6f last season; back to winning ways at Thirsk last time (6f, good to firm; cheekpieces back on); not ideally drawn but a contender off 4lb higher.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Tough type who has been on the go since last December, winning at Nottingham and just touched off at Leicester (both at about 1m), but seemingly suited by the shortest trip he's tackled this year when making all over C&D 11 days ago; back up 3lb; can go well again.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Good run of form in May and June once hooded, finishing runner-up in two 7f handicaps before getting off the mark at Wetherby (1m, good to firm); ran out of steam after failing to settle at Nottingham (about 1m) 12 days ago but this test, in a less competitive race, should suit better.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: All races at 5f and he hasn't achieved much in either handicap, for previous yard and this one; some encouragement in pedigree for a step up in trip but probably best watched even so.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Unraced as 2yo; useful start when a close third of six at Redcar (6f) in April but less convincing since and her effort petered out when upped to 7f at Musselburgh on handicap debut; still early days but work to do for now.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
OwnerName: Buist, Shields, Thompson And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Three wins in 2025 included two over C&D (good to firm); this is a belated return by his standards but he has won after a break and would be very competitive if reasonably straight for his comeback.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Faded over C&D on his yard debut following a break and has been kept to 6f since, improving for the fitting of cheekpieces when winning at Ripon (good) in May; in nothing like the same form in two starts since, though.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho
Spotlight: Well held in two AW runs, including on handicap debut at Newcastle (7f) 19 days ago but she had run quite promisingly, despite being beaten about 7l, on her reappearance at Thirsk last month and ought to have some potential in ordinary handicaps.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Won 6f maiden on Newcastle AW as 2yo for previous yard but has found it a mighty struggle since, without and with headgear (first-time visor today); has much to prove over this stiff test and needs more weight off back.

OwnerName: Nicholas Fahy/Mrs Orla Fahy/Two Sheets to the Wind Syndicate
Spotlight: Sent off favourite on debut in a Thurles bumper and found just one too good; failed to build on that when tailed off at Punchestown (reportedly made a respiratory noise) and tongue-tie goes on for hurdles debut after six months off; market can guide.

0-8 over hurdles but has ability and was a good second in a mares' maiden hurdle over C&D (good) on penultimate; not as good upped to 2m6f on latest; the drop back in trip is a plus here but rated 88 so probably place claims at best.

Point-to-point winner; not beaten far when third in a mares' maiden hurdle at Navan (2m, heavy) in November (close up behind useful yardstick Switch From Diesel); didn't quite get home over 2m4f (good) here last time so this shorter trip should suit; tongue-tie goes on; big chance.

Point-to-point winner; 0-14 over hurdles but loads of placed efforts to her name, including off 92 in a C&D handicap on penultimate; should go well but rated 95 and likely vulnerable to less exposed rivals; new headgear combination tried.
Soundly beaten on debut in a Cork bumper in May; marginally better when beaten 14l in another bumper at Roscommon latest; major improvement required on first start over timber.
Failed to win in seven point-to-point starts; inauspicious start over hurdles at Punchestown (21.5f, gd-yld) when 33-1, weakening quickly; much more needed dropped in trip.

4yo; promise in academy hurdles in the winter on testing ground; modest fourth in 4yo maiden at Roscommon (2m, yld-sft) on return from break last month, earning a rating of 104; half-sister to two good ground winners so could improve for this surface and leading chance.
1-6 in point-to-points but has failed to complete on four occasions, including when pulling up the last twice; can only be watched on hurdling debut.
Represents a top yard and sent off at just 7-2 on debut in a Leopardstown bumper in March but never landed a blow and was beaten 36l; bred to have a future over jumps and too soon to write off.
No sign of winning potential in three maiden runs at 2m-17.5f; tongue tie now goes on; seems safe to rule out on return from 119 days off.

OwnerName: Claire Wylie & Mrs Orla Cleary-O'Kane & Mark McGuckin
Spotlight: Placed in a point-to-point; respectable first two runs over hurdles and in a bumper at this venue but may be seen to better effect when tackling handicaps.

A maiden under both codes and now exposed over hurdles, being 0-14; however, he's finished runner-up on seven occasions and, although below his useful best in 2026, he has the strongest form claims in this weak a maiden.
Has looked limited in going 0-5 in points and makes no obvious appeal on rules debut.
Three-time Flat winner; 0-9 as a hurdler but a repeat of his third in a Tramore handicap two runs back shouldn't see him far away; beaten about 11l in another handicap here last time.
Weak 16-1 chance when an early casualty at Roscommon last week; Jack Kennedy rides the yard's other one.
40-1 when tailed off on yielding to soft ground at Roscommon a month ago.

Three runs in Flat maidens for Donnacha O'Brien; best when 6l second over a near-1m2f trip on heavy at Gowran in September; 20-1, jumped moderately and beaten 20l on hurdle debut at Punchestown in December.
Ability in bumpers and ran okay to be a 13l fourth in a 2m3f maiden here a month ago when tongue tied for the first time; each-way claims.
AW winner whose five defeats over hurdles include a laboured handicap debut in March.
British point form nothing to shout about, nor his run in an Aintree bumper; stable/hurdle debut.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won at Cork last July off 1lb lower; hasn't really clicked into top gear yet this term and well beaten on latest but handicapper is at least giving her a chance so can't be totally discounted.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: C&D winner in mares' handicap hurdle in May off 8lb lower; good run in novice hurdle over C&D on penultimate; another solid effort in a Wexford handicap chase on latest; goes well here and should be on the premises again.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden was placed on a few occasions when trained by Tony Martin; modest first two runs for this stable; beaten 45l on latest; cheekpieces now reached for.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles; hasn't really threatened in first three starts in handicaps; beaten 32l at Listowel on latest; eased 3lb but hard to make a case for on all known form.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time Flat winner is 0-5 over hurdles; far from disgraced on debut for this yard at Wexford 11 days ago (2m1f, good); most of best form is on soft ground, however, so could probably do with some rain.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Nigel O'Hare & Mark Devlin & Philip Smith
Spotlight: Much improved second of 14 over C&D last month; was still beaten 10l, however, so place chance but needs to find a bit more to win off the same mark.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ordinary form in a trio of maidens including two at this venue; not obviously well treated for her handicap debut but related to winners so could improve; worth a market check.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-15 over hurdles but runner up on three occasions, including twice over C&D; sent off at short enough odds here the last twice but failed to deliver; place claims if produces best.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won twice at Clonmel last spring on heavy (2m) and good (18.5f); is just 1lb higher now than for the latter win and this trip more suitable than when soundly beaten over 2m6f here last time.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time winner; veteran last scored over 3m at Perth last August; signs of life on latest at Wexford but this trip may be a touch sharp.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden was pulled up when last seen over hurdles in September 2024; similar story in three point-to-points earlier this year; new yard now and tongue tie goes on; watch.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sole success came off 4lb lower at Kilbeggan in April 2025 (2m2f, gd-yld); soundly beaten in two runs since returning from a year off; needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two wins on the Flat but 0-15 over hurdles; didn't run badly in this sphere at Navan in November but pulled up the next day at Fairyhouse and fell at Tramore on return from break latest; others more persuasive.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Just a glimmer of promise in six starts over hurdles; modest form in point-to-points on last three starts; tongue tie goes on for return to hurdling for new yard; likely best watched.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles; two runs this year haven't suggested a trip to the winner's enclosure is imminent; others appeal more.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Didn't show much in two points and can only be watched on rules debut.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Best run was here when beaten a little under 8l in a bumper this May; modest since, including on hurdle debut.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten single-figure lengths in two bumpers at Kilbeggan so looks up to winning races, and the dam won over hurdles.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Three non-completions in points and beaten knocking on a distance on hurdle debut.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 2m4f point winner; ran in these colours when based in Britain, running his best race when third in a Cheltenham bumper in October 2023; later beaten 8l and 12l in two runs over hurdles; back from a long absence but retains some potential on debut for Willie Mullins.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Dead-heated in a point but that was a modest race; headgear applied for rules debut; best watched.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Only 1-16 in points and now 0-13 under rules, including 11 defeats over fences; opposable.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 200-1 when beaten a long way in a 2m4f maiden at Limerick.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Latest run in a Punchestown handicap needs forgiving but runner-up in five of his 11 races over hurdles and brings the best form into this maiden.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Half-sister to 3m hurdle/3m1f chase winner Winnie Woodnutt; dam unraced half-sister to winners Vics Canvas (bumper and useful 2m4f-3m4f hurdle/chase), Art Lord (2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Horse Force One (bumper/2m2f hurdle); likely one to watch on debut.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Point winner who is 0-5 over hurdles and has a mark of 91; shouldn't be good enough unless transformed by cheekpieces.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Half-sister to two winning hurdlers and dam an unraced half-sister to high-class bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Racing Demon; a 4yo filly with all the allowances.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner who won a C&D handicap off 92 here in May; similar performance when runner-up on last month's return visit; looks vulnerable in a race of this nature.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a point and added a 3m maiden at Punchestown in May (good), which is very much his standout performance under rules; has since disappointed at Wexford but in first-time blinkers that are swiftly replaced by cheekpieces; difficult to know what to expect.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Leopardstown maiden hurdle winner (2m, soft) in March and runner-up to the smart Switch From Diesel in a novice two runs back; only seventh in a handicap since then but was beaten far; every chance on these terms.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Well held in two bumpers and she beat a limited 250-1 chance by 2l in a C&D maiden; not sure that entitles her to a mark of 109, but she's a half-sister to the smart Fun Fun Fun, so could have more to offer.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Hooded for the first time when beating 12 rivals in a 2m6f maiden at Tramore (good) and got pestered up front when beaten 5l in a 2m2f novice here a month ago; should give his running and might not be far away back up in distance.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Limited maiden who has failed to complete her last three races; now blinkered.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: In excellent form in 2026; beaten a head at Ayr in March (2m, soft); won easily over this C&D (good) early last month; denied by a neck at Cartmel (2m6f, good) on latest; 12lb higher now than when successful here but can give another good account.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs T Giles-Fahey & Miss S Mangan
Spotlight: In excellent form and has won two of her last three, both over C&D; is now 17lb higher than when winning easily here in May and this is stronger than the race she won last time but clearly loves this place and in the mix again.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Point-to-point winner; not beaten far on handicap debut at Wincanton in October 2024; pulled up when last seen at Southwell in April 2025; has joined top yard; market can guide.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a novice handicap over 17.5f (yielding) here in October; subsequently out of depth in grade 3 and tailed off on return from a break in a handicap at Perth in May; unproven over this far and needs to leave that last run behind.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two wins in 2023 for Gordon Elliott on soft and heavy ground; no joy since but step in the right direction in a handicap chase here on latest (19.5f, good); needs to back that up now back over hurdles.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three wins last season and shaped nicely on return to hurdling at Bellewstown (24.5f, good) 10 days ago; never won off a mark this high but definite place shout dropped back in trip.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has returned from six months off in fine fettle; won at Wexford (2m4f, gd-yld) off 96 and beaten a neck off revised mark of 102 at Punchestown (23.5f, good); go well again off just 1lb higher.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner is just 1-6 over hurdles; running just okay on the level recently and has stamina to prove for this trip now back over timber; others appeal more.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won twice last season, over 19.5f (good) at Clonmel and over 23.5f at Tipperary; well held on return from 306 days off at Punchestown but should strip fitter now, eased 1lb and steps back up in trip; can't discount.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Minor promise in maidens; well beaten on handicap debut at Navan (3m1f, yld-sft) when last seen in December 2024; worth checking in the market on return from long absence.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two-time Flat winner; makes handicap debut over hurdles after not showing a whole lot in qualifying runs; potential improver and needs checking in the market for shrewd yard.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Glimpses of ability in maiden hurdles; only midfield on handicap bow at Leopardstown in December; off since but eased 2lb and has run well fresh before so worth a market check.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
HorseName: Its Time For A Run right
Spotlight: Tailed off in two maidens at Thurles but has since been switched to Emmet Mullins; market informative.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Purchased for 20,000euros since finishing runner-up in two 3m points and should have a future for her leading stable.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Dam useful 2m3f-3m hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to useful winners Mister Hyde (2m4f-3m hurdle/chase) and Glenquest (2m-3m hurdle/chase); a 4yo filly with the allowances and from a respected yard.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
OwnerName: Mrs Gemma Casey & Ms Bernadette Leigh
Spotlight: Half-brother to five winners including Royal Kahala (bumper/smart 2m3f-3m hurdle), Collectors Item (useful 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Robbie Rabbit (point/3m hurdle); unraced dam out of top hurdler Lady Rebecca; worth a market check.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: 28-1 when beaten nearly 20l in a Punchestown bumper and will need to leave that behind.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Finished a close fourth at Kilbeggan (good to yielding) despite finding a bit of bother in running; can improve.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: No worthwhile form in a point, two maidens and latterly in a bumper; cheekpieces fitted.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Second in his first point and held every chance when falling late on in the second; latest effort wasn't so clever but now embarks on rules career with Gordon Elliott.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-brother to useful 2m hurdle/chase winner Pisgah Pike; dam 2m3f hurdle winner (also placed 1m2f-1m4f Flat; RPR 62); he wouldn't be the obvious answer on debut.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: No show in a couple of points and readily opposed here.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten four times but placed in his first three races and was beaten under 4l when ninth of 21 in the Coventry at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm); he'll be difficult to beat with a repeat of that fine effort; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 215,000euros breeze-up buy; showed ability when third in a four-runner field at Carlisle (5f, good to firm); he was the only colt against fillies that day; has to improve plenty if he's to trouble Jaan Ki Tukri.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 150,000gns foal, 210,000gns yearling; by No Nay Never; second foal; dam Group-placed 6f winner (RPR 98), half-sister to useful 6f 2yo/7f winner Elinor Dashwood; has to be of interest.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has finished last in both his races and handicaps will be more like it.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40,000euros yearling and 90,000gns breeze-up buy; brother to German 7f winner Elli Pirelli, closely related to German Group-placed 8.5f 2yo/1m1f winner El Loco (RPR 104); market support would heighten interest on debut.
First two runs were at Ascot and Goodwood; dropped back to 5f when third at Leicester last time (good to firm), running on well in the closing stages; should have a major say here on nursery debut.
His standout run is the close third at Windsor (5f, good to firm) and on that performance this mark looks realistic.
Hasn't shown a lot but handicapped accordingly and a tongue-tie now goes on; market helpful.
Useful pedigree but no closer than 7l in three races over the minimum distance and this mark doesn't look obviously lenient, though Taryn Langley takes off a handy 5lb.
Well down the field in all his races, in cheekpieces in final two starts; has to step up to defy this mark.

80,000gns yearling; went off 28-1 at Southwell (6f AW) but saw her race out well to be third and back in fourth was last week's Newmarket handicap winner Planet Seeker; dropping back to 5f wouldn't be an obvious move, but she should have every chance with improvement surely on the cards.
Had lots of chances now (0-10) and although she's regularly knocked on the door, there have been off days and Pontefract last time was one of those; others preferred.
Becoming exposed after six defeats and two of those were at odds-on, including the latest at Brighton; consistent though and won't be far away provided the new visor doesn't trip him up.
RPRs have regressed in each of her runs and she has no obvious form claims here; had wind surgery and now tongue tied.

OwnerName: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore
Spotlight: A belated debut for this gelding who is out of a 5f winner and sister to Listed-placed 7f/1m winner King's Field; likely best watched.

OwnerName: Int Plywood (lmporters) Ltd And C Mason
Spotlight: Half-sister to 5f AW/6f turf winner Outer Edge (RPR 67); dam 5f-6f winner (80); unlikely to provide the answer to this on her debut.
Finished a remote fifth over 7f at Chepstow (good to firm) and limited appeal after that; drops back 2f.
6f novice winner who is 0-4 in handicaps but was bang there in a Class 4 at York a month ago (7f, good) and has major claims with a repeat of that.
Exposed six-time winner who has performed moderately in her last two races over 1m; on the plus side, she hasn't had many chances at 7f over which she was a close fourth on her comeback.
Has won on the AW and heavy ground (here); only fourth of eight here two weeks ago and that was a Class 6.
He was 1-16 when winning a weak novice at Chelmsford in November (7f, AW); hasn't kicked on from there and yet to get overly competitive in handicaps.

Both wins for Michael Dods were on ground softer than good; picked up for 1,000gns and ran quite well on his stable debut over C&D as he was quite fresh after a break; possibilities if building on that.
Two AW wins for this yard last year but has yet to look as effective on turf (0-6); well beaten on comeback at Lingfield (7f, good).
Both turf wins have come at Doncaster where he beat only one home last time out (7f, good); that was his fourth disappointing run in succession.
No wins in eight; had placed form for Edward Bethell but signed off for that yard under a cloud; would have claims if he can turn a corner for new yard.
Eight-time winner who has slipped to a good mark but last season didn't end well and this campaign has yet to get going; a 9yo now.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Behind Racing Demon last time but was only 4l away over 1m and his sole win was at this trip; can run well.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
OwnerName: Mrs R F Greener And North Farm Stud
Spotlight: Lightly raced but was slowly away on last month's handicap debut (1m, good) and never counted; work to do with Racing Demon and Homeland on that running.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
OwnerName: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward
Spotlight: On a good mark and beaten a neck at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) two runs back, form that has worked out well; poor last time over an extended 1m3f but that trip seems too far.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: The neck defeat on good to soft at Bath (1m) offers hope but lesser efforts have followed; needs one of her good days.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Better for going handicapping when beaten 4l into third at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) and she was keeping on; one of the more appealing options.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Has regressed since a half-promising debut and was nowhere in a classified last time.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Keeps missing the break but he still went close two starts ago at Chepstow and it wasn't a bad run here last time, again over 1m; returning to further looks a good move.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Tailed off in her first handicap but in a first-time visor she only just failed to get up in a 1m2f classified race; if the headgear works again then she should be competitive.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Modest maiden over jumps and hasn't performed well in 7.5f-1m2f non-handicaps on the Flat.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
OwnerName: Lady Bamford , Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster
Spotlight: Cost 290,000gns in 2024; edged out Grand Pier with the pair dominant on her handicap debut (1m2f, soft) and then nearly defied a 3lb rise at Nottingham (good to firm); up another 2lb but she'll surely be in the mix, provided she lasts home over this longer trip.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
OwnerName: Spiers & Hartwell Ltd & Mrs E Evans
Spotlight: Five-time winner; first run since March when a rallying third over C&D three weeks ago; thereabouts.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
Spotlight: On a good mark and knows how to win; however, three of his four runs since a break have been underwhelming.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
Spotlight: Has been running well over hurdles and this dual AW winner hasn't had many Flat chances on turf (0-3); on a competitive mark and Hugh Horgan takes off 5lb.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Smart maiden for this stable at two, made the frame on all four starts, notably when third in the Goffs Million and fourth (demoted from third) in a 1m fillies' Group 3 event; placed in three of her four starts for Brendan Walsh in the US; has returned to Joseph O'Brien, who fit a tongue-tie; some element of risk attached to her eight-race maiden status, but hard to oppose.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Ran well in a 1m4f Limerick claimer; well beaten in two maidens since then; takes a sharp drop in distance now; would receive 25lb from Celtic Motif in a handicap.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Favourite for her debut in a 17-runner maiden at the Curragh last September, broke slowly, recovered to challenge before fading late; questions to answer after a disappointing run at Leopardstown in October; heavy ground may have been against her then.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs Wendy O'Leary
Spotlight: Closely related to Australian Group 2 winner Sixties Groove, half-sister to Flat/hurdle winner Smiling Eliza; shaped quite well when fifth behind year-younger rivals on belated debut in a 7f maiden at the Curragh in March; ground was heavy then. may be better suited by a sound surface.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Tenth foal; Harzand filly is a sister to minor 1m2f winner Harseva, half-sister to four winners, including prolific AW scorer Aramis Grey; dam unraced half-sister to high-class miler Intikab; stable in magnificent form with four winners last Saturday

Concerning that he has failed to build on his debut second behind two Aidan O'Brien-trained colts in a 7f juvenile maiden at the Curragh; ran only twice last season, and his best effort was when second at Gowran on the first occasion; same pattern this season, fair second at Dundalk before falling to improve at Gowran, perhaps a tongue-tie will make the difference
Fair form in maidens at Gowran and Limerick before a disappointing AW run; failed to run up to his handicap mark in an auction maiden at Ballinrobe last time, visored now.

Fairyhouse third (7f, yielding) last September her best effort; unplaced favourite on heavy ground at Galway on her final start last season; official rating of 73 is high enough to put her in the mix in a maiden of this standard.

OwnerName: Mrs Suzanne McAuley & Ms C A Jennings
Spotlight: Winning hurdler; 0-11 on the Flat, second in a Clonmel maiden last season; best of four runs this term when a staying-on third in a C&D maiden; down the field in a Leopardstown handicap; made a lot of the running before fading into fourth in a maiden over an extended 1m1f at Ballinrobe.

First seven runs were on AW, initially for Andrew Balding; placed in three of five Dundalk outings for this stable, including a 3l third over 1m in March; made little impression at Limerick on turf debut; better when fourth over C&D in May; closely matched with Could Dreamer on that running.
Low-rated hurdler; moderate sixth of nine on Flat debut in a 1m5f maiden at Down Royal.
Has a useful Flat pedigree but no sign of ability; tailed off in an academy hurdle last December; dismal run in bumper at this venue in May; held by Coul Dreamer on Ballinrobe running.

Has slipped to a basement mark and has begun to show winning potential; stayed on well for second behind an easy winner at Bellewstown; faces a rematch with third-placed Stonyfalls, as well as Beau Army and Wicklow Gap; holds a leading chance.

Showed ability at two with a third placing in a 7f Down Royal nursery; saddle slipped on her reappearance at Cork; mot beaten far in fifth over a shorter trip at Limerick on Saturday; place possibility with Oisin Murphy up.
Winner over 6f/7f at Dundalk; ran her best race on turf when fourth behind the progressive Meriden three starts ago; needs to reverse recent Bellewstown form with Cause I Like You and Stonyfalls.

Showed some ability on AW over 5f-6f during the winter; failed to beat a single rival in his two races after being gelded; took a step in the right direction at Limerick on Saturday, though Zheleznaya Dama was in front of him then.
Ordinary form in AW handicaps for Gavin Cromwell, much the same at Naas on stable debut; trainer brought off a superb four-timer last Saturday.

Narrowly denied on nursery debut at the Curragh last September (7f, yld-sft); again ran well when a close-up fourth at Dundalk in October; flopped on heavy ground on reappearance; struggled again on good ground at Roscommon; questions to answer.
Not a bad run from a difficult draw at Leopardstown in her first handicap; possibly capable of better.
First of her three runs was her best, form well-advertised by the winner Bobby McGee; down the field at Ballinrobe in her first handicap; a new headgear combination tried.
Failed to make much impact at Naas on handicap debut; hard to make a case for her, unless this shorter trip suits better.
Modest form in maidens, paid the price for racing too keenly in a 1m1f handicap at Leopardstown last time.

Two less encouraging runs since starting the season brightly with a fourth placing at Roscommon; testing ground probably the issue at Leopardstown; less easy to overlook his Bellewstown failure behind Cause I Like You, Stonyfalls and Beau Army then.
Good run at 50-1 when third over 1m on handicap debut at Dundalk in March; has failed to build on that; gelded since his last run.
C&D maiden winner on yielding to soft a year ago; handicap form in unconvincing; in rear at Limerick on seasonal debut in April.

Winner on AW in January; unlucky not to finish closer when sixth at Gowran, short of room when travelling well; backed that up with a promising second at Limerick; failed to make his presence felt when a well-supported favourite at Gowran two days later;  the race may have come too soon.

All three wins have been gained at the Curragh; eight of his 17 places have also come at that venue, including a fine second there on his latest start; not guaranteed to produce his best form, though he managed a second placing here in 2023 on his only visit.

Got off the mark in a Gowran handicap over this trip in May and was a close second at Ballinrobe next time; doubts raised by his two most recent outings; beaten favourite for the Curragh race in which No More Porter was second
Four wins to his name, including a C&D maiden success in 2021; last win was in 2023; fair form last year; no encouragement from this term's four starts
Not one of her better efforts at the Curragh last time; hard to win with; best run this season when second in a six-runner contest; others preferred.
1m1f Leopardstown winner last year for his previous yard; recent seconds at Gowran and Naas have been over longer trips than this; holds a definite chance all the same; Oisin Murphy booked.
Fair form in maidens last year; disappointing on handicap/seasonal debut at Leopardstown; poor run at Roscommon over an extended 1m2f; better when dropped to 7f at Fairyhouse 13 days ago.

Sole win came in a 7f Brighton maiden in 2024; nothing of note in his form since finishing third of seven at Limerick 12 months ago; in rear in this year's edition of that race on Saturday; has been struggling in handicaps.

Has a better strike rate on AW (7-24); however, capable enough on turf (3-24, placed four times) to have a say in a race like this. best of three starts on turf this year when second over 1m at Limerick in April.

Maiden produced back-to-back solid runs at Ballinrobe and the Curragh earlier this season; has failed to reach the same standard on last three starts, the most recent in a claimer; held by Mythical Rock on Limerick running
Two wins in 2025, a 7f claiming maiden at Roscommon (good) and a Naas handicap (7f, good); a long way below his best on seasonal debut at Leopardstown in April.
Showed enough in three AW maidens last winter to suggest he could pick up a race; tongue-tie and cheekpieces on for turf/seasonal/handicap debut.
Second to a front-running winner in a weak 7f Roscommon contest was his best run last season;  absent since a poor AW effort on handicap debut last November.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Showed promise on her Leopardstown debut and followed up with a battling success over 1m1f on easier ground at Gowran; handicapper has not taken many chances with her mark, but there might be more to come over this trip and the homecoming champion jockey is booked.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time winner over 1m with ease in the ground on all occasions, including at this meeting last year; handles good ground too; has never tackled a trip this far, but just failed over 1m1f at Gowran and every chance that she will get it; big player if she does.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
OwnerName: P J O'Brien & Mrs M V Magnier & Mrs P Shanahan & K Blake
Spotlight: Dual winner in France with plenty of cut in the ground. First run here for Joseph O'Brien after nine months off; stable could not be in better form, but a watching brief may be the best policy.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Landed a Dundalk maiden early in the year; has found life a little tougher in handicaps since and was out of her depth in Listed company last time; others preferred here.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
OwnerName: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin
Spotlight: Did just about enough to land a Dundalk maiden in January and did similar when following up in a 1m2f handicap at Leopardstown; a little bit more exposed on her two subsequent handicap efforts under a small penalty, but has the ability to get involved here.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Good servant to her connections; four-time winner from 7f to 1m2f, but very much at home on much softeer ground than she meets today; up against it taking on some promising fillies with younger legs today.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Soft ground winner on debut over 1m2f at Leopardstown in June; overcame some adversity and showed a good attitude; better ground here, but a promising filly and the handicapper looks to have given her a chance.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins over hurdles for Joseph O'Brien and her sole Flat win was in a Tramore maiden over 1m4f last summer; first run since October having changed stables; market will guide but likely to come on for this.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner last year for Richard Hannon; bought to go hurdling but little to recommend in that discipline; best watched on her return to the level.
Five-time winner; travels well in her races and returned to the level in grand style when accounting for the very useful Eagle Fang in a QR contest at Leopardstown; looks a big player here.

Premier handicap winner over 1m2f at Dundalk last season; upped in trip this year to decent effect; good third in the Chester Plate in May; made no impact in the Northumberland Plate last time but will appreciate the calmer waters here.

Undergoing very much a new lease of life, winning three of his last four, and a ride of 17lb could still be manageable; unproven over this trip but in the form of his life and will be popular with Oisin Murphy booked.
Looked a horse of some promise early last season but has failed to progress; coming down the handicap but stepping up to this trip has not improved his record.
Dual winner on the Flat; ran way too bad to bad to be true at Fairyhouse last time but a repeat of his previous effort over this C&D would give him a definite chance here.

Three wins on the Flat and two over hurdles for this versatile performer; most recently landing a 2m4f handicap hurdle at Gowran in October; ground versatile in the past although might just prefer it a bit softer these days.
Dual winner for Charlie Johnston; fair effort on his stable debut in a maiden hurdle here in May; booking of Billy Lee suggests better is expected today.

Very smart performer; acquitted himself extremely well when second in very valuable handicap hurdles at Aintree and Haydock in the spring; just outpointed by the smart Gaoth Chuil in a similar contest at Leopardstown last month for this rider; the one to beat.

Dual bumper winner and maiden hurdle winner; lightly raced individual and unlikely they have got to the bottom of him yet; back from a five month break, but dangerous if the market gives the right signals.
Bumper winner and very useful hurdler/chaser on his day; lost his way last season, but could well play a hand here if he can put it together.
Hunter chase winner at Down Royal in May; well beaten on his first try in a Flat maiden last month; much more needed here.

Recent efforts over hurdles a little underwhelming but earned a mark of 84 after three good runs in Flat maidens last year, including when third in this race; major player if able to run up to that form.
Has ability; not beaten far in a better contest at Leopardstown last month; each-way possibilities.

OwnerName: P Haughey & P M Haughey & John Ball
Spotlight: Won a Cork novice hurdle and a Wexford beginners chase last summer; good run when fifth in a 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden before falling over fences at Wexford two weeks ago; respected.
Soundly beaten in a bumper and two maidens; needs to improve a good deal to be a factor here.
Bumper and four-time hurdles winner; unraced under Flat rules and comes here on the back of being pulled up in the Connacht National at Roscommon; best watched.
Bits and pieces of form; third in a Wexford maiden hurdle last summer and not disgraced at Leopardstown last month; more needed here though.

Better chaser than hurdler, as he showed when winning a handicap chase off 120 at Ballinrobe in May; no impact in a 1m4f Tramore maiden since but this longer trip and more conventional track is more likely to suit; could go well.

JockeyName: Mr W T Ladd Finn right
Spotlight: A very infrequent visitor the track; third in a Curragh maiden in 2022 but a mark of 51 fairly reflects his chance.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Upped markedly in trip with cheekpieces added for nursery debut at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) ten days ago and justified 11-10 favouritism in five-runner race by leading on the post; Tiger In The Tree nearly 2l back in third; up 3lb and bare 7f may be on the short side but this should be a weak field.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Finished a bit closer in each of his three runs, albeit still beaten about 7l when fifth of nine in C&D maiden (good; prominent long way at 66-1) last time; gelded soon afterwards and now wears cheekpieces; needs improvement on this handicap debut.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Twice showed a bit of ability from her four starts, notably on latest when keeping on from the back to be about 2l third of five to Emerald Bay on nursery debut at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm); dropped just 1lb but 5lb claimer and cheekpieces are enlisted; not entirely dismissed.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Best when fifth of eight at 100-1 for nursery debut at Kempton (upped from 5f to 7f, AW) 12 days ago, having hung badly left turning for home; cheekpieces (worn on penultimate start) return; another 5lb claimer takes over; capable of better if keeping far more of a straight line.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Closest finish at Wetherby (7f, good to firm; helped to set pace) on second start, before fourth again when 25-1 again on Newcastle AW (7f); greater market interest might signal she has a chance on this nursery debut.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: D Kilburn, C Wright, F Mahony & Partner
Spotlight: Low-level form on all four starts, 33-1 for latest on her nursery debut on Kempton AW (7f, AW); needs to show something very new.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Last on his first two starts (6f; bit slipped on first occasion) but beating one home in a six-runner maiden at Hamilton (5f, good to soft; 50-1) four weeks ago was a first sign of significant ability; gelded since and makes nursery debut over 7f, which looks much more the ticket on pedigree.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Late foal who's been never dangerous in her three starts, all over 5f; dam was a 2yo 7f winner (RPR 84) and this daughter could be much more interesting now trying that trip on nursery debut; worth a market check.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Tongue tied for nursery debut at Chepstow (6f, good to firm; 12-1) on latest start and it was her closest finish, albeit beaten about 6l in last of seven under Olivia Tubb; blinkered this time; others have more substantial claims.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Six races, runner-up in her only two on AW; improved efforts when blinkered by new yard for last two starts, close second in a maiden at Newcastle latest on the step up to 7f; needs better again on this nursery debut.

62,000euros yearling, 110,000euros breeze-up 2yo; tenth foal; Ghaiyyath half-brother to winners Sir Andrew (7f-1m1f including HK Group 1; RPR 126), Katara (Spanish 1m) and Alejandria (Spanish 7f-1m2f); dam Italian 7f/1m3f winner, out of 7.7f 2yo winner; late foal; the market can guide on debut.

11-2 when making his belated debut in novice at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft) in May, close up 1m before finishing sixth of ten; trainer won this race in 2018, 2020 and 2021; could be a big improver.

OwnerName: Mrs R F Greener And North Farm Stud
Spotlight: 50-1 for maiden at Sandown (1m2f, good) one month ago and slowly away but he made a big move in the penultimate furlong before running out of steam late on, beaten 3l in fifth of six; likely to prove a good deal better and, although stamina is questionable on pedigree, he's one to take seriously.
Fair form when second at Kempton (7f, AW) last October and when beaten just under 6l at Newbury (1m/1m2f, good) on both starts this season; big player.
Slowly away when beaten more than 20l in good-ground maidens at Newbury (1m2f) and Pontefract (1m4f) so he has no form claims.

11l second to the subsequent Royal Ascot flop Water To Wine in novice at Kempton (1m3f, AW) on debut in May; weak 11-1 at Doncaster (1m4f, good to soft) 18 days later, however, and readily left behind from over 1f out to finish a well-beaten last of five; mixed messages.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Gained sole win here last June and beaten a neck over C&D late last month; front-running with first-time blinkers (retained today) at Carlisle five days ago did not work out but he needs a second look on return to Leicester.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Beaten about 3l into fourth of five at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) on her second start but well beaten on starts either side; lacks solid achievements but this is her handicap debut, so early days.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Bagged a hat-trick last summer, including his second C&D win; back to his last winning mark and second at Chepstow (1m4f, good to firm) suggested on penultimate start that he is almost as good as ever; big shout.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up on stable debut at Southwell (1m3f, AW) in February but unable to get competitive since; latest start was a rare turf race and (poor form in this sphere) and overall score is 1-19.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: One win from 14 races; three seconds from five starts this year, beaten a head at Newmarket (2m, good to firm; made most) 18 days ago in first-time cheekpieces; they are now replaced by blinkers; last time he raced over shorter than 1m6f was in June 2025 but this isn't a strong race; good chance.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Nine wins over the years, including two early last summer, but he's been going the wrong way this term; admittedly badly hampered on latest outing; 6lb below his last winning mark.

Off 19 months before this season; her second race back (seventh attempt overall) was her best when off the mark at 25-1 in a classified at Southwell (5f, AW) and in quite taking style too, so she needs a second look despite her handicap mark having been revised upwards in a major way.

OwnerName: T Beeches, C Stone, J Gallagher
Spotlight: Wore this equipment combination on last three starts, beaten a nose in a Brighton handicap (5.2f, good to firm) on first occasion and making all in a classified here (5f, good) on second; wide draw and interference leaving the stalls prevented those tactics at Windsor three weeks ago; 5lb claimer is on board this time; should be of renewed interest.

Two bits of modest form in his five races; last of 11 when 40-1 for last month's handicap debut (6f, soft) seemed to confirm that there are more solid options but, in fairness, it was his first race for 640 days.

Modest maiden after six starts (five for Jack Channon) but she was prominent for a long way under Rose Dawes when fourth of 12 at Newbury (good to firm) five days ago and that was clearly her best show from three handicaps; first attempt at 5f for this drop back in class might also offer some hope.
2-30 overall, with the second win in a classified at Bath (5.7f, good) early last month; ran moderately under the same 7lb claimer at Brighton two weeks ago and may need his best run since last May.

Switched to handicaps when fourth of nine at Brighton (5.2f, good to firm) in May in a first-time visor; no show there (7f) last Tuesday and the visor is now removed; needs a marked overall step forward.

Modest form in her three Irish races and no better in the two in Britain, although she has been more competitive with the switch to handicaps (5.7f/6f); needs to build on that with today's step back up in trip.

Off the mark at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in May and second in two of his last three starts; latest at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) six days ago was just a six-runner race but it looks like he will be bang there again.
Made her mark in 6f/7f Brighton nurseries last season but she's hardly beaten a rival this term; blinkers go on.

Front-runner on last two outings; not proven on firmer than good after three attempts, two before she went handicapping and the other on reappearance; it's therefore possible to forgive those and, after two wins (7f) and two seconds from her last five starts, she needs major respect.
Low-level maiden after seven races, which has still resulted in minor honours in three handicaps over 1m/7f before her form dipped over 6f on latest start; even best form needs improving on.

Ran poorly at Redcar on latest start but went into that in good form, including for a narrow Redcar win; all ten races have been 6f but he looks worth a go at 7f; one to consider even though he may need to bounce back with some improved form.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
TrainerName: Dr Richard Newland & Jamie Insole right
OwnerName: Michael P Tudor & J P M O'Connor
Spotlight: Fair maiden who has taken minor honours in two of her four handicaps, a rallying second of eight at Ripon (1m2f, good) on latest outing; unraced on firmer than good; firmly in the mix if she acts on it.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Switched to turf for last three starts and best of those when winning under Tom Kiely-Marshall over C&D (good to firm) on latest, albeit in a three-runner race; back up 3lb but considered seriously in another small field.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Similar form on all four starts, latest her handicap debut at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) when weakening into fourth of five; tries 1m2f for the first time and needs to find a bit extra.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won two in a row on AW in March and continued in good form on next two starts; considered on that form but beaten 20l on last two outings, on turf debut (1m2f, good) last time.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
OwnerName: J May, R L Field, W Trenchard, J Pook
Spotlight: Sprung a 50-1 surprise in a handicap at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January, for his only win from 21 races, but right out of form since.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 0-21; latest of his three seconds was in February in first-time cheekpieces; slipped leaving the stalls on latest start; has since left Joey Ramsden; being visored first time didn't spark anything on penultimate outing but now it's the turn of blinkers and he has one of the better chances on ratings.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole win from 12 starts came at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January on penultimate outing for James Owen; comparable form in two of six turf races since for new yard and not totally disgraced on latest, so he's not entirely ruled out despite not being proven over quite this far.
