RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Low-level form after six starts, which was nevertheless good enough for minor honours on good ground in two of her three handicaps (6f/5f) and the exception was on soft; unraced on firmer than good; best form running on over 5f on latest start and headgear is now enlisted; needs improvement but given a second look.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 23-race maiden when she turned up for a handicap at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) 17 days ago but she won that seven-runner race, always prominent; that was a clear career best and it's striking that it was her first race at short of 6f; big player if the form is backed up.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Low-level form in her six runs in Ireland for Donal Commins; off 17 months before well held in a 5f classified on AW last month; best watched.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole win (12 races) was in a 6f AW classified in February; switched to turf (good to firm) for his last two outings and needs to produce much better than those efforts.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Chelmsford (6f, AW) in December was his only win from 17 races; three minor honours on good ground this spring, before lesser shows on last two outings; place chance if back to form.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Modest maiden at his best in 2024 and 2025; not so good for new yard this year but latest start, when wearing first-time cheekpieces over 7f, was at least a step back in the right direction.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 9yo who has had 16 wins on AW, in January most recently; sole turf win (from 19 attempts) was at Yarmouth in 2022 but he came within a neck of another in a classified at same track (6f, good to firm) 18 days ago, which was the first time this year that he's worn his usual cheekpieces; has a shout if that form is repeated.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole 2yo run was for Karl Burke; cut little ice that day or in her two recent outings; big drop in class and a visor is given a go, but no form claims.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Evidently well suited by soft turf when displaying improvement to win 1m2f Ffos Las maiden by nearly 5l last month, her third start; on a different surface here but could still have more to offer over the new trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Narrowly won 1m4f Southwell novice in April, her second start; yet to build on that performance in two subsequent handicaps but may not have been suited by making her own running on first occasion and would also have preferred a stronger overall pace when third at Kempton last time; has a bigger run in her if/when there is more emphasis on stamina.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Hasmonean Racing, C Fahy And Marchants
Spotlight: Made all for clearcut success on handicap debut here (9.4f) in January and came second on both subsequent AW starts (9.4f/1m4f); absent since lesser effort on turf two months ago and her tendency to race too freely is a nagging concern but this return to Tapeta is sure to suit and she remains unexposed over the trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Lighty raced for her age and made quite promising seasonal debut when third at Lingfield (1m2f) in April but ought to have been well suited by the good to soft turf when below that form at Ffos Las (1m4f; favourite) last month and has a point to prove here.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who displayed clear promise on all three 2yo starts (1m-8.6f); not obviously well treated for this seasonal/handicap debut but has plenty of scope for improvement over 1m4f.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Safely held when fourth of five in tactical C&D contest on handicap debut in March and not seen since; that defeat is not veiwed that harshly and she may still have potential but others appeal more all the same.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won in good style on last season's reappearance outing (1m2f, good) but was not in the same form on any of her five subsequent runs last year and was safely held when back in action in April; off for another 75 days since and not yet proven over this far; needs to get career back on track.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Gained all three wins at around 1m on turf; yet to hit top gear after three runs turf this season (1m-1m1f) and moves up in trip with a quite bit to prove.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden; left Roger Varian after disappointing run in the autumn and was well beaten on both starts for new stable this spring (1m2f/8.3f; had wind op in between); back up in trip with cheekpieces tried here; big leap of faith required to support her.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Failed to beat a rival home on his debut at Bath (5.5f, firm) last year but stepped forward from that when a 3l second of eight at Yarmouth (6f, soft; winner has won again since) last month; that was his first start for ten months and he's shortlisted on his AW debut.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looked a difficult ride (pulled hard and hung left) on her debut at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) last month and again hung left when keeping on into third at Brighton (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; clearly has ability, but may not be the easiest and stall 12 makes things difficult.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Mayson filly; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Lethimfly (5f 2yo) and Infinity Blue (6f), out of 7f 2yo winning half-sister to HK 1m Group 1 winner Xtension; interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looked green on her debut at Bath (5.5f, good) last month but kept on well to finish fifth off 11 (beaten around 3l); wasn't able to build on that at Southwell (7f, AW) nine days ago but this half-sister to seven winners remains capable of better.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed up well for a fair way before fading into sixth on his debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last month but proved disappointing at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) 11 days ago; drops in trip and makes his AW debut (out of a Listed winner on the AW) with better needed.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing & Alfa Site Services
Spotlight: Missed the break and looked in need of the experience on her debut here over 5f four weeks ago, beating only one rival home; steps up in trip and she's open to improvement.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ran green when fifth of six on her debut at Kempton (5f, AW; 28-1) last April when trained by Mark Usher, failing to settle and hanging left, and has been absent since; best watched unless the market speaks favourably on her yard debut.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed little when last of eight on his debut at Lingfield (6f, good; 66-1) last month and now switches to the AW with a big step forward needed.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ran well to a point on her debut at Lingfield (6f, good) last month but went backwards from that effort when beating only one rival home at Nottingham (5f, good to firm) ten days ago; likely one for handicaps after one more run.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: A'Ali gelding; fourth foal; half-brother to 7.5f/1m2f winner Princess Niyla (RPR 76); dam 9.4f/1m2f AW winner (99), out of unraced half-sister to Group-placed 1m2f winner Tiffany Diamond and 1m2f/1m4f winner Nizamar; market should be informative on his debut.

OwnerName: Jim And Claire Ltd And Jon & Kay Collins
Spotlight: Bettered disappointing seasonal debut when fifth of seven at Goodwood (6f, soft) last month and is now back on same mark as when dead-heating at 80-1 at Chelmsford in the autumn but others have more convincing profiles.
Five AW wins, the latest from the front off 2lb lower at Newcastle (6f) in February; well below that form on turf lately but still worth keeping an eye on in the betting.
Looked as good as ever when close third of 16 on seasonal debut at Ripon (6f, good to soft) in April but his form has dipped since and he has questions to answer on this AW debut.
Dual 6f Southwell novice winner this spring and again ran well when keeping-on second on last month's handicap debut at Kempton; could still have more to offer back on Tapeta; high on the list.
Came good at seventh attempt, making all for clearcut success in C&D novice six weeks ago; contests deeper race on this handicap return but is evidently in good nick; no surprise if he features.

Low-mileage 4yo who won twice over 5f on Tapeta (once here) in the autumn and was placed over that trip on handicap debut at Newcastle in January; not quite in same form on turf last month but that run came after a break and he could quite plausibly have untapped potential over 6f back on AW.

Belied 50-1 odds when going close at Kempton (6f; AW debut) in May, his second start for Mark Loughnane, but was quickly back in the doldrums (albeit on good to soft ground) on next outing; hard to predict.
Showed career-best form when winning at Lingfield (6f, soft) two starts ago and is also suited by this C&D but could be a shade vulnerable off a mark this high.

Managed only fifth of eight at Kempton last month and is now 0-6 for current stable; continues to slip down the weights but his tendency to race too freey remains a concern; swaps cheekpieces for first-time blinkers.
2-4 over 7f for this stable last year but made a low-key return over that trip in May and is not sure to be suited by this drop back to 6f.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a French Listed-placed 7f winner; bettered low-key debut when respectable third of six at Pontefract (6f, good) last month and has possibilities if he makes any further progress over this longer trip.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a useful and well-connected 1m 2yo winner; made promising debut when keeping on from the rear for fourth of ten at Newbury (6f, good) in May and subsequent odds-on defeat at Catterick (7f; hampered) can be excused; sets the form standard here and still has significant potential.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 175,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; third foal; dam unplaced 1m2f turf/1m3f AW (RPR 51), closely related to 1m Listed winner Mooharib, half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Trethias, out of maiden half-sister to 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winner Dar Re Mi; with respected stable; no surprise if he features on debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; dam 1m-1m4f winner (including 2yo/Group 1; RPR 117), half-sister to winners Midas Touch (1m2f Group 2; runner-up in St Leger and Irish Derby) and Streetcar To Stars (useful 1m2f); stable has only occasional first-time-out 2yo winners but this filly has an excellent pedigree.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a half-sister to an Australian 1m6f Group 2 winner; never looked dangerous when midfield in 13-runner Newbury maiden (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago but that still goes down as an encouraging debut and she can improve over this longer trip.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Flopped when warm favourite for Brighton debut in May and showed only minor promise when sixth of seven at Ffos Las (also 6f) a fortnight ago; up in trip for AW debut; improvement required.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £62,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; raced too freely and finished last of nine when 16-1 for recent debut at Lingfield (7f, AW).

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well down the field on both starts (6f/7f); needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 525,000gns yearling; first foal; dam ran twice at 7f (RPR 47), half-sister to winners Royal Champion (KSA 10.5f Group 1) and Outbox (1m4f Group 2), out of 1m4f Group 2 winning half-sister to 7f/1m Group 1 winner Dubawi; with that price-tag and pedigree, she's a newcomer to take very seriously.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 130,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; third foal; half-brother to winners Sun Never Sets (Group-placed 7.7f-1m3f; RPR 106) and Proletariat (US Graded stakes-placed 5f/6f turf including 2yo); dam Spanish 1m/US 1m1f winner (103); interesting pedigree, but it's unnerving to spot headgear on a newcomer.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 14th foal; sister to 7f AW winner Beta Reader (RPR 66), half-sister to four winners including Bedale (1m2f; 107), Big Meeting (1m/1m2f AW; 100) and Ex Gratia (6f AW; 84); dam 5f and 5.5f (Listed) 2yo winner (102), half-sister to Arc winner Bago; well worth a glance in the betting.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a half-sister to triple 5f Group 1 winner Bradsell but always behind when 50-1 for debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; remains best watched.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: No wins in 23 but has been banging at the door in recent months and won't mind returning to 7f.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Dual winner for Roger Varian (7f/1m; AW/good to firm); has slipped to a good mark and he halted the slide in his fortunes with a third at Yarmouth; considered despite an awkward draw.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Has won on turf but better on the AW and may have needed it at Wetherby last time after a break (and wind surgery); perhaps more effective at 6f, though.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
OwnerName: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club
Spotlight: Not well drawn for one who likes to race prominently and last two runs have been underwhelming; others safer.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Both his wins have come on AW, the latest here in June off 5lb lower; current mark will be no excuse and a greater concern would be his last run at Musselburgh.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Three-time winner on the AW in 2025; back in good form after three competitive runs over 7f and quite appealing.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner over 6f; yet to offer much over this far (0-5) and hasn't beaten a rival in his last two races.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Current wellbeing is an issue as he's performed poorly since his win in January; best known for sprinting exploits.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Won over 1m at Southwell in October (1m AW; 5lb lower) but not many positives to draw on since that win; off since January.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Only seventh over C&D last time but she's usually reliable here and was a winner in March; nothing wrong with her mark.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Arrives seeking a hat-trick after 7f turf wins at Musselburgh (good) and Catterick (good to firm), the latter with ease and his best effort for almost two years; still well treated on this first AW start since March and a repeat of either of his last two efforts would make him tough to beat.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 19-race maiden who wasn't too far off her best when under 2l second over C&D latest; appears to lack gears at the business end of her races but that recent form suggests she'll be an each-way player.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Moderate form in her three qualifying starts but improved on entering this sphere at Chepstow (6f, good) latest, when she raced prominently but didn't quite have the pace late on; the step up in trip looks positive for her first start on Tapeta; drops 2lb; not ruled out.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 1m turf winner (soft/good to firm) who is 1-16 on AW (won over 1m on Polytrack); unexposed over 7f but to have a serious chance he'll need to raise the bar based on his last four starts.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: C&D winner when 7lb lower last December; reasonably consistent in five starts since scoring at Lingfield; tongue-tie now tried; each-way player but looks in need of a favour from the handicapper before winning again.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 7f turf winner (good to firm) when with Charles Hills; 0-9 for current trainer but his last two efforts have offered more encouragement; each-way player.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: C&D winner who is without success in over four years; went close over C&D in March when 3lb higher but needs to bounce back after being well beaten (6f, good to soft; first-time blinkers) at Chepstow latest.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 1m turf winner (good) who arrives with a bit to prove over this trip; drops 1lb after his latest 4l fourth at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft); unplaced from six attempts on AW surfaces; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 6f Polytrack winner; on the slide in the handicap for some time; drops again in grade for this first start at the track; has shown he acts on Tapeta at Newcastle and is on a very handy mark in relation to the form he showed there last October; blinkers go on; remains unproven over this trip.

Posted an improved effort when getting off the mark at the sixth attempt at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) 18 days ago, beating a subsequent winner; a 4lb rise looks fair and he's open to further progress, albeit he does have his stamina to prove for this longer trip.

Well beaten in two starts last year at around 1m and it was the same story on her reappearance at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) in May; dam won over 1m4f and she now steps up in trip for her handicap debut.

C&D winner last April off a mark 2lb higher than today's; travelled well but didn't see things out as well as the first two when third at Redcar (1m6f, good; first-time visor) last month and has solid claims returned to the AW.

Showed the benefit of a wind operation when winning a classified event at Windsor (1m4f, good to soft) last month and backed that up with a good second behind an in-form rival at Ayr (1m5f, soft); acts on the AW and she makes the shortlist on this return to handicap company.

Ex-Irish filly who has been in good form since stepping up in trip, winning at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) before following up at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) ten days ago; up 3lb for that latest success but she leaves the impression this trip will suit and could well complete the hat-trick.
Well held in each of her three starts in novice/maiden company (1m/1m2f) but she's well related and may yet do better now stepping up in trip on her handicap debut; makes some appeal.

Sole win from 16 starts came on her handicap debut at Lingfield (1m5f, AW) in December; runner-up on three occasions since, twice over this C&D, but she's been behind Nala The Lioness on her last two starts.

OwnerName: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya
Spotlight: Overcame a wide trip when narrowly winning here over 9.5f in April and posted another good effort when third of 11 here last month; has since left Kevin and Lauren Frost and steps back up in trip, which is a concern, on her stable debut.

Three-time course winner, the most recent coming last March over this trip when trained by James Owen; 0-6 since joining this stable and he was well beaten in a classified event at Newcastle last month.

Beat just one rival home in each of her two starts on the AW for Ralph Beckett (1m3f/1m4f) and she was beaten 16l when last of five at Thirsk (1m4f, good; wind surgery) on her stable debut four weeks ago; much better needed on her handicap debut.

Spotlight: Yet to be beaten and stepped up in trip on every run since her debut in April, beating Zlata the last two times, most recently in a 1m4f Group 3 at Chantilly on good ground; also in front of Pink Panthera before than when winning a 1m3f Listed event here; sure to have more to come and has strong claims.

Won a 1m1f110yd Listed event at Bordeaux Le Bouscat in May on good to soft, but was behind Behrayna and Zlata here over 1m3f on very soft before that; took a major step forward last time, though, when finishing a short-neck runner-up in the Prix de Diane (1m2f110yds, good to firm); anything like that sort of level of performance would make her a big player back up in trip, with Pierre-Charles Boudot on for the first time.

Debut fifth has been followed by three wins, the most recent her career best when landing a 1m2f Listed event here on good to soft, when Proxima Du Centaur was fourth; needs to step up again but unexposed and should improve; first time Colin Keane rides.

Finished over 4l behind Behrayna two runs back when fourth of six and then a shade over 5l behind Dispatches 37 days ago, both races coming here; Ryan Moore an interesting jockey booking on first at over 1m4f but others far easier to make cases for.

Won on her debut over a mile on soft in September and shown steady progression this campaign, although she has been about 2l behind Behrayna on her two most recent runs; stays this trip fine but will need to up her game a bit to get the better of the Francis Graffard filly this time.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Typical Haggas improver who followed his narrow win in the Lingfield Derby Trial with a fine second to Christmas Day in a soft-ground Derby; no issues with the return to a sounder surface and been given plenty of time to get over those exertions; big chance.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Well beaten on soft ground in the Derby but that run flanked by Listed win at Newmarket and a fine second to Causeway (pair a long way clear) in the Group 2 King Edward VII at Royal Ascot; bit more needed but will likely get the run of the race and could go well.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Mostly progressive; followed Group 3 win at Saint-Cloud with a creditable fifth to subsequent Eclipse winner Constitution River in the Jockey Club; shapes as though the step up to this trip will be in his favour - backed up by pedigree - and he'll likely give a good account.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Arc entry who is unbeaten in three starts and turned in best effort when taking the Group 3 Hocquart over 1m3f at this venue in May; this will likely be the quickest ground he's tackled and more needed again in grade but he's a fascinating runner.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Record is one of steady improvement and he turned in his best effort when winning a Group 3 over this trip on quick ground at Chantilly last time; Andre Fabre has won this a record 14 times but more needed to win going up to this grade for the first time.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Improving sort whose best effort came when winning the Queen's Vase, form which was franked at Newmarket last week,; down in trip and up in grade, so more needed if he's to take the winning run to five.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Fourth to Derby winner Christmas Day on his debut but has won his other five starts, with his best effort in refitted cheekpieces when beating Ancient Egypt in a Group 2 at Royal Ascot; decent claims for a trainer who is bidding to win this a sixth time.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won the Group 2 Red Sea Turf (1m7f, good to firm) earlier in the year and followed that with some solid efforts until recording a career best in the 1m4f Hardwicke Stakes last month when cheekpieces were tried; going back up in trip will suit and tongue-tie goes on, so only issue is the 3lb he has to give away to all rivals.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
OwnerName: Qatar Racing Ltd, Ecurie Skymarc Farm & Ecurie Des Monceaux
Spotlight: Displayed plenty of smart form as a 3yo, winning three times, including a 1m4f Listed event, and performing creditably in Group races; shaped okay on only run over a staying trip following a gelding operation over the winter on soft ground and comes into this off a career best, achieved at 1m4f; unexposed at the distance and one of the more interesting ones.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won this in 2024 and a two-time Group 1 winner over 1m7.5f; generally a reliable performer as a stayer and enjoying another good season, his sixth in the Group 1 Prix Vicomtesse Vigier last time not far off his best, so solid chance if running his race.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Improving stayer who heads into this searching for a five-timer having won a 1m7f Listed race at Saint-Cloud recently on good ground; no telling where his improvement will take him but does need more up against some proven Group performers on first start in this company.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Sent off 33-1 when fifth of six in the race 12 months ago (Double Major third), the only other time Cristian Demuro rode, and run creditably at times since; matched his career best two runs ago but, on balance, has something to find being more exposed than some.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won a 1m7f Group 3 here last August on very soft, but not added to that win since, although run plenty of good races over a range of distances, albeit nearly 3l behind Sons And Lovers (getting 2lb) in Saudi Arabia in February; going back up in trip should be a positive and will be thereabouts if producing his best.
